intro windup: The person begins with a light, rhythmic bouncing motion, stepping forward and backward while their arms move in sync with their steps. This movement appears as if the person is initiating a dance.
The figure starts with arms outstretched to the sides, then swings them down and back in a preparatory motion. Simultaneously, the shoulders shrug loosely while the knees remain slightly bent.
A person bounces rhythmically in place by bending and straightening their knees. Their bent arms swing gently forward and backward while their shoulders rise and fall in sync with the movement.
The person bounces lightly, swiveling on their feet and swinging their arms back and forth, while their shoulders move up and down in a loose, rhythmic manner.
A person performs a stylized bouncy walk, taking alternating forward steps while swinging their feet out to the sides. They swing their bent arms in opposition to their legs.
The figure begins moving slowly, performing alternating knee lifts while raising their bent arms upward one after the other.
Starting from a T-pose, the person takes light, rhythmic steps.
The person swings one leg sideways twice while bouncing rhythmically.

The figure's arms swing alternately forward and back with elbows bent and held close to the body. Their shoulders rise and fall in sync with the arm motion, accompanied by a subtle, rhythmic bounce in the knees.

The figure lowers its outstretched arms, swinging them slightly forward and back. Simultaneously, it performs a light, rhythmic bounce in place, swiveling on its feet and loosely shrugging its shoulders.

Starting from a T-pose, the figure lowers its arms and bends them at the elbows in front of the body. Simultaneously, it begins a light, rhythmic bounce by bending and straightening its knees, causing the shoulders to move up and down.
Starting from a T-pose, the person lowers their arms to their sides and stands still.
The person slowly bounces to the rhythm, then begins kicking their legs backward while swinging their shoulders rhythmically.

A figure stands with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height, then drops them to their sides. They vibrate their body slightly and kick each foot out to the side, swinging each arm out in synchronization. They then stop, lean forward slightly, and walk forward.
Starting from a T-pose, the person lowers their arms and stands still.
A person stands and sways rhythmically from side to side. They begin taking light, alternating steps in place as a preparatory movement for a dance.
Starting from a T-pose, the person lowers their arms to their sides and begins gently bending their knees in a subtle, alternating rhythm.
The person takes rhythmic steps, lightly kicking one foot sideways. As they move slowly backward, they bend their torso and then straighten back up.

The individual performs light, bouncy steps, shifting weight from side to side with a slight swivel of the hips and feet. Their arms swing loosely forward and back in opposition to their leg movements.

Starting from a T-pose, the person drops their arms to their sides and steps back with their left foot. They then lean forward and lift their left leg while pointing their arms toward the ground. Next, they straighten up and step forward with their left foot, bending the right elbow to bring the forearm horizontally in front of the torso. Finally, they step backward and lower the right arm to their side.
From a standing position, the person leans forward slightly and then straightens up.
Standing upright, the person rhythmically twists their heels in and out.
While hopping on one leg, the person rhythmically swings their other leg sideways multiple times, then repeats the motion with the legs switched.
cross step: The person steps out with one foot and crosses the other behind it. Their arms swing in sync with the legs, each moving in the same direction as the leg on that side.
Leaning forward, the figure swings their arms in an alternating motion while walking in place with a subtle backward slide.
With arms extended, the person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps out with their right foot, and then crosses their left leg over in front.

The person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps to the side with the right foot, and then crosses the left leg in front of the right. Throughout this movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.
The person crosses their left leg behind their right with their arms extended to the sides, then steps out with their right foot and crosses their left leg in front.

With arms extended to the sides and the torso bent forward, the figure crosses their left leg behind the right, steps their right foot to the side, and then crosses their left leg in front of the right.
Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps the right leg out to the side while holding their arms out wide.

The figure performs a cross-step by bringing the left leg behind the right, stepping out with the right foot, and then uncrossing the left leg to step to the side. Their arms are held wide, moving in slight synchronization with the leg movements.

With arms raised and elbows bent, the person steps forward by crossing their legs. They then kick their left leg forward and pull it back, repeating the motion once more without letting the foot touch the ground.
The figure performs a cross-step, first crossing one leg behind the other before stepping out, then repeating the motion on the opposite side. The arms swing in opposition to the leg movements.

The figure performs a cross-step by crossing the right leg behind the left and then stepping out with the right foot. This pattern is immediately repeated on the opposite side as the left leg crosses behind the right, while the arms move in opposition.

The performer starts with their right leg crossed behind the left, their right arm extended forward, and their left arm bent. They then uncross the right leg and step out to the side, shifting their weight in a fluid motion.
The person crosses their left leg behind the right, steps to the left, and then crosses their right leg in front, keeping their arms bent and raised throughout the movement.
The person starts leaning forward with their left leg crossed behind the right. They then step out to the left and cross their right leg in front.

A person performs a Charleston cross step, starting with their left leg crossed behind the right. They step their left leg out to the side and then cross it in front of the right leg while keeping their arms extended outwards.
The person crosses their right leg behind the left and steps the left foot out to the side while keeping their arms extended with slightly bent elbows.
The person steps out to the side with one foot and immediately crosses the other leg in front. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs throughout the movement.
The person swings their arms and steps forward with one foot, then crosses the other foot in front while moving from side to side.

The figure executes a cross-step, first crossing the right leg behind the left and stepping out with the left foot. To complete the sequence, the right leg crosses in front of the left while the arms are held up and bent, swaying with the motion.

Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps the right foot out to the side and then immediately crosses it in front of the left, swinging their arms forward and back in opposition to the leg movements.
The figure starts by crossing the left leg behind the right with arms extended, then steps the right foot to the side and immediately crosses the left leg back in front.

A person kicks one leg forward, crossing it over the other in a Charleston-style motion while their arms swing in opposition to the rhythm. They then execute a clockwise spin, keeping the left leg forward and the right leg behind with arms held straight up for balance. After completing the spin, they resume the cross steps.

Starting from behind the left leg, the right leg steps out to the side and then crosses over in front. As the leg crosses, the left arm swings forward and the right arm back while the body leans to the left.
The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps to the right, and then crosses their right leg back in front of the left, all while keeping their arms extended outward.

The person starts with their right leg crossed behind their left, steps their right foot out to the side, and finishes by crossing their left leg in front of the right while holding their arms bent and out to the sides.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps out to the left and finishes by crossing the right leg in front of the left with their arms bent and raised.
The person slowly crosses their left leg in front of their right. They then step their right leg out to the side to uncross them and repeat the motion on the opposite side.
The person alternates crossing one leg behind the other and stepping out to the side. As the right leg crosses behind and steps out, the left leg follows in a continuous motion.
A person performs cross-steps forward while swinging their arms rhythmically. Starting from a low position, the arms gradually move upward, ending with the elbows swinging.
The person rhythmically crosses one leg in front of the other, then steps the other leg out to the side to uncross.
The person starts with their right leg crossed behind the left, steps the right foot out to the side, and then crosses it in front of the left while holding their arms out to the sides.
The person steps forward, rotating their torso from side to side and swinging their arms in coordination with the opposite legs.

The figure crosses the left leg behind the right, steps the right leg out to the side, and then returns the left leg to the front. Throughout this sequence, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The person kicks one leg forward and crosses it over the other to take a step, repeating these motions. Simultaneously, the arm opposite the kicking leg swings forward while the other arm moves back, as the torso twists slightly in rhythm.
A person performs a side-stepping dance with their arms extended and shaking. Throughout the motion, they rotate continuously while moving in a circular path.
The person walks forward, crossing each foot over the midline while continuously twisting their hips.

The person performs a cross-step by alternately crossing one leg behind and then in front of the other, followed by a step out to the side. With elbows bent, the arms swing in opposition to the leg movements as the body leans in the direction of the step.
The person crosses their right leg behind their left and steps out to the left, maintaining a low crouch with their arms bent and raised throughout the movement.
A person walks forward with a pronounced hip twist, swinging their arms in opposition. They then transition into jumping and walking, throwing their arms out to the sides.

The performer crosses their left leg behind their right and steps out with the right foot. They then uncross the left leg while keeping their arms extended to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.
Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps out to the right and then crosses the left leg behind the right foot while swinging their arms in opposition.
A person bends forward slightly with their elbows bent in front of them. They move sideways while rotating to face the side, then swing their arms at their sides and kick their right foot forward.

With arms raised and extended, the person starts with their right leg crossed behind the left. They then step the right leg out to the side and cross it in front of the left leg, shifting their weight as they move.

The figure crosses their right leg behind their left and then steps out to the side with the left leg. Throughout the movement, the arms are held bent at shoulder level, moving in opposition to the legs.

With arms extended to the sides for balance, the person crosses their left leg behind their right. They then step the left leg back out to the side, shifting their weight in preparation for the next movement.
The figure performs a cross step by crossing the right leg forward over the left, followed by the left leg stepping out to the side. They repeat this motion side-to-side by alternating legs.
A person repeatedly steps from side to side, crossing one leg over the other.

Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the performer steps out to the side with the left foot. As the left foot lands, the right leg swings forward and across the body while the arms swing in opposition with bent elbows.

The individual rotates 90 degrees counterclockwise and steps back, swinging their arms in coordination with the opposite leg. They then turn counterclockwise to the side and step forward, swinging their arms upward as they move.

Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps the left leg out to the side and crosses the right leg in front. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.

Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the individual steps the left foot out to the side and finishes by crossing the right leg behind the left while lowering their arms from an extended position.

The figure crosses the left leg behind the right, steps out with the right foot, and then steps out with the left to uncross. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, and the torso remains relatively still.

The performer crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the right, and then brings their left leg across the front. Their arms swing in opposition to the leg movements, starting with the right arm forward, followed by the left.

The figure performs exaggerated cross-steps, stepping one leg diagonally across the other while swinging their bent, raised elbows. This pattern continues as the figure smoothly transitions into a circular motion.

The person hops on one leg while kicking the other across the front. Upon landing, they either twist their hips or take a few steps. Throughout the sequence, the arms are raised and bent, swinging and twisting with the body’s motion and occasionally stretching outward during the steps.

A person performs a Charleston cross-step, moving from side to side. They alternate crossing one leg in front, stepping out, and crossing the other leg behind. Their arms are held up and swing in opposition to their legs.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides, bent at the elbows. They bend slightly and shuffle a little bit from side to side while alternating swinging their arms as if swimming freestyle, before standing up straight.
The figure crosses their right leg behind the left, steps out to the left, and finishes by crossing the right leg in front of the left, with the arms swinging in opposition.
A person crosses their right leg behind their left and steps out with the left foot. Their arms swing in opposition as the right leg moves forward to repeat the motion.

The person steps their left leg forward across the right and turns their body by stepping forward with the right leg. They then rotate counter-clockwise by stepping the right leg forward and twisting their body, while holding both arms out to the sides with bent elbows.
The person steps forward, crossing one leg over the other while moving, then performs a single spin by untwisting their legs.

The performer executes a Charleston cross-step, bringing the left leg behind the right with a slight kick. The arms are held out to the sides for balance throughout the sequence of crossing, stepping out, and crossing back over.

Standing upright with arms bent in front, the person moves from side to side, swiveling their hips and shifting their knees in tandem. After two steps, they kick their outer leg and move in the opposite direction.
The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps out to the side while extending their arms, and then crosses their right leg back in front of the left.
The figure crosses the right leg behind the left, then steps the left foot out to the side. As the right leg uncrosses to step forward, the bent arms swing in opposition.
The person crosses their left leg behind the right, shifting weight onto the right foot. They spread their arms wide, holding the right arm higher than the left.

The performer crosses their right leg behind their left, bending both knees to lower their stance. Their arms swing in opposition, with the left arm moving forward and the right back, creating a twist in the torso.
The person crosses their left leg behind the right, steps out to the left, and then crosses their right leg in front, keeping their arms extended to the sides with elbows bent.
The person puts one leg in front of the other and taps twice, then repeats the motion with the other leg.
The individual crosses their left leg behind the right, leaning their torso slightly forward and to the right. They extend their arms to the sides, with the left arm held higher than the right.

The figure crosses the right leg behind the left, steps out with the left leg, and then crosses the right leg over the front. Throughout the sequence, the arms swing in opposition to the leg movements.

Starting with arms extended to the sides, the individual crosses their right leg behind their left while leaning their upper body to the left. They then step out with the left foot and cross the right leg in front of the left.

The figure starts with the left leg crossed behind the right and arms outstretched, then steps the left foot out to the side. As the right leg crosses in front, the arms simultaneously swing inward into a bent position.

The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the side with the left foot, and then crosses the right leg in front. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the left side, and then crosses the right leg in front of the left. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out with bent elbows as the torso twists in coordination with the leg crossings.
The figure crosses the left leg in front of the right while swinging the right arm forward in opposition, lowering the body slightly and twisting to the right.
The person moves forward, placing their left leg in front of their right leg.
A person repeatedly hops forward in a fixed split stance, with one leg extended forward and the other back, while keeping their arms out to the sides for balance.
The individual steps forward with one leg while swinging the opposite arm forward, then repeats the motion with the other leg and arm.

Leaning slightly forward, the person performs a Charleston cross-step by crossing their right leg behind the left, stepping out with the left, and then crossing the right leg back in front, while moving their arms in opposition to their legs.
The person performs cross steps with slight hops in a bouncy rhythm, maintaining a crouched torso while swinging their arms back and forth.
The performer crosses their right leg behind their left, sinking into a low stance with bent knees. They then step the right foot out to the side to uncross, keeping their arms extended for balance.

The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps to the left, and then crosses their right leg in front. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out to the sides with bent elbows, keeping the left arm raised higher than the right.

Starting from a stance with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps to the left and then crosses the right leg in front, swinging their arms in opposition to the movement of their legs.

The person performs alternating cross steps, crossing one leg behind the other with a side step in between each. Throughout the movement, the torso remains hunched forward while the arms swing in rhythm with the steps.

A person performs a cross step by first crossing their left leg behind their right. The left leg then steps out to the side before crossing back in front of the right leg, shifting their weight accordingly.
The person crosses their left leg behind their right and steps their right foot out to the side, then uncrosses their left leg back toward the center while keeping their arms bent.

The person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps out to the side with their right foot, and then crosses their left leg in front. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, swinging in opposition to the leg movements.
Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps out into a wide stance and then crosses the left leg behind the right, swinging their arms in opposition to the leg movements.

A person performs a cross-step by crossing their right leg behind their left while swinging their arms for balance. They then step their left foot out to the side, uncrossing their right leg and bringing it back to the front.
The figure crosses their left leg behind the right and steps out to the left, shifting their weight. They then cross their right leg in front as their arms swing in opposition.
The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps out to the right, and then crosses it back in front. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, moving in sync with the footwork.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps out to the left and then crosses the left leg in front of the right as their arms swing in opposition.

The dancer crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the side with the left foot, and then crosses the right leg in front. Throughout the movement, the arms are held with bent elbows and move in opposition to the legwork.

The individual crosses their left leg behind their right, then steps the right foot out to the side. This movement is paired with opposite arm swings, with the left arm moving forward as the right leg steps out.
The figure crosses their right leg behind their left, twisting their torso slightly. They then step out to the left while keeping their arms extended to the sides.

The figure stands with their arms out to the sides and their left leg in front of their right. They lean back slightly and kick their right foot forward, adjusting their arms as they do so. They then run a few steps forward and turn 90 degrees clockwise, dropping their arms to their sides. Next, they walk two steps forward and rotate counterclockwise to face the front again, throwing their arms straight out to the sides. They then step forward and rotate 180 degrees clockwise, dropping their arms to their sides with the elbows bent and the forearms pointing forward. Finally, they walk forward for two steps and rotate counterclockwise, with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent so that the forearms point downward.

The person crosses their left leg in front of their right and then steps out to the right side. Simultaneously, their arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving in a circular motion. The sequence ends with a forward kick of the right leg across the left leg.
Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the individual steps the right leg out to the side while keeping the torso bent forward and both arms raised with bent elbows.
The person crosses one leg behind the other, steps out, and then crosses back over.

The individual crosses their right leg behind their bent left leg, touching their right toes to the ground. Simultaneously, they raise their arms in a V-shape with bent elbows, holding the left arm higher than the right.
The person crosses their left leg in front of their right.

The person crosses their left leg behind the right, steps out with the right foot, and then crosses the left leg in front. Throughout this sequence, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving in coordination with the footwork.

A person performs a cross step by crossing their left leg behind their right, stepping out, and then crossing the left leg back in front. Throughout the sequence, the arms are held up with the elbows bent.

The individual crosses their right leg over their left, sinking into a low, bent-knee position. They then uncross and extend their left leg to the side, keeping their arms outstretched and looking downward.

A person stands with their right arm pointed upward and their left arm at their side as they step to the side, swinging their left leg in a semicircle. They then turn to face the other side, lowering their right arm to their side.

A person performs a Charleston cross-step by crossing their right leg behind their left, stepping out with the left foot, and then crossing the right foot over in front. Throughout the movement, their arms are extended to the sides for balance, with the right arm held slightly higher than the left.

Starting with slightly bent knees and arms extended to the sides, the person crosses their left leg behind the right, steps out with the right foot, and then crosses the left leg back in front to complete the sequence.
The person crosses their right leg in front of their left while their whole body performs a circular motion.
The person steps their left foot to the side while crossing the right leg behind it, swinging the right arm forward and the left arm back to twist the torso.

The performer crosses their left leg behind the right, steps to the left, and crosses the right leg in front. Throughout the movement, the arms are held in a raised, bent position, with the left arm higher than the right.
The figure steps from side to side, crossing one leg over the other with each step. The arms are held out to the sides in a stable, bent position for balance.
The figure starts with the left leg crossed behind the right, then steps out to the side. Next, the right leg crosses in front of the left as the arms swing in coordination.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps the left foot to the side and then crosses the right foot in front of the left, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.

The figure crosses the left leg behind the right, steps out with the right foot, and then crosses the left leg back in front. Throughout the movement, the arms are held raised, with the left arm mostly extended and the right arm bent at the elbow.
A person crosses their right leg behind their left and steps out to the right while swinging their arms down and forward from an outstretched position.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps out to the left. As the left foot plants, the right leg crosses in front while the arms swing in opposition to the legs.

A person performs a cross-step by crossing one leg behind the other and then stepping the front leg out to the side. The motion concludes as the back leg is brought forward to cross in front, all while the arms are held out for balance.

A person walks in a circle, crossing their right leg behind their left and then stepping forward and out with their left leg. They sway their hips while keeping both arms extended to the sides with slightly bent elbows.

The person performs a Charleston cross-step, beginning by crossing their right leg behind their left. They then step out to the side with their left foot and cross the right leg back over in front, keeping their arms extended for balance.

The person performs a cross step by pivoting on the left leg and crossing the right leg behind, accompanied by a circular rotation of the body. Throughout the pivot, the left arm is raised and bent while the right arm swings slightly in front of the body. The motion is then repeated on the opposite side, switching legs and arms.

The person crosses their left leg behind the right, steps their right foot out to the side, and then crosses their left leg in front of the right, keeping their arms extended outwards throughout the motion.

The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps to the side with their left foot, and then crosses their right leg in front. Throughout the movement, their arms remain extended to the sides for balance.
The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps out to the side with their left leg, and then brings their right leg back to meet the left while keeping their arms extended.

The figure stands with their left arm extended to the side at shoulder height and their right arm bent with the forearm in front of the body. They step sideways, crossing the left foot in front of the right and then stepping the right foot out to the side, before walking sideways in the opposite direction while maintaining their arm positions. They walk sideways again and repeat the turn to return to the center, then step forward and walk in a clockwise circle while keeping their arms in the same position.
The person gently kicks their right leg forward while extending both arms straight backward. As soon as the right leg is brought back, they perform a cross-side step.

The person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps their right foot to the side, and then crosses their left leg back in front. Throughout the movement, they keep their arms bent and raised near shoulder level.
The person crosses their left leg over their right arm and rotates clockwise.
The person shuffles their feet while rotating.

The individual performs a cross step, crossing the left leg behind the right, stepping out with the right, and then crossing the left leg back in front. The arms swing in coordination with the leg movements, with the right arm moving forward as the left leg crosses behind.
With arms extended to the sides, a person crosses their left leg in front of their right, steps to the right with their right foot, and then crosses their left leg in front again.
With arms extended to the sides, a person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps the left leg out to the side, and then finishes by crossing it back in front of the right leg.
The person steps out with their left foot.

The individual steps forward with the left leg and performs a quick kick with the right leg beside it, then steps the right foot to the side. Simultaneously, the torso shifts to the side and the arms swing slightly.

The person crosses their left leg behind the right, steps out with the right foot, and then crosses the left leg back in front. Throughout this sequence, their arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.
The person crosses their right leg behind the left with their right arm extended forward, then steps their right leg out to the side as their arms switch positions.

The individual performs a cross-step by crossing their left leg behind the right and stepping the right foot to the side. They then uncross the left leg and step it to the side while keeping their arms extended.

The individual crosses their left leg in front of the right and steps out to the right. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows, swinging gently from the forearms.

The performer crosses their left leg behind their right, twisting their torso and swinging their right arm forward in opposition. They then uncross the leg and step it back out to the side as their arms return to a neutral, open position.
A person performs a Charleston cross step, crossing their left leg behind their straight right leg. Their arms are extended to the sides with slightly bent elbows to maintain balance.
The person jumps forward and turns around immediately upon landing.
Standing upright, the person steps forward with their left foot, followed by their right, while turning to face the side. Their arms swing slightly at their sides with elbows bent.
The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps out with the left foot, and then crosses the left leg behind the right, keeping their arms extended to the sides throughout the movement.

A person performs a cross-step by crossing their right leg behind their left, stepping out with the left foot, and then crossing the right leg back in front. Throughout the movement, their arms swing reciprocally to maintain balance.
The person performs a cross step, bringing their right leg in front of the left while twisting their torso to the left. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides to maintain balance.
The person steps forward onto their right leg and immediately jumps into a turn.

The person takes long, quick strides forward with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent. They then turn to the side, bending over slightly while moving their left leg and both arms forward.

A person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps it out to the side, and then crosses it back in front of the right leg. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out wide with slightly bent elbows.

The figure performs a Charleston cross-step, first crossing the left leg behind the right and then stepping out with the right foot. To complete the move, the left leg crosses back in front of the right while the arms are held out to the sides.

A person stands with their arms bent at the elbows by their sides. They swing their left foot forward and their right foot to the side, rotating their body in a circle. They then take another step with each foot along the circular path while lightly swinging their arms.
A person begins by crossing their left leg behind their right with their arms bent and held out to the sides. They step the left foot out to uncross, then cross it back in front of the right leg.
While maintaining a wide right-leg lunge stance, the person moves sideways with their torso leaning forward for balance, then finishes the movement by kicking their right leg forward.

The person performs a Charleston cross step, crossing their right leg behind their left while maintaining an upright posture. Their arms move in opposition, with the left arm bent and raised high and the right arm bent and held lower.
A person steps to the right, crossing their left leg in front of their right. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance, adjusting slightly as the torso twists with the movement.

The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the side with the left foot, and then crosses the right leg in front. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward as the right leg crosses behind.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps their left foot out to the right while swinging their arms back and forth.

A person performs a Charleston cross step by crossing their right leg behind the left, stepping out to the side, and then crossing it back in front. Throughout the movement, their arms are held bent at shoulder level, swaying slightly with each step.

The individual performs a Charleston cross step, crossing the right leg behind the left, stepping out with the left leg, and then crossing the right leg back over in front. The arms swing in opposition to the leg movements throughout the sequence.

Angled to the right, a person crosses their right leg behind their left with both knees bent, then steps out to the side, kicking their right leg in front of their left. They keep their arms extended straight out to the sides for balance.
The performer stands on their right leg, crosses it in front of the left, and then swings it to the side while their arms swing forward.
A person stumbles and breaks into a diagonal run, leaning their torso slightly forward and swinging their arms back and forth to maintain balance.

The individual performs a Charleston cross step, starting by crossing their right leg behind the left while extending their arms to the sides for balance. They complete the sequence by stepping out and then crossing the leg back over in front.
The person performs a Charleston cross step by crossing their right leg behind the left, stepping out with the left foot, and then crossing the right leg back in front of the left.

The individual stands with their knees bent and arms raised, elbows flexed. They lower their forearms and rotate clockwise on their right foot, pushing their arms slightly forward while keeping their elbows bent.
Cross the left leg in front of the right leg and then rotate.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps the left leg forward and out to the left. Simultaneously, the upper body twists to the left with both arms extended to the sides.
The person steps sideways by lifting their left leg and landing it to the side, while their arms swing inward from the outside.
A person performs a cross-step, starting with their left leg crossed behind the right. They then uncross their legs by stepping the left foot out to the side while swinging their arms slightly.
The individual performs a Charleston cross step, starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, then steps out to the left and finishes by crossing the right leg over in front.

The person cross-steps their left leg in front of their right while swinging their arms. The arm movements follow a sequence of both arms moving forward, followed by the right arm moving sideways, and finally the left arm moving sideways.
Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps out to the right while swinging their left arm forward and right arm back in opposition.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person steps their left foot out to the side and swings it across the front of the right leg while holding their arms out.

The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the side with their left foot, and then crosses the right leg back in front. Throughout the movement, their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to their legs.
The person bends down and crouches to the side, crossing one leg in front of the other.
The individual crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the side with the left foot, and then crosses the right leg in front, while the upper body remains stationary.

A person stands slightly bent over with their arms pointing forward. They step forward with the right leg, then the left, while swinging their arms at their sides. They then jump back and to the right, throwing their right arm up and keeping their left arm bent at the elbow with the forearm in front. Facing 90 degrees clockwise from their original orientation, they swing their arms at their sides with their elbows bent.
Starting with the left leg crossed behind the right, the person swings it out to the side and slightly forward, holding their arms out to maintain balance.

The person performs a Charleston cross-step, crossing their right leg behind the left, stepping the left foot out to the side, and then uncrossing the right leg. Throughout the movement, the left arm remains extended to the side while the right arm is bent.

The person stands upright with their right arm at their side and their left arm in front, elbow bent and forearm across the torso. They turn 90 degrees clockwise and step forward with their left foot, swinging the left arm down to their side while bringing the right arm forward with a bent elbow.

A person crosses their left leg behind their right and steps out with their right foot while keeping their arms raised and bent. They then uncross their left leg, bringing it back to the left side to complete the step.
The individual crosses their right leg behind the left, steps the left leg out to the side, and then crosses the right leg in front of the left while their arms swing in opposition.
A person stands with their arms pointing straight to the right. They swing their arms to the left, then bring them to their sides and slide to the right, lifting their left knee.

Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps the left foot out to the right side. As the legs uncross, the right arm is raised and bent, while the left arm is held in a lower, bent position.
Viewed from the side, a person steps their right leg out from behind the left and then crosses their left leg in front of the right while keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.

The individual performs a Charleston cross-step, crossing their right leg behind the left and stepping out to the side. They then complete the motion by crossing their left leg in front, while their bent arms swing in opposition.

Starting with arms outstretched, the person crosses their left leg behind the right and steps out with the right foot. They finish the sequence by crossing the left leg back in front while bringing their arms down and forward.
The person crosses their left leg behind their right, then steps back out to the left. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left leg crosses back.

Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps out to the side with the right leg. Simultaneously, the left arm is held in a bent position near the head while the right arm extends outward.

The dancer performs a cross step by alternately crossing one leg behind the other, stepping out, and repeating the motion with the opposite leg. The arms are held bent near the waist and swing outward as the movement progresses.
The person places one foot behind the other and then rotates.

Standing on the left leg, the individual has their right knee bent and pointed back while their left arm is raised straight up. They step down onto the right foot and rotate 180 degrees counterclockwise, swinging the left arm down to the side and punching forward with the right arm.
The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps to the left, and then crosses their right leg back in front. Throughout the sequence, their arms swing in opposition to the leg movements.
The person crosses their left foot in front of their right foot.
The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps to the side with the left foot, and then crosses the right leg in front of the left while the right arm swings slightly forward and back.

The person performs a cross step by crossing their right leg behind the left, stepping the left foot out to the side, and then bringing the right leg forward to cross in front of the left as their arms swing in opposition.
A person stands with their arms pointed to the right and slides in that direction, then spins around and slides back, ending with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent.

A person stands upright with their arms raised to shoulder height and elbows bent, positioning their forearms in front of them. They move their hands together and then apart while taking two steps back.

The person crosses their right leg behind their left, steps out with the left foot, and then brings the right leg forward to cross over in front. Throughout the movement, the arms are held bent and away from the body.
The person slides to the right and crosses their left leg in front of their right.
The individual stands slightly bent over with their arms pointing down, then walks in a small circle while occasionally pushing their arms outward.

The figure performs a Charleston cross-step, crossing the right leg behind the left, stepping out, and then crossing it back over in front. The arms are bent and swing in opposition to the leg movements, creating a twisting motion in the torso.
The person crosses their left leg in front of the right and then steps out to the side.

A person starts in a deep bend with their knees flexed, touching the ground with their right hand while extending their left arm backward. They bring their left hand down to the floor next to the right and shift their left foot. Then, they rise and step both feet back, extending their arms to the sides with bent elbows while remaining slightly bent over.

The person stands in a wide stance with knees bent, leaning forward with their arms held in front, elbows bent, and forearms positioned before the torso. They then rise up and spin their arms while rapidly rotating clockwise.

A person performs a Charleston cross-step, crossing their right leg behind their left while in a crouched, forward-leaning position. Their arms are bent in opposite directions—left arm forward and right arm back—creating a dynamic, twisted posture.

The performer crosses their left leg behind their right, then steps out to the left and shifts their weight. Their arms are raised and bent in opposition, with the right arm held higher than the left.

The figure performs a cross step by crossing the right leg behind the left, stepping out with the left foot, and then crossing the right leg over in front. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward during the back cross and the right arm moving forward during the front cross.
The person performs side-to-side cross steps with swinging arms. Between the cross steps, they twist their knees from side to side to glide sideways.
The person crosses their right leg in front of their left while kicking the left leg back, then steps out with the left foot as their arms swing in opposition to their legs.
A person performs a Charleston cross step, starting by crossing the right leg in front of the left.

A person stands bent over with their elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They pivot 180 degrees clockwise on their right foot, stepping their left foot around, and then repeat this motion while their arms swing at their sides.

Starting with the right leg crossed behind the left, the person steps the right foot out to the side. As they move, the arms swing in opposition, with the left arm moving forward and the right arm back.

The person crosses their right leg behind the left, steps out to the side with the left foot, and then crosses the right foot in front of the left. Their arms remain extended to the sides for balance throughout the movement.

The individual performs a cross step to the left by crossing one foot behind the other and then stepping out. As this step completes, they clap their hands in front of their body while swinging the right leg out to the side with a bent knee. The person then moves to the right with a cross step.
The dancer crosses their left foot in front of their right.

The individual stands slightly bent over with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms in front. They rapidly rotate 180 degrees counterclockwise and step forward with their right foot. They then swing their arms in front of their body and straighten up, pointing their bent arms upward as they step forward.

The individual steps out with their left foot while alternately punching both arms forward. They then walk forward, turn around, and walk in the opposite direction with their arms bent at the elbows and held at their sides.
Step the left foot across in front of the right leg, rotate, and then hop.

Leaning slightly forward, the individual crosses their left leg behind their right while swinging the right arm forward. They then step their left foot out to the side, swinging the left arm forward in opposition.

A person steps to the right with their left leg, crossing it over the right leg. As they step, the left arm swings out straight to the side while the right arm moves inward toward the chest. Then, the person takes a step to the right with their right leg, uncrossing their legs. The sequence ends with the left knee bent and the right leg extended straight to the side.
A person crosses their left leg behind their right, steps out with the right foot, and brings the left leg forward while swinging their arms in opposition to the leg movements.

The individual steps their left leg out from behind the right and then crosses their right leg in front. Throughout the motion, the arms swing in opposition, with the left arm moving forward as the right arm moves back.
knocky knees: The dancer bends their knees and rhythmically twists their legs, alternating between bringing the knees together and moving them apart. The upper body remains upright with arms extended to the sides.
A person bends their knees and swivels their lower legs, causing the knees to move inward to touch and then back out as they slowly move to the side. Their arms swivel in tandem with the legs.

A person stands in a crouched position, rhythmically moving their knees inward and outward so they nearly touch before separating. As they shift from side to side, they move their bent arms in coordination with their leg movements.

The figure leans forward in a scarecrow pose with bent arms, rhythmically shaking their legs inward. They finish with a small jump, swinging their arms forward and up before returning to the starting position.
Standing on their toes with arms bent and raised, the person rhythmically swivels their knees inward to touch and then outward again.
From a squatting stance, the person rhythmically swings their knees in and out while slowly circling their straight arms in large arcs.
In a squatting position, the person rhythmically swings their knees side to side while holding both arms extended straight out to the sides.

A person bends both knees, bringing them together and then moving them apart. They repeat this motion rhythmically while slowly moving sideways. During the movement, the person performs a single right-leg kick and then changes their direction.
Starting with a small jump, the person pulls their arms in and settles into a low crouch. They rhythmically knock their knees together and apart, waving their arms at their sides to maintain balance.

A person holds their right hand on their hip and the other above their head, swaying from side to side while moving backward and slowly lowering the raised arm. Once the raised arm reaches hip level, they swing both arms out to their sides.
The person steps back with their left arm raised. Simultaneously, they twist their knees inward and outward while leaning their upper body slightly forward to maintain balance.
A person performs a knock-knee move by rhythmically bringing their bent knees together and apart while keeping their arms straight behind them.
A person walks forward, keeping their upper arms at their sides while swinging their forearms from the elbows.
A person steps forward with bent knees, swaying their hips from side to side. Their arms are raised to shoulder level with bent elbows, moving in sync with the hip motion.

The performer executes a "knock-knee" Charleston step by keeping their feet in place while rhythmically moving their knees together and apart. Throughout the motion, the arms are held out to the sides.
With arms extended to the sides, the performer bends their knees and swivels them inward and outward by pivoting on the balls and heels of their feet.

Standing with knees bent, a person rhythmically twists their hips and torso from side to side while keeping their feet planted. Their arms swing in the opposite direction of the hip movement, creating a dynamic contra-body motion.

Standing with bent knees, a person rhythmically twists their legs to bring their knees together and then apart in a continuous knocking motion. The upper body remains relatively upright and stable throughout the movement.

The individual rhythmically bends their knees inward until they meet, then moves them back apart. This movement is accompanied by a slight torso twist and a small, synchronized swing of the bent arms.

The figure performs a knock-knee motion, rhythmically bending the knees inward and then outward. The arms, bent at the elbows, mirror this movement by swinging toward the body's centerline and back out in sync with the legs.
With arms held out to the sides, the individual rhythmically bends their knees, bringing them inward to touch and then moving them apart, repeating the sequence in a fluid, continuous motion.
A person jumps into a crouch and leans forward, then moves their bent arms and legs from side to side.
A person stands and rhythmically swivels their knees inward until they nearly touch, then outward again. The upper body remains relatively still, with arms held out to the sides.
A person stands with their arms extended to the sides, rhythmically bending and knocking their knees together while leaning slightly forward.

A person leans forward slightly with their elbows bent at their sides. Keeping their knees bent, they repeatedly move them toward and away from each other while the upper body remains relatively stable, eventually looking slightly upward.

A person kicks their right foot out and brings it back beside the left foot while bending both knees and rotating them outward. Simultaneously, they extend their arms to the sides with bent elbows, rotating them in tandem.
The person brings their knees inward and pauses, holding their upper arms horizontally at shoulder level with forearms pointing downward.

The figure performs a rhythmic motion, bending the knees to bring them together and then apart. The arms are raised and bent at the elbows, with the left arm held higher than the right while the torso sways slightly.

The figure kicks out with its right foot, then stands with feet apart and rhythmically bends its knees. As the knees bend, they turn inward while the figure sways from side to side. The arms, initially held at the sides, bend at the elbows to point toward the hips.
The person rhythmically moves their knees in and out while rolling their hands up and down with their elbows bent and raised sideways.
In a squatting stance with the torso bent forward, the person rhythmically swings their knees inward and outward as their arms swing side-to-side in coordination.

A person performs the Charleston, characterized by a "knock-kneed" motion where the knees are bent and alternately brought together and moved apart. The arms are held bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to the rhythm.
A person stands with their knees bent, performing a knee-knocking motion. They rhythmically bring their knees together and apart, pivoting on their feet with each movement.

Leaning slightly forward, the person repeatedly brings their bent knees together and apart. Their arms are initially extended to the sides with bent elbows but straighten as the person stands more upright.

A person stands with their knees bent and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. Bending forward at the waist, they swing their arms in and out while simultaneously moving their knees inward and outward. They then straighten up and jump, performing a slight kick with their left leg, before bending over again to repeat the sequence.
In a squatting stance with a bent torso, the person swings their knees in and out while swinging their forearms side to side.
A person stands in place, bent deeply forward at the waist, rhythmically bringing their knees together and apart while swinging their torso and arms from side to side.

A person performs a Charleston knock-knee move by rhythmically bringing their knees together and apart. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, while the upper body remains relatively stable.

The individual holds their left arm up, bent at the elbow, and their right arm bent at the elbow pointing toward their hip while vibrating their legs. They then move back slightly and kick out their left leg while swinging their right arm out and back to its original position.
A person stands in place, rhythmically bending their knees together and then apart. This repeated "knock-kneed" motion causes the upper body and arms to move slightly in response to the leg action.

Leaning slightly forward, the person rhythmically moves their knees inward and outward in a knock-kneed fashion while pivoting on the balls of their feet. Their arms are bent at the elbows and mimic the movement of their legs as they shift from side to side.

In a squatting stance with both elbows bent and raised to the sides, the person rhythmically swings their knees in and out. The elbows mirror this motion, swinging in and out in sync with the knees in a fast and energetic rhythm.
Standing with feet apart and knees bent, the person rhythmically brings their knees together and apart. The arms are held up and bent at the elbows, with minimal upper body movement.

Leaning forward from the waist, a person rhythmically bends their knees inward until they nearly touch, then moves them apart. Their arms are bent and held up, with the upper body twisting slightly to the right as the knees come together.

Starting from a crouched position, the person rhythmically twists their legs, moving their knees inward and then outward. The feet pivot on the heels and toes to facilitate the motion, while the arms remain bent and relatively stationary.

The person moves sideways by bending their knees and rhythmically bringing them together to touch, then moving them apart. Their arms are raised to the sides with bent elbows, moving in sync with the knees.
In a squat stance, the person swings their knees in and out while moving sideways. Their arms also swing in and out in rhythm with their knees.

A person stands with feet apart, rhythmically bending their knees inward to touch and then back outward. Throughout the motion, their arms are held out to the sides while the upper body remains stable.

The person stands in place with their arms in front of their body and elbows bent. They lean forward and place their hands on their knees, moving them in and out. They then place both hands on their right knee while continuing to move their knees in and out. As the knees come together again, they shift their hands to the left knee and repeat the motion. Finally, they bring their knees together, place their hands in the center, and rotate their body slightly counterclockwise.

The person rhythmically bends their knees inward until they nearly touch, then moves them back outward. With their arms raised and elbows bent, the upper body sways slightly from side to side in sync with the leg movement.

A person stands with bent knees, rhythmically twisting their legs to bring the knees together and apart. The arms are held low and slightly bent, while the upper body remains relatively still throughout the movement.

Standing with knees slightly bent and elbows tucked at their sides, the person slides from side to side. They draw their knees toward each other while rhythmically lifting and lowering their upper arms, occasionally changing the direction of their movement.

The person assumes a slight crouch with knees bent and torso leaning forward. They rhythmically bring their knees together to touch and then apart while swinging both forearms in and out in synchronization with the knees. Throughout the movement, the person travels sideways.
In a squat stance, the person rhythmically brings their knees inward to touch and then outward again, swinging both forearms in and out in synchronization with the knees while moving sideways.
A person performs a Charleston move by bending their legs and rhythmically knocking their knees together and apart while clapping their hands.

The person begins in a low, crouched stance with a hunched torso and bent knees. They perform a rhythmic "knock-knee" movement, bringing their knees together and then spreading them apart. Synchronized with this leg motion, their arms are held up with bent elbows and move in and out in a clapping gesture. After a few repetitions, they straighten their legs and torso to rise into a standing position. The motion concludes as they extend their arms out to the sides, finishing in a T-pose.

A person rhythmically moves their bent knees inward and outward while slowly walking sideways. Their arms are slightly raised and bent at the elbows, moving up and down in time with the rhythm of their walk.
The person stands and rhythmically bends their knees inward to touch and then outward again, repeating this "knock-knee" motion while clapping their hands.

The person stands with their feet apart and rhythmically bends their knees, moving them inward toward each other and then back out. While performing this "knock-kneed" motion, they hold their arms out and alternate between pointing upward and downward.
The person stands with bent knees, rhythmically moving them together and apart. Simultaneously, they rotate their hands from an upward to a downward position while maintaining bent elbows.
Starting from a low crouch, the person rhythmically bends their knees and knocks their right knee to the side while moving to the right and clapping their hands.
In a slight crouch, the person performs a "knock-knees" movement while walking sideways. Their hands are held together in front of the chest with the elbows pointing outward.

The person stands with bent knees, rhythmically bringing them together and apart while turning in a clockwise direction. This "knock-kneed" motion is performed by twisting the feet inward and outward on the floor while clapping their hands.
After kicking the right leg while clapping, the person turns counter-clockwise by swinging both knees in and out while clapping in front of the chest.

The person moves to the right, rhythmically bending their knees and moving them together and apart with both arms bent and raised to the sides. After kicking their right leg, they move to the left by swinging their bent knees in and out while clapping.

The person moves to the right, rhythmically bending their knees together and apart with both arms bent and raised to the sides. After kicking their right leg, they move to the left while swinging their bent knees in and out.

The person moves to the right, rhythmically bending their knees together and apart with a bent torso and hands crossed in front of the body. After kicking their right leg, they move to the left and repeat the movement.

The person moves to the right, rhythmically bending their knees and moving them together and apart with both arms bent and raised to the sides. After kicking their right leg, they move to the left by swinging their bent knees in and out.

The person performs a rhythmic dance by repeatedly bringing their knees together until they touch and then moving them back apart. This lower-body action is the primary movement, while the upper body and slightly bent arms remain relatively stable.
The individual bends their knees, moving them inward and outward. Their arms are held out to the sides and move slightly up and down in coordination with the lower body.
The person knocks their knees inward and then shakes them in and out.

A person stands with their arms bent and raised, then bends their legs and brings their knees together. Moving toward the right, they repeatedly move their knees in and out while clapping their hands.

The figure performs the knock-knee motion by bending their legs and rhythmically bringing the knees together and apart. As the knees move in and out, the feet twist in place, and the arms swing slightly for balance.
In a squatting stance, the person swings their bent knees inward and outward while clapping and moving to the right.

A person stands with bent legs, rhythmically bringing their knees together and then apart. With both hands held together in front of the body throughout the motion, they move sideways and turn by kicking out one leg in coordination with the knee movements.
A person stands with their knees bent, rhythmically bringing them together and then apart. Keeping both arms extended to the sides, they move sideways and turn to travel in the opposite direction.

A person stands with bent knees, rhythmically bringing them together and then apart. Their hands are clasped in front of their body, moving back and forth in sync with the rhythm of the knee movements.

A person rhythmically performs a knock-kneed Charleston move by bending their knees and bringing them together to touch, then moving them apart again. Their arms are held up and bent at the elbows, remaining relatively still while the legs perform the primary motion.
A person rhythmically brings their knees together while shifting their weight and lifting one foot off the ground, holding their bent arms out to the sides for balance.

A person performs the knock-knee move characteristic of the Charleston while standing with their knees bent. They rhythmically swivel their lower legs and feet, causing their knees to move inward until they nearly touch and then outward again.
The person claps while bringing their knees together to knock them.
A person stands with their knees bent, rhythmically knocking them together and then moving them apart. The upper body remains still, with the arms extended to the sides.
A person in a crouched position rhythmically knocks their knees together and apart. The upper body is slightly twisted, with the arms held in stylized, bent poses.
A person claps while knocking their knees together.

The person stands upright with their arms slightly bent and held out to the sides. They rhythmically bend their knees, bringing them together and then apart while moving sideways. They then swing their arms out from the elbows and begin shifting sideways in the opposite direction.
The person knocks their knees inward and outward while moving sideways to the left. Their arms perform isolated movements that point in different directions.
The person leans forward in a Charleston pose with one arm extended and the other bent back. They perform a knock-kneed motion, bending their knees and swiveling them outward from a closed position.

A person performs the Charleston "knocky knees" by rhythmically knocking their bent knees inward and outward. With their arms raised and elbows bent, the upper body sways in sync with the leg movements.
A person stands in a slight crouch with their arms bent in front of them, rhythmically bringing their knees together and apart. They then straighten up and raise their arms to shoulder height.
The person swings both arms horizontally at shoulder level in large circles while rhythmically shaking their bent knees in and out.

Balancing on a bent right leg with the left leg extended to the side, the person rhythmically moves their knees inward and outward in a knocking motion. The upper body remains still, with the arms held out to the sides.
random: The person performs a Charleston kick sequence, alternating forward kicks with the left and right legs, followed by a backward kick with the left leg. They maintain a bouncing rhythm with loose, bent knees, swinging their arms in opposition to their leg movements.
A person walks forward in a low crouch with their torso bent forward, keeping both arms extended straight behind them throughout the movement.
The figure walks forward with hands held at the chest, finishing with a small jumping turn to the left and raising the left arm.
The figure walks in a circle, lifting one leg slightly higher at the end of each step. With every forward stride, the opposite arm swings in the same direction as the walk.
Maintaining a low squat, the person rhythmically pulses their knees in and out while performing wide, exaggerated claps with bent arms in front of their chest.

The figure bounces in a Charleston-style step, kicking the left leg forward and the right leg back. The arms swing freely in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left arm moves back.
The figure turns clockwise with the right arm raised and the left hand on the hip, stepping forward with the left foot while pivoting on the right.
The figure performs a counter-clockwise turn with the left arm raised and the right hand on the hip, initiated by stepping forward with the right foot while the left foot pivots in place.
The person kicks their left and right legs forward while swinging their arms, then takes a large hop backward while leaning their torso forward for balance.
Maintaining a slight crouch on one leg, the person takes several steps with the other leg in a circular motion while rotating their body.
A person performs a bouncy dance, alternating low forward kicks with loose, bent knees. The torso swivels with each kick as the arms swing freely in opposition to the legs.
A person bounces on a relaxed supporting leg while kicking the other leg forward with a bent knee. Their arms swing freely in opposition, with the right arm moving forward and the left arm back.

The performer executes a high-energy Charleston dance, featuring alternating forward leg kicks and swivels while maintaining a constant, light bounce. Their arms swing freely in opposition to the leg movements, with the knees kept bent to facilitate the bouncy rhythm.
The person performs a bouncy step, kicking their right leg forward while their hips and torso swivel to the left. Their arms swing loosely for balance, with the left arm extended out to the side.

The figure performs a Charleston kick, alternating between kicking the right and left legs forward. The arms swing freely in opposition to the legs, while the bent knees create a continuous, bouncy motion.

The person performs a Charleston step, alternating forward kicks while twisting their torso from side to side. They maintain a steady bounce on the standing leg as their arms swing loosely in opposition to their legs.

A person performs a basic Charleston step, maintaining a constant bounce with loose, bent knees. They kick their legs forward and back in an alternating rhythm while swinging their arms freely in opposition.
A person takes small, quick steps forward, then leans forward and thrusts both arms out.
The individual takes small, abrupt steps backward while bending forward at the hips, their upper body waving rhythmically.
The figure retreats with alternating forward arm waves, finishing by crossing the left leg over the right in a preparatory move.
With the upper body bent forward, the person takes a step backward with their right leg while dragging the left. Simultaneously, their arms flail.
A person performs a jazz dance, stepping side to side with a low, bouncy posture and bent knees. Their arms swing in sync with their feet as they execute quick taps and small forward kicks.
The performer kicks their right leg back while twisting their torso to the left and swinging their arms in opposition, all while maintaining a continuous bounce on a bent left supporting leg.

The person performs a bouncy Charleston step, kicking their right leg forward while swinging their left arm forward, then alternating sides. Throughout the motion, the knees stay loose and bent, and the arms swing freely in opposition to the legs.
The person steps forward and claps their hands as the trailing foot follows. They then pause briefly in a locking pose with arms bent downward and knees slightly flexed.
With both arms raised and elbows bent sideways, the person steps forward. After a light hop, they step backward with their torso leaning forward.
A person hops on one leg in a circular motion.

The dancer kicks their right leg forward while maintaining a steady bounce on their bent left leg. Their arms are extended to the sides, swinging freely in sync with the swiveling motion of the hips and legs.

The figure performs a Charleston step, kicking the right leg forward and then the left leg backward. The movement is executed with a continuous bounce on bent knees, while the arms swing freely in opposition to the legs.

A person performs a bouncy Charleston step, kicking their right leg forward and to the side while swinging their arms in opposition. The movement is characterized by loose, bent knees and a constant up-and-down bounce on the supporting leg.
A person steps forward with one leg and pivots in a circle around their other leg while keeping their arms extended to their sides.

A person kicks their right leg forward and upward while keeping the left supporting leg bent. Their arms swing in opposition—the right arm moving forward and the left arm back—creating a dynamic, bouncing motion.

A person performs a Charleston kick, extending their right leg forward and slightly upward. Their arms swing in opposition—left arm forward and right arm back—while they maintain a bouncy posture with bent knees.

A person performs the Charleston, alternating forward kicks with each leg while maintaining a steady, rhythmic bounce. The arms swing loosely in sync with the swiveling and kicking motions of the lower body.

A person performs a series of quick, alternating forward kicks while maintaining a constant, rhythmic bounce on bent knees. The torso swivels from side to side in sync with the kicks, and the arms swing freely in opposition.
The person performs the Charleston, kicking one leg forward and the other backward while maintaining a steady bounce. Their arms swing loosely in opposition to their leg movements.

The individual performs a Charleston step, swiveling on the balls of their feet with bent knees. They kick their right leg forward and then their left leg back in a rhythmic bounce, swinging their arms in opposition.

The figure performs a forward kick with the left leg, swinging the right arm forward and the left arm back in opposition. This Charleston-style movement is executed with bent knees and a rhythmic bounce.

The person bounces in a staggered stance with their left knee bent forward and right knee back, holding their right arm at their side and their left arm raised with a bent elbow. They then stand upright while straightening both arms upward.
The person starts with a small jump into a split stance with their arms extended and bent, then bounces in place while maintaining the leg position.
With loose, bouncy knees, the person repeatedly brings their knees together and moves them apart. The arms are held straight down and remain still throughout the movement.

The figure begins by throwing their arms out to the sides and backward while moving backward with their torso slightly bent forward. They hop on their left leg while swinging their right leg backward, extending and bending the right knee.
The person steps around while swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.

A person kicks their left leg forward while swinging their arms in opposition, lowering the right arm as the left rises. The motion includes a slight bounce and a twist through the torso, maintaining a loose and free-flowing feel.
A person walks backward with a slight forward lean, swinging their arms in opposite directions at their sides.

A person performs a high forward kick with the left leg while keeping the right knee bent to create a bouncing effect. Simultaneously, the arms swing freely to the sides with a slight twist in the torso.
The dancer performs a forward kick, lifting their right knee high while the supporting left leg bounces. Their arms are spread wide, swinging freely to maintain balance and add flair to the movement.

While bringing the right leg toward the left, the person claps in front of their body. They then step back on their left leg while swinging both arms straight back. Finally, they kick their left leg forward while swinging both arms straight forward.

The figure performs a Charleston kick sequence, alternating kicks between the left and right legs with loose, bent knees. The arms swing freely in opposition to the leg movements, while the entire body maintains a constant, slight bounce.
The figure performs a Charleston-style kick sequence, maintaining a steady bounce on relaxed, bent knees. The arms swing freely in opposition to the alternating forward kicks.

The performer performs a bouncing Charleston step, kicking the left leg forward while swinging the right arm, then repeating the motion on the opposite side. Throughout the movement, the knees stay bent and loose, with the arms swinging in opposition to the legs.
The dancer kicks their right leg forward while swinging the left arm forward and the right arm back. The movement features a distinct bounce with loose, bent knees and a lowered center of gravity.

The person performs a Charleston step, kicking their left leg forward and then back while twisting their torso and swiveling on their right foot. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs as they maintain loose, bent knees, creating a characteristic bouncing rhythm.
The person kicks forward with the right leg and then the left, swinging their arms dynamically in opposition to the leg movements.

A figure performs a bouncy Charleston step, kicking the left leg forward and back while twisting the torso. The bent arms swing freely in opposition to the leg movements, maintaining a constant, loose-kneed bounce throughout the sequence.
The person turns and steps sideways, swinging their arms up with bent elbows, then turns toward the other side.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They spin 180 degrees counterclockwise and move sideways to the left while pushing the right arm forward and the left arm back, switching their positions. They then step backward and forward, repeating the same arm movements.

The person performs a bouncing, swiveling Charleston-style step, kicking the left leg forward and the right leg back. The arms swing freely in opposition to the legs, with the knees remaining loose and bent throughout the sequence.

The figure performs a continuous bouncing motion with alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg. The arms swing in opposition to the legs—the left arm moving forward with the right kick and vice versa—while the knees remain loose and bent throughout the movement.

The person alternately taps one foot while bending the knee and keeping the opposite leg straight. This continues until they strike a pose with one leg forward, the other back, and hands on their waist. Afterward, the alternating tapping motion resumes.
The person performs a slow stepping motion, alternating between bending one knee and extending the other.
The person performs a rhythmic stepping motion while moving in a circle, alternating between bending one knee and extending the other, which creates a light hopping effect.

A person performs an energetic dance featuring alternating forward kicks and swiveling leg movements while maintaining a steady bounce. Their arms are held out to the sides, swinging loosely in coordination with the lower body.

A person performs a Charleston step, bouncing on bent knees while twisting their torso and kicking. Their arms swing freely and widely in opposition to the leg movements, with one arm extending forward as the other swings back.

The figure performs a bouncy Charleston step, kicking the right leg forward and the left back before switching. The arms swing freely in opposition to the legs, maintaining a constant up-and-down bounce.

Bent slightly forward, the figure swings their arms back and forth, moving their knees in coordination with the opposite arm while shifting position. They then place their hands on their hips with elbows bent and drop into a kneel, with the left knee forward and the right knee back, before standing back up with swinging arms.

The person stands in a slightly bent-over position with their arms at their sides. They hop forward and to the side while remaining bent, stretching their arms out. They straighten as they hop to the opposite side and lower their arms, before bending over their right knee, straightening, and bending over it again as the body moves forward and to the right. The torso rotates counterclockwise as the arms extend to the sides, elbows bent with the forearms pointing forward.
