They then turn 90 degrees counterclockwise and hop forward, lowering their arms slightly. Dropping their arms to their sides, they vibrate forward and bend their elbows to point their arms up and out to the sides while walking forward with rapid, small steps.

A person maintains a low, crouched stance while twisting their knees and shaking their shoulders rhythmically. They then continue in the low stance, taking rotating steps one leg at a time. One arm is extended forward while the other is stretched backward, moving in rhythm with the steps.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking their right leg back while balancing on a bent left leg. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the left arm forward and the right arm back, creating a dynamic, bouncy posture.

Swiveling on the right foot, the person kicks their left leg forward and across the body in a classic Charleston step. The arms swing freely in opposition to the legs, while the knees remain bent, maintaining a light, constant bounce throughout the motion.

With the right hand raised, the person sways their hips from side to side while stepping forward. They then stumble and step backward while swinging their arms in rhythm, before stepping forward once again.
The person steps out with their left foot and slides in that direction, straightening their back. Their arms, initially bent at their sides, extend and reach upward as the person straightens.
The person slides their right foot forward and then brings their left foot to meet it. While sliding, they extend their arms to the sides.
A person steps their right leg across their body to the left into a wide stance, leaning into the movement. Simultaneously, they circle both arms from their sides up and over their head.

A person performs a Charleston-style kick by swiveling on one foot while lifting the opposite knee and kicking the lower leg forward. They maintain a constant bounce with loose, bent knees as their arms swing freely in opposition to their legs.

A person performs a bouncy swivel step, fluidly kicking their left leg forward and then their right leg back. Their arms swing freely in opposition to their legs with loose, bent knees, characteristic of a Charleston dance.

The figure leans over their bent left leg, swinging their left arm in front of it, then straightens and repeats the motion on the right side. They then touch their left hand to their right shoulder, followed by their right hand to their left shoulder.
The person performs a Charleston kick, lifting their right leg forward and up while stepping back with the left. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs as the torso swivels to the right.
Starting from a low crouch, the dancer kicks their right leg out to the side while extending both arms. They then step back with the left leg, initiating a low, rumbling motion.
The person performs the Charleston, alternating forward kicks while maintaining a continuous bounce. With each kick, the body swivels as the arms swing freely in opposition to the legs.

The person performs a Charleston kick, thrusting their right leg high and forward while bouncing on the supporting left leg. Their arms are spread to the sides with bent elbows, swinging freely to maintain balance and rhythm.

A person performs a Charleston-style dance, maintaining a continuous bounce while executing alternating low forward kicks and swivels. Their arms swing freely in opposition to their legs, with their knees remaining loose and bent throughout the sequence.
The person leans over their left knee and swings their left arm in front of it, while keeping the right knee bent and extending the right arm upward and back.
The person swings their arms in large arcs up and down with their torso bent forward. Simultaneously, they rhythmically alternate between extending one knee and bending the other.

A person begins by walking forward with their elbows bent at their sides as their head sways back and forth. They then transition to moving backward, swinging their arms alternately. The motion concludes with a sudden jump forward, landing in a lunge with the left leg forward and both hands brought together at the chest.
A person jumps while twisting their knees from side to side, swinging their arms to maintain balance.
A person kicks their right leg forward while bouncing on a bent left leg. Their arms swing freely outward and upward, capturing the loose, dynamic quality of a Charleston dance move.

A person kicks with their right foot and then their left, then leans over their left leg while swinging their left arm at the elbow and extending their right arm upward. Finally, they jump slightly backward, landing on both feet.

While in a slight squat, the person rhythmically shakes and twists their knees together from side to side. Their arms move in the opposite direction of the knees, creating a coordinated full-body twisting motion.
A person slowly squats down while twisting their torso from side to side, punching forward and to the sides as they descend.

A person performs a quick forward kick with their left leg while swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back in opposition. The movement is characterized by a constant bounce, loose knees, and free-swinging arms, consistent with the Charleston dance style.

A person performs a Charleston-style forward kick with their right leg while keeping the supporting left leg bent. Their arms swing in opposition—the left arm moving forward and the right arm back—maintaining a dynamic, bouncing posture.
A person performs a Charleston dance move, kicking their right leg forward while bouncing on bent knees. Their bent arms swing rhythmically out to the sides in a dynamic motion.
The figure performs a bouncy Charleston-style step, kicking the left leg forward and upward while swinging the right arm forward for balance.
A person performs a slow swivel step, twisting their torso in a circle and then from side to side with their arms held out for balance. They bounce their hips rhythmically throughout the motion.

A person performs a Charleston-style dance, kicking their left knee high while keeping both knees loose and bouncy. Their arms are spread wide and swing freely to the sides, adding to the dynamic and energetic movement.

A person performs a Charleston step, bouncing on their left leg while kicking their right leg forward and slightly to the side. Their arms are bent and raised, swinging loosely in opposition to the leg movements.

A person performs a bouncing Charleston step, alternately kicking and swiveling each leg forward and to the side. This motion is characterized by loose, bent knees and arms that swing freely in opposition to the leg movements.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking their left leg forward while swiveling on the right foot. Their arms swing in opposition to the leg movement as the body maintains a slight bounce throughout the motion.

The person performs a series of alternating high forward kicks, starting with the right leg. The arms swing freely in opposition to the legs, while the body maintains a constant bounce with loose, bent knees.

The figure kicks their right leg forward while twisting their torso and swinging their arms in opposition, then mirrors the motion on the opposite side, maintaining a continuous bounce with loose, bent knees throughout the sequence.
A person takes a step back with their left leg and brings their right leg back while lowering their torso, placing both hands on their knees.
The performer kicks their right leg while swinging both arms wide to the sides, maintaining a constant bounce through loose, bent knees and a forward-leaning torso.

A person performs the Charleston, kicking their right leg forward while swinging their left arm forward and right arm back in opposition. The movement is characterized by a constant bounce, loose knees, and free-swinging arms, creating a fluid and rhythmic motion.
A person kicks one leg forward with arms extended to the sides, then transitions into a bouncing, twisting step on the balls of their feet, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.
The person takes a running stride, then abruptly plants one foot to pivot sharply. After changing direction, they immediately push off into another run.
The person starts from a still standing position and then jumps, landing in a wide stance with slightly bent knees.
The person bounces in place by bending their knees while their bent arms swing back and forth in opposition.
The person steps forward and then claps their hands.

After clapping, the person turns to their right by tapping their left foot forward and rotating their body to face the back. They then step forward on their left leg as their bent arms swing up and down in an alternating motion.
A person walks a few steps, then turns their body in a smooth, rhythmic motion, with their arms swinging in coordination with their legs.

The person steps forward on one foot and taps with their feet twisted outward, then swings their knee while untwisting them. They repeat these twisting steps with both arms held outward as they move in a counter-clockwise circle.
single: The figure performs a Charleston step, alternating between stepping back on one foot and kicking that same foot forward. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward during the left leg kick and vice versa.

The figure executes a Charleston step, alternating between stepping back on one foot and kicking that same foot forward. As each leg kicks, the arm on the same side swings out and forward while the opposite hand is held at the waist.
The figure kicks their right leg straight forward while the left leg remains planted, then returns the right foot to the ground as the left foot taps forward.
The person performs alternating outward and inward leg swings, moving their arms in coordination with their lower body.
A person kicks backward while simultaneously striking with the opposite elbow in a rhythmic, stepping motion.
With arms extended to the sides, the person steps back on the right foot while kicking the left leg forward. They then step forward with the left foot and tap the right foot behind it.
The person steps back on their left foot while kicking their right leg forward, arms extended to the sides. They then step forward onto the left foot as the right foot taps the ground behind it.

The figure performs a single Charleston step, stepping back with one foot and kicking forward with the other. The kicking foot then taps the ground in front as the arms swing in opposition to the legs.

A person kicks one leg forward while swinging the opposite arm, then steps back and crosses that foot behind the other leg. This alternating kick-and-step motion is characteristic of the Charleston step.
A person steps back on their right foot and then kicks forward, swinging their arms to maintain balance.

The person steps back with one foot while swinging their arms at their sides, then steps forward again. They then turn in a circle while stepping back and kicking their legs to the sides, maintaining the same arm movement.
A person steps back onto their right foot while kicking their left leg forward with arms extended to the sides. They then step forward onto their left foot and tap their right foot next to it.

The figure performs a high forward kick with the left leg while swinging the right arm forward in opposition. They then step back with the left leg, step forward with the right leg, and tap the ground with the left foot.
The figure steps back on the left foot and kicks it forward. Then, the left foot steps forward and the right foot taps forward while the arms swing in opposition.
The figure steps back on the left foot while kicking the right leg forward and up, keeping both arms bent and held out. It then steps forward onto the right foot and taps the left foot next to it.

A person steps back on their right foot, kicking their left leg forward with a bent knee while swinging their arms in opposition. They then step forward onto the left foot and tap the right foot next to it.
The figure kicks their right leg high and forward, swinging the left arm forward for balance. After the kick, the right foot steps down in front as the left leg reaches back to tap the ground.

The person kicks their left leg high and forward while swinging their arms in opposition for balance. They then step forward onto the left foot, finishing the motion with a tap of the right foot behind.
A person steps back onto their left leg while kicking their right leg and swinging their right arm forward. They then step forward onto their right foot, tapping their left foot beside it.
The person steps back with the left foot while kicking the right foot forward, swinging their arms in opposition. They then step forward with the right foot and tap the left foot behind it.

A person steps back onto their left foot while kicking their right leg forward in a classic Charleston motion. Their arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving forward and the left arm back to maintain balance.

The person steps back on one leg while leaning the upper body forward, then kicks the same leg forward while returning to an upright position. Throughout the movement, the arms swing in opposition to the legs.
A person steps back on their left foot while kicking their right leg forward with a bent knee. Their arms swing in opposition—the left arm forward and the right arm back—as the torso twists slightly.
The person kicks one foot forward and back, repeating the motion with the other foot while rotating in a circle. Their arms move in tandem with the opposite foot throughout the movement.
The person bounces while stepping forward and backward with alternating feet, extending the opposite arm for balance.
The person taps forward and backward with alternating legs, swinging their opposite arms back and forth to maintain balance.
The person steps forward with their right foot, swings their left leg forward and back, and then steps back with their right foot while swinging their arms at their sides.

The person steps back on their left foot while kicking their right leg forward, keeping their arms bent and held out to the sides. They then step forward onto the right foot and tap their left foot on the ground.

A person takes a wide step out with their left foot, then kicks it forward and slightly across their body. As the left leg kicks, the right arm swings forward and the left arm swings back, followed by a backward kick with the same foot.
The person taps their right foot forward and then moves it back with their arms extended for balance.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking their left leg forward and upward while balancing on a straight right leg. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides, with the right arm raised higher than the left.
Maintaining a low squat with arms raised and bent, the individual alternately taps their right and left feet out to the side, quickly returning each foot to the center after every tap.
The individual steps back on the left foot while kicking the right leg forward and up, then steps forward onto the right foot and taps the left foot on the floor behind it.
A person performs a rhythmic dance step, kicking each leg out to the side twice with a bent knee. As each leg kicks out, the corresponding arm moves backward in a synchronized motion.
The person jumps rhythmically while alternating forward steps.
A person rhythmically taps one foot forward and returns it, then taps the other foot backward and returns it.
The dancer kicks one leg forward across the body, then kicks the other leg backward in a crossing motion, with the arms swinging naturally throughout.
A person rhythmically performs sideways step-kicks.

The person kicks one leg forward, crossing it over the other leg, and brings it back. They then kick the opposite leg backward and return it. Throughout the motion, the arms swing dynamically in opposition to the legs.
The person rhythmically alternates steps, kicking one foot sideways with a bent knee while swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.
A person steps back on their right leg, shifts their weight to the left, and then kicks their right leg forward. Their arms are bent and move in opposition to the legs for balance.
The performer taps their left foot forward and brings it back.
A person steps back on the right foot while kicking the left leg forward with a bent knee, then steps forward on the left foot and taps the right foot beside it.
The person kicks their right leg forward while keeping the left leg back, swinging their arms in opposition. They then lower the right foot to tap the ground in front.
The dancer performs alternating kicks to the side and backward with bent knees, swinging the same-side arm outward in coordination with each movement.

A person steps back on the left foot while kicking the right foot forward, then steps forward onto the right foot to tap the left foot. Throughout the movement, their bent arms swing in opposition to the legs.
The person steps backward, starting with the left foot and then the right, while creating a distinct bounce in their hips and swinging their arms back and forth in a coordinated rhythm.

A figure performs a forward kick with the right leg while swinging the left arm forward and the right arm back. After the kick, they step forward on the right foot and bring the left foot forward to tap the ground.
The person steps back on their left foot while kicking their right leg forward and up. Their arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving forward as they kick.
A person steps backward while alternately kicking each leg out to the side. While slightly bent over, they swing both arms in the direction of the kicking leg.

The individual steps back on their right foot and kicks their left leg high and forward, swinging their arms in opposition. They then step forward onto their left foot and tap their right foot behind them to complete the Charleston step.
A person steps back with the left foot while kicking the right leg straight up and forward. The torso leans back to counterbalance the kick, and the arms move in opposition for balance.
The figure performs a Charleston step, stepping back on the right foot and kicking the left leg forward, then stepping forward onto the left foot and tapping the right foot on the ground.
A person steps back onto their right foot while kicking their left leg high and forward with arms outstretched. They then step forward onto their left foot and tap the ground with their right foot.
A person steps back with their left leg and then kicks it forward and upward, while swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back to maintain balance.

The person kicks their right leg forward and upward while raising their left arm and extending their right. They then step forward onto their right foot, bringing their left foot up slightly for a tap.
A person rhythmically alternates steps, stepping one leg forward and back to center, then the other leg backward and back to center. The arms swing back and forth in opposition to the legs.
A person rhythmically alternates steps, stepping one leg forward and back to the center, then the other leg backward and back to the center, while swinging their arms in opposition to the legs.

A person performs a basic Charleston step by stepping back on the left foot while kicking the right leg forward, then stepping forward on the left foot and tapping the right foot on the ground behind them.
Leaning forward, a person steps back on their right foot while kicking their left leg forward, with their arms extended to the sides for balance.
A person steps back with their left foot while kicking their right leg forward and swinging their arms in opposition, then brings the right foot down to tap the ground in front.
A person steps back with their left foot while kicking their right foot forward and up. Their arms swing in opposition, with the left arm moving forward and the right arm back to maintain balance.
The person steps back on one foot and kicks the other leg high and forward while leaning their torso back. They then step forward onto the kicking foot and tap their rear foot on the ground.

A person performs a Charleston step, starting by stepping back with the right foot followed by a forward kick with the same foot. They then step forward onto the right foot and tap with the left foot while swinging their arms in opposition.
The person taps their right foot forward and then backward in a rapid motion.

The person rhythmically steps their left leg forward across the right and returns it, then kicks their right leg backward across the left and returns it. This sequence repeats while the arms swing back and forth in opposition to the leg movements.

The person rhythmically steps their left leg forward across the right and back, then kicks their right leg backward across the left and back. This sequence repeats as the arms swing back and forth in opposition to the legs.
A person steps back on their right foot and prepares to begin Charleston steps.

A person performs a continuous side-to-side cross-stepping motion, swinging their arms forward and backward in opposition to their legs. While repeating this movement, they slowly rotate their body in a circle.

The figure keeps their arms vibrating slightly at their sides. They kick their left leg forward and back, then sweep their right leg in a half-circle, touching it down behind them, to the side, and then in front. The leg vibrates briefly in place before the sequence repeats.
The person steps their left leg forward across the right and returns it, then taps their right leg backward and returns it. Their arms swing back and forth rhythmically in opposition to the legs.

A person dynamically alternates stepping one leg behind the other and back while turning clockwise. The arm on the same side as the stepping leg swings forward and sideways in rhythm with the steps, while the other arm remains at the waist.

The person rhythmically steps their left leg forward across their right leg and brings it back. They then step their right leg forward across their left leg and bring it back. The arms swing back and forth in opposition to the legs.
A person alternately steps one foot forward across the other and then back, with their arms swinging in opposition to the legs.

A person steps back on one foot and kicks the opposite leg forward and upward while swinging their arms in opposition. They then step onto the kicking foot and bring the trailing foot forward to tap the ground.

A person repeatedly steps their left foot forward across their right, returns to center, and then steps their right foot back before returning to center. Throughout the movement, their arms sway slightly back and forth.

A person repeatedly steps their left foot forward across their right, brings it back, and then steps their right foot straight back before returning to center. Throughout the motion, their arms sway slightly back and forth as they turn in a clockwise direction.

A person repeatedly steps their left foot forward across their right, returns to center, and then steps back with their right foot before returning to the starting position. Their arms sway gently back and forth throughout the movement.

A person repeatedly steps their left foot forward and across their right, returns to center, and then steps their right foot backward before returning to the start. Throughout the movement, their arms sway slightly back and forth.
The person kicks one foot forward, places it in front of the other foot, and then moves back. The other leg repeats this motion.
A person extends one arm upward while pointing the other hand toward their hip. They kick the leg on the same side forward and then backward, then repeat the sequence on the opposite side.
A person performs a cross-step, alternately stepping one foot in front of the other. Maintaining a stable and upright torso, they swing their arms gently in rhythm with their leg movements.
The person steps to the left and crosses the right leg behind, then steps to the right and crosses the left leg behind. The arms swing gently forward and backward in rhythm with the steps.
A person steps forward, crossing their feet in an alternating pattern while rhythmically extending one arm in sync with each step.

A person repeatedly crosses their left foot forward over the right, returns to center, and then steps their right foot deeply back across the left leg before returning to center again. Throughout the motion, their arms sway slightly back and forth.

A person repeatedly steps their left foot forward across their right and back, then kicks their right foot backward, rolling it outward in a circular motion to return it. Throughout the movement, their arms sway slightly back and forth.

A person repeatedly steps their left foot forward across their right and brings it back, then kicks their right foot backward and rolls it outward in a circular motion to return it. Throughout the motion, their arms sway slightly back and forth.

A person steps back with their right foot and kicks their left leg forward, swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back. They then step forward with their left foot and tap their right foot on the ground behind them.

A person kicks their right leg forward and steps back to cross behind their pivoting left leg, swinging their arms dynamically for balance. The pivoting left leg hops slightly in rhythm with the kick.

The person performs a cross-step, stepping back onto their left leg while delivering a high forward kick with their right. Their arms are bent in opposition to their legs, with the left arm held forward to maintain balance during the kick.

A person kicks their right leg forward and sweeps it back to cross behind their left leg, which simultaneously performs a small hop. The arms swing dynamically to maintain balance throughout the movement.
The person taps their right foot in front of their left foot and then taps it behind their left foot.
The person kicks their left foot forward and then steps back.

A person kicks their left leg straight forward while supporting themselves on their right leg behind. Their arms are bent and raised, swinging in opposition to the movement with the right arm forward and the left arm back.
A person stands upright and kicks their left foot forward, then leans forward slightly while walking backward, swinging their arms and legs at their sides.
A person takes a rhythmic step backward.

The person kicks their left leg forward while swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back. They then bring their left leg back to its starting position and kick their right leg backward with arms outstretched in different directions.
A person steps back onto their right foot while hopping on their left leg, keeping the upper body relatively still.

A person steps back on their left foot while kicking their right leg forward, keeping their arms extended to the sides. They then step forward onto their right foot and tap their left foot on the ground behind it.
A person kicks their right leg straight forward and brings it back while leaning their torso backward, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
The person steps back on the right foot and then kicks it forward with the opposite arm extended to maintain balance.
A person kicks their right leg forward, steps onto it, and taps their left foot on the ground behind. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, moving in opposition to the legs.

A person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They kick their right foot behind the left and step it forward, maintaining their pose. They then kick their left foot forward and back, swinging the right arm forward and the left arm back. Next, they bend over, step the right foot back and then forward, and straighten up while swinging their arms at their sides so that the left arm moves with the right foot and the right arm moves with the left. They kick the left foot forward and back again, rotate slightly clockwise, and begin stepping backward while continuing to swing their arms in the same motion. Finally, they bend their left knee, step the right leg back, and reach down to touch the ground with their right hand.

A person performs a Charleston step, stepping back on their left foot while kicking their right leg high and forward. The arms swing in opposition to the legs for balance, with the right arm moving forward as the right leg kicks.
A person steps back on their right foot and kicks their left leg forward. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs as the weight shifts forward, preparing for a forward step and tap.

A person performs a Charleston step by stepping back on one foot and kicking it forward. They complete the move by stepping forward with the other foot to tap, while swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.
Starting from a low squat, the individual performs alternating cross-steps by bringing one leg in front of the other while maintaining a forward-leaning torso and extended arms.
The individual bounces their torso while performing alternating cross-steps, moving back and forth.

The dancer performs a Charleston step by stepping back with the right foot while kicking the left foot forward. They then step forward on the left foot and tap the right foot behind them, keeping their arms bent and raised to the sides.
The person steps to cross their legs and then uncrosses them while swinging both arms up, down, and back and forth in rhythm with their steps.
The person kicks their right leg forward and up while leaning their upper body back. To maintain balance, the left arm swings forward as the right arm moves back.

A person performs a single Charleston step by stepping back with the right foot and kicking it forward and up. They then step down in front with the right foot while tapping the left foot on the ground behind them.
The figure steps from side to side, alternating which foot crosses over the other while swinging their elbows up and down.
The person bounces their elbows up and down at their sides while performing Charleston single steps, stepping forward with one leg and to the side with the other.
scarecrow: The figure kicks its left leg forward and across its body, then sweeps it back, simultaneously bending its knees and elbows to lower into a crooked, scarecrow-like stance.
A person kicks one leg across their body and then sweeps it back. The opposite arm extends forward while the other arm moves backward.
The person kicks their left leg across their body and sweeps it back, landing in a crouched pose. Upon landing, they extend both arms to the sides with bent elbows, mimicking a scarecrow.
The figure kicks their left leg forward and across the body before sweeping it back, landing in a wide, crouched stance with arms extended to the sides and elbows bent, resembling a scarecrow.
The individual kicks their right leg forward and across their body, then sweeps it back behind the left leg. They then turn to face a different direction and repeat the motion.

Starting from a scarecrow pose with bent knees and elbows, the figure kicks its right leg across to the left and sweeps it back out. The upper body remains relatively still, keeping the arms raised and bent throughout the movement.
The figure kicks its left leg across its body while twisting its torso and raising its arms. The leg then sweeps back as the figure bends its knees and elbows, settling into a crooked, angular pose.
A person sweeps their left leg back from a crossed position while spreading their arms wide, then bends their knees and elbows to pull into a crooked, scarecrow-like pose.

The figure kicks its right leg forward and across its body while raising its left arm and lowering its right. It then sinks into a deep bend on the supporting left leg, holding an asymmetrical, crooked pose with bent limbs.
spank the baby: A person taps their right foot to the side, then brings it behind their left leg while swinging their right arm down and back to slap their left buttock.

Standing on the left leg, the person kicks their right leg back and across the body. Simultaneously, they swing their right arm back to slap their right buttock while extending their left arm forward for balance.
Maintaining a ballroom pose with the left arm extended and the right arm bent, the person moves diagonally forward using a step-and-follow footwork pattern.
A person steps to the left with their left foot, then brings it back behind the right leg. As the left leg moves back, the left arm swings down to tap the right buttock.
A person steps one foot out and vibrates in place, then pulls it back to kick the other foot out and vibrate again. Throughout the movement, their arms are held at their sides with the elbows bent.
The person steps from side to side with arms outstretched in a slight wavy motion, while their hips bounce rhythmically in the direction of each step.
The person rhythmically twists their hips and arms in a squat stance. They then raise both arms straight out to the sides while stepping side-to-side before twisting again.
A person slides side to side with bent knees, first to the left and then to the right, while raising their arms to the sides. Their arms gyrate slightly as the body moves.

A person rhythmically alternates tapping their right and left feet out to the side. As each foot taps out, the torso twists while the arms, initially fixed in a pose with one extended and one bent, wave in coordination with the foot movements.

A person rhythmically taps their right and left feet out to the side alternately. As each foot taps out, the torso twists while the arms, initially held in a pose with one arm extended and the other bent, wave in sync with the foot movements.
A figure taps their right foot out to the side with arms raised, then swings the right leg behind the left and reaches back with their left hand to slap their right buttock.
A person steps side-to-side with bouncing hips, their arms extended and moving in a wavy motion synchronized with the hip movement.
A person steps side to side with bouncing hips. Their arms are extended out to the sides, moving in a wavy motion synchronized with the movement of their hips.
The person slides from side to side, swaying their hips and tapping one foot out. The arm on the sliding side extends outward, while the opposite arm bends in toward the hip.
The person moves side-to-side by stepping out with one foot and bringing the other to follow. Their arms are held horizontally and move in sync with their steps.

A person steps to the side with their left foot, raising their left arm straight up while keeping their right hand at their waist. They then switch to the right side with mirrored arm movements. Throughout the motion, the person follows the underlying rhythm.

A person raises their right arm up and to the right, then kicks their right leg out and pulls it back while hopping to the right on their left leg. Simultaneously, their left arm moves outward with the elbow bent.
The dancer hops sideways on the right foot while bending and straightening the left leg. The arms bend and extend outward and upward, resembling a cheering motion.
The person taps their right foot to the side and slides to the right while pointing both arms in the same direction.
A person slides two steps to one side while pointing their arms upward in the direction of travel, then repeats the motion in the opposite direction.
A person taps their right foot out to the side and brings it back toward the standing left leg. As the right foot returns, the right hand swings down and back to slap the left buttock.
The figure kicks their right foot out to the side while raising their left arm. As the right foot returns, the right hand swings across the body to slap the left buttock.
The person taps their left foot out to the side, then circles it back to cross behind the right leg, while the arms wave in opposition to the leg movements.
The person alternately taps each foot to the side and returns it to the center. As the foot returns, the opposite hand swings back to slap the glute of the standing leg.
A person taps their right foot to the side, then swings it behind their supporting left leg. As the right foot kicks back, the right hand swings down to slap the left buttock.
The performer taps their right foot to the side while extending their arms, then swings the right leg behind the left while reaching back with their right hand to slap their left buttock.
A person facing forward taps their left foot out to the side and then their right foot to move to the right, with both arms pointing in the direction of movement.
The figure taps its right foot out to the side and then swings it back behind the left leg. Simultaneously, the right arm swings down and back toward the right hip in a slapping motion.
The individual taps their right foot out to the side and crosses it behind their left leg while swinging their right arm down and back to slap the back of their left thigh.

A person taps their right foot out to the side while keeping their left arm raised and bent. They then bring their right foot behind their left leg, swinging their right arm down and back to slap their left buttock.
A person taps their right foot out to the side and pulls it behind their left leg. Simultaneously, the left arm swings back in a slapping motion while the right arm extends forward.

The figure taps its right foot out to the side and draws it back toward the stationary left leg. As the foot returns, the right arm swings behind toward the left hip while the torso twists to the right.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the torso. They extend their arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height, then kick with the left leg and shift sideways to the left, followed by kicking with the right leg and shifting sideways to the right. Keeping their arms in the same position, they then step backward while alternating leg kicks.
The person taps their right foot to the side while moving to the right and pointing both arms in the same direction.
A person stands with their left arm raised to the side and their right arm bent in front of their body. They move sideways to the left, then lower their arms and kick out their right foot.

The individual starts with their arms at their sides, steps forward, and rotates in a circle while extending their left arm out and placing their right hand at their hip with the elbow bent. After rotating 270 degrees, they bring their arms in front of their body with elbows bent, then step forward. They then place their left hand at their hip, hold their right arm out with the elbow bent, and spin counterclockwise on their left foot. Once facing forward again, they step their left foot forward and back with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms forward, before gradually straightening their arms down to their sides.
A person taps their left foot out to the side and brings it back behind their right leg. Simultaneously, they twist their torso and slap their left buttock with their left hand.

Standing on the left leg, the person brings their right foot behind the left. Simultaneously, they reach across the body with their right arm to slap the left buttock while extending the left arm forward.

Standing on the right leg, the person repeatedly taps their left foot out to the side before bringing it back behind the standing leg. The arms swing in opposition, with the right hand moving toward the right hip as the left foot taps out.

Standing on the right leg, the person raises their left knee and then swings the leg back behind the body. As the leg extends, the left hand reaches down to touch the right buttock while the right arm is held out to the side for balance.
The person moves slowly to the left with their arms waving overhead, stepping with the left leg first followed by the right.

The person raises their arms to their sides while extending their left leg for a side tap. They then bring the left leg back in, swinging their right hand down to slap their right buttock while leaning their torso slightly forward and to the right.

Standing on their right leg, a person taps their left foot out to the side and slightly forward. As they lean forward, they slap their right hip with their right hand while keeping their left arm extended to the side.
The person taps their left foot to the left, and the right foot slides to follow. The entire motion is smooth and sliding.
The person taps their foot to the left while moving to the side.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent, keeping their forearms in front of their chest. They extend their right arm and step to the right, swinging both arms backward and then forward. They then step back and rotate 90 degrees clockwise, walking sideways to the right while swinging their forearms from the elbows and rotating their torso. Finally, they drop their right arm to their side.
The person performs side steps by bringing one leg to the other without crossing them. The hips sway lightly from side to side.
The person performs side steps by bringing one leg to the other without crossing. The arms are held straight out to the sides throughout the movement, while the hips sway lightly from side to side.
skating step: The performer pivots on the right foot while lifting the left knee. The arms move in opposition to the legs, with the right arm swinging forward and the left arm moving back to facilitate the rotation.

The person performs a skating motion by twisting on the ball of the right foot with the left leg extended back. They then shift their weight forward onto the left foot, hitching the right knee as their arms swing in opposition.
A person performs a skating step by pivoting on the right foot and transferring weight to the left. With the left leg initially hitched back, the arms swing in opposition to the leg movements.
The person takes quick, short steps forward while swinging their hips from side to side, their arms moving back and forth in rhythm.
The person takes side steps with a slight hop while swinging both arms from side to side.
The dancer shifts their weight onto the left foot, twisting their body to the right. Simultaneously, the right leg hitches back and up while the right arm swings forward in opposition.

The person twists their torso and hips while performing a skating motion, alternately kicking one leg forward as the other moves back. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the opposite arm and leg moving forward simultaneously.

The individual performs a Charleston skating step, alternating forward kicks while pivoting on the standing foot. As the left leg kicks forward, the left arm swings forward in sync, with the movement then mirrored on the right side. This creates a rhythmic, twisting motion with the torso leaning forward.

Standing on the right foot, the person twists their torso to the right while simultaneously lifting the left knee and swinging the left arm forward. They then shift their weight onto the left foot, preparing to repeat the motion on the other side.
A person stands in place, rhythmically twisting and bouncing their hips from side to side while extending their arms outward in sync with the movement.
The figure pivots on the right foot while kicking the left leg up and back. The left arm swings forward and the right arm swings back in opposition, creating a dynamic skating motion.
The person pivots on their standing foot while hitching the opposite leg and arm forward, then shifts their weight to the other foot to repeat the motion in a skating-like manner.
With arms extended straight out to the sides, the person sways their hips from side to side by bringing one foot to the other.
The person hops from one leg to the other while rhythmically swinging their elbows, creating a skating step by shifting their weight from side to side.
The person alternates kicking each leg forward while holding both arms straight out to the sides. The rhythm of the kicks creates a subtle wave-like motion throughout their body.
With a squat stance, the individual rhythmically twists their feet and arms side-to-side.
The person pivots on their standing left foot, gliding forward while hitching the right knee high. The left arm swings forward in opposition to the right leg, as the right arm moves backward.
shimmy: A person performs a shimmy by rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso from side to side. Their arms are extended outward and slightly bent, while the lower body remains relatively stationary with the weight centered.
The person walks in place while vibrating their arms, then leans forward and swings both arms while sliding both legs back slightly.

A person performs a shimmy, rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso from side to side while keeping the lower body relatively still. Their arms are raised with bent elbows, moving in sync with the shoulder movements.

A person shimmies by moving their shoulders and shoulder blades in opposition, with one moving forward as the other moves back. Their bent arms move in sync with the shoulders while the lower body remains stationary and centered.
Standing with arms extended, the individual shimmies by twisting their upper body. The arms mimic a wave motion while they take gentle steps.
A person moves forward while shaking their shoulders with their arms at their sides, then kicks one leg to the side and moves it in a circle.
With a hunched torso, the person rapidly shimmies their shoulders while taking small steps backward, keeping their knees bent and arms straight.
The performer executes a shimmy, rapidly moving their shoulders from side to side while holding their arms bent and outward. The lower body remains relatively stationary, keeping the weight centered.

A person walks forward while slightly bent over, vibrating their shoulders with their arms at their sides, then turns and walks back the way they came. After stopping at a wall and kicking their left leg, they turn and walk to the left in the same manner.
The individual kicks each leg out, rotating at the knee while stepping forward. Leaning slightly forward, they extend their arms to the sides and vibrate their shoulders.
A person walks in a circle while performing a shimmy, rapidly moving their shoulders and upper torso from side to side.
A person steps forward with their left foot on a bent knee and leans forward before standing back up, all while shaking their arms at their sides.
Standing with weight centered, arms out to the sides, and knees slightly bent, the person performs a shimmy by rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso from side to side.
The person first swings their foot outwards, and then the swinging motion moves up to the knees.
A person stands in a T-pose with arms outstretched, vibrating slightly. They then bring their hands to their hips and jump backward, flinging their arms out to the sides.
Step forward on the left foot and return. Step backward on the right foot and lean forward slightly.
A person performs a shimmy by moving their shoulders and extended arms from side to side, keeping their lower body stationary and weight centered throughout the movement.
The person leans their torso diagonally while shimmying their shoulders. Simultaneously, they take small steps while maintaining a low squat.
A person performs a rapid shoulder shimmy with arms extended while sliding forward in a smooth, continuous motion, keeping their upper body centered over the sliding footwork throughout the movement.

The upper body twists from side to side with the shoulders alternating forward and backward. The arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, while the lower body remains relatively stationary, keeping the weight centered.
The dancer leans their torso back while performing alternating low kicks. Simultaneously, they shimmy by slowly waving their bent arms and shoulders up and down.
The person shakes their shoulders with their arms at their sides while alternately kicking their feet out.
The person shakes their shoulders with their arms at their sides, alternately kicking their feet out.
The person performs a shimmy, rapidly moving their shoulders and upper body from side to side. Their arms are held out straight while the lower body moves forward.
The person stands with their arms extended to the sides, vibrating their entire body while moving backward. They then bend forward, swinging their arms straight out in front of them.
A person stands with their arms extended to the sides and knees slightly bent. They perform a shimmy, rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso while keeping the lower body stable.

A person performs a shimmy by rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso from side to side while keeping their weight centered. Their arms are raised and bent, and their lower body remains relatively stationary with slightly bent knees.

A person performs a shimmy by rapidly moving their shoulders and shoulder blades from side to side while slowly squatting down. Their knees stay together as their straight arms swing forward and backward.
The individual leans forward slightly and shuffles forward before standing upright again, keeping their arms straight at their sides and pointing downward.
A person performs a shimmy by rapidly alternating their shoulders forward and backward. The lower body remains stationary, with the feet planted and the weight centered.
The person steps backward carefully while rapidly shaking their shoulders.
The person takes a step forward and shakes their shoulders with arms extended to the sides.
Standing with arms extended to the sides, the individual steps forward with their right leg, then steps back and lowers their arms.

The person performs a shimmy by rapidly moving their shoulders and upper torso from side to side. Their arms are extended to the sides as they move their hips and take a step forward with their left foot.

The person performs a shimmy, rapidly shaking their shoulders while keeping their arms extended to the sides. Their lower body remains stationary, and their weight stays centered throughout the movement.

A person stands with their arms extended to the sides and takes two steps forward with a vibrating motion, then brings their hands to their hips and steps back. They return their arms to the starting position and repeat the sequence.
A person performs a shimmy, rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso while walking forward.

The person performs a shimmy, rapidly moving their shoulders and upper torso from side to side. The lower body remains stationary and centered, while the arms move naturally in response to the shoulder motion.

The figure performs a shimmy by rapidly alternating the shoulders and shoulder blades in a side-to-side motion. Throughout this upper body movement, the hips and legs remain relatively stationary, keeping the weight centered.

A person performs a shimmy by rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso from side to side. Their arms are bent and held out, while their legs are spread wide to maintain a stable, centered weight.

The figure performs a shimmy by rapidly shaking their shoulders and upper torso from side to side while keeping their weight centered. With knees bent, the right leg executes a small kick forward and then returns to its starting position.

The person stands in a staggered stance with their arms bent at chest level. They perform a shimmy by rapidly shaking their shoulders side to side while keeping their lower body and torso relatively stable.

The figure performs a shimmy by rapidly alternating their shoulders in a side-to-side motion. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, while the lower body remains stationary, maintaining a centered weight distribution.
Standing upright with arms at their sides, a person shakes their shoulders while leaning forward.

The person performs a rapid side-to-side shimmy with their shoulders and upper torso while keeping their knees bent. Their arms are held up with bent elbows, moving in sync with the torso's twisting motion.

The person performs a shimmy by rapidly alternating their shoulders and shoulder blades back and forth. While the upper body is in motion, the arms are held out with bent elbows, and the lower body remains relatively stationary with the weight centered.

Standing with knees bent and a stable lower body, the individual performs a rapid side-to-side shaking motion originating from the shoulders. This causes the upper torso and bent arms to sway rhythmically, with each shoulder moving alternately up and down.
messaround: A person bounces on the balls of their feet while swaying their hips from side to side. They make large circles with their right hand over their belly, while their left arm is bent and held out to the side.

The person stands in place with their hands on their hips while rotating their pelvis. They then extend one arm and spin on one foot, using the other leg to drive the rotation, while continuing to rotate their hips and leaning slightly forward.
A person bounces on the balls of their feet, making large circles on their belly with their left hand while extending their right arm straight out to the side for balance.
Bouncing on the balls of their feet with bent knees, the person extends their left arm to the side while moving their bent right arm in large circles in front of their body.

A person bounces rhythmically on the balls of their feet, bending their knees with each movement. Their right hand traces large circles in front of their chest while their left hand rests on their hip.
The person bounces on the balls of their feet with a slight side-to-side hip sway. Their left arm is bent near the torso, making small circular motions, while the right arm is extended outward.

The figure bounces on the balls of its feet while twisting its hips from side to side. Its right arm traces large, continuous circles in front of the abdomen, while the left arm is extended to the side, also moving in a circular motion.
A person bounces while twisting their torso from side to side, keeping the right arm bent near the chest as the left arm sweeps outward to an extended position.

A person bounces on their feet while twisting their hips and torso from side to side. Their left hand makes a large circular motion in front of their stomach while their right arm is extended outward.
The person bounces on the balls of their feet with their legs held close together. Their arms are extended to the sides, nearly parallel to the ground, with slightly bent elbows.
The individual bounces on the balls of their feet with bent knees. While holding one hand stationary near the chest, they sweep the other arm in a large circular motion in front of the body.

A person bounces on the balls of their feet while twisting their hips from side to side. Their left arm is bent with the hand circling near the torso, while the right arm remains extended out to the side.

The figure bounces on the balls of their feet while circling their hips with their arms extended to the sides. They then turn in a circle while slightly vibrating their arms, maintaining the same footwork.
The person bounces rhythmically on the balls of their feet, keeping one hand on their belly while the other arm extends outward and moves in a large circular motion.

A person stands in place, bouncing on the balls of their feet and twisting their torso. Their right hand makes circular motions in front of their stomach, while their left arm is raised with a bent elbow.
A person bounces on the balls of their feet with bent knees, keeping one hand near their stomach while making large, sweeping circles in front of their body with their other extended arm.

A person bounces on the balls of their feet, holding their left hand against their stomach while making large, sweeping forward circles with their right arm. Their body sways and twists slightly to follow the movement.

A person stands with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height, elbows bent so the forearms point downward. They bend forward at the waist and rotate their hips counterclockwise, then stretch upward and perform a slight jump before repeating the sequence. Next, they straighten up and swing their forearms, then lean forward again to move their forearms before performing another counterclockwise torso rotation while shaking their forearms side-to-side. After one more torso rotation, they return to a standing position, holding their right arm at their side while the left arm bends at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward.
A person bounces on the balls of their feet while circling their bent right arm in front of their torso, holding their left arm out to the side for balance.

A person bounces on the balls of their feet, making a large circular motion with their left hand in front of their stomach. Their bent right arm is held near their head, also performing small circular motions.
A person rhythmically bounces on the balls of their feet while bending their knees. They hold their left arm out to the side and circle their right hand in front of their torso.

The figure bounces on the balls of its feet, moving up and down while swaying slightly from side to side. Simultaneously, the right arm is bent with the hand near the abdomen, performing a circular stirring motion.
The person bounces up and down by bending and straightening their legs. Their right arm is extended straight out to the side, while their left hand is held in front of their stomach.
A person stands in a low, bouncy stance with knees bent, holding their left hand in front of their stomach while extending their right arm to the side.

A person bounces on the balls of their feet while twisting their hips and shifting their weight from side to side. Their right hand makes circular motions near their stomach, while their left arm is bent and held out to the side.

The person bounces on the balls of their feet while shifting their weight from side to side. They make large circular motions with their left hand over their stomach while holding their right arm bent out to the side.

A person bounces rhythmically on the balls of their feet with bent knees, twisting their torso from side to side. One arm is extended to the side, while the other hand makes a circular motion in front of their stomach.

The figure stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They lean back and then bend forward, pushing their arms out and pulling them back toward the body. They repeat this motion while circling their right leg in front. Next, they straighten their arms and push them downward while bent over, then stand up and extend their left leg to the side, holding their arms at shoulder height with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They spin in a clockwise circle with their arms extended, then finally stop, straighten up, and drop their arms to their sides.
A person taps their right foot to the side and then performs a messaround.

The figure rhythmically bounces on the balls of the feet, bending and straightening the knees. Simultaneously, the left arm performs large, sweeping circular motions in front of the torso while the right arm remains bent and held close to the body.
opposites: The figure swings their arms back and forth and then laterally, moving their feet in time with the rhythm and beat.
The person swings both arms forward, backward, and side-to-side in unison, while their hips move in opposition to the arm movements.
The person swings both arms backward, forward, and side-to-side in unison, while their hips move in opposition to the arm movements.
A person performs energetic jumps while pumping their arms forward and back. This coordinated movement creates a rhythmic pulse as the arms extend and retract with each jump.
The performer bows forward, bending the torso deeply while keeping the legs straight. Both arms are held straight and extended backward.
A person steps back onto their right foot while kicking their left foot forward, swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back in opposition.
The person moves their hips forward, backward, left, and right while swaying both arms in the opposite direction.
The person swings their arms forward, backward, and then out to the sides.
The person shakes their arms forward and to the side in an alternating rhythm while performing a slight squat and bouncing to accentuate the movement.
A person with slightly bent knees swings their arms forward and their hips back, alternating these motions three times before swinging their arms from side to side.

A person performs a basic Charleston step, alternating between stepping back with one foot and kicking the other leg forward. The arms swing in an ipsilateral pattern, with the arm on the same side as the kicking leg moving forward in sync.
A person sways their hips forward, backward, and side to side while swinging their arms in the opposite direction. They then take a few steps to turn and repeat the hip-swaying motion.
Starting from a standing position, the person reaches both hands forward and bends their torso while pushing their hips back, then suddenly jumps forward.
The figure steps back on one leg while kicking the other forward, swinging their arms in opposition to create a classic Charleston motion.
The person swings both arms back at their sides while pushing their hips forward, then reverses the motion. Afterward, they swing their arms from side to side while swiveling their hips.
The person moves their hips in different directions while their arms sway in the opposite direction.
Both feet remain in place while the body weight shifts dynamically from side to side in a side-lunge motion. Simultaneously, the torso twists diagonally as the arms swing widely.
A person steps forward with their right foot, keeping their back straight and their arms in front with elbows bent. They then bend at the waist, straighten up, and bend forward once more.

A person kicks their right leg forward while raising their elbows and leaning their torso back. They land in a left lunge and immediately spring into a forward-bending jump, kicking their legs backward in opposition to the torso's movement.

A person steps forward with their right leg, bending the knee and leaning back with their arms held in front and elbows bent. They then stand upright and punch forward with the right arm, followed by the left, bending slightly during the movements.
The person performs repeated lunges while both feet remain in position. The torso swings with the lunge movement, and both arms accentuate the motion with elbows bent and pointing upward.

The person steps their right leg to the side with bent knees, pushing both arms out to the side. They then bend forward, straighten up to push again, and bend once more before rising and raising their arms.

A person stands with a wide stance, shifting their weight to perform forward and backward lunges. Their arms are raised to shoulder height with elbows bent, swinging back and forth in sync with the movement.

A person stands with their feet wide apart and performs a series of lunges, shifting their weight forward and back. Their torso and arms swing in coordination with the lunging motion of the lower body.

A person performs a Charleston dance step, alternating between stepping backward with one foot and kicking the other foot forward. The arms swing in opposition to the legs; as the left leg kicks forward, the right arm swings forward, and vice versa, creating a rhythmic and coordinated motion.
A person in a wide stance sways back and forth, bending one knee while keeping the other leg straight. Their upper body and raised arms lean in the opposite direction of the weight shift.

A person steps back on one foot while kicking the other foot forward, swinging their bent arms in the same direction as the leg on that side. They then stand upright and step forward, returning their arms to their sides.
The person kicks their right foot forward and then steps back.
A person steps back with their right foot while kicking their left leg forward. Their arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving forward and the left arm moving back.

Starting in a right-leg lunge stance, the person shifts their weight back and forth by bending their knees. As the weight shifts, the torso and arms twist dynamically in the opposite direction of the lower body.
In a wide lunge stance, the person dynamically shifts their weight back and forth by bending each knee. As the weight shifts, the torso and arms twist in the opposite direction of the lower body.
The person kicks their right foot out while their arms and upper body sway back and forth.
A person sways their upper body and both arms back and forth.
The person stands with their legs wide apart, swaying their upper body back and forth while circling their arms.
Starting in a right lunge stance, the person brings their left leg forward to meet their right, then rotates their torso in a large circle.
double: The dancer performs a repeating step-kick sequence, alternating legs by kicking one forward as the other steps back. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows for balance.

The person repeatedly performs a step-side-kick-step sequence while balancing on the supporting leg. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with elbows slightly bent for balance. While stepping, the person slowly rotates in one direction.

A person performs a step-kick combination, kicking forward with the left leg first, then stepping down to kick with the right. Their arms swing in opposition to each kick, creating a classic Charleston movement.

A person performs alternating forward kicks with their right and left legs in a bouncy rhythm. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to the leg movements, while the upper body twists slightly with each kick.

The person performs a rhythmic Charleston step, alternating forward kicks between the right and left legs. Their arms swing in opposition for balance, with the left arm extending forward during the right kick and the right arm moving forward during the left kick.

A person performs a rapid step-kick combination, kicking the right leg forward and stepping back, then immediately repeating the sequence with the left leg. The arms swing in opposition to the legs—right arm moving forward as the left leg kicks and vice versa—creating a rhythmic Charleston motion.
A person performs a repeating step-kick combination, alternating legs with each forward kick in a fast, double-time rhythm while holding their arms out to the sides for balance.

The person performs the Charleston by executing a repetitive step-kick sequence, alternating which leg steps and which kicks forward. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, moving back and forth to maintain balance and match the double rhythm of the footwork.

The person performs a swift, repetitive step-kick combination, alternating between left and right leg kicks in a double rhythm. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward during the left kick and the left arm moving forward during the right kick.

The person performs a repetitive step-kick combination, alternating between the right and left legs. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the opposite arm pulling back as each leg kicks forward, while the upper body twists slightly.

A person performs a double-time Charleston kick, repeatedly kicking their right leg forward and backward while balancing on their left leg. Their arms are extended to the sides, swinging back and forth in rhythm with the kicking motion.

The person performs a step-kick combination, kicking one leg forward while the opposite leg steps back, then repeating the motion on the other side. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out to the sides for balance and maintain a relatively upright torso.

The person performs a double Charleston, kicking their right leg forward before stepping back to kick their left leg backward. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, and the torso leans forward with each kick.

The figure performs a Charleston step, stepping forward on one foot while kicking the other leg forward and up. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, moving forward in coordination with the opposite kicking leg.

The person performs a Charleston dance move, characterized by a repeated step-kick pattern. They alternate between stepping back with one leg and kicking forward with the other, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs to maintain balance and rhythm.

Viewed from a front-side perspective, the individual performs a double-time Charleston with a repeating step-kick sequence. Their bent arms swing in opposition to the quick, alternating leg movements.

The person performs a Charleston double, characterized by an alternating forward step-kick sequence in a quick, double-time rhythm. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, creating a twisting motion through the torso.

The person performs a quick, alternating step-kick combination characteristic of the double Charleston. First, the left leg kicks forward with the arms held out to the sides, followed by a step and a forward kick with the right leg as the arms move closer together.

The performer executes a Charleston kick, extending the left leg forward and upward while swinging the right arm forward in opposition. The movement then repeats on the opposite side, with the right leg kicking forward as the left arm swings, demonstrating the characteristic step-kick pattern.
The person performs a Charleston dance, alternating forward kicks and steps in a quick, double-time rhythm.

A person stands in a slight crouch with one leg in front of the other and their arms bent at the elbows. They alternate their leg positions while rhythmically moving their knees and shoulders toward and away from each other.

A person performs a Charleston by repeatedly executing a step-kick combination, alternating between kicking the right and left legs forward. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, maintaining balance throughout the bouncy, rhythmic movement.

A person rhythmically performs a Charleston step, alternating between stepping forward on one foot and kicking with the other. Their arms are held out to the sides for balance as their body rocks back and forth with the movement.

The figure performs a Charleston step, alternating between kicking the right leg forward and the left leg backward. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, creating a rhythmic twisting motion through the torso.

A person performs a Charleston dance, characterized by a repeating step-kick-step-kick sequence in a double rhythm. They kick one leg back while balancing on the other, synchronizing their arm movements with the steps.
kicks: The dancer kicks their right leg sideways and backward while rotating rhythmically with extended arms. This rotation involves a slight hopping motion on the supporting left leg.
A person performs alternating low forward kicks, starting with the right leg and then the left, while keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance throughout the motion.

The individual performs Charleston kicks, alternating between kicking their left and right legs forward and slightly upward. As each leg kicks, the opposite arm swings forward while the body remains mostly upright with a slight, rhythmic bounce.

The individual performs a Charleston kick, alternating between kicking the left and right legs forward and slightly upward. With each kick, the opposite arm swings forward while the body maintains an upright posture with a slight bounce.
A person performs a high forward kick, lifting their right knee toward the chest while the supporting left leg is bent. To maintain balance, they extend their arms out to the sides and slightly back.
The individual balances on their left leg and kicks their right leg forward with a bent knee. Their upper body leans back slightly, with arms bent and raised to the sides.

The person kicks one leg forward and hops on the other, pushing both arms toward the ceiling. They then leap backward, landing on the first leg and stepping back with the second while bending over slightly.

The person performs alternating low forward kicks, first extending the right leg and then the left. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides for balance while the upper body maintains a slight forward lean.
The person kicks their left leg high and forward while leaning back with arms bent at their sides. As the left leg lowers, the right leg swings forward to continue the alternating kick sequence.

A person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg and then the left, while maintaining an upright posture. Throughout the movement, their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows for balance.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting by extending the right leg forward and down, then immediately switching to the left. Their arms are held out to the sides for balance, moving slightly in opposition to the kicks.

The figure performs a Charleston kick, stepping the right leg forward and down before immediately kicking the left leg forward. The arms swing in opposition to the legs in an alternating motion, with the right arm moving forward during the left kick.

The figure performs alternating forward kicks in a rhythmic, Charleston-style motion. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left leg kicks, and vice versa.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg and then the right. The arms swing in opposition to the legs as the upper body twists slightly with each kick.

A person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg and then the left, while keeping their arms extended to the sides. Each kicking leg remains relatively straight as it swings up and down.
A person performs alternating high forward kicks, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs. The upper body leans back slightly to counterbalance the motion.
A person performs a series of alternating forward kicks while leaning the torso backward and backward kicks while leaning the torso forward. The arms swing back and forth in opposition to the legs.

A person performs a series of alternating forward kicks while leaning the torso back, followed by backward kicks while leaning the torso forward. The arms swing back and forth in opposition to the kicking legs.
The person performs a series of alternating forward kicks with pointed toes, swinging their arms in opposition to the kicking leg.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg as the right arm swings forward. They immediately switch, kicking the right leg forward while bringing the left arm forward in opposition.

A person performs a Charleston kick, extending their left leg forward and then immediately switching to kick with their right. Throughout the movement, their arms are held out to the sides, slightly bent at the elbows.

A person performs a series of alternating forward kicks, swinging each leg up as the other lands. Their arms swing in opposition to the kicking leg, accompanied by a slight torso twist and a continuous bounce.
The person performs a series of small steps forward, forward kicks with the right leg, small steps backward, and backward taps with the left leg.
A person performs a series of alternating high kicks, swinging each leg forward and backward. The torso leans in the opposite direction of each kick to maintain balance throughout the movement.
A person kicks their right leg forward and upward with arms bent and held out, then quickly switches legs, bringing the right leg down while simultaneously kicking the left leg forward and upward.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg and then the right, in a classic Charleston motion while keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
A person performs alternating low forward kicks, starting with the left leg and then the right, while their arms swing back and forth in opposition to each kick.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg. As each leg kicks upward, the opposite arm swings forward in a continuous motion.

The person kicks their bent left leg forward while keeping their right leg planted on the ground. Their arms move in opposition, with the right arm swinging forward and the left arm back, in a typical Charleston-style kick.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg and then the left. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with each arm moving forward as the opposite leg kicks.

A person kicks their left leg forward and up with their arms extended to the sides for balance. They then lower the left leg while bringing the right leg forward to perform the next kick in an alternating pattern.
The person kicks their legs forward in an alternating motion, swinging their arms back and forth in opposition to the kicks.
A person performs alternating forward kicks in a continuous motion, lowering one leg as the other rises. Their arms are held bent out to the sides for balance.

A person performs a Charleston kick by rhythmically alternating between kicking their left and right legs straight out in front. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out to the sides with bent elbows for balance.
A person in a slightly crouched stance repeatedly kicks one leg forward, raising both arms upward and outward in synchronization with each kick.
The person kicks their left leg to the side, then immediately repeats the movement with their right leg. Throughout the motion, their arms extend outward in sync with each kick.

Standing on one leg with a slight forward lean, the person extends both arms outward twice while kicking the other leg out. They then repeat the motion on the opposite side, all while traveling backward on the supporting leg.

Standing on one leg with a slight forward lean, the person extends both arms outward twice while kicking the opposite leg out. They then repeat the sequence on the other leg, all while moving backward on the supporting leg.

A person stands with their elbows bent and arms at their sides. They clap their hands in front of their body and kick their left leg out before turning 90 degrees clockwise and stepping forward. Standing on their left foot, they repeatedly thrust their arms upward while kicking out with their right leg. They then rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and stand on their right foot, repeatedly thrusting their arms upward while kicking out with their left leg. Turning 90 degrees clockwise, they stand on their left foot and repeatedly thrust their arms outward while kicking with their right leg. Finally, they turn 90 degrees counterclockwise to stand on their right foot and thrust their arms and left leg outward.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, snapping each leg down before immediately switching to the other. Simultaneously, their arms extend in opposite directions to maintain balance during the rhythmic footwork.
The person performs alternating forward kicks while walking in a continuous circular path.
