The individual performs alternating forward kicks with a slight bounce while keeping their knees bent. Their bent arms swing rhythmically in opposition to the legs.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg and then the right. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing slightly in coordination with the leg movements.
A person kicks their right leg forward with a bent knee while balancing on their left leg, holding their arms out to the sides for balance.
The individual hops on their left foot while rotating in a circle, simultaneously performing a series of forward kicks with their right leg.

A person kicks their left leg forward while swinging their right arm forward, then quickly switches to kicking their right leg forward while swinging their left arm. The torso remains relatively upright with a slight forward lean throughout the alternating kicks.

The person moves forward rhythmically, kicking one leg back and down before immediately kicking the other forward. Their arms are held straight out to the sides, swinging slightly in time with the steps.

With arms extended straight to the sides, the person rhythmically kicks their right leg backward and down, followed immediately by a forward kick with the left leg. They then turn to the right and kick their left leg forward again.

A person performs a series of alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg. The arms move in opposition to the legs: as the right leg kicks, the left arm is raised and bent while the right arm extends to the side.
The individual performs a Charleston kick, swinging their right leg forward and down while the left leg moves back. Their arms are bent at the elbows and held up, moving in opposition to the legs.

The dancer performs alternating forward kicks, swinging the opposite arm forward for balance. With each kick, the supporting leg bends and hops slightly as they continuously switch from one leg to the other.
Standing upright, a person swings their left leg behind them and kicks it out to the side. Simultaneously, they swing their arms in opposite directions with their elbows bent.
A person kicks their left leg forward and downward with a bent knee. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance, with the elbows slightly bent.
The person performs alternating low forward kicks, lifting each leg with a bent knee. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, swinging in opposition to the legs to maintain balance.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg followed by the right. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving in opposition to the legs to maintain balance.

A person kicks their right leg forward with a bent knee while keeping the left supporting leg bent. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance, with the left arm held straight and the right arm bent at the elbow.
The person performs alternating kicks, extending one leg forward while keeping the other bent, holding their arms out to the sides for balance throughout the movement.
A person kicks their right leg forward while keeping the supporting left leg bent. They lean their torso back and extend their arms to the sides for balance.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg and then the right. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance as the body's weight shifts from one leg to the other.
The person kicks their right leg forward and immediately switches to their left leg, with their arms swinging in opposition to maintain balance.

The person kicks their right leg forward and up with a bent knee while balancing on their bent left leg. They alternate between kicking each leg forward, holding their arms out to the sides for balance.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, swinging their arms in opposition to maintain balance, with the opposite arm moving forward as each leg kicks.

A person performs the Charleston by rhythmically kicking one leg forward and up while swinging the opposite arm forward. This motion repeats continuously, alternating between the left and right legs in a steady pattern.
The person kicks their left leg forward and upward while leaning their torso slightly back. Their bent arms move in opposition for balance, with the left arm swinging forward and the right arm back.

A person begins in a squat, shaking their knees in and out. They then quickly stand up to perform alternating cross-body kicks. As each leg kicks across the body, the opposite arm swings forward, creating a twisting motion in the torso.

A person performs alternating forward kicks characteristic of the Charleston, lifting one leg straight out while the other remains grounded. As the legs switch, they hold their arms out to the sides for balance and stylistic flair.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg. Their arms are held up with bent elbows and remain relatively stable throughout the movement.
The person performs a Charleston kick, extending the left leg forward and quickly switching to kick the right. Their arms are held up and bent at the elbows, moving in opposition to the legs.
A person performs a Charleston kick, alternating between kicking their left and right legs forward and down. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows for balance.
The individual performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg and immediately switching to the left, while holding their arms out to the sides for balance.

The person kicks their left leg forward twice, then quickly switches to a right leg kick followed by another left leg kick. Their arms swing back and forth in coordination with the alternating movements.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, swinging one leg up and then the other in a continuous motion. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs to maintain balance.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg followed by the right. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, moving in opposition to the legs to maintain balance.
The individual performs a continuous series of alternating forward kicks. As one leg kicks high and forward, the other moves back while the arms are extended to the sides for balance.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg and then the left. Their arms are held out to the sides, moving slightly in coordination with the leg movements.
The person kicks their left leg forward and up, then quickly alternates with the right leg. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows for balance throughout the movement.

A person stands on their right foot, leaning slightly forward with their arms bent at their sides. They kick their left foot forward and then back, swinging their arms in coordination with the movement.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg as the right arm extends forward. As the left leg lowers, the right leg kicks forward while the left arm swings forward in opposition.
The individual performs alternating forward kicks, first extending the left leg while swinging the right arm forward, then immediately switching to the right leg and left arm.
The person kicks their left leg and brings it back.

The figure kicks their left leg forward while swinging their right arm forward, keeping the supporting leg bent. They then immediately switch sides, kicking the right leg forward as the left arm swings to the front in a fluid, alternating motion.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the right leg followed by the left. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to the leg movements.
The person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg and then the right. Their arms extend straight out to the sides during each kick and retract in between.
flapper walk: A person walks forward with their arms raised, alternately flicking their heels up and back. They then bend over while continuing the same leg movements.
The person performs alternating leg swings from outward to backward with their arms raised, followed by side-to-side arm movements.
With a bent torso, the person steps while swinging both arms straight back and forth in rhythm with their steps.
The person walks forward, swiveling their hips and torso with each step. As each leg advances, the corresponding heel kicks up toward the rear while the arms swing in opposition.
The person raises both hands and swivels their foot outward.
The person steps and kicks from side to side in place with slightly bent knees while shaking their hips. Both arms are raised straight up and swing rhythmically.
The person steps sideways and backward while keeping their torso facing forward. Simultaneously, they raise their arms high and swing them side to side in rhythm with their leg movements.
A person walks forward with a distinctive swiveling motion of the legs. With each step, they raise their arms and wave them in the same direction.
A person performs a flapper walk while stepping forward.

The figure performs a flapper walk, stepping forward on alternating feet while keeping the arms extended to the sides. With each step, the torso swivels as the trailing foot kicks back, flicking the heel up.

A person walks forward while swinging their raised arms from side to side and alternating kicks. Each kick is performed with the leg opposite to the direction the arms move. They briefly lower their arms to their sides, then raise them above their head and back down, all while maintaining the side-to-side swinging motion.
A person performs a flapper walk, stepping forward while swiveling their legs and hips. Simultaneously, they hold their arms high above their head, shaking them rhythmically from side to side.
The person raises both hands while alternately kicking one leg outward.
The figure walks forward, swiveling its torso with each step while kicking its heels inward. The arms are held up and wave in synchronization with the body's twisting motion.
jazz square: The person performs a jazz square by stepping forward with the left foot, crossing the right foot over, stepping back with the left, and finally stepping to the right. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with the elbows slightly bent.

Maintaining a low squat, a person performs rhythmic side-to-side steps, twisting their torso and knees in the direction of movement. To change direction, they cross the trailing leg over the lead leg with a small hop that raises their hips. Throughout the motion, their arms swing back and forth in opposition to their leg movements.

The figure performs a four-step jazz square, starting with a forward step on the left foot followed by the right foot crossing over in front. The motion concludes by stepping back with the right foot and then to the side with the left, as the bent arms swing in opposition.
A person takes rhythmic diagonal steps backward, moving away from the viewer. Their slightly bent arms swing from the elbows in rhythm.
The person steps forward and backward, swaying their hips from side to side as they move backward.
A person repeatedly steps forward and backward while swinging their bent elbows from side to side.

The person performs a jazz square by stepping forward with the left foot, crossing the right foot over the left, stepping back on the left, and stepping out to the right. Throughout the four-step sequence, their arms remain bent and swing loosely in rhythm with their legs.
The individual performs a crossover step, moving their right foot from behind to step in front of the left, while keeping their arms raised and bent at the elbows throughout the movement.

The figure performs a jazz square by stepping forward with the left foot, crossing the right foot over, stepping back with the left, and stepping out to the right. The arms are held up and bent at the elbows, swinging slightly in opposition to the leg movements.

The individual begins a box step by stepping their left foot forward and across their right leg. They complete the square pattern by stepping the right foot back, the left foot to the side, and the right foot forward to return to the starting position.

The individual begins a box step by stepping their left foot forward and across the right leg. They complete the square pattern by stepping the right foot back, the left foot out to the side, and finally the right foot forward to return to the starting position.

The person performs a jazz square, stepping forward with the right foot, crossing the left foot over, stepping back with the right, and finally stepping the left foot to the side to complete a square pattern. Throughout the sequence, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows and remain relatively still.

The dancer crosses the left foot over the right, steps back with the right foot, and then steps to the side with the left, tracing a square pattern on the floor. Throughout the sequence, the arms swing in opposition to the legs.

The dancer performs a jazz square by stepping forward, crossing the opposite foot in front, stepping back, and then stepping out to the side. Throughout the sequence, the arms are held out and swing for balance, with the left arm bent and raised higher than the straighter right arm.
The person takes a step backward with their left foot while swinging their left arm forward, finishing in a slightly hunched, light squatting posture.
The person takes a large, deliberate step backward with their left leg while leaning their torso forward, then immediately transitions into a forward walk with their arms swinging in rhythm.

The individual performs a jazz square, starting by stepping forward with the right foot and crossing the left foot over it. They then step back with the right foot and to the side with the left while keeping their arms bent and raised.

The individual begins a box step by stepping their left foot forward and across their right leg. They complete the square pattern by stepping the right foot back, the left foot to the side, and finally the right foot forward to return to the starting position.

The individual performs a quick, rhythmic square step, starting by crossing their left foot forward over their right leg. They then quickly step back with their right foot, out to the side with their left, and finally forward with their right. Throughout the movement, their arms swing back and forth.

The figure performs a jazz square, stepping forward with the right foot, crossing the left foot over, stepping back with the right, and stepping to the left. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with one arm bent forward and the other extended back.

Starting bent at the waist, the person performs a jazz square by stepping forward, crossing over, stepping back, and then stepping to the side. Their expressive arms swing from the front to an extended position at their sides, complementing the footwork.

A person stands bent over with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their torso. They walk forward and side to side while gradually straightening up, before moving their arms to their sides and walking backward.

The person performs a jazz square by stepping forward, crossing the opposite leg over, and stepping back with a distinct torso twist. Their arms swing in opposition to the leg movements, finishing extended out to the sides in an open position.
A person stands bent over with their elbows bent at their sides. They walk backward while moving their arms up and down and gradually straightening their posture.

The person performs a jazz square, tracing a box pattern on the floor by stepping forward, crossing one foot over the other, stepping back, and then stepping out to the side. Their arms are held in a high, bent position, moving in coordination with the footwork.

The person steps forward with their left foot and crosses their right foot over the left, bending their knees to lower their body. Their arms are held bent and raised, mirroring the leg movements as they shift.

The person performs a jazz square by stepping forward with the left foot, crossing the right foot over the left, stepping back with the left, and finally stepping out to the right. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The person performs a jazz square by stepping forward with the left foot, crossing the right foot over the left, stepping back with the left, and finishing with a step to the right. The arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving in coordination with the leg movements.
A person steps forward with their left foot and then crosses their right foot in front of it. Their arms remain bent at the elbows and raised throughout the movement.

A person performs a jazz square by stepping forward with their left foot, crossing the right foot over in front, and then stepping back with their left foot. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out to the sides for balance.

Starting with their arms at their sides, the person steps forward and leans over, extending their arms straight out at shoulder height. They then step back and to the side, returning to an upright position with their arms at their sides.
swings: The person swings their arms widely from the front to the sides while simultaneously performing a gentle kick with the leg on the same side. With each step, they perform a slight hop, shifting their weight from one foot to the other.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, swinging the opposite arm in sync. The body sways from side to side with each step while maintaining a mostly upright posture.
A person kicks their left leg forward and upward, swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back in a contralateral motion as the torso twists slightly to the right.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking their right leg forward while swinging their left arm forward and their right arm back. This oppositional movement is executed with a slight forward lean of the torso.
The person kicks one leg forward and upward while swinging the opposite arm forward, then repeats the motion on the other side in a rhythmic, alternating pattern.
The person swings their arms forward and back in opposition to their stepping legs. With their torso bent forward, their limbs alternate rhythmically from side to side.

A person performs a Charleston swing, kicking their right leg forward as the left arm swings ahead, then alternating sides. Their arms and legs move in opposition, creating a rhythmic, back-and-forth swinging motion.
A person kicks one leg forward while swinging the opposite arm forward, then repeats the movement on the other side in a rhythmic, alternating pattern.
The person kicks one leg forward while swinging the opposite arm forward and the same-side arm back, alternating sides to create a continuous, rhythmic motion.
A person raises their left arm while keeping their right arm bent at their hip, then shifts to the left while swinging their left arm in a circle and performing a slight kick with their left leg.

A person kicks one leg forward while swinging the opposite arm forward and the same-side arm back. This motion is then repeated on the other side, with the limbs moving in a rhythmic, alternating pattern.
The individual swings their arms and legs in opposition, starting with the left arm and right leg forward and then continuously alternating sides in a rhythmic motion.

The person performs a Charleston swing, stepping forward with one leg while swinging the opposite arm forward. This movement is then mirrored on the other side, with the arms and legs moving in constant opposition.
squats: A person kicks each leg out while simultaneously raising the corresponding arm.
The person performs small hops while simultaneously extending one arm and one leg, maintaining this repetitive jumping motion throughout the sequence.
helicopter: Standing on their left leg, the person swings their right leg in a wide, circular motion from back to front, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
A person balances on their right leg while flicking their left leg backward and swinging it in a wide circular motion around their body, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.

Standing on the left leg, the dancer flicks the right leg up from behind and swings it outward in a wide circular motion. The arms extend to the sides, moving in opposition to the leg to maintain balance.
Standing on their right foot, the person rotates their left leg at the knee while holding their arms out and rotating their right arm at the elbow.
The person leans forward on their left leg and swings their right leg in a wide circular arc behind them, holding their arms out to the sides for balance during the rotation.

Standing on the right leg, a person performs a helicopter-like motion by swinging their left leg in a wide circle, starting from behind the supporting leg and sweeping forward. The arms remain extended to the sides for balance throughout the movement.
A person stands on their left leg and flicks their right leg backward, then swings it around their body in a wide, circular, helicopter-like motion driven by momentum.

The person kicks their left leg behind their standing right leg, then swings it outward in a wide, circular motion. The upper body and arms twist in opposition to maintain balance throughout the rotation.

Standing on the left leg, the dancer flicks the right leg from behind the supporting leg, swinging it forward in a wide, circular helicopter motion. The arms are held up and bent, shifting slightly to maintain balance throughout the swing.
charleston walk: A person performs a Charleston walk, stepping forward with one leg while the other kicks back and up. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, and the torso twists with each step.

The person steps forward, crossing one leg over the midline of the body, then repeats the movement with the other leg. Their upper body leans slightly forward while the opposite arms extend to maintain balance.

The person steps forward with the right leg while kicking the left leg back and flicking the heel up. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, accompanied by the distinct hip and leg swivel characteristic of the Charleston.

The figure walks forward, kicking their feet slightly outward with each step, before walking backward in the same manner. Their arms remain at their sides while moving forward, but as they walk backward, they lean forward slightly and extend their arms straight out to the sides.
The person steps forward onto their right foot while flicking their left heel back. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides, swinging in opposition to the leg movement.

The person begins in a low, crouched stance with their torso bent forward. They take steps backward while simultaneously performing a small sideways kick with the other leg before bringing it back to the center. Throughout the motion, both arms are stretched backward.

A person performs a Charleston walk, stepping forward and kicking the right leg out while swinging the left arm forward. They then repeat this alternating motion on the opposite side, kicking the left leg out and swinging the right arm forward.

Viewed from the side, a person performs a Charleston walk by stepping forward with alternating legs. With each step, the back leg kicks up and flicks the heel while the arms swing in opposition to the legs.
The person bends down slightly while extending their arms to the sides. They then step back, placing one foot behind the other.
The figure steps forward onto one leg while the rear leg kicks back and up with a flick of the heel. The arms swing in opposition as the movement repeats on the other side.

The person performs a Charleston walk, stepping forward and backward with alternating legs in a swiveling motion. With each step, the rear leg kicks back with a noticeable heel flick while the arms are held out to the sides.
spin: Pivoting on the balls of the feet with knees held close, the figure spins to the right. During the turn, the left arm is raised and bent, while the right arm is held across the chest.
The person begins a spin with arms extended, pivoting on the balls of their feet. As they rotate, they pull their arms and knees in close to their body to complete a swift turn.
Starting with arms outstretched, the individual pulls them in while pivoting on bent knees to complete a full counter-clockwise spin.

A person performs a full spin by pivoting on their feet with their knees bent and held close together. During the turn, their arms are extended to the sides for balance, and the upper body leans slightly forward.
Starting with arms extended to the sides, the person spins by pivoting on their feet. During the turn, the right arm lowers and moves across the body while the left arm bends and lowers.
A person spins to the right, pivoting on the balls of their feet while keeping their knees close together and their arms extended to the sides for balance.

The individual pivots on the balls of their feet, keeping their knees bent and close together to initiate a spin. As they turn, they hold their arms out to the sides for balance while the upper body and hips rotate in unison.
The dancer spins counter-clockwise by pivoting on the ball of the left foot, keeping the knees together and arms extended to the sides for balance.

The person executes a full 360-degree spin by pivoting on the balls of their feet while keeping their knees bent and close together. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, rotating in sync with their upper body.
A person pivots on their right foot, initiating a full-body spin to the left. As they rotate, they lift their left leg and tuck it close to the body while holding their arms out and bent for balance.
The figure spins by pivoting on the balls of their feet while keeping their knees together. As the lower body turns, the torso twists and the bent arms rotate along with the upper body.
The individual steps their left leg across their right and spins in a circle, keeping their arms bent at their sides.

The person spins counter-clockwise, pivoting on their right foot with the left leg lifted and bent. Their arms extend to the sides for balance, swinging in opposition to the legs to complete the turn.
The person spins to the left, pivoting on the balls of their feet with their knees held close together. As they rotate, their legs alternate positions while their arms are extended to the sides.
The individual initiates a turn by pivoting on the balls of their feet with their knees bent and held close together. During the spin, the left arm extends straight out to the side for balance.
A person pivots on their feet to execute a full 360-degree counter-clockwise spin. Throughout the turn, they maintain bent knees and hold their arms out to the sides with bent elbows.

The person performs a spin by pivoting on the balls of their feet with their knees bent and close together. They extend their arms to the sides for balance as the body rotates approximately 180 degrees.
The person rises onto the balls of their feet with their knees close together to begin a spin. As they rotate, they hold their arms bent out to the sides for balance.

Pivoting on the right foot, the person lifts their bent left knee to initiate a spin to the right. They extend their arms to the sides for balance as their upper body and hips rotate through the turn.
The person performs a full counter-clockwise spin, pivoting on their feet with knees bent and held close together. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance throughout the rotation.
With knees bent and held close together, the individual pivots on the balls of their feet to perform a full counter-clockwise spin, extending their arms to the sides for balance.
The person initiates a spin by pivoting on the balls of their feet with their knees held close together, extending their arms to the sides for balance as they turn.
Pivoting on the balls of their feet with knees held close together, the person performs a counter-clockwise spin while holding their arms bent out to the sides.

The individual performs a clockwise spin by pivoting on the balls of their feet, keeping their knees close together. They hold their arms out to the sides for balance while alternating their legs between bent and extended positions.

The performer executes a spin by pivoting on the right foot, initiating the rotation by crossing the left leg over. During the turn, the arms are extended to the sides in a T-pose for momentum and balance before being drawn back toward the body.
A person pivots on their left foot, spinning to the right. As they turn, they bring their bent right knee across their body while extending their arms to the sides for balance.

A person initiates a spin by pivoting on their feet with their knees bent and held close together. With arms extended, the upper body rotates around the vertical axis, followed by the lower body to complete the turn.
A person initiates a spin by pivoting on their feet with their arms extended to the sides. They keep their knees bent and close together as they rotate their body to the right.
A person performs a full counter-clockwise spin, pivoting on their feet with their knees held close together and their arms extended out to the sides.

Pivoting to the left on the balls of their feet, the person initiates a spin while keeping their knees bent and close together. They hold their arms out to the sides with bent elbows throughout the turn.

A person performs a counter-clockwise spin with their arms extended horizontally. The turn is initiated by pivoting on the balls of the feet with the knees bent and held close together, driving the rotation of the upper body.

Starting from a low stance with knees bent, the figure pivots on the balls of their feet into a quick spin. The turn concludes with the torso twisted, the left arm bent across the chest, and the right arm extended outward.
The individual pivots on the right foot to spin left, lifting the left leg with a bent knee. The upper body twists into the turn, with arms held out to the sides for balance.
The person performs a full clockwise spin in place with their knees bent and close together. Starting with arms extended to the sides, they pull them inward during the turn.

The figure executes a full clockwise spin, pivoting on the balls of their feet with knees bent and held close together. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, held away from the body to maintain balance.

A person performs a pivot turn, rotating their entire body while twisting their torso and head. They pull their outstretched arms in toward their body and keep their knees close together to facilitate the spin.

The individual performs a clockwise spin, pivoting on the balls of their feet with their knees bent and kept close together. The spin is initiated by crossing the left foot over to the right side of the right foot.
The person spins to the left on the balls of their feet, keeping their knees close together. Their arms are bent and held out from the body to maintain balance throughout the rotation.
With knees bent, the individual pivots on their feet in a counter-clockwise spin, extending their arms to the sides for balance as the upper body initiates the turn.
The individual pivots on their feet, turning 90 degrees to face forward while extending their arms into a T-pose.

Pivoting on the balls of the feet with knees bent and held close together, the dancer performs a full counter-clockwise spin. The arms are bent and held away from the body, moving in sync with the torso's rotation.
The figure initiates a turn by twisting their torso to the left while keeping their knees bent and close together. Their arms are held up and bent near the upper body as they complete the pivot.
A person pivots on the balls of their feet with knees bent and held close together, spinning to the right while extending their arms to the sides for balance.

A person performs a full counter-clockwise spin, pivoting on the balls of the feet with their knees held close together. The arms, initially extended to the sides, are pulled in toward the body to facilitate the turn.
The dancer crosses their left leg over the right and pivots on the left foot to execute a turn, holding their arms out wide to the sides for balance throughout the spin.
The dancer performs a counter-clockwise spin by pivoting on their feet while keeping their knees close together, pulling their extended arms in toward their body as they rotate.
The figure initiates a spin by pivoting on its feet, keeping the knees bent and close together. Initially held out to the sides, the arms follow the body's rotation.

The figure starts with arms extended to the sides and executes a spin by pivoting on the balls of the feet. To initiate the turn, the arms are pulled inward and the knees are kept close together as the body rotates.

The figure initiates a clockwise spin by pivoting on its feet with bent knees held close together. As the body rotates, the arms—initially extended—are pulled inward, moving with the momentum of the turn.
The figure spins to the left by pivoting on the balls of the feet while keeping the knees close together. The arms swing in opposition to generate momentum and maintain balance throughout the turn.

The person spins by twisting their torso and hips while pivoting on the balls of their feet. Throughout the rotation, they keep their knees bent and close together, with their arms tucked in near the body.
Pivoting on the ball of the left foot, the dancer kicks the right leg out to the side to initiate a spin. The arms are bent and held away from the body as the torso rotates to complete the turn.
With arms extended to the sides, the person bends their knees and keeps them close together. They then pivot clockwise on their feet, initiating a full-body spin led by the lower body.

Starting with their arms extended to the sides, the person executes a rapid spin to the right. As they turn, they pull their arms and legs toward the center of their body, pivoting on their feet to complete the rotation.
Pivoting on the balls of their feet with knees bent and held close together, the person spins to the left with their arms extended for balance.
The person spins, punctuating the motion by kicking their right leg backward while stretching their left arm forward and their right arm backward.

The person steps to the left, shifting their weight so their hips and upper body sway in the same direction. Simultaneously, they hold their arms in a raised, bent position, extending the left arm further out during the movement.
The dancer begins by kicking their right leg forward and then swinging it back, followed by a sequence of dynamic, alternating toe taps as their hips bounce.
A person sways their hips from side to side while slowly stepping backward. Throughout the movement, their torso and arms remain slightly bent, swinging back and forth in rhythm.
The person shifts their weight to the right, causing a prominent hip sway. Simultaneously, the left leg kicks forward and the right leg kicks back as the torso twists slightly.
The individual sways their hips from side to side while stepping. As they shift their weight from the right leg to the left, their arms cross in front of their body.
The figure steps from side to side, swaying their hips rhythmically. The arms are raised and bent at the elbows, moving slightly in opposition to the hips.
The person rhythmically alternates stepping forward with their left and right legs. With each step, their hips sway from side to side while their bent arms move in opposition to their legs.

A person performs a Charleston-style step, alternating kicks forward and across the body. This movement is accompanied by a pronounced side-to-side hip sway, with bent arms swinging in opposition to the legs.

Starting with their arms bent at the elbows and pointing toward their hips, the person sways their hips and knees from side to side, kicks their right leg out, and then points their arms up and to the right.

The character sways their hips from side to side, bending the corresponding knee while keeping the other leg relatively straight. As the hips sway, the arms move expressively, with one hand resting on the hip and the other reaching up and behind the head.
The person rhythmically sways their hips from side to side, shifting their weight through alternating steps while keeping their arms extended to the sides.
A person steps from side to side, swaying their hips prominently in the direction of each step. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving slightly in sync with the body’s rhythm.

The figure rhythmically sways its hips from side to side in a semi-circular motion, shifting weight with each alternating step. Throughout the movement, the left arm is held high while the right arm remains positioned near the waist.
The individual takes alternating steps forward and backward while swaying their hips from side to side. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with bent elbows moving back and forth.

A person shifts from side to side, swaying their hips and swinging their arms slightly for balance. After reaching one side, they move back to the other in the same manner, slightly adjusting their position before swiveling in place.
Shifting weight from side to side, the person sways their hips laterally while taking small alternating steps with their arms held out and elbows bent.
A person stands with their hands on their hips and elbows pointing outward. They then rhythmically repeat a sequence of kicking followed by swaying their hips side to side.

The person steps from side to side, shifting their weight from one foot to the other, which drives a prominent swaying motion in the hips. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving slightly in rhythm with the body.
The person moves in a circle to the left while extending their left arm.

A person sways their hips from side to side while taking small steps, shifting their weight between their feet. Their arms are held up with bent elbows and remain relatively static, with the left arm positioned higher than the right.
A person sways their hips from side to side, shifting their weight while taking alternating steps. Their arms are raised with bent elbows, moving in rhythm with their hips and torso.
The figure taps their left foot to the side while rotating, then turns around and taps their right foot while continuing to rotate.

The person rhythmically sways their hips from side to side, shifting their weight and taking small steps with each movement. Throughout the motion, their arms remain extended to the sides in a stable T-pose.

The individual sways their hips from side to side, shifting weight from one leg to the other in a rhythmic stepping motion. Their arms are held out with bent elbows, moving in coordination with the body's sway.
The figure steps from side to side, shifting weight between their feet. With each step, the hips sway prominently in the same direction while the upper body remains relatively stable.
A person performs a Charleston step, alternating kicks forward and back. This motion is paired with a prominent side-to-side hip sway and arms swinging in opposition to the legs.

A person sways their hips from side to side while taking wide steps backward with a bent torso and clapping. They then continue to sway their hips while walking forward. During the backward steps, both arms are stretched out to the sides, waving in sync with the hip movement.
A person sways their hips and arms from side to side while stepping backward, tapping one foot next to the other with each step.
The dancer steps from side to side, leading each movement with a pronounced hip sway. They hold their arms out with bent elbows, keeping their knees bent throughout the rhythmic, bouncy motion.
The figure steps side to side with a pronounced hip sway. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, with one arm forward and the other back.

The person performs a Charleston step, alternating forward kicks while stepping side-to-side. This movement is characterized by a distinct lateral hip sway, with bent arms held up and moving in rhythm with the body.

The person performs a Charleston step, alternating between kicking one leg forward and the other back while swinging the opposite arms. With each rhythmic step, the hips sway from side to side, creating a twisting motion through the torso.
A person steps from side to side, led by a prominent sway of the hips. Their arms are bent and move in rhythm with the lower body as weight shifts from one foot to the other.
The person sways their hips from side to side in a Charleston-style motion. With each sway, the opposite leg kicks slightly forward while the arms remain bent and raised.
The dancer sways their hips from side to side in a pronounced manner. This hip motion is synchronized with alternating steps, where one foot taps next to the other before stepping sideways.
The dancer sways their hips from side to side in a pronounced manner. This motion is synchronized with alternating steps, where one foot taps next to the other before stepping sideways.
The person rhythmically steps one leg forward across the other and back while swinging their arms back and forth.

The figure sways their hips from their right to their left in a rhythmic fashion. In a second, the person starts walking backward while maintaining swaying motion with hunched torso and clapping with the walking rhythm.
The person steps backward while swaying their hips from side to side.
The performer steps from side to side, shifting weight from one leg to the other with a pronounced hip sway in the direction of each step while keeping their arms extended to the sides.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking one leg forward then the other while stepping side to side. This movement is accompanied by prominent hip swaying and bent arms swinging in opposition to the legs.
A person walks backward, swaying their hips from side to side while clapping.

The person starts by walking backward with a hunched torso, clapping their hands together in front of their chest. They then transition to walking forward, extending both arms straight out at shoulder level while swaying their hips from side to side.

The figure performs a Charleston step, characterized by a pronounced side-to-side hip sway as weight rhythmically shifts from one foot to the other. While stepping in place with bent knees, the figure holds their arms out to the sides, moving them in coordination with the lower body.
A person steps from side to side with a pronounced hip sway. Their arms are bent and held up, moving in rhythm with the lower body's motion.
The figure sways its hips from side to side while stepping with alternating bent legs. The arms are extended to the sides and remain stationary throughout the movement.
While swaying their hips from side to side with each step, the person holds their arms out wide. Simultaneously, a wave motion propagates from one hand through the shoulders to the other.
The person steps forward while swaying their hips left and right.

The figure steps from side to side while moving backward. With each step, the hips sway noticeably in the same direction. The elbows are bent and raised sideways, while the forearms swing lightly from side to side.
The person sways their hips side to side while swinging their arms in rhythm with the motion.
The person rhythmically sways their hips from side to side. As the hips shift, the opposite leg bends and lifts in a stepping motion while the arms are held out to the sides.
The person rhythmically sways their hips from side to side while taking alternating steps. Their upper body twists slightly, and their bent arms move in coordination with their leg movements.
The person performs a hip sway by stepping forward with one leg and kicking the other back in an alternating rhythm, driving a pronounced side-to-side swing of the hips.

A person performs side steps while swaying their hips from side to side, creating a rhythmic swinging motion through the torso. Their bent arms move back and forth in opposition to the hip movements, maintaining balance and a steady dance flow.
The person steps backward while slowly swaying their hips and clapping, keeping their torso in a crouched position throughout the motion.

The person steps forward and back with their left leg while keeping the right leg mostly in place, creating a prominent side-to-side hip sway. Their arms are held out to the sides and swing in opposition to the leg movements.

The person sways their hips from side to side while shifting weight between their feet, synchronized with a rhythmic Charleston step where the right leg kicks slightly forward and the left leg kicks back.

The person sways their hips from side to side, shifting their weight while stepping alternately with each foot. Their arms are held out to the sides for balance as the lower body performs a Charleston-style hip sway.
The person stands in place, alternately lifting their knees while leaning their upper body forward. Throughout the motion, the arms mirror the movement and positioning of the legs.

The dancer steps forward with the right foot while swaying their hips to the right, maintaining bent knees and a twisted torso. Their arms are extended for balance, with the left arm bent upward and the right arm held lower and to the side.
A person rhythmically sways their hips from side to side while stepping forward and backward. Their arms are held bent and extended to the sides, maintaining balance throughout the dynamic movement.

The person sways their hips from side to side while stepping, kicking one leg forward and then back. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, with one moving forward as the other moves back.
The person sways their hips from right to left, shifting weight while stepping from the right foot to the left, with their arms held up and extended out to the sides throughout the movement.
The individual holds their arms out to the sides and begins stepping backward, lengthening their strides and lowering their arms further with each step.
The person reaches their left leg forward and sways their hips while moving in a circle.
An individual sways their hips from side to side while taking alternating steps forward and backward, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance throughout the movement.
A person steps side to side with a prominent hip sway, keeping their arms extended to the sides throughout the motion.
A person sways their hips from side to side while taking small steps, with their arms raised and bent at the elbows, moving in rhythm with the hip movement.
The performer sways their hips from side to side while rhythmically kicking one leg back at a time, swinging their arms back and forth in opposition to the leg movements.

The person steps from side to side in a low squat, swaying their hips with each weight shift. Their arms, bent at the elbows, move in opposition to the legs, with one hand moving forward while the other moves backward in front of the chest.
A person sways their hips from side to side with their arms held overhead while moving forward.

The figure sways its hips from side to side while taking small, alternating steps backward. The arms are held out to the sides, moving slightly up and down in coordination with the sway. The figure continues swaying its hips to the rhythm while bringing both arms forward.

The dancer sways their hips to the right, shifting weight onto the right leg while lifting their bent left knee. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, creating a balanced and rhythmic pose.

The figure sways its hips from side to side while taking small, alternating steps forward. Initially, the arms are held out to the sides, then raised straight up, and finally held sideways with bent elbows.
A person sways their hips side to side while taking alternating steps forward and backward. Their arms are bent and swing in opposition to the legs, maintaining a rhythmic motion.

A person sways their hips from side to side while stepping, alternating their weight from one leg to the other. Their arms are held straight out to the sides, waving in rhythm with the swaying of their hips.

A person sways their hips from side to side while taking alternating steps forward and out to each side. Their arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows, remaining relatively stable throughout the movement.
Maintaining a T-pose with arms extended, the individual sways their hips from side to side, synchronized with a subtle stepping motion where one leg is positioned forward and the other back.
A person extends their arms straight out to the sides in a T-pose and takes large steps forward while vibrating their body.

The individual sways their hips from side to side, shifting weight between their legs. With each shift, they lift and bend the unweighted leg, bringing the foot toward the opposite knee while keeping their arms extended to the sides.
Maintaining a squat with both arms held straight forward and down, the person waves their hips while stepping backward.
stomp forward: The person steps forward with their right foot, then stomps forward with their left. Their arms are raised and bent, with the left arm forward and the right arm back, swaying slightly with the movement of the body.
The person steps forward with their right foot, placing it in front of the left, while holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows and leaning the upper body slightly forward.
The person steps forward in a continuous stomping motion, alternating feet. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in opposition to the leg movements.
The person moves forward by kicking their left leg out with a small hop and then kicking the other leg out with another hop.
The figure steps forward onto the left foot while kicking the right leg back and up from a bent-knee stance. The arms remain bent and raised, with the right arm forward and the left arm back.
The person performs forward stomps, alternating between the right and left foot. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides, raised and bent at the elbows.
The person steps forward with their left leg and then their right, keeping their arms at their sides. They then kick with their left leg and begin walking backward.
The person raises their left knee high before stomping their foot forward. As weight shifts to the left, the right leg kicks up and back in a counter-motion.

The figure steps forward with the right foot, stomping it down forcefully. As the right foot lands, the left leg lifts to begin the next step while the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.
A person takes alternating stomping steps forward, starting with the right foot. Their arms are bent and raised, swinging back and forth in coordination with each step.

A person performs a Charleston stomp, stepping forward onto the right foot while kicking the left leg back with a bent knee. The arms are extended to the sides for balance, and the torso leans slightly forward.
The person steps forward with their left foot, extending the leg while keeping the right foot planted behind. Their arms are raised and bent as the upper body leans slightly forward into the step.
The person alternately lifts their knees high and stomps each foot forward. Small kicks are performed in between, and the upper body leans back slightly as each leg is raised.
The person slowly steps backward while swaying their hips from side to side. They then step forward while swinging their raised, bent elbows up and down with their forearms held forward.
With a hunched torso, the person steps backward while clapping. They then rhythmically step in place while swinging their arms side to side.

A person performs a Charleston stomp by stepping forward with the left foot. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward and the right arm backward in coordination with the step.

The individual performs a forward Charleston stomp, stepping the right foot down firmly while the left leg kicks back. The arms move in opposition, with the right arm swinging forward and the left arm swinging back in coordination with the legs.
The person steps forward and stomps with their right foot. Their upper body leans backward while kicking and then bends forward with hip movement.
The person stomps forward with their left foot while moving their right foot back, holding their arms up and out to the sides with bent elbows throughout the movement.

Starting with the left leg forward and the right leg back, the person performs a forward stomp. They then step forward, driving the right knee up high while swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.
A person walks forward, twisting their torso exaggeratedly from side to side with each step. Their arms are raised with bent elbows, swinging in opposition to their legs.
The person steps forward onto their right foot with their left leg bent and trailing behind, while their arms are held out to the sides, bent and moving in opposition to the legs.

A person steps forward in a rhythmic, stomping motion, starting with the left leg and then the right. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left leg steps, and vice versa.

The person performs alternating forward stomps, starting with the left foot and then the right, while leaning slightly back. Simultaneously, they bring their arms from their sides to the front and then push them back with their elbows bent.
A person performs a stomping walk, stepping forward firmly with the left foot as the right arm swings forward. This motion is repeated on the opposite side, creating an alternating pattern.
The figure kicks one leg out at a time while walking forward, with the upper body leaning slightly back.
A person stomps forward with their right foot while keeping the left leg back. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows in a dynamic pose, with hands held near the shoulders.
The person steps forward, alternating stomps between the left and right feet. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, moving forward and back with each step.
A person takes long strides forward with their arms extended to the sides and slightly bent at the elbows, then moves their arms in front of them.
The person steps forward, lifting and planting the left leg followed by the right. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent as the body maintains a slight bounce.
A person takes high-knee stomping steps forward, alternating between the right and left feet. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the opposite arm moving forward as each knee lifts.
A person takes an exaggerated step forward.
The person stomps forward, starting with the right foot and then the left, while their arms are bent at the elbows and move in coordination with each step.
A person steps forward on alternating feet while leaning their torso slightly back, keeping their arms bent and elbows flared to the sides throughout the movement.

The figure executes a stomping motion by alternating forward steps with high knee lifts. As each foot lands, the body dips to emphasize the stomp, while the arms remain bent and held slightly out to the sides.

The figure performs a forward stomp by lifting the right knee high and stepping down as the left leg kicks back. The arms swing in opposition—the right arm moving forward and the left arm back—creating a dynamic, rhythmic motion.
The person steps forward, stomping first with the right foot and then the left. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, and the left arm is positioned higher than the right.
The person kicks their left foot out, stomps it down heavily, and then repeats the motion with the other foot.
The person lifts their right knee high and then stomps their foot forward and down. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the left arm moving forward as the right knee is raised.
The person slowly steps forward while swaying their hips sideways. Their arms are spread wide with the elbows bent; both the elbows and shoulders wave gently in coordination with the hip movement.

The person steps forward, starting with the left foot and alternating with the right in a stomping motion. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left foot steps and vice versa.

The person raises their left knee high before stepping forward and stomping their left foot down. Simultaneously, they hold their arms in a raised, bent position, moving the right arm slightly forward during the stomp.

A person performs a Charleston stomp, starting with the right leg forward before stepping onto the left leg. The left leg kicks up slightly and then stomps down as it moves forward, while the arms are held bent at the sides.
A person stomps forward, stepping first with the right foot and then the left. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward as the right foot steps and vice versa.

A person performs a Charleston stomp by stepping forward with their right foot, lifting the knee before bringing the foot down. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides for balance while the left leg remains behind.

The person performs a Charleston stomp, alternating forward steps starting with the left foot and then the right. Throughout the movement, their arms are held out to the sides, nearly parallel to the ground, with slightly bent elbows.

The person takes a large step forward with their right leg, bringing the foot down in a stomping motion. They continue walking forward by alternating legs while keeping their arms extended wide to the sides, parallel to the ground.
The person steps their left foot out and plants it heavily, then repeats the movement with their right foot, while holding both arms in front of their chest.
The person takes two stomping steps forward, starting with the right foot followed by the left. Their arms swing back and forth, with the arm on the same side as the stepping foot moving forward.
The person steps forward, stomping with the right foot while swinging the left arm forward, then repeats the motion with the left foot and the right arm.
The person stomps forward, alternating steps and bringing each foot down firmly. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs while their upper body bobs to the rhythm.

The figure performs a stomping motion by alternating forward steps with the left and right feet, bending the knees with each step. The arms are held out to the sides and move slightly in coordination with the leg movements.

A person steps forward with their left foot while lifting the right foot behind them, arms bent at the elbows. They repeat the movement by stepping forward with the right foot and lifting the left, creating a rhythmic forward stomp.

A person performs a Charleston stomp, stepping forward with the left foot and tapping the right foot beside it, then repeating the motion starting with the right foot. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
With arms extended to the sides, the person takes small steps forward, alternating legs with a slight kick as their hips sway smoothly from side to side.

A person stands slightly bent over with their hands touching in front of them, then straightens up while extending their arms into a T-pose. They then walk forward, swaying their arms with their elbows slightly bent.
The person steps forward with their left foot and then their right, while keeping their arms in a relaxed position.
toe tap back: A person leans forward slightly and hops backward on their right leg while bouncing their raised left leg at the knee, with their arms extended to the sides.

A person leans forward and hops backward on their right foot, tapping their left toes on the floor. Their arms are extended to the sides until they finish the movement, at which point they stand upright with their arms resting at their sides.

The figure performs a toe tap back by alternately stepping one foot back to tap the toe while the standing leg bends. The arms swing forward and inward in coordination with the legs as the upper body twists slightly.
The person alternates stepping back to tap their toe on the ground, starting with the right foot, while holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows throughout the movement.
A person steps backward with their left foot, tapping their toe on the ground while their body moves in a circle.

The figure steps back with the left foot, tapping the toe while swinging the right arm forward, then repeats the motion on the opposite side, stepping back with the right foot as the left arm swings forward.
The person turns in a circle using right-foot taps, with their arms extended for balance.
Standing on their right foot, the person rotates in a circle with their left toes touching the floor and arms extended to the sides while slightly bent forward.
The person taps their right foot out while rotating it.
A person hops on their right leg while kicking their left leg forward and then back. Both arms are held straight behind the body, and the torso is tilted slightly forward.
The person alternately steps one foot back to tap their toe on the ground, leaning their torso slightly forward with each tap while keeping their arms extended to the sides.

The figure stands with their arms at their sides and rotates clockwise on their right foot, repeatedly kicking their left leg out while swinging their arms forward and back. After completing a full circle, they shift to their left foot and move backward, repeatedly kicking their right leg out while continuing the same arm motion. Finally, they adjust their stance to kick with the opposite foot while stepping forward, keeping their arms at their sides with elbows bent.

Starting with arms at their sides, the individual moves backward by kicking one leg out while balancing on the other and moving their arms, then repeats the motion with the opposite leg. They then walk forward, swinging their arms in opposition to their steps.
A person hops in a circle on their right leg while kicking their left leg forward and up. Simultaneously, they lean their torso forward with their arms extended straight back and out to the sides.
A person hops on their left leg in a circle while kicking their right leg and keeping their arms extended sideways.
The person keeps tapping their left foot behind them while moving backward.
The person steps back to tap their left toe, returns it forward, and repeats the movement with the right foot, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.

A person steps back with one foot, extending the leg to tap their toe on the ground while keeping the front knee bent. Their arms are bent and move in opposition to the legs, with one swinging forward and the other back.

A person steps back with their left foot, extending the leg to tap their toe on the ground while keeping the right leg bent. Holding their arms out to the sides for balance, they repeat the movement with the other foot.
The person hops on their left leg while spinning in a circle, kicking their right leg forward and pulling their slightly bent arms in for balance.
A person hops on one foot while spinning in a circle with their arms extended, kicking out with the other leg. They then switch feet and repeat the motion.
The person hops in a circle on their right foot with their arms extended, kicking their left leg out as they spin.
The person performs backward toe taps by stepping one foot back and tapping the floor while swinging their arms in opposition, smoothly alternating between feet.
A person performs a Charleston step, alternating feet as they step back to tap the toe on the ground. The arms swing in opposition to the legs while the upper body maintains a slight forward lean.

The person performs a Charleston toe tap, alternately stepping back and tapping their toe on the ground. Throughout the movement, they keep their arms raised to shoulder level with bent elbows, held out to the sides.

The individual starts with their left foot crossed in front of their right, then steps back with the left leg to tap the toe on the ground while keeping the right knee slightly bent for support. They then bring the left leg forward and step out with the right leg, holding their arms bent at their sides.

A person performs a Charleston toe tap by stepping back on the right toe, bringing the foot forward, and then repeating the sequence with the left foot. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The individual alternates stepping backward with the right and left feet, tapping the toe of the trailing foot on the ground. The arms are held out to the sides and move subtly in opposition to the legs for balance.
knees sway: A person stands with arms extended to the sides, rhythmically bending their knees. The knees twist inward and outward while the feet pivot on the floor.
The person stands in place with knees slightly bent, keeping their upper body still while rhythmically bringing their knees together and then pushing them back out.
The figure stands with arms extended to the sides and knees bent, rhythmically swiveling their legs so that both knees move in the same direction.

Standing with bent knees, the individual rhythmically twists them from side to side while hopping with their feet together. Their arms swing in the opposite direction of their knees, moving in time with the twisting rhythm.
The person swings their right arm out to the side while holding their left arm bent at the elbow in front of their body, then bends their knees and swivels from side to side.

A person performs a rhythmic Charleston knee sway, bringing their knees together and then pushing them apart. As the lower body swivels, the arms remain extended to the sides while the torso sways slightly in coordination with the leg movement.

A person performs a three-part dance sequence, starting with several backward steps while hunching their torso and swinging their bent arms. They then transition into a fast forward walk with both arms stretched behind them. The sequence concludes with a small jump in place, resetting the person to begin the backward walk again.

The person stands with bent knees, rhythmically swaying their lower body from side to side. They move their knees together and apart, with the left leg crossing slightly in front of the right during each inward motion.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking their right leg forward while bending and swiveling their knees inward and outward. Their arms are raised, moving in coordination with the twisting torso and leg movements.
Standing with bent knees, the person rhythmically twists them inward and outward while taking small, alternating steps. Their upper body and extended arms remain relatively stable.
The person steps with bent knees while rhythmically shaking their shoulders. Their raised arms are bent at the elbows with hands pointing downward, swinging in synchronization with their steps.
The person shifts their weight from side to side, rhythmically bending each knee and swaying it inward. Their arms are held out with bent elbows, moving in coordination with the legs.
The person leans forward slightly and rhythmically bends their knees, swaying them while moving backward with their arms bent at their sides, before finally standing straight.
In a squat with bent elbows raised sideways, the person moves forward by swaying their knees and arms from side to side. After clapping, they step backward with their torso fully bent.
A person rhythmically bends their knees inward while lowering their body and moving backward.
The performer bends forward and walks backward, swinging their arms slightly at their sides. They then stand up and step forward, swinging their arms while turning slightly to the side.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, lowering the left arm to their side while swinging the right arm forward. They pull the arm back and kick their right foot forward, then return it to push their left arm and leg forward. Finally, they walk backward while swinging their arms at their sides.

A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of their torso. They jump backward, swinging their arms at their sides while keeping their elbows bent. They shuffle backward while swaying their hips from side to side and swinging their arms. They then step forward and clap their hands in front of their body. Next, they jump and move backward again, swaying their hips from side to side and swinging their arms at their sides. Finally, they stop and extend their arms up to the right, with the left arm bent in front of the body and the forearm pointing toward the right.
The person walks backward in a crouched position with their chest facing the ground, rhythmically swiveling their knees inward and outward.

The person performs a Charleston knee sway, alternately kicking their left and right feet forward and slightly outward. With each kick, the knees bend and swivel rhythmically in and out while the arms are held to the sides.
The person twists both knees in one direction and then rotates them the opposite way while moving backward.
A person stands with bent knees, rhythmically swaying their lower body as they move their knees inward toward each other and then back apart.
A person stands with arms extended to the sides and knees bent inward. They perform a Charleston knee sway, rhythmically swiveling their knees in and out while keeping their upper body still.

The performer moves backward in a swaying motion, swinging their bent knees from side to side. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows and swing in coordination with the movement.

A person performs a Charleston move by bending their knees and rhythmically moving them in and out. Their arms are bent and held up while their upper body twists slightly in sync with the leg movements.
The person rhythmically twists their knees side to side while shaking their elbows. Afterward, they walk backward with both arms extended behind them.
The person steps back while alternately swaying their knees in and out.
The person steps in place with their upper body bent forward, swaying their knees from side to side in the same direction.
The person steps backward with a hunched torso, twisting their hips and swinging their arms in rhythm with their steps.

A person bends their knees and rhythmically sways them from side to side while shifting their weight and moving backward. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving in synchronization with the leg movements.
The person walks forward with bent knees, holding their arms out with elbows bent and vibrating them as they move. They then bring their hands together in front of their body and step backward.
The dancer rhythmically swivels their knees inward and outward. As the knees move apart, the left leg kicks back while the arms swing in opposition to the legs.
The person bends their knees and swivels them outward, driven by their feet pivoting in place to create a rhythmic twist in the lower body.

With arms extended to the sides, the person rhythmically bends their knees, bringing them together and pushing them apart. This swaying motion is driven by a slight twisting and pivoting of the feet on the ground.
A person performs a Charleston step, rhythmically bending and swaying their knees inward and outward while alternating forward kicks.
Standing with bent knees, a person rhythmically twists their lower body by swaying their knees inward and outward as their feet pivot in place.

With knees bent and pressed together, the person twists their lower body from side to side, pointing the knees left and then right. The upper body and arms rotate in the opposite direction to counterbalance the movement.
The person moves forward while twisting both knees in the same direction.
The person sways one knee out at a time while moving forward, with their arms held out to the sides and swaying.
The person sways their knees in the same direction while moving in a circle.
A person stands with knees bent, rhythmically swaying them inward and outward. Their arms are held bent at their sides, and their upper body sways gently in sync with the leg movements.
The person performs a Charleston step, swaying their knees and twisting their torso while swinging their arms, before concluding with a forward bow.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent. They swivel their hips from side to side while moving backward, leaning forward slightly. Next, they straighten their arms and point them out to the sides before pointing them straight forward. They jump back, bending the right elbow to point the forearm toward the hip and raising the left arm with the elbow bent. Moving backward again, they sway their hips from side to side while slowly lowering the left arm, finally straightening both arms to swing the left one upward and the right one across to the left.

A person performs a Charleston step, kicking each leg forward and across the body in an alternating rhythm. As the legs cross, the knees bend and sway in and out while the arms swing in opposition to the leg movements.
A person bends over, swinging their arms at their sides while kicking their left foot out. They then move forward quickly, swinging their arms in opposite vertical directions.
The person sways their knees while moving to the side.
The person stands in a crouched position with arms extended to the sides. They rhythmically sway their lower body, moving their knees from side to side as the rest of the body follows the movement.
A person rhythmically sways their bent knees from side to side while lightly swinging their straight arms and moving laterally.
A person rhythmically sways their bent knees from side to side while swinging their straight arms up and down, moving side to side throughout the performance.
Holding their arms out in front, the person takes large steps forward with their knees bent, swinging their arms in wide loops.
The person bends down and sways both knees in the same direction while stepping back.
The figure performs a rhythmic knee sway by bending and swiveling the knees inward and outward. The upper body coordinates with this motion, swaying from side to side with arms raised and bent.

The individual stands upright with arms out to the sides and elbows bent. As they walk forward, they push their arms out and back, then bring them together in front of the body with bent elbows and touching hands.
The person rhythmically twists and sways their knees while moving forward, holding both arms straight out to maintain balance.

A person performs a rhythmic Charleston knee sway, bending their knees to bring them together and then straightening them as they move apart. Throughout the movement, their arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.

A person walks forward in a crouched position, rhythmically swaying their knees inward until they nearly touch and then outward again. Their arms are raised with hands pointing downward, unfolding outward in synchronization with the swaying rhythm of the knees.
The performer stands in a crouch, rhythmically swiveling their bent knees from side to side. Their arms are raised and bent, moving in opposition to the legs.

A person performs a Charleston knee sway, standing with bent knees and swiveling their lower legs to rhythmically bring their knees together and apart. As they take small steps forward, their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows and hands pointing downward. The arms move in sync with the legs, with one arm unfolding and extending outward as the knees sway.

A person rhythmically pulses their bent knees in and out while twisting their hips and torso from side to side. The left arm is extended and the right arm is bent, both moving slightly in coordination with the body’s sway.

kick step: The person alternately kicks forward in place while swinging the arms in opposition to the legs. They then kick backward in place with the torso leaned forward for balance. Finally, they spin counterclockwise.

The person performs a kick-step, alternating between kicking one leg forward and stepping onto it. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs with bent elbows, creating a slight twist in the upper body.
The figure alternately kicks forward in place while swinging the arms in opposition to the legs. They then step backward and move forward while continuing the kicking steps.
A person kicks one leg forward while shifting the other back slightly, then kicks the same leg backward and bends forward.
The figure performs low, rhythmic forward kicks in an alternating stepping pattern. The arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in opposition to the legs.
The person rhythmically repeats a kick-and-step pattern in place while swinging their arms from side to side. In between, they lean forward slightly and kick sideways by lightly twisting their body.

The figure kicks their left leg forward, steps down, and immediately kicks their right leg forward in an alternating pattern. The arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows to maintain balance throughout the rhythmic motion.

The figure performs a kick step, alternating between kicking the left and right legs straight forward. The arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, and the torso twists slightly in coordination with the kicks.
The person hops on one foot while kicking the other leg out to the side, swinging their arms in rhythm with the movement.
The individual performs alternating forward kick-steps while spinning, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
A person performs a kick-step, alternating forward kicks and steps. Their upper body rocks side-to-side in a relaxed manner while their arms swing in coordination with the leg movements.
A person alternates kicking one leg forward and stepping down onto it. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the opposite arm moving forward with each kick.

The person stands on their left leg with the left arm extended sideways at shoulder height, the right leg bent at the knee and pointed backward, and the right arm extended toward the left. They kick their right leg and bring their arms to their sides, then walk briefly before sliding backward on their left foot while repeatedly kicking the right foot forward. Finally, they stop and stand upright.

The figure performs a series of kick steps, alternately kicking one leg forward and then stepping onto it. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the opposite arm moving forward with each kick.
Balancing on the right leg, the dancer kicks their left leg forward and slightly upward, extending their arms to the sides for balance.

The person performs a kick step by extending one leg forward while the supporting leg is bent. They then step onto the extended foot and repeat the movement on the opposite side, keeping their arms raised for balance.
