The person performs alternating kicks with their left and right legs, stepping back down onto each foot after every extension. Throughout the movement, they keep their arms held out to the sides for balance.
The performer kicks their left leg forward and steps onto it, then repeats the motion with their right leg. Throughout these alternating steps, the arms are bent and swing in opposition to the legs.

The individual performs alternating kick steps, first kicking the right leg forward and then the left. Their arms are held out to the sides and sway slightly, while the upper body leans away from each kick.
The figure walks in a circle with long strides, swinging their arms at their sides with bent elbows.
The person alternates kicking each leg forward, stepping down to transfer their weight. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, swaying in opposition to the leg movements.
A person performs a kick-step by alternating legs, kicking one foot forward and then stepping onto it. Their arms swing in opposition to the kicks while the body maintains a slight crouch.

The dancer alternates kicking one leg forward and stepping onto it, starting with the right. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward during the right kick and the right arm moving forward during the left.

The person performs a low forward kick with their left leg and immediately steps onto it to transfer their weight. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward during the kick for balance.
The person kicks their right leg forward and then steps onto it.

The performer kicks their right leg forward with a bent knee, then lowers it to take a step. The supporting left leg remains bent as the torso leans slightly back, with the arms held up and bent in front of the body.
A person performs a kick step, first kicking their right leg forward and then their left, while holding their arms out to the sides in a bent position for balance.

Standing on the left foot with the right knee bent and foot pointed back, the person extends their arms to the sides. They pivot 180 degrees clockwise on the left foot, step down onto the right foot, then turn 90 degrees clockwise and begin walking in a circle.

The person kicks their right leg out to the side and immediately crosses their left leg behind it, then repeats the sequence on the other side. Throughout the movement, their arms swing in the direction of the leg crossing behind.

A person performs alternating kick steps, kicking the right leg forward and stepping onto it, followed by the left. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows for balance.
The person kicks their left leg out, twists it, and rotates their hips, repeating this sequence while holding their arms out to the sides.

A person kicks their right leg forward and upward while swinging their left arm forward. They then step down and immediately repeat the movement on the opposite side, kicking their left leg forward while swinging their right arm forward.

A person performs a kick step, kicking their right leg forward and upward with a bent left knee. They then step onto the right foot while simultaneously swinging the left leg forward to repeat the motion on the opposite side.
The person moves forward while pointing their right hand upward, then suddenly executes a dynamic left leg kick as both arms sweep up and out.
A person performs a rhythmic kick-step, alternating forward kicks and steps. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, and their upper body sways from side to side with a slight bounce.

The person performs a kick-step by extending their right leg forward with a pointed toe and then stepping onto it. Simultaneously, the left knee is raised and the arms are held up with bent elbows, creating a dynamic forward motion.
The person kicks their right leg out and back while leaning their torso forward, swinging the right arm forward and the left arm back, then brings the right leg forward to take a step.

The person performs alternating kick-steps by kicking one leg forward with a bent knee and then stepping onto that foot. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows to maintain balance as the weight shifts from side to side.
A person performs alternating forward kicks, stepping onto each leg immediately while keeping their torso upright. Their arms are held bent at their sides with minimal movement.
The individual alternates kicking each leg forward while keeping their torso upright. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition, with the opposite arm rising as each leg kicks out.

A person alternates kicking one leg forward and stepping onto it, creating a continuous forward walking motion. The arms swing in opposition to the legs for balance, with the right arm moving forward as the left leg kicks, and vice versa.

The person stands on their left foot with arms extended to the sides and the right leg bent back at the knee. They spin 90 degrees clockwise, thrusting the left arm and right leg forward. They then point their arms to the right, thrust the right arm forward and the left arm back, and push the left leg forward to straighten it.
The person hops sideways to the right on their left leg.
The person steps forward, squatting with each step while keeping their elbows raised throughout the motion.
The person kicks their right leg forward and upward while balancing on a bent left leg. Their torso leans back slightly, with arms bent and moving in opposition for balance.

The person kicks their left leg forward with a bent knee and steps onto it, then repeats the movement with the right leg to travel forward. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing slightly in opposition to the legs for balance.

A person walks forward with alternating high kicks, swinging the opposite arm forward with each stride. They step down onto the kicking foot after each kick, repeating the sequence on the other side to create a continuous motion.
The person kicks their right leg forward while squatting and balancing on their left leg. Their raised arms swing rhythmically up, down, and side to side, creating a bouncy effect.

The person performs a kick step, alternating between kicking one leg forward and stepping down onto it. Their arms are held in a raised, bent position, moving rhythmically in coordination with the lower body.

The person rhythmically kicks their left leg forward and low before stepping onto it, immediately repeating the motion with their right leg. This kick-step pattern creates a continuous forward shuffle, with the arms swinging in opposition to the legs.

The person performs a high forward kick with their left leg while balancing on a bent right leg. Throughout the motion, their arms are held straight out to the sides and their torso leans slightly forward.

The person stands on their left leg with the right leg raised backward and both arms extended straight to the sides. They swing their arms downward while straightening their body in a bouncy, rhythmic motion.

A person kicks one leg straight forward and steps onto it, immediately repeating the movement with the other leg. Throughout this alternating sequence, they keep their arms extended to the sides for balance.

A person performs alternating forward kicks, crossing their legs over the midline while swinging their arms rhythmically. The scale and intensity of the movement progressively increase throughout the sequence.
The person kicks their right leg forward with arms extended out to the sides, then steps onto the right foot and kicks their left leg forward while pulling their arms in and bending them.

A person performs a kick-step by alternately kicking one leg forward and stepping onto it. The arms swing in opposition to the legs for balance, with the opposite arm moving forward as each leg kicks.
Kick the left foot forward.
A person kicks one leg forward and steps onto it, alternating sides. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
Starting from a squat with knees together, the person steps forward with their right leg. Upon landing, the upper body hunches slightly, and the arms hang loosely.
A person performs a high forward kick with a straight left leg while keeping the supporting right leg slightly bent and extending both arms to the sides for balance.

Starting from a crouched position, the person kicks their left leg forward and upward while leaning their torso back. Their arms remain bent and held out to the sides for balance throughout the movement.
The person alternates kicking each leg forward and stepping onto it, creating a rhythmic, marching motion. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows as the upper body sways gently.
A person kicks their right foot forward.

The person performs a kick-step, starting by kicking the left leg forward while swinging both arms forward. They then hop onto the left foot, landing with their feet apart, and immediately kick the right leg forward while swinging the left arm in a rhythmic, outward motion.
A person balances on their right leg while kicking their left leg forward and up, keeping their arms raised and bent with their hands near their head.

A person stands in a crouched position with their left foot and right arm forward, and their right foot and left arm back. They then straighten up and switch their leg positions in a running motion, swinging their arms at their sides.
The person steps forward and then suddenly jumps to land with their feet apart. They then turn to the right, ending in a right lunge stance.

The person performs a kick-step, alternating between kicking one leg high and forward and stepping onto that foot as the other leg moves back. Throughout the motion, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, maintaining a stable posture.

A person stands on their left foot, leaning forward slightly, with their left arm at their side and their right arm extended at shoulder height. They bring their right foot down and clap their hands together before running in place, still slightly bent over.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, stepping onto each foot in a continuous, flowing motion. The arms swing in opposition to the legs while the body maintains an upright posture with a light bounce.
The person performs kick-steps by extending one leg forward and stepping onto it, alternating legs on each beat. Their arms are bent and held high, moving in coordination with the kicks.

The person performs alternating forward kicks, stepping onto the kicking foot with each repetition. The arms swing in opposition to the legs—the right arm moving forward as the left leg kicks and the left arm forward as the right leg kicks—creating a rhythmic motion.
The person performs a kick-step, starting with a forward kick of the left leg followed by the right. Their arms are raised with elbows bent, moving slightly in sync with the rhythmic leg movements.

The person kicks their right leg forward and steps onto it, then repeats the movement with the left leg in an alternating sequence. Their bent arms swing back and forth in opposition to the kicking legs.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms in front of their body. They walk to the side, pushing their arms forward while keeping their elbows bent. They then step backward, alternately pushing their arms forward and upward. Finally, they walk backward in a slight circle while leaning forward slightly.
A person kicks their left leg forward and upward while swinging their right arm forward in opposition. They lean back slightly, bending the supporting right leg to maintain balance.

The person performs a kick step, starting with a forward and upward kick of the right leg while leaning the torso back and raising the arms. They then lower the right leg while simultaneously kicking the left leg forward and up, repeating the same upper body motion.
The person rhythmically steps one leg out to the side and brings it back. As the foot taps the ground, they swing both arms straight out to the sides.

The person rhythmically extends one leg to the side and brings it back. As the foot taps the floor, they pull both elbows backward. After several repetitions, the person begins rolling their forearms in front of their body while maintaining the stepping motion, extending both arms sideways whenever the leg is stretched out.
A person performs a kick-step, extending their right leg forward while balancing on a bent left leg. Their arms are raised and bent, with the left arm held higher than the right to maintain balance.

The person stands with a slight forward lean and arms at their sides. They step back with the right foot while keeping the left foot extended forward, raising their arms to the sides at shoulder height. Next, they swing their arms to their sides and step forward, leaning over slightly. They then step back and thrust their arms straight out to the sides, bending their elbows to point the forearms upward as they slightly straighten their posture. They rotate their torso 90 degrees clockwise while thrusting their arms in front of them. Finally, they step forward while pushing their arms ahead and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise, ending in a wide stance with arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height.

The individual stands with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They bend and straighten their knees, bringing their arms in front of the body with the elbows bent and the forearms pointing upward. They bend forward and step the right leg back while maintaining this arm position. Next, they step the right leg forward and the left leg back, pushing their arms slightly upward and bending over, keeping their arms at their sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They fling their arms straight out to the sides and step back, bending their elbows and moving their arms to their sides. They then rotate 90 degrees clockwise and back, before turning slightly counterclockwise and walking in place while swinging their arms. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and back, bending their elbows to bring their forearms in front of their upper arms with their arms held out at shoulder height. Finally, they spin 180 degrees clockwise and drop the left arm, then spin back and around 90 degrees to face left; they step back, holding their arms out to the sides with the elbows slightly bent.
The individual kicks one leg forward and steps onto it, shifting their weight. Their arms are extended to the sides, moving slightly in rhythm with the body.
A person alternates kicking one leg forward and stepping onto it, shifting their weight. Their arms swing in opposition to the kicking leg while the torso leans slightly backward with each kick.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their body. They step forward while swinging their arms at their sides and turn 90 degrees counterclockwise, continuing the arm movement before bending forward slightly. Next, they turn 90 degrees clockwise to face front, then another 90 degrees clockwise while continuing to swing their arms. They then rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise to face front again and bend over. Finally, they step the right leg back and straighten up, extending the left arm out to the side and bending the right arm so the forearm points left across the front of the body.
The person kicks their right foot.

The dancer performs a series of high forward kicks, stepping onto the kicking foot between each repetition. The arms swing in opposition, extending the arm opposite the kicking leg forward for balance and style.

The person repeatedly performs a kick-step dance motion, kicking one leg forward and then stepping with the other leg either forward or backward. As they tap one foot on the ground, their arms swing up and down.

The person repeatedly performs a kick-step dance motion, kicking one leg forward and then stepping with the other either forward or backward. While tapping one foot on the ground, their arms swing up and down.

A person stands with their left leg crossed over their right, holding their arms at shoulder height with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They step their right leg forward and extend their arms straight out, then transition into a stance with the right leg in front of the left. With both knees bent and leaning slightly forward, they hold their arms in front of their torso with the elbows bent.

A person stands in a bent-over position with their arms at their sides and forearms pointing downward. They straighten up and thrust their arms forward while stepping forward with the right foot. Next, they lean forward and bend over, keeping the left arm at their side and the right arm bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward. They partially straighten and rotate counterclockwise, then step back and turn 90 degrees counterclockwise, leaning over slightly with their arms extended to the sides. They slide slightly to the right and straighten, pointing their forearms forward, then bend over with the right forearm in front of the torso before stepping back and returning their arms to their sides.

A person performs alternating forward kicks, starting with the left leg, stepping onto it, and then kicking with the right. The arms are held out to the sides for balance, with the left arm bent and the right arm extended.
A person performs rhythmic kick steps, alternating legs by kicking forward and stepping down. Their arms are held raised with the elbows bent in front of the body.

The person performs alternating kick-steps, first kicking the right leg forward and stepping onto it, followed immediately by the left. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, the right arm higher than the left, as the torso sways from side to side in synchronization with the leg movements.
A person kicks their right leg forward and upward while balancing on their left leg. Their arms are extended to the sides, with the left arm raised higher than the right for balance.
A person kicks one leg forward, followed by a step, while their arms swing dynamically. The kick-step sequence is repeated rhythmically, and the person performs one seamless full-body turn.
The person performs a kick-step combination, kicking forward and then stepping while swinging their arms naturally. The rhythmic stepping creates a bouncing effect throughout the body.
The dancer alternates kicking each leg forward, stepping down with a slight bounce. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with bent elbows moving back and forth in rhythm with the steps.
The person kicks one leg and steps forward, swinging their arms back and forth in rhythm with their steps. While performing the kick-steps, they move from side to side.
The person kicks their right leg and steps backward while swinging both arms.

The person performs a sequence of kick-steps, first kicking the left leg forward and then the right, landing on each foot after the kick. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, following the rhythm of the dance.
The person steps backward while pointing their arms in various directions.
Balancing on a bent left leg, the person performs a low forward kick with their right leg. Their arms are raised with bent elbows, the left arm held forward and the right arm extended to the side.
The person kicks their left foot forward, steps their right foot behind the left, and then moves to the left.
heel step: The individual takes alternating forward steps, landing on the heel before the ball of each foot.
A person steps forward onto their right heel while bending their left knee. Simultaneously, the right arm swings forward and the left arm swings back as the torso twists to the left.
The person steps forward with their right foot, landing heel-first before shifting weight. Simultaneously, the left arm swings forward and the right arm swings back in a coordinated motion.

The person rhythmically twists their torso from side to side, performing a slight squat with each rotation. Their arms are held out to the sides, bending and swinging in coordination with the movement.
A person steps forward with their right foot, landing heel-to-toe. They maintain a slight crouch with their arms bent and raised.
The person steps forward with the left foot, landing heel-first, while holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows.
The person steps forward onto their left heel while keeping the right foot behind, leaning their upper body forward with arms extended to the sides.
The individual steps forward with the right foot, leading with the heel before transferring weight, while keeping their arms bent at the elbows and held out to the sides.
The dancer steps forward with the left leg, landing on the heel and rolling onto the ball of the foot. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows to maintain balance.

With both knees bent, the person steps forward while turning their body clockwise. They then repeatedly tap their left foot forward and bring it back while swinging their bent right elbow up and down.
The person repeatedly taps one foot forward and back while swinging the opposite bent arm up and down, then switches feet to repeat the movement.

A person stands with their arms at their sides. They bend their elbows, pointing the right forearm toward the hip and the left forearm upward. Stepping forward, they tilt their torso to the right while maintaining their arm positions. They then walk in a circle; upon facing the opposite direction, they drop their left arm to their side and raise their right arm straight up.
Balancing on their right leg, the person steps backward with their left foot, landing heel-first.
A person stands with their arms extended to the sides. They kick one leg in front of the other and then repeat the movement with the opposite leg.
A person steps forward with their right foot, landing heel-first and rolling onto the ball of the foot. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows for balance.
A person steps forward, landing on the heel of one foot. The back leg remains bent for support as they repeat the stepping motion in rhythm.

The person stands with their left arm extended upward and their right arm bent with the hand pointing toward the waist. They walk in a circle taking large steps and finish by lowering their arms to their sides.
The person steps on their heels while leaning back slightly.
A person starts in a low crouch and then walks forward, stepping with their heels first.
The person repeatedly steps back and forth with their left leg while swinging their right arm up and down.
The person repeatedly steps their right leg back and forth while holding their left arm straight out to the side. They then switch to the other leg to perform repeated taps back and forth.
A person takes two steps forward, starting with the right foot and then the left, landing heel-first with each stride. Throughout the movement, their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The person steps forward with their right foot, landing heel-first before the rest of the foot makes contact with the ground. As weight transfers to the right leg, the left leg pushes off from behind while the torso rotates slightly to the right.
The person taps forward, alternating between the left and right toes. Both arms swing back and forth in rhythm with the tapping.
The person taps forward, alternating between their left and right toes, while both arms swing back and forth in rhythm with the tapping.
The person steps on their heels, alternating between both feet.
A person steps forward with their right foot, leading with the heel. As weight shifts onto the right leg, the left leg extends back for balance.
A person taps their heels out one at a time.

A person steps forward onto their right foot, shifting their weight as they plant it. As the right step completes, the left leg lifts and moves forward for the next stride while the arms swing rhythmically.
The person steps forward with their left foot, landing heel-first and rolling onto the ball of the foot. Throughout the step, their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
A person steps forward, landing on their left heel while the right foot remains in place. Their arms are bent at the elbows and held out to the sides to maintain balance and rhythm.
A person performs a heel step by lifting their bent left leg and stepping forward onto it. Simultaneously, they extend their left arm to the side while keeping the right arm bent.
The person is tapping their heels alternately.

The person stands upright with their arms at their sides. They step their left foot forward and raise their forearms above their head while keeping their upper arms at their sides, then alternate moving each foot out.

The person steps out to the right on their right heel while keeping their arms in a raised, bent position. The left foot then steps toward the front-right before quickly retracting to align with the right foot.
The person lifts their right knee toward their chest while balancing on their left leg, then steps forward, landing on their right heel followed by the rest of the foot.
The person steps forward with the left foot, landing heel-first, and then repeats the motion with the right foot.

The performer steps forward with the left leg, pushing the hips back and sliding the right leg behind. The arms swing in opposition to the legs to maintain balance, with the left arm moving forward and the right arm moving backward.

Standing upright with elbows bent and forearms in front, the person holds their left arm up and their right arm at their side. They shift their right leg out to the side while vibrating their left forearm.

The dancer steps forward with the right foot, landing on the heel and rolling through to the ball. The arms swing in opposition—the left arm forward and the right arm back—creating a balanced and rhythmic movement.
While standing on the left leg, the person turns their body and steps onto the right leg, simultaneously raising their left arm.

A person steps forward, landing on their right heel with a straight leg while the left supporting leg is bent. The upper body leans forward, with the left arm raised and bent in front of the chest and the right arm held back.
The person jumps in place and then steps side-to-side while swinging their arms in the opposite direction of their legs.

A person stands with their arms at their sides, leaning slightly to the left. They kick their right leg out while rotating slightly counterclockwise, then step the right leg forward while holding their arms slightly out. Finally, they rotate clockwise and step back, returning their arms to their sides.

The performer executes a heel step by extending the left leg forward onto the heel while lifting the right leg with a bent knee. Their arms are held in a dynamic, bent pose, with the left arm positioned higher than the right to complement the leg movement.
After lightly hopping on the left leg, the person steps sideways and forward with their right leg while swinging their arms back and forth.

The person performs heel steps by alternating forward strides with their left and right feet. With each step, the body rocks onto the leading foot while the arms swing back and forth in opposition to the legs.

A person steps forward with their right leg, landing on the heel and rolling onto the ball of the foot. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, held loosely at chest height as the body sways with the movement.
A person rhythmically taps one foot out to the side while pushing their chest in the same direction, alternating sides in a continuous pattern.
A person rhythmically taps one foot forward and to the side while raising the opposite arm, repeating the movement on the other side in an alternating pattern.
The person walks back and forth while swinging both arms in unison. When changing direction, they stretch their leg to create a bouncy movement.

The person stands bent over with their arms pointing down. They kick their left foot out while rising slightly, extending their arms to the sides. They then pull the left foot back and step the right foot forward, bringing their hands together in front of their face with their forearms held vertically in front of their torso. They then repeat these two movements.

A person steps forward onto their left heel, shifting weight to the ball of the foot as the right leg kicks back. Simultaneously, the left arm swings down and across the body while the right arm swings backward.
The person performs heel-toe steps while swinging their arms in opposite directions, simultaneously rotating their left wrist in a quick circular motion.

Standing with arms at their sides, the person steps forward with their right foot and left arm, then repeats the movement with the opposite limbs. They then clap in front of their body and turn 90 degrees counterclockwise.
The person stands with their arms at their sides and shuffles backward while moving their arms, then bends over while continuing the motion.
The person steps in place, alternating legs by leading with the heel and pulling back, while swinging their straight arms in opposition and turning their body to the right.
toe touch: The figure alternates extending each leg forward with opposite arm swings for balance, repeating this pattern while moving from one location to another.

Standing on a bent left leg, the figure repeatedly taps the right toe on the ground in front. The arms are raised with elbows bent, and the upper body sways rhythmically in coordination with the leg movement.

Starting from a crouched position, the individual extends their left leg forward to tap their toe on the ground. The right leg remains bent to support their weight, while the arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent for balance.
A person balances on their left leg, extending their right leg forward to tap their toe on the ground while holding their arms out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
Standing on the right leg, the person swings the left leg forward from the hip to tap the toe on the ground. The arms are extended to the sides for balance, and the torso remains upright.
Balancing on the left leg, the right leg extends forward to tap the toe on the ground. The arms are raised and bent, with the right arm extended outward and the left arm held close to the body.
The person performs alternating toe taps in front of their body. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the opposite arm reaching forward as each foot taps down.

The figure repeatedly taps one toe on the ground in a back-and-forth motion while swinging their bent elbows slightly up and down. They then switch to the other foot to perform the same toe-tapping movement.
The figure repeatedly taps one toe back and forth on the ground while dynamically swinging their bent elbows up and down. They then switch to the other toe to perform the same movements.

The figure repeatedly taps one toe on the ground in a back-and-forth motion while swinging their bent elbows slightly up and down. They then switch to the other foot to perform the same toe-touching movements.
With bent elbows raised to the sides, the person alternately steps each leg outward and back, repeating the motion while rhythmically bouncing their body.
The person raises their left knee high, then extends the leg forward and down to tap their toe on the ground while the right leg remains stationary for support.
The performer shifts their weight between bent legs with a slight bouncing motion. With each weight transfer, the free leg extends forward to lightly tap the toe on the ground.
While balancing on the right leg, the dancer extends the left leg forward to tap the toe on the ground. The torso leans slightly forward as the arms swing in opposition for balance.
Standing on the left leg, the figure extends the right leg forward to tap the toe on the ground. During the tap, the torso leans slightly forward, and the arms are held bent in front of the chest.
Balancing on the left leg, the person extends their right leg forward to tap their toe on the ground. The arms are held bent in a dynamic pose while the upper body leans slightly forward.
A person stands on one leg and repeatedly taps their other toe to the side and back, swinging the arm on that same side up and down in rhythm with the taps.
A person stands on one leg and repeatedly taps their other toe to the side and back, while swinging the arm on the same side up and down in rhythm with the foot taps.

A person lifts their right leg, bending at the knee to tap their toe on the ground in front of them. They maintain balance by extending their arms to the sides and slightly bending their supporting left leg.
A person stands on a slightly bent left leg with their arms extended to the sides, repeatedly extending their right leg forward to tap their toe on the ground in front of them.

The person starts with their arms straight at their sides and claps their hands in front. They then kick their left leg out while extending their right arm, followed by a right leg kick while moving their left arm back.

A person stands upright with their arms at their sides. They step their right leg out while swinging both arms back, then step their left leg forward, bringing their arms together in front with the elbows bent so they nearly touch.
The person taps one toe forward and brings it back, then repeats the motion with the other foot.

The person extends their right leg forward to tap the ground with their toe while swinging their arms back. They then move their left leg and both arms forward to repeat the motion, finishing with a jump to the side with both feet.
The person extends their right leg forward from behind the left, tapping their toe on the ground in front of them. The upper body remains relatively still, with arms raised and bent at the elbows.
A person moves their right leg forward to tap their toe on the floor while simultaneously lowering their outstretched arms and bringing them slightly inward.
The individual balances on their right leg with arms raised and bent, then brings their left leg forward to tap their toe on the ground in front of them.
The person raises a bent left knee and taps their toe on the ground in front of them, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
Balancing on a bent left leg, the person extends their right leg forward and taps their toe on the ground. Their arms are raised, moving outward from a bent position to maintain balance.
A person hops on one leg while kicking the other leg forward and across the body to tap their toe on the ground. This sequence alternates as the arms swing back and forth for balance.
Standing on a bent left leg, a person leans forward and extends their right leg to touch their toe to the ground, holding their arms out to the sides for balance.
With both elbows raised up and to the sides, the person steps backward rhythmically.
The person rhythmically swings their bent arms up and down while jabbing forward, simultaneously stepping in place by alternating between toe and heel taps.

The individual performs alternating toe touches, beginning by tapping their left toe forward while extending their right arm. They then fluidly switch, tapping their right toe forward as the left arm extends in opposition.

The person stands bent over with their elbows bent in front of them. They step back and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise, then step back again while pushing their arms out and shaking them at their sides.
The individual extends their left leg forward to tap their toe on the ground, supporting their weight on a bent right leg and leaning their upper body forward with arms bent and raised.

Leaning forward slightly, the person extends their right leg to tap their toe on the ground in front of them. Simultaneously, the left arm swings forward and the right arm swings back in a coordinated motion.
A person extends one leg forward to tap their toe on the ground while simultaneously raising and stretching their arms out to the sides.
A person hops on one foot while extending the opposite leg forward to tap their toe on the ground, alternating sides while keeping their arms raised and elbows bent.
Starting in a crouched position, the person extends their left leg forward to tap their toe on the ground, then immediately retracts it back to the starting position.
indian step: The dancer hops from side to side, crossing one leg over the other in a scissoring motion. Their bent arms swing in opposition to the legs as the upper body twists with each step.
The person hops, crossing one leg in front of the other while maintaining a low, bent-knee stance with their arms extended to the sides, parallel to the ground.
A person performs a small hop while crossing their left leg over their right. Their arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows, moving in sync with the legs for balance.
The person rhythmically hops on one foot while crossing the other leg over, keeping both arms raised sideways for balance.
The person rhythmically hops on one foot while crossing the other foot over, with both arms raised sideways for balance.

With arms extended to the sides, the person performs a sideways shuffle while keeping one leg in front of the other. They then switch leg positions and shuffle back in the opposite direction, repeating this rhythmic side-to-side movement.

With arms extended to the sides, the person performs a sideways shuffle while keeping one leg in front of the other. They then switch leg positions and shuffle back in the opposite direction, repeating the rhythmic side-to-side movement.
A person hops on their left leg while crossing their right leg in front of their body. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance, with the right arm held higher than the left.
A person hops from side to side, alternately crossing one leg in front of the other. Their arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.
A person hops from side to side, crossing one leg in front of the other with each jump. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows for balance.

A person hops on their right foot while lifting and crossing their left leg over the right. Their arms are bent and held in a dynamic pose, with the left arm higher than the right as the upper body twists.
A person hops in place, crossing their left leg over their right while extending their arms out to the sides, with the left arm held slightly higher than the right.

The person hops in place, alternating between crossing one leg behind the other with each bounce. Their arms are held out to the sides and swing in opposition to the leg movements, causing the upper body to twist slightly.

The person performs an Indian step by hopping and crossing their left leg in front of their right. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, and their upper body maintains a slight forward lean throughout the motion.
The person performs the Indian step by hopping while alternately crossing one leg over the other. Their arms are extended to the sides, moving slightly to maintain balance throughout the movement.

The person leans slightly to the right while hopping and kicking their left leg forward across their body. Their legs then quickly swap positions in a crossing motion as their arms swing outward to maintain balance.
The person walks sideways with alternating steps while rotating their bent arms above their head. They then turn and straighten, placing their arms at their sides.
The individual hops on their left foot while crossing their right leg in front. Simultaneously, they raise their arms to the sides with bent elbows and lean their upper body slightly forward.
A person performs rhythmic hops on their right foot while crossing their left leg in front. The arms move in opposition for balance, with the right arm extending forward as the left leg crosses over.
The figure hops on the right foot, crossing the left leg in front of the body while extending the arms out to the sides for balance.
The person hops in place, alternately crossing one leg in front of the other. Their arms move in coordination, with one arm bent and raised while the other swings lower for balance.
The performer hops in place, rhythmically crossing one leg over the other. The arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows to maintain balance.
The person hops from foot to foot, alternately crossing one leg in front of the other with each hop while keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
The person hops on their left leg while crossing their right leg in front. Simultaneously, they bring their arms from a side extension down and forward.

The individual stands slightly hunched with their right arm extended out and up, while the left arm is bent at the elbow with the forearm held horizontally across the body. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise and straighten up, pointing both arms forward and down. They then turn another 90 degrees clockwise and rapidly step in place while swinging their bent arms forward and back. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees counter-clockwise and thrust their bent arms forward with the forearms pointing ahead, standing in a slight crouch with their knees bent.
The dancer hops from side to side, alternately crossing one leg in front of the other. Their bent arms swing in opposition to the leg movements as the upper body remains relatively upright.
A person hops in place on their right foot while crossing their left leg in front. Their arms are held high and bent, swaying from side to side with the movement.

A person hops on their right foot while bringing their left leg up and across their body. Their arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward as the left leg crosses and the torso twisting slightly with the motion.
kick ball change: The dancer performs a kick ball change, starting with a forward kick of the right leg, stepping back onto the ball of the right foot, and then stepping in place with the left foot. Throughout the motion, the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
The figure steps back with one foot and kicks it forward across the body, swinging the opposite arm out in a punching motion to maintain balance.

A person claps in front of their body, then quickly steps back and to the right in an 'L' shape, crossing the left foot over the right. Immediately after the step, they kick their right leg forward and upward while swinging both arms forward and to the right. Finally, they step sideways to the left into a squat stance.
The person kicks their left leg forward and up while balancing on a bent right leg. They then step onto the ball of the left foot behind the right leg, transferring their weight.
The person leans forward and kicks their left leg back while reaching their arms in front of them. They then step back and take a step forward to the side, keeping their arms at their sides.
The person kicks their left leg forward and to the side, steps back onto the ball of the left foot, and then steps onto the right foot as the right arm swings forward in opposition to the kick.

The person kicks their left leg forward, quickly brings it back to step on the ball of the foot, and transfers their weight to the right. Throughout the motion, their arms are held bent out to the sides.
The person kicks one leg twice in the air, once forward and once to the side. They then step backward and switch legs to repeat the motion, while their arms swing back and forth.

With both arms extended straight out to the sides, the person rhythmically kicks their right leg backward and down, followed immediately by a forward kick with the left leg. They then turn to the right and kick their left leg forward.

The figure stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward and down. They straighten up, kick their right leg out, and stretch their arms to the sides. Next, they rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and step forward, bringing their arms closer to their body with the forearms pointing forward. They then kick their right foot out and swing their arms to the left as they begin another counterclockwise rotation.

The person kicks their right leg forward and to the side, then steps back onto the ball of the foot. They immediately shift their weight to the left foot while keeping their arms bent and held out to the sides.

The person kicks their left leg forward, moving their arms in opposition for balance. They then bring the left foot back to step on the ball of the foot, quickly transferring their weight to the right foot to complete the movement.

The left leg kicks forward, then quickly steps back onto the ball of the foot, followed by a step onto the right foot. Simultaneously, the arms move from a high, bent position to full extension out to the sides.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides. They kick their left leg out and pull it back while hopping backward, swinging their arms out and down as they bend forward and rotate their torso clockwise. They then walk forward and straighten up, pushing their arms out in front while lifting their right knee. Finally, they step back and lean forward slightly, swinging their arms back to their sides.
A person walks in a counter-clockwise circle with a hunched torso and rhythmic steps. They clap their hands together at chest level and then swing them backward in time with their movement.
The person kicks their right foot forward, steps onto the ball of that foot to shift weight, and then kicks their left foot in the same way.

A person stands with their elbows bent and hands joined in front of them. They push their arms outward while kicking one foot out, then stumble to the side to repeat the motion with the opposite foot.
A person kicks their left leg forward, brings it back to step on the ball of the foot, and shifts their weight to the right foot while keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
The person repeats a sequence of kicking one leg and then stepping around, occasionally turning their body while doing so.
The person repeatedly kicks one leg and then steps around, occasionally turning their body while stepping.
The person kicks their right foot forward and back while swinging their arms at their sides. They then move their right foot to the left side, holding their arms straight out at their sides.
Hunched over, a person walks back and forth in a small area, performing alternating kicks with their legs as they move.

A person kicks their left leg forward, then performs a ball change by stepping back onto the ball of the left foot and shifting weight to the right. Their arms are held up with bent elbows, moving in synchronization with the lower body.
The figure kicks their left leg forward and out to the side with arms bent and raised, then brings it back behind the supporting right leg to prepare for a ball change.

A person stands with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms held horizontally in front of the torso. They step their left leg out to the side while straightening their arms forward and swinging them out to the sides. They then walk backward, sideways, and forward before kicking their right leg forward and clapping their hands in front of it. After lowering the leg, they clap their hands again vertically, swing their arms out to the sides, and kick their left leg out. Finally, they step back while extending their arms out to the sides.
The performer kicks their right leg forward while extending their arms to the sides for balance, then steps back onto the ball of the right foot and shifts weight forward onto the left foot.
With a hunched torso, the person kicks one leg sideways while twisting their torso toward the same side and extending both arms straight out. They repeat the movement, alternating legs.
With a hunched torso, the person kicks one leg sideways while clapping in front of their chest. They repeat the kicking and clapping motion, alternating legs.
Hunched over, the person kicks one leg sideways and hops on the other leg before clapping.
With a hunched torso, the person kicks one leg sideways and hops on the other leg before clapping.
The person performs cross-steps, tapping one leg forward across the other and then stepping outward.

The person performs cross steps, tapping one leg forward across the other and then stepping outward. After a few cross steps in the same direction, they turn while swinging their arms straight sideways in rhythm with their steps.

A person kicks their left leg forward while swinging their right arm forward for balance. They then step back onto the ball of the right foot and shift their weight to the left foot to complete the movement.
The performer kicks their right leg forward and across the body, steps back onto the ball of that foot, and then steps their left foot out to the side to complete the weight transfer.
The person kicks their right leg forward, steps back onto the ball of the foot, and then steps back with their left foot.
After stepping back with the right leg, the person kicks it forward while hopping rhythmically on the left leg. Their arms swing back and forth in time with the hopping rhythm.
pas de bourree: The dancer executes a quick three-step footwork pattern, stepping back, to the side, and then forward. Throughout the sequence, the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows to maintain balance.
The person steps back with their left foot, takes a side step with their right, and then steps forward with their left, all while keeping their arms bent and held out to the sides.

The person steps back with the right foot, moves the left foot to the side, and finishes by stepping forward with the right foot. Throughout this three-step sequence, the arms are held out to the sides, moving slightly with the body.

The person steps back with their left foot, to the side with their right, and then brings their left foot forward. Their arms swing dynamically in opposition to their legs, with the left arm raised high.

The person performs a pas de bourrée by stepping their left foot back, right foot to the side, and left foot forward. Their arms swing rhythmically in opposition with bent elbows, complementing the smooth, traveling three-step sequence.

The person performs a pas de bourrée by stepping back with the right foot, to the side with the left, and forward with the right. Throughout this fluid three-step sequence, the arms are held in a raised, bent position.

The person performs a pas de bourrée, stepping the right foot back and across the left, then stepping the left foot out to the side. They finish the sequence by stepping the right foot forward as the arms swing in opposition to the legs.

A person performs a three-step sequence, stepping back with the left foot, to the side with the right, and forward with the left. Throughout the motion, the arms are held up with elbows bent, shifting slightly in sync with the body.

The performer executes a pas de bourrée, stepping the left foot behind the right, the right foot to the side, and the left foot forward. Throughout this quick three-step sequence, the arms are held up with bent elbows and move in synchronization with the feet.
The person performs a swift three-step sequence, stepping backward, to the side, and then forward, while holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows to maintain balance.
The dancer steps back with the right foot, brings the left foot to the side, and steps forward. Simultaneously, they raise their arms to the sides with their elbows bent.

The dancer executes a pas de bourrée, a quick three-step sequence, by stepping back with one foot, to the side with the other, and forward with the first. Throughout the footwork, the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows to maintain balance and enhance the house dance style.
The person alternately performs side steps and kicking movements while moving side to side. When kicking to the side, they clap simultaneously and then change direction.
The person alternately performs side steps and side kicks while moving side to side. When kicking to the side, they swing the opposite arm upward.

The person performs a three-step sequence, stepping back with the right foot, to the side with the left, and forward with the right. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out to the sides with slightly bent elbows for balance.

The dancer executes a pas de bourrée, a three-step sequence, beginning with the right leg stepping behind the left. They then transfer their weight, stepping forward with the left leg while twisting the torso and drawing the arms in from an open position.

The person performs a three-step sequence, starting with a step back on the left foot followed by a side step to the right. Throughout the movement, the arms are held up with bent elbows, with the left arm raised higher than the right.

The person steps back on one foot, brings the other foot to the side, and then crosses the first leg over to step forward. Throughout this three-step sequence, the torso remains hunched forward with the arms bent.

A pas de bourrée is performed as the left leg steps back and across, followed by the right leg stepping out to the side. The sequence concludes with the left leg stepping forward while the arms adjust from an outstretched position to maintain balance.
A person performs a pas de bourrée by stepping back with the right foot, to the right with the left foot, and forward with the right foot, while keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
The individual takes three steps to one side while swinging their arms, then repeats the movement in the opposite direction.

Starting with one foot crossed behind, the person performs a quick three-step sequence, stepping the back foot out to the side and the front foot forward. Simultaneously, the arms extend outward from a bent position close to the body.
The person crosses one foot behind the other and steps the front foot out to the side, simultaneously raising and extending their arms outward.

A person performs a pas de bourrée by crossing the left leg behind the right, stepping the right leg to the side, and returning the left leg to its original position. Throughout this three-step sequence, the arms swing gently with the motion.

The figure performs a pas de bourrée, stepping the left foot behind the right, the right foot to the side, and then the left foot forward. Throughout this three-step sequence, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, and the torso leans slightly forward.
The figure stands with arms at their sides and elbows bent. They kick their left leg out and pull it back, then step slightly with their right leg, repeating the sequence while turning in a circle.

The person performs a pas de bourrée by stepping the left foot to the side, moving the right foot backward, and stepping the left foot forward. This three-step pattern is then repeated on the opposite side by stepping the right foot to the side, moving the left foot backward, and stepping the right foot forward.

The person steps back with their left foot, then out to the side with their right. They complete the three-step sequence by stepping their left foot forward across the right leg while keeping their arms bent and raised.

The person performs a low, shuffling three-step pattern, stepping back with the right foot, to the side with the left, and forward with the right. Throughout the motion, they maintain a slight crouch with their arms extended to the sides for balance.

The figure performs a three-step sequence, stepping the left foot behind the right, the right foot to the side, and the left foot forward. Throughout this fluid motion, the arms are held bent at the sides, moving in coordination with the legs.
The figure performs a pas de bourrée, stepping the right foot back, the left foot to the side, and the right foot forward. Simultaneously, the arms are raised from the sides upward and outward.

The person performs a three-step sequence, stepping back on the right foot, to the side with the left, and forward again on the right. Their arms are bent and swing in opposition to the leg movements for balance and style.

The dancer steps back with the left foot, then quickly steps to the side with the right while shifting weight. The sequence concludes with the left foot stepping forward, as the arms swing in opposition to the legs.

With the torso hunched forward, the person crosses the left leg behind the right, followed by a side step with the right leg. The left foot then steps forward to complete the sequence as the arms swing lightly from side to side.

A person performs a three-step pas de bourrée, initiating the movement by stepping back with the right foot and then moving to the side. Their arms are raised high and bent, while the upper body twists slightly with the transfer of weight.

The individual stands slightly hunched with their arms in front of their torso and elbows bent. They take a quick step back, lowering the right arm to the side while swinging the left arm forward with the elbow bent and the forearm pointing upward.

A person performs a pas de bourrée by stepping their right foot back, their left foot to the side, and then their right foot forward in front of the left, while holding their arms out to the sides for balance.

A person performs a pas de bourrée, stepping the right foot back, the left foot to the side, and the right foot forward in a smooth sequence. Their arms are held in a raised, bent position, moving fluidly with the upper body as the weight shifts from side to side.
A person punches their left arm forward and turns around, then walks backward while swinging their forearms in front of them.

A person performs a fluid three-step sequence, stepping one foot behind the other, moving to the side, and finally bringing the lead foot forward. Their arms are bent and swing in coordination with the footwork, adding to the rhythmic motion.
Starting with arms at their sides, the individual steps forward, pushing their left arm forward and their right arm back, then repeats the motion.
toe twist: A person stands on one leg, rhythmically tapping the floor with the other foot in a toe-heel-toe sequence. With arms held out to the sides and elbows bent, the upper body sways before the movement repeats on the opposite side.
Standing on one toe and the other heel, the person rhythmically and dynamically twists their legs from side to side. Their hands swing up and down with bent elbows raised sideways.
The person kicks their left leg sideways and touches their toe to the ground while hopping on their right leg.

A person performs a toe twist by planting their heels and swiveling their feet and lower legs from side to side. Their arms are extended outward for balance while the upper body remains relatively stable.
A person performs a toe twist by swiveling their feet from side to side while pivoting on their heels. The upper body remains mostly stationary, with arms held out to the sides for balance.
A person stands and twists their lower body from side to side, pivoting on their heels. The upper body remains relatively still with a slight sway, while the arms are held out to the sides.

In a low, crouched stance, the person pivots on their heels to twist their feet and knees from side to side, while their upper body and bent arms swing in the opposite direction to counterbalance the rotation.
Standing with knees slightly bent, the person twists their feet from side to side while pivoting on their heels. Their upper body remains stable, with their arms bent and held up.
heel toe: The figure performs a heel-toe tap with the left foot, extending it forward to tap the heel and then dropping the toe. During this movement, the body weight shifts from the right leg to the left and back, while the arms swing slightly for balance.
The person shifts from side to side, moving their knees in and out while keeping their arms at their sides. They then straighten up and begin shifting their hips.
Facing forward, the performer quickly taps their right heel and then their toe on the ground, while their bent arms swing from side to side in opposition to the leg movement.
A person steps rhythmically in place, alternating feet, then twists their slightly bent knees sideways.

Balancing on the left leg, the individual performs quick, rhythmic taps with the right foot, alternating between striking the ground with the heel and the toe. The upper body remains upright and stable, with the arms bent at the elbows.
Balancing on their left leg with arms out to the sides, a person extends their right leg forward and quickly taps their heel followed by their toe on the ground.

Starting from a slight crouch, the person extends their left leg forward to quickly tap their heel and then their toe on the ground. The upper body leans slightly forward with arms bent and held out to the sides for balance during the rapid footwork.
Standing on the right leg, the individual lifts their left knee and taps their left heel on the floor, followed quickly by a toe tap in the same spot.

A person stands on their left leg and extends their right leg forward, performing quick, alternating heel and toe taps. Their arms are held in a raised, bent position to maintain a stable posture throughout the rhythmic footwork.
Balancing on a bent left leg, the person quickly taps their right heel and then their toe on the floor. Their arms are bent and held up, moving slightly in coordination with the footwork.
The person stands on their left leg, quickly tapping their right heel and then their toe on the ground in front. Their upper body leans forward with their arms bent and raised.
spongebob: The performer executes high-knee steps, planting the right foot forward and down while lifting the left knee high. The arms are bent and swing in opposition to the legs to maintain balance and rhythm.

The person rhythmically alternates lifting their knees high across their body in a stepping motion. As each knee rises, the opposite arm bends and swings upward, creating a twisting motion in the torso.

The individual performs the "Spongebob" move by alternating high knee lifts. They cross the right foot in front of the left and then the left in front of the right, tracing a V-shaped pattern on the floor while holding their arms out to the sides.
A person alternates lifting their knees high while stepping in place, swinging their bent arms back and forth in coordination with their leg movements.
A person performs high-knee marching steps, moving their feet in a forward and outward pattern while keeping their arms bent near the chest.
A person performs a shuffling footwork pattern, crossing one leg over the other before stepping out to the side, with their arms swinging in opposition to their leg movements.
A figure performs a box step, moving its feet in a square pattern on the floor. Its arms remain bent and move in coordination with the legs to maintain balance.

The person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They thrust their arms forward and then to the left, widening their stance and sliding their feet to the left. They then jump up and swing their arms up and down while walking back toward their starting position. Finally, they stop, slap their right hand against their right knee, and straighten up to stand with their arms at their sides.
The person stands hunched over in a wide stance with their knees bent and arms pointed downward. They rhythmically move their arms and knees up and down.

The dancer performs the Spongebob house move by lifting their right knee high while kicking their left foot back. They then step out with the right foot, shifting their weight between legs in a shuffling motion with bent arms.
A person performs sharp, alternating knee lifts while stepping back, with their arms swinging outward and across the body in coordination with the leg movements.
A person performs a hopping dance, alternately lifting their knees high toward their chest while the supporting foot hops continuously to trace a square pattern on the floor.
bedrock: The person walks forward with slight kicks, holding their left arm bent and pointed upward while keeping their right hand on their hip. They then walk backward, swinging their arms out and jumping their feet apart to land in a slight squat.
The dancer steps in place while swaying their hips from side to side. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, accompanied by coordinated, rhythmic shoulder movements.

The person moves their knees from side to side with their arms bent at the elbows and held at their sides. They then lean forward and step backward, continuing the lateral knee motion while maintaining the same arm position.

A person hops in a short zigzag pattern, lifting their knees high in an alternating motion. The right hand is held near the waist, while the left arm is bent and raised above the shoulder with the hand pointing upward.
The individual stands in a slightly crouched position with their arms bent in front of them. They then drop their arms to their sides and move backward, swiveling their knees from side to side.
The person kicks each foot, then extends their left arm upward and places their right hand on their waist with the elbow bent. They then turn in a circle, kicking each leg alternately as they rotate.
The person steps backward on their heels and then walks forward while maintaining a stable upper body.
The person first kicks their left leg outward and then performs alternating knee raises. Throughout the movement, the upper body remains stable and upright with the arms held out to the sides.
A person steps in place while keeping their upper body still and stable.

A person steps from side to side, rhythmically shifting their weight from foot to foot. While the lower body moves, the upper body remains remarkably still and stable, with the arms held in a fixed, slightly bent position.
rond de jambe: Balancing on a slightly bent right leg, a person sweeps their bent left leg in a circular path in front of them. Their torso leans forward, and their arms are extended to the sides, parallel to the ground, for balance.
Standing on the left leg, a person sweeps their right leg in a circular motion in front of them, with arms extended to the sides for balance.
Standing on the left leg with arms extended to the sides for balance, the person sweeps their right leg in a circular motion from the front out to the side.
Balancing on their right leg, a person sweeps their bent left leg in a forward circular arc. Their arms are raised and bent to maintain balance and style.
Balancing on their left leg with arms extended to the sides, a person reaches their right leg forward and sweeps it in a wide circular arc to the right.
Balancing on the left leg with arms raised and elbows bent, the performer extends the right leg forward and sweeps it out to the side in a circular motion, completing a rond de jambe.

While balancing on the left leg, the right leg executes a circular rond de jambe, sweeping from the back through the side to the front. The arms are held in a raised, bent position to maintain a stable posture throughout the movement.
Standing on the left leg, the individual lifts the right leg forward and sweeps it in a semi-circular arc to the side and back, holding their arms out to the sides for balance.

Standing on the left leg, a person lifts their right leg and sweeps it in a semi-circular path across the front of the body, moving from the inside to the outside with the motion originating from the hip.
A person balances on their left leg and extends their right leg to trace a circular path in front of them, holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows for balance and style.
Balancing on the left leg with arms extended to the sides, the person extends their right leg forward and sweeps it in a circular motion to the right and then toward the back.
A person takes several dynamic steps backward in a wide stance, their arms swinging naturally in coordination with the movement.

A person balances on their right leg while sweeping their left leg in a semi-circular arc in front of the body, keeping the foot close to the floor. The arms are held low and bent, with the upper body remaining relatively still.
Balancing on the right leg while keeping the upper body stable, the person extends their bent left leg forward and sweeps it in a semi-circular arc to the side and back.
Balancing on the right leg, the person lifts their left knee, extends the leg, and traces a circular path in the air from the center outward and back again.
The person balances on their right leg while extending their left leg forward. They then sweep the left leg in a semi-circular motion to the side, keeping their arms raised for stability.

A person stands on their left leg with their right leg extended backward and the knee bent. They swing the right leg forward in a semi-circle, maintaining the bend in the knee, as their hands meet in front of their torso with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, except for the left arm, which is bent at the elbow with the forearm held horizontally across the torso. They slide sideways to the right, swinging both arms up to point to the right. Then, they face forward and swing their arms to the left before turning 90 degrees counterclockwise, swinging their arms at their sides.

Balancing on the left leg, the person performs a rond de jambe with the right leg. The right leg extends forward and sweeps out to the side in a circular motion, completing the circle in front of the body.

The person stands with their arms bent and pointing to the right. They rotate 180 degrees counterclockwise on their right foot, then push their arms forward and pull them back, finishing with their upper arms extended to the sides at shoulder height and their forearms held in front of them.

The individual stands slightly bent forward with their elbows bent and forearms positioned in front of the torso. They move backward by swinging their legs and arms back in wide arcs. Finally, while remaining hunched over, they balance on their right foot while swinging their left leg and both arms in wide arcs.
sidewalk: The person performs a side-to-side sliding step, leading with one foot and bringing the other to meet it. This motion incorporates various improvisations, such as small kicks, body rotations, and knee lifts.
hop: A person hops on their right foot only with the left foot in the air, while their arms are extended behind them.
Following a clap, the person hops on their right leg while lifting their left leg straight forward, extending both arms to the sides for balance.

A person rhythmically hops in place, alternating from the right foot to the left while rotating in a circle. Their arms are bent and swing in opposition to their legs to maintain balance during the hops.

A person hops on their left foot, lifting their right knee high toward their chest with arms held out to the sides for balance. They then switch in mid-air to land on their right foot, raising their left knee to continue the rhythmic motion.
The person hops and turns on their left leg with their right leg and both elbows raised.
The person hops on their left leg while keeping the right leg raised, then lands on their right foot and immediately drops into a low squat.
A person hops from their left foot to their right while leaning forward. The left arm is raised high and extended forward, while the right arm remains bent close to the body.
After hopping to one side and landing on the right foot, the person lightly hops forward on both feet with the torso bent forward.
lotus: A person crosses their left leg in front of their right while drawing their left arm across their chest. They then uncross and step the left leg behind the right, swinging their right arm across the body in a continuous side-to-side motion.

A person in a squatting stance alternately extends each leg to the side while repeatedly lowering and rising. As they stand, the opposite arm swings outward and upward in rhythm with the leg movements.
jack: A person stands with their arms at their sides, then leans backward while extending their arms straight out at shoulder height before leaning forward again.
The person performs a jacking motion, bouncing on bent knees while rolling their torso. They take light steps as their arms swing, extending their left arm high as if shooting a ball.
coupe turn: The person lifts their left leg with a bent knee, bringing the foot close to the right ankle, and then performs a full 360-degree spin by pivoting on the ball of their right foot.
The person lifts their right leg, bringing the right foot to the left ankle, and pivots on the ball of the left foot to execute a turn with their arms extended to the sides for balance.
gallop shuffle: The dancer performs a quick, bouncy shuffle by kicking the right foot forward and stepping back while the left foot steps in place to create a galloping rhythm. The arms are raised with bent elbows as the torso leans slightly forward and twists in coordination with the leg movements.
roger rabbit: The person hops from side to side, kicking one leg slightly forward as they land on the other. The hips and torso twist in opposition to the kicking leg, while the arms are bent and held up.

The figure hops, alternating between kicking the left foot forward and the right foot back, then switching. The arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, while the hips and torso twist slightly from side to side.

drag: Balancing on a slightly bent right leg, a person drags their left foot backward along the floor. Their arms are raised and bent, with the left arm held higher than the right, while the upper body remains stable.
Balancing on a bent left leg, the person drags their right foot straight back across the floor, leaning their upper body slightly forward with arms extended to the sides.
Balancing on a bent right leg, the person extends their left leg to the side. They then drag their left foot across the floor, leaning forward with their arms bent and held out for balance.

Standing upright with their arms bent at the elbows in front of them, the person moves their forearms up and down while rotating their torso counterclockwise and pulling their right leg toward their left.

A person balances on their right leg, leaning their torso forward while slowly dragging their left foot backward along the floor. Their arms are raised and bent on either side of their head for balance and style.

Balancing on a bent right leg, the person drags their left leg from back to front, keeping it close to the floor. As the left leg moves forward and across the body, the torso twists to the left while the arms adjust for balance.
The person balances on their left leg while turning their body counter-clockwise in a semi-circle, dragging their right leg.
The person steps forward with their right leg while opening their chest by pulling both elbows back with hands raised, then steps backward.
Balancing on a bent left leg, the person drags their right foot forward along the floor. The upper body leans to the left with bent arms held up for balance.
Balancing on their left leg, the person leans their torso forward while slowly dragging their right foot straight back along the ground, arms extended to the sides for balance.
The person balances on their left leg, leaning forward while dragging their right foot backward and inward across the floor, with their arms extended to the sides for stability.
The person leans forward, balancing on a bent left leg while extending the right leg straight back and slowly dragging it across the floor.

With both hands on the left knee and balancing on the left leg, the person slowly brings the right leg toward the left. Suddenly, they stand up and drag the left leg to the right while raising their right elbow high.
Balancing on their right leg, a person extends their left leg forward before dragging it back across their body. Simultaneously, they raise their arms and sway them fluidly from side to side.
Balancing on a bent right leg, a person sweeps their left foot backward and out to the side, keeping their arms raised and bent for balance.
Balancing on the right leg, the person leans forward and drags their left foot backward across the floor. The arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving forward and the left arm back.

With the right arm extended forward and the right leg back, the individual swings their right arm down while dragging their right leg across to the left. They then perform a small jump, bringing both hands together in front of their hips.
side step: The person walks forward with their arms held at chest height and elbows pointed outward, then performs alternating leg extensions that swing outward and then forward.

The person maintains a forward-leaning posture while performing alternating side steps, pausing briefly as each leg extends straight. The arms move in coordination with the legs; one arm extends forward with a sharp, punctuated motion while the opposite arm remains bent with the elbow pointing outward.

The person pivots counterclockwise on their left foot, performing two quick kicks with the right leg before stepping forward. Throughout the motion, their arms remain bent at the elbows and move rhythmically at their sides.
The person takes a step forward with the right foot, turns around, and takes a step forward with the left foot.
The person performs side steps, stepping out to the right and bringing the left foot in, then repeating the movement to the left. Their arms are held up and bent, swaying in rhythm with the body.

The person performs rhythmic side steps, moving from right to left and back again in a crouched position. They shift their weight with each step while keeping their arms bent and raised in front of their chest.

The person performs a side-stepping dance motion, bringing one leg toward the other sideways and then shifting into a deep side lunge. During the lunge, the torso hunches forward while the opposite hand reaches down. The movement is repeated from side to side.

The person steps to the right, bringing their left foot to meet the right, then repeats the sequence to the left. Throughout the motion, they keep their arms raised to shoulder level and bent at the elbows, held out to the sides.

The person steps to the right, pulls the foot back, and steps out with the left foot before pulling it back and stepping out to the right once more. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows as the torso and arms vibrate subtly.

The person starts with their left leg extended to the side and their arms raised. They then bring their left foot in to meet the right, lowering into a slight crouch while swinging their arms down and forward.
The person stands with their arms raised and steps to the side, then bends forward and shifts from side to side.
The person keeps tapping their left foot to the left side.

The person performs side-to-side steps using only the right leg while keeping the left knee bent. Each time the right leg steps out, the left elbow points upward and the torso twists slightly in rhythm with the movement.
