With arms bent at their sides, a person alternates raising and lowering their shoulders while kicking out their left foot. They then stop and move the left foot slightly forward.

The person steps to the right with their right foot while shaking their arms at their sides, then returns to the center and steps to the left with their left foot while continuing to shake their arms.

A person takes a side step to the left, bringing their right foot to meet the left, and then repeats the motion to the right. Throughout the movement, their arms are held up with bent elbows and sway in the direction of each step.
A person takes a side step to the right by extending their right leg and drawing their left leg in to meet it, maintaining a crouched posture with arms held up and bent throughout the movement.

A person steps to the right, extending their right leg while keeping the left leg planted. Simultaneously, they strike a dynamic pose, bending their left arm upward and extending their right arm to the side.

The individual takes a side step to the left, leading with the left foot and bringing the right foot to meet it. Throughout the movement, the arms are held up and bent at the elbows, with the hands positioned near the chest and head.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides. They step forward with their left leg and rotate 90 degrees clockwise while lifting their right arm above their head, bending the elbow to point the forearm forward. They then step to the side, vibrating the raised arm above their head before pointing both arms backward. Next, they jump slightly and swing their arms forward in an underhand motion. Finally, they step back with their right foot and swing both arms back again, keeping them straight.
The person takes a side step with the other foot following, starting with an upright posture and then bending their upper body downward.
The person steps their left foot out to the side and slides their legs back and forth, then repeats this movement with the right foot.

A person takes a side step onto their right leg while waving their upper body. The left arm is raised to shoulder height and bent at the elbow, while the right arm swings back and forth in coordination with the step.
Bending forward with arms pointed downward, the individual moves their legs from side to side while vibrating their hips.

The individual steps to the right, bringing their left foot to join the right, then repeats the motion to the left. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing rhythmically in sync with the side-to-side steps.
A person bends deeply at the waist while taking large, alternating side steps, swinging their extended arms around the torso in a twisting, windmill-like motion.

A person takes a wide step to the right, lunging onto a bent right knee while keeping the left leg extended. Their arms are raised in an asymmetrical bend, with the right arm held higher and further forward.
The person stands upright in a T-pose. They bend their left knee and step to the side while turning, lowering their arms to their sides with their elbows bent.
The person brings the right leg from the side to the center, then performs several kicks with the left leg.

The person steps to the right with their right foot and brings their left foot in to meet it. This rhythmic side-stepping motion is performed with arms raised and bent at the elbows, swaying slightly with the movement of the body.
The person takes a side step to the left, bringing their right foot to meet the left. Throughout the movement, their arms remain raised and bent, with their hands held near their head.

The individual stands hunched over with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They thrust their arms and left leg out to the side, followed by their arms and right leg. They then rotate 180 degrees clockwise while throwing their arms up, before sliding forward and again raising their arms as they extend the right leg to the side. Rotating 90 degrees clockwise, they thrust their left leg back and throw their arms up, then rotate another 90 degrees clockwise to thrust their arms forward and left leg back, bending over as they do so. Finally, they pull their arms back and push them to the left, then slide to the right while leaning left with their arms extended out at shoulder level.

A person takes a side step by shifting their weight onto their left leg while bending and lifting their right leg. Their arms are raised to shoulder height with bent elbows and held out in front of the body.
The figure stands with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They move sideways in a counterclockwise circle, swinging their arms forward and back as they go.

The person performs a crossover step to the side, crossing one leg in front of the body while twisting their torso. Their arms move in opposition, with one held high near the head and the other sweeping across the chest to accentuate the turn.
The person performs side steps one foot at a time by bringing the right leg to the left and moving the left leg further out, swinging their arms as they move.

The person performs a side step, moving one foot laterally and bringing the other to meet it. Throughout the movement, the arms are bent and raised, with the right arm held higher than the left, as the body sways slightly with each step.

The person stands in a wide stance with their left arm at their side and their right arm bent, the forearm held in front of the body. They rapidly move their left leg to the side and back while clapping, moving their bent arms toward and away from each other in front of the torso.
The person takes a large side step to the left while swinging both arms toward their body in a vertical circular motion, starting from a downward and forward position.
A person steps to the right, shifting their weight onto the right foot and bringing the left foot to meet it, while keeping their arms raised and bent in a dynamic pose throughout the motion.
knee bend: The person stands with knees bent and bounces one leg toward their body and back out, then repeats the motion with the other leg.
The person performs a rhythmic up-and-down motion by alternating knee bends in a semi-squat position, while their arms bend slightly in sync with the movement.
A person stands with their legs apart and bounces rhythmically up and down by bending their knees, with their arms raised and bent out to the sides.
The person performs two consecutive arm bends, pulling their elbows back slightly in sync with alternating leg flexions.
The person performs rhythmic, alternating knee bends while in a semi-squat position. Their arms are slightly bent, and their shoulders rotate in coordination with the movement of their knees.

The person performs a rhythmic knee bend, lowering their body by flexing their knees and hips and then returning to the starting position, all while keeping their arms extended out to the sides throughout the movement.
back step: The performer steps backward with their right foot and then brings it forward, returning to the starting position. As the foot returns, the forward-bent upper body straightens and rises to balance the weight.

The individual steps back with their left leg, leaning their torso forward and extending their arms out to the sides. They then bring their right foot back to meet the left, returning to a standing position slightly behind where they started.
A person swings one leg back while bending forward, then steps forward and straightens up, swinging their arms throughout the movement.

The performer steps backward with their right foot and then brings it forward, passing through the starting position. As the right foot returns, the upper body straightens from a forward bend and moves upward to balance the weight.
The performer steps back with the right foot while leaning the torso forward, then returns the right foot forward while straightening the upper body into a balanced, upright stance.

A person steps back with their right foot, leaning their upper body forward with arms bent out to the sides. They then bring their right foot forward again, returning to an upright position and moving their arms back to the starting position.
A person steps back with their right foot, bending their knees and leaning their torso forward, before kicking forward with the same foot.

A person standing upright kicks their right foot forward, pulls it back, and then kicks forward again. Simultaneously, their left arm moves in sync with the right leg while their right arm moves in the opposite direction.

The person steps back with one leg while leaning the upper body forward, then returns to the starting position while straightening the torso. Throughout the movement, the arms swing in opposition to the legs.

The individual steps back with their right foot, bending the knee while keeping the left leg straight. Simultaneously, they raise and bend both arms, positioning their hands near the head and shoulders.

A person steps forward with their right foot, kicks it back while bending over, and then steps forward again. As they bend over, they touch their right arm to the ground, then pull it back and swing their left arm forward as they step forward once more.

A person in a low stance steps backward with their left foot, lowering their body further to touch the ground with their right arm while their left arm moves back. They then stand up by bringing their right leg forward.
The individual steps backward with their right foot while moving their left arm back, then turns slightly and steps back again with their right foot, extending both arms out to their sides.
A person steps back onto their right foot and lifts their bent left leg behind them. Their arms are raised with elbows bent as the torso leans forward throughout the movement.

The person crouches, drawing their right leg inward from the side with the knee bent toward the center. Simultaneously, the left arm pulls back in an elbowing motion while the torso tilts slightly sideways to emphasize the movement.
The person steps back with their right foot, leaning their torso forward while swinging their left arm forward. They then return the right foot to meet the left, completing the motion.

The performer steps their right foot back into a deep lunge, hinging the torso forward to touch the ground with the right hand while extending the left arm vertically. They then push off the back foot to return to a standing position, lifting the right knee forward.
The person steps back with their right foot, pauses briefly, and then kicks forward.
The person steps back with their left foot and brings their right foot to meet it while extending their arms to the sides and leaning their torso forward.
A person steps backward with their right foot and then returns it to meet the left. Throughout the motion, the arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.

A person steps back with their right foot while keeping the left foot in place, bending their torso fully forward. The right arm reaches forward to touch the ground, while the left arm extends backward for balance.
The person kicks their right foot back, leans forward, and reaches their right hand forward, then returns to the original position.

The person bends forward and kicks their right leg back, touching their right hand to the ground while extending their left arm to the side. They then straighten up and step forward with their right foot, letting their arms fall to their sides.
The person kicks their right leg backward while sweeping their right arm inward across their body. They bend their left leg with the left arm raised sideways for stability.

A person bends forward and kicks their right leg back, touching the ground with their right hand while extending their left arm to the side. They then straighten up and step their right foot forward, bringing both arms to their sides.
The person kicks the left leg forward while swinging both arms sideways. They then step the right leg backward and forward again while swinging the arms back and forth in opposition to the legs.
A person rotates to the side, bending slightly as they quickly swing each leg back. Simultaneously, they thrust one arm upward and the other backward in rapid succession.
A person steps back with their right foot, placing it behind the stationary left foot. Simultaneously, they raise their arms in a bent position, holding the right arm higher than the left.
A person taps backward.
The person steps back with their right foot and kicks it forward and up. The right arm swings forward with the kick, while the left arm swings back for balance.

Stepping back with the right foot, the person bends their torso forward until the right hand touches the ground. The left foot remains stationary as the left arm extends straight back for counterbalance.

The person bends forward and kicks their right leg back while touching the ground with their right hand and extending their left arm up and out to the side. They then return to a standing position with their arms by their sides.

The person bends forward and kicks their right leg back, touching the ground with their right hand while extending their left arm up and to the side. They then return to a standing position with their arms at their sides.
The person steps backward with their right foot, bringing the left to meet it. While stepping, their bent arms swing back and forth as their body leans forward.

A person steps back with their right foot while keeping their left foot planted, causing the torso to lean forward. To maintain balance, the right arm reaches forward while the left arm extends backward.
The person steps back with their right foot, leaning forward while keeping their arms at their sides. They then bend their arms and straighten up, kicking the right foot forward again.
The person steps back and to the right with their right foot, then immediately kicks out.

Starting from a standing position with arms bent at the elbows, the person bends their right leg and kicks back, then crosses it in front of their left leg to touch the floor before bringing it forward again, all while swinging their arms at their sides.
A person leans forward while stepping back with their left foot toward their right. Their left arm extends forward and their right arm bends upward as their torso twists slightly to the right.

The figure stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They step the right leg behind the left and back, bending over. They then straighten up and jump back, extending the right arm behind them and holding the left arm bent in front. Next, they jump to the side and lean forward slightly with their arms pointing down, kicking the right leg behind the left. They shuffle to the side and raise their arms up and to the left, with the left elbow bent and the forearm in front of the body. Holding their arms out in front, they shuffle to the left and kick the right leg behind the left again, keeping the right arm straight and the left arm at their side with the elbow bent and forearm forward. They then shuffle to the right, swinging the right arm straight back while the left arm is in front with the elbow bent and forearm pointing to the side. Finally, they shift to the left with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms forward.
A person steps backward while slightly bending their knees and leaning their torso forward. Both arms circle forward and backward in rhythm with the steps.
The person steps back with their left foot, placing it behind their right, while extending their arms out to the sides for balance.
The person walks backward with bent knees and swinging arms while leaning forward slightly. They then slowly turn to the side while continuing these movements.
A person steps back with their right foot and brings their left foot to meet it, while keeping both arms raised and extended to the sides with a slight bend at the elbows.
The person steps backward while twisting their knees and waving their arms in circles.
The person steps back with the left foot, crossing it behind the right leg while maintaining a slight crouch. The arms are held up and bent, with the left arm positioned higher than the right.
The person reaches their right foot back, taps the ground with their toe, and then kicks forward.
The person steps back with their right foot, extending both arms to the sides for balance, and then brings their left foot back to meet the right.

The individual steps backward with their left foot, leaning their torso forward and slightly bending their right knee. As they step, the right arm swings across the chest while the left arm pulls back.

The person steps backward with their right foot, leaning forward and extending their arms to the sides. They then bring the left foot back to meet the right and straighten up, while each arm swings in coordination with the opposite leg.
The person steps back with their right foot and brings their left foot to meet it, while keeping their arms slightly bent and out to the sides.
The person steps back with their left foot while keeping their right foot in place. Both arms are raised to shoulder height, bent at the elbows and held out to the sides.
A person stomps their left foot and steps back while holding their arms forward with bent elbows, slightly vibrating their forearms.
The figure steps back with the left foot and brings the right foot to meet it, while keeping the arms raised out to the sides with bent elbows.
The person steps backward while maintaining a hunched posture, with their torso pointed downward.
Starting in a squat, the person rotates their left shoulder to the right while their left arm swings naturally. They then jump backward and turn their body.
A person stands bent forward with their arms held out, elbows slightly bent. They push their arms forward and pull them back, then rotate in a circle while keeping their arm position fixed.
From a side-facing, crouched position, the person steps back with their left foot and brings their right foot to meet it, maintaining a low posture with bent knees throughout the movement.
With arms bent and raised, the person steps back with their right foot while maintaining a stable torso, then brings their left foot back to meet the right.
A person steps back with their right foot and brings their left foot to meet it, holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows.

From a crouched position, a person steps backward with the right foot followed by the left while slightly rotating their torso to the right. Their arms swing with the movement, finishing extended out to the sides.
The person steps backward while rhythmically extending their arms outward and pulling them back toward the body.
Bending forward at the waist, the person raises their right knee high. They then land on the right foot and take a bouncy step backward, bringing the left foot back to meet it.
The individual takes a step back with their left leg and brings their right leg to meet it, keeping their arms bent and held out to the sides.
The person steps back with the right foot and brings the left foot to meet it, while keeping their arms raised and bent at the elbows in a hip-hop style.

Starting from a crouched position, a person taps their right foot behind the left with both arms raised sideways. They then step the right foot forward, aligning it in front of the left foot while swinging their right arm backward and placing their left hand in front of the chest. Finally, the person steps the right foot back again to tap behind the left foot, swinging their left arm straight back and positioning their right hand in front of the head. Throughout the movement, the person maintains a low stance with bent knees.
Starting from a slight crouch with raised, bent arms, the person steps back with their right foot and brings the left to meet it, shifting their entire body backward.
A person steps back with their left foot and brings their right foot to meet it, returning to a standing position. Throughout the motion, the arms are held slightly out to the sides with bent elbows.

The performer takes wide, backward crossing steps, tapping one foot behind the other while maintaining a low, forward-leaning posture. Both arms swing dynamically forward and out to the sides in sync with the steps.
The person stands with their arms out to the sides at shoulder height and elbows bent. They then walk backward, pushing their forearms up and down as they move.
A person steps back with their right foot while holding their arms bent and slightly raised, then steps back with their left foot.
The individual steps back with the right foot, moving it from a forward position to behind the left foot. Simultaneously, they raise their arms with bent elbows and move them in a rhythmic motion.

Starting from a hunched squat with the hips pushed back, the person steps backward while rising to a standing position. Both arms first swing toward the body, then the right arm raises upward while the left arm moves downward.
The person steps back with their right foot, lowering their body as their weight shifts. Simultaneously, the right arm extends to the side while the left arm crosses over the chest.
The person steps back with their right foot, leaning their torso slightly backward. Their arms are bent and move in opposition, with the right arm swinging forward and the left arm back.
The person takes slow, wide steps backward while swinging their raised arms.

A person performs a back step by stepping backward with their right foot and bringing the left foot to meet it. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, and their upper body leans forward slightly during the movement.
A person steps back with their arms at their sides and then lifts them.
With arms extended to the sides, the person takes a step back with their right foot while simultaneously waving their upper body lightly forward and down.
The person takes a backward step with the left foot and brings the right foot to meet it. Synchronized with the steps, their arms swing in opposition while the upper body pops rhythmically.

Standing on their right foot with the left leg pointed forward, the person extends both arms to the left with the right elbow bent. They then move the left arm to the side and step back with the left leg while bending over, keeping the right arm in place.
front step: The person steps forward into a slight lunge with their right foot, keeping their arms bent and raised to the sides. They then bring their left foot forward to meet the right, maintaining the same arm posture throughout the movement.
The performer steps forward with the left leg and brings the right leg to meet it. Throughout the movement, the arms are kept bent and raised, with the left arm held higher than the right.
The person steps forward with their left foot and then returns it to the starting position. Simultaneously, they extend their arms outward and then draw them back toward their body.

A person steps forward with their left foot and spins on their right foot, moving the left foot forward again while continuing to turn. Their arms move at their sides before joining their hands in front of their face with the elbows bent.
A person steps forward with their left foot and pivots 180 degrees, then steps forward with the left leg while rotating back to face the front.
A person steps forward while slightly bent over, swinging their arms from side to side. They then straighten up slightly and place their hands on their hips.
The person performs an exaggerated, rhythmic walk, incorporating occasional turns.
The person steps forward with their left foot, turns around without moving their feet, and then moves backward.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides. They walk forward and step onto their left foot, keeping their arms out to the sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They then rotate 135 degrees clockwise, extending the right arm straight out and raising the left arm upward. Stepping forward with the left leg, they swing their arms forward and rotate 180 degrees clockwise on the right foot, ending with their arms pointing out to the sides and downward.
The person takes a forward step, rotates 180 degrees, and repeats this motion to keep turning around.
A person kicks their right leg back, brings it forward, and immediately begins walking in a circle with an exaggerated arm-swinging motion.
A person walks in a circle with their elbows raised, taking large steps. Their arms swing slightly in a rhythmic motion synchronized with their steps.
A person walks rhythmically, stepping slightly inward.
A person steps forward with their right foot to meet their left. As they step, their right arm swings forward and up while their left arm swings back and down.

The person steps forward with the left foot and brings the right foot to meet it. Maintaining a slight crouch with bent knees, the left arm is held low and points in the direction of movement, while the right arm points in the opposite direction.
A person steps forward with their left foot, brings their right foot slightly closer, and bends over to touch the ground with their left hand while extending their right arm upward.
The person steps forward with their right foot, takes a small hop, and brings the right foot in front of the left foot.
With a hunched torso, the person steps back and forth in a bouncing motion, swinging their arms in the opposite direction of their legs.

Leaning slightly forward with the left arm extended back and the right arm bent in front, the person steps back with their right foot and then steps forward with the right followed by the left, swinging their arms in opposite directions.

Bending slightly forward, the individual steps forward with their right foot while extending their arms out to the sides. They then bring their left foot forward to meet the right, swinging their arms by their sides.

The person steps forward with their left leg as the right trails behind, then hops forward slightly and brings the right foot forward to land on both feet. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, swinging at their sides as they move forward.
A person steps forward rhythmically.
A person stands with their arms slightly forward at their sides and takes quick steps forward. They then kick their right foot across to the left while turning slightly.
A person brings their right foot to meet their left before widening their stance again, while keeping their arms mostly still.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm extended horizontally at shoulder height. They step forward, bending the right elbow to bring the forearm toward the hip while pointing the left arm straight up. They then walk in a clockwise circle, swaying their hips to the left and back. Once facing the original direction again, they extend both arms to the sides at shoulder height and bend their elbows to point the forearms downward and forward.
The person steps forward, pausing briefly each time their feet touch the ground.
The person steps forward onto their left foot while extending both arms out to the sides into a T-pose.
The person steps forward onto their left foot, shifting their weight while simultaneously extending their right arm out to the side and crossing their left arm in front of their chest.
The person steps their right foot forward and then brings it back, with their arms extended to the sides.

Viewed from the side, a person starts with their right foot forward and arms raised. They then step their left foot forward to meet the right, bringing their feet together while maintaining the raised arm position.
With arms extended to the sides, the person lunges forward on their left leg and then brings their right foot forward to meet the left, completing the front step.

The person stands upright with their arms at their sides and moves sideways, alternating their arms in up-and-down motions from the shoulders. They then turn and move sideways in the opposite direction.
The person squats while taking a short step forward with the left leg. The right arm moves forward with a bent elbow while the left arm moves backward.
Standing on their left leg with arms bent at their sides, the individual kicks their right leg out and steps forward, lifting their torso and arms slightly as they move.
The figure steps forward with the left foot while keeping the arms slightly bent at the sides, then brings the right foot forward to meet the left.
The individual steps forward with the left foot and kicks the right leg before stepping it out to the side, while keeping their arms at their sides.
A person walks forward.
A person walks in a counter-clockwise circle.
A person hops forward, alternating between their right and left legs, while moving in a large clockwise circular path.
A person hops forward, alternating between their right and left legs.
A person hops forward in a large circle by alternating their feet while keeping the right leg consistently in front. The upper body remains relaxed and still throughout the motion.

The person starts with their arms at their sides and their right leg bent at the knee, foot pointing back. They drop the foot to the ground and swing their arms to the left with elbows bent, then lower them back to their sides. Moving forward, they push their right leg back and then forward, leaning over slightly with their elbows bent and forearms held in front.

The performer steps forward with their left foot, landing on a bent knee as they shift their weight. Their arms are raised and bent, with the right arm moving forward in coordination with the left leg.

The person steps forward with the left foot while leaning their torso slightly to the left. They then bring the right foot forward to meet the left, using their bent arms to make small movements for balance.
A person stands with their arms extended straight ahead, then runs forward while lowering their arms to their sides.
A person takes small alternating steps in place while gradually lowering into a squat. Simultaneously, the upper body leans forward at the waist with the arms swinging slightly.
The performer stands with their left arm extended outward and their right arm pointing back, elbows bent. They step forward, vibrating their arms before dropping them to their sides.
A person walks slowly in a circle with bent knees, holding their arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
The person runs sideways in a circle with their right forearm held in front of their chest.
The person turns left and steps forward.

The person starts by standing on the left leg with the right leg back and hands together, then steps forward with the right foot. As they step, they simultaneously open their body by swinging both arms sideways.
The person steps forward with their right foot, shifting their weight forward. They then bring their left foot to meet the right, completing the movement with their arms held slightly out and bent.
The person steps forward with their right foot, bending the knee, and then brings the left foot up to meet it. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides and bent at the elbows.
The dancer steps their right foot forward and then brings it back.

The person stands leaning forward with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They jump up and clap their hands before rotating slightly counterclockwise and swinging their arms to the left. Rotating clockwise, they punch their left arm forward, then lean over slightly and drop the arm to their side while keeping the right arm in place. Next, they rotate counterclockwise to face front, keeping their arms at their sides with elbows bent. They turn slightly to the right and punch their right arm straight forward before returning it to their side with the elbow bent. They turn to the side and back, swinging their arms forward and back, then face the side again. Finally, they step back and bring their hands together in front of their body.
A person steps forward with the left foot to meet the right, then immediately steps forward again with the right foot. The body rises and falls with each step as the bent arms move rhythmically.

The performer steps forward with the right foot while leaning their upper body back and swaying to the right with arms bent and raised. The left foot then follows to meet the right, completing the forward step.

The person bends over with their arms pointing down and forward. They straighten up and pull their arms back, then step the right foot forward, stretching the leg. They step back and bend over with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They then straighten, rotate clockwise, and step the right foot to the side with their arms at their sides. Next, they step the left foot to the side and point both arms to the right, then step forward and lean back, pointing both arms down with the right arm bent at the elbow. Finally, they straighten and stretch the left foot back while pointing both arms to the right; the right arm is extended straight from the shoulder, and the left is bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing right.
The person steps forward with their left leg and immediately swings it back for a tap while rhythmically bouncing. Their arms swing back and forth in time with the rhythm of the steps.

The person stands with their arms at their sides. They bend forward and swing their right arm up and to the side while placing their left arm, bent at the elbow, across their torso. They then rise and kick their left leg to the right before returning to the starting position.
A person steps forward with their left leg, bending their knee and leaning their torso forward, then brings their right foot forward to meet the left.
A person steps forward onto their right foot, planting it firmly. They then bring their left foot to meet the right while holding their arms out to the sides with bent elbows.

A person steps forward with their right foot while crouching slightly and extending their right arm. They then bring their left foot forward to meet the right, returning to a neutral stance with feet together.
A person steps forward while swinging both hands up and down in front of their body.
The person steps forward while maintaining a low stance. With each step, both arms swing forward, backward, and then upward, pausing briefly when both feet are on the ground.
A person steps forward with their left foot and brings the right foot to meet it. Simultaneously, they raise both arms in a bent position, with the left arm held higher than the right.
The person steps forward with their right foot and brings the left foot to meet it. Simultaneously, they raise and bend their arms, holding both hands close to their head.

The person stands in a wide stance with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They drop their arms to their sides and step back, bending their elbows with their forearms tucked near their hips. Stepping back with the left foot, they lean forward and kick their right leg back, extending the right arm forward and the left arm backward. They then bring their arms in front of their body with bent elbows, straighten their posture, and step forward, extending their arms to the sides at shoulder height with the forearms pointing down.
A person steps forward with their left foot, swinging their right arm forward and their left arm back in a coordinated motion.
A person steps their right foot forward and then steps back.
The person steps forward with their right foot while keeping their arms bent and raised to the sides, then steps back with the right foot.
The person steps forward while punching with their left hand.
The person steps forward with their right leg, bending the left knee and swinging both arms out to the sides. They then step back with the right leg while swinging their arms to the side of the body.

A person performs a hip-hop front step, stepping forward with the right foot and bringing the left foot to meet it. Maintaining a slight crouch, they swing their arms, keeping the left arm bent and held high while the right arm is extended.
The person stands with their elbows bent at their sides and forearms pointing forward. They step their right leg forward while extending their left arm, continuing to move forward in this pose.

Starting slightly hunched with their elbows bent at their sides, the person straightens up and walks in a circle while swinging their arms. They then stop and bring their arms forward, bending their elbows to position their forearms horizontally in front of their torso.

The person steps their left leg forward and taps the ground with a straight knee, bending their right knee while swinging their left arm backward and their right arm forward with bent elbows. Then, they sweep their right leg to the side in a large arc while swinging their left arm forward.
A person steps forward with their right foot while keeping their knees bent, then brings their left foot forward to meet the right, holding their arms bent at chest level throughout the movement.

The figure stands with their arms at their sides. They push their arms forward with slightly bent elbows while jumping in place. They then hold their arms out to the sides at shoulder height with their forearms pointing downward and step forward, moving their legs in circular loops.

With their forearms crossed in front of their chest, the person performs forward sliding steps by bringing one leg behind the other and then swinging it forward and outward. This movement causes the person to lightly rotate their torso from side to side.

The person kicks their left leg forward while slightly bending the right knee and crossing the right foot. Simultaneously, the torso twists to the left as the left arm pulls back and the right arm swings forward. They then return the left leg to its original position while reversing the arm swing.
The person steps their right foot forward and across to tap the ground on the left while raising their arms and bending their elbows, then returns their left leg to its original position.
A person steps forward with their right foot.
Step forward with the left foot and slide back.
Keeping the right foot planted, the person taps their left foot forward and back, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.
The person steps forward with the right foot and brings the left foot to meet it, while keeping their arms out to the sides with bent elbows throughout the movement.

The individual stands leaning back with their arms extended to the sides. They step forward, bringing their arms in front of their body and then back out. They then move their arms in front again and step backward, returning them to the sides.
Standing upright, the person leans forward, steps ahead while adjusting their arms at their sides, and then straightens back up.

The person steps forward with the left foot and brings the right foot to meet it. Simultaneously, they raise their arms with bent elbows, holding the left arm higher than the right while leaning the upper body to the side.

Starting in a lunge on the right leg with the right arm raised, the individual initiates a turn to the right by stepping with the left leg while pivoting on the right. As they turn, they raise their right elbow in front.
knee in: A person sways their hips and knees from side to side, swinging their arms from the elbows while moving backward. Then, they place their hands on their knees and move them outward while slightly bending over.
The person bends over with arms at their sides and steps back twice while swaying slightly, then straightens up while subtly moving their arms with elbows bent.
With knees bent and a forward pelvic tilt, the person brings one knee inward while swinging their hips side to side. They repeat this motion while slowly walking and stepping.
The person alternately lifts their knees, pointing them inward.

A person lifts their left knee, then their right, and then their left again before performing a slight jump. They briefly pull their arms back and repeat the sequence, keeping their arms extended in front throughout the movement.
The person raises their right knee upward and inward, crossing it over the left leg. Simultaneously, the right arm swings down and across the body as the torso twists.

The performer lifts their right knee high and brings it across the body's centerline while bending the supporting left leg. The arms move in opposition, with the right arm swinging forward and the left arm back to maintain balance.
The person alternately lifts their knees and moves them inward toward the body.
A person rhythmically alternates stepping one leg back while bending both elbows. Throughout the movement, they maintain a low, squatting posture and stay in place with their torso slightly hunched.
A person kicks their left leg back while bringing the knee slightly inward, as both arms fold inward in time with the kick. They then alternate legs to repeat the movement.

Maintaining a squat with arms held straight forward, the person kicks one leg backward and returns it, drawing the knee inward. Simultaneously, the elbows fold or the shoulders move in rhythm with the kicking motion.
Standing on their right leg, the person lifts their left knee up and inward across the body's midline. Their arms are held out to the sides with a slight bend for balance.
Starting from a squat, one leg kicks backward and returns forward, pulling the knee toward the body's centerline. The arms move rhythmically with the kick, bending and straightening at the elbows.

A person stands on their left leg, lifting their right knee up and across the body toward the left shoulder. The torso twists slightly to the left to facilitate the movement, while the arms move subtly for balance.

The person stands with their elbows bent and leans forward slightly before pushing their arms forward. They then move backward, performing small alternating kicks while pushing their arms forward with each kick.
Standing with arms straight at their sides, the person performs three alternating kicks, pulls their arms slightly back, and returns to the starting position. This sequence is then repeated.
Balancing on their left leg, the person raises their right knee high and across their body. The left arm is extended and the right arm is bent and raised to maintain balance.
The person raises their left knee high and brings it inward across the body's centerline while the upper body remains relatively stationary.
A person stands with their knees bent and arms extended forward. They perform slight alternating kicks with each leg while rhythmically pulling their arms in and out.
A person raises their left knee high, drawing it inward across the midline of their body. As the knee rises, the torso twists to the right while the arms move in opposition to maintain balance.

A person bends over slightly and kicks their left leg, followed by a clap in front of them. They then perform a slight kick with their right leg, keeping their knees bent throughout the entire motion.
Starting from a low crouch, a person lifts their left knee high across the front of their body. Their arms are held in a dynamic, bent pose, with the left arm crossing over their head.
A person stands bent over with their arms extended downward. They kick their right leg inward followed by their left, then straighten up slightly and clap their hands.

Starting from a squatting position, the person alternates drawing one knee inward while folding the corresponding forearm. As the knee returns to its original position, the arm extends back out to the side. This movement is repeated rhythmically.
A person stands on their left leg, lifting the right knee up and across the midline of the body. The upper body twists toward the raised knee, with both arms bent and held in front of the chest.
A person raises their left knee toward their chest with their arms bent and held close to their body, then extends both arms out to the sides.
runnin in place: A person runs in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating pattern. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, creating a dynamic and bouncy motion.
The person runs in place with high knee lifts, driving their knees toward the chest. Their bent arms swing in opposition, with the forward elbow rising above shoulder level.
A person runs in place with high knees, alternating legs while swinging their bent arms back and forth in opposition.
A person runs quickly in place, lifting their knees high toward the chest in an alternating motion. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in coordination with the opposite legs.

A person runs in place at a high-energy, quick tempo, lifting their knees high toward their chest. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left knee rises, and vice versa.
After clapping, the person lightly jogs forward with their hands on their waist.
After swinging both arms back and forth a couple of times, the person lightly jogs forward with their hands on their waist, then turns around while swinging their arms.
Starting from a standing position, the person runs in place while leaning their torso forward and swinging their arms.
A person runs with a bent torso, looking downward, while their arms swing dynamically in rhythm with the movement.
The person runs in place at a quick tempo, lifting their knees high in an alternating pattern while swinging their bent arms in coordination with their legs.
The person runs in place at a quick, energetic pace, driving their knees high toward their chest in an alternating motion. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to their legs.
A person quickly jogs in place with high knees. Their right hand remains on their waist while the left arm swings up and down by bending and extending the forearm.
A person is jogging in place at a fast pace with high knees. Their right hand remains on their waist, while their left arm swings up and down by bending and extending the forearm.

A person performs a series of dynamic, alternating kicks. Balancing on one leg, they lean their torso forward and kick the opposite leg high behind them while reaching the opposite arm toward the ground. After several repetitions, the person begins rotating in a circle while maintaining the motion.
A person runs in place, driving their knees high toward the chest in an alternating motion. Their arms are bent and pump in coordination with their legs, while the torso leans slightly forward.

The individual performs an energetic run in place, alternating high knee lifts toward the chest. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to the legs, maintaining a quick and rhythmic pace.
A person runs in place, lifting their knees high toward their chest in a rapid, alternating motion. Their arms are bent and pump back and forth, synchronized with their leg movements.

A person runs in place at a quick tempo, lifting their knees high toward the chest in an alternating fashion. The arms swing back and forth in coordination with the legs, maintaining an energetic rhythm.
roger rabbit: The dancer stands with their upper arms extended horizontally at shoulder height and elbows bent so that the forearms point downward. They move their arms to the sides, keeping the elbows bent, and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise while walking backward with bouncy steps and swinging their arms at their sides. They return their arms to the initial position with forearms pointing down, then hold them out to the sides while stepping forward on the left leg. They kick the right leg forward, then the left leg forward, and then the right leg backward. Finally, they rotate clockwise in a circle while kicking their feet out and back; during this turn, the left arm is bent and points upward, while the right arm remains straight at the side.
The figure hops backward, alternating kicks behind them while keeping their arms bent. With each kick, the hips twist slightly in the direction of the leg.
A person hops backward on one foot while kicking the other foot forward in a continuous motion, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance throughout the movement.
The performer hops back and forth with their feet wide apart, twisting their hips with each step. Their arms swing gently back and to the side in rhythm with the leg movements.
A person hops while alternating between kicking one leg forward and the other back. Their hips twist from side to side with each hop, as their bent arms swing in sync with the leg movements.

The person performs the Roger Rabbit by hopping on one foot while sliding the other backward in a continuous, rhythmic motion. This movement is synchronized with a slight twist of the hips as the arms are held out to the sides.
The individual hops backward and forward with their feet held close together. With each hop, the hips and torso twist from side to side while the arms remain bent and raised.
A person hops in place, alternating forward leg movements while twisting their hips and swinging their bent arms in opposition to their legs.
The person walks backward with a bouncy rhythm.
A person rhythmically hops on one foot while kicking the other leg back, alternating feet in a continuous shuffling motion. This movement is accompanied by a slight twist of the hips and torso.

The person hops backward by alternating between sliding one foot along the ground and lifting the other. With arms bent and held near the chest, the hips and torso twist in coordination with the leg movements.

A person hops back and forth in a running motion, lifting one knee high as the other leg pushes off. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, accompanied by a slight twist in the hips and torso.
A person hops backward in a shuffling motion, alternately kicking their feet forward. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, and their hips twist slightly with each hop.
The person swings both arms up and down with their elbows bent. Simultaneously, they swing one leg backward and hop forward.
The person dynamically swings both arms up and down while keeping their elbows bent. Simultaneously, they swing one leg backward and hop forward.
intro windup: The person performs a deep bounce by bending their knees and shifting their weight from side to side. Simultaneously, they execute small, rhythmic head isolations while bending their left arm and bringing it toward their right shoulder.

The person stands in a high stance with slightly bent knees, rhythmically shifting their weight from one foot to the other. This side-to-side sway is accompanied by relaxed arm movements and subtle head isolation.

A person begins in a crouched stance with both arms extended to the sides. They then perform a large, slow waving motion, sweeping their right arm high into the air while pointing their left arm toward the ground.

The person begins a rhythmic bounce, bending their knees deeply and shifting their weight from side to side. The upper body sways loosely as the arms extend outward and the head performs small, isolated movements.
The figure begins in a neutral standing position for one second, then performs body waves and swings their arms in preparation for the dance.
A person walks forward while circling their forearms in front of their body.
The person walks clockwise while swinging their arms back and forth.
The person moves clockwise, spinning their body while hopping on one leg.
The person forcefully swings their left arm forward and then suddenly runs forward while rolling their forearms in front of their body.

A person performs deep, rhythmic knee bounces while shifting their weight from side to side. The upper body sways with the movement, keeping the shoulders relaxed as the head performs small, rhythmic isolations.

The figure stands with elbows bent at their sides, shaking their arms before stepping back. They then roll their forearms around each other in front of their body, before moving their arms to their sides and making larger circles with their forearms.
Starting from a T-pose, the person steps forward into a trudging walk, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs as they transition into a street dance.

A person twists their torso to the right while drawing their arms inward with bent elbows, crossing the left arm in front of the chest. The head turns to the right, and the knees remain slightly bent throughout the motion.

The person performs a deep, rhythmic knee bounce while shifting their weight from side to side. Their arms swing loosely in coordination with the movement, while their head makes small, isolated motions.
At the start of the dance sequence, the person raises their left arm to shoulder height and rolls it in circles.
At the start of the sequence, the person crosses their left leg in front of their right while moving to the side.
The person starts from a T-pose and then moves in a circle while bouncing their arms.
The person starts in a T-pose and then relaxes different parts of their body.
Starting from a T-pose, the figure lowers their arms to their sides with the elbows bent and steps forward with each foot while remaining in place.

The person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent, forearms pointing forward. They lean to the side and back while dragging their right foot to the left, bending their elbows to point their forearms upward before dropping their arms back to their sides. After grooving their body to the side, the person steps their right leg forward while swinging both hands forward, then raises and lowers their bent elbows.
In a slight squat, the person steps around rhythmically.
Starting in a slight squat, the person swings their forearms in front of their body while swaying their right leg. They then turn to the left, transitioning into a lunge on the left leg.
Starting in a T-pose, the person lowers their arms and brings both hands in front of their chest.

From a standing stance with both hands held in front of the body, the person lowers the arms and swings the right leg back and forth. With both feet on the ground, they lightly bounce by bending the knees.
Starting from a T-pose, the person lowers their arms. They then step backward while bouncing their body and swinging their arms.

The person bounces the body rhythmically. They then drag the left foot in a large circular motion on the ground while turning the body. After turning, they continue bouncing the body while swinging the elbows up and down.
A person performs a six-step breakdance move.

Starting in a T-pose, the figure drops their arms to their sides and turns to walk in a circle. While walking, they hold their elbows bent with forearms pointing forward, then straighten their arms at their sides.

With knees bent, a person rhythmically shifts their weight from one leg to the other, causing their hips and torso to sway side to side. Their arms swing loosely with the movement while their head makes small, isolated circular motions.
The figure bounces rhythmically by bending at the knees and shifting weight from side to side. Their arms are held loosely with bent elbows, and their head bobs in time with the bounce.
The person steps around and performs light jumps while raising their arms before changing direction. The entire movement follows a foundational hip-hop style.
The person steps and bounces to the rhythm while swinging both arms up and down. Before changing direction, they lightly jump and raise both arms upward.
The person steps and bounces to the rhythm while swinging both arms up and down. Before changing direction, they jump lightly and raise both arms upward.
The person moves their shoulders rhythmically while stepping around.
After raising and lowering the left knee, the person steps around and bounces rhythmically.

A person stands with bent knees, bouncing rhythmically while shifting their weight from side to side. Their upper body and shoulders remain relaxed as their head performs a subtle side-to-side isolation.
Starting from a standing position, the person steps around.
The person rhythmically swings their upper body, then steps back and forth with a bouncy motion.

Standing in a low stance with bent knees, a person rhythmically shifts their weight from side to side. As the hips and torso sway, the shoulders remain relaxed while the head performs small, isolated movements.
Starting from a T-pose, the person lowers their arms and lightly swings their body.
A person rhythmically bounces their upper body up and down.
The person rhythmically performs side steps while bouncing their body.
Starting from a standing position, the person slowly gets into the rhythm while walking.
The person walks slowly and rhythmically in a circle while swinging both arms back and forth in unison.
The person walks slowly and rhythmically in a circle while swinging both arms back and forth together.
Starting from a standing position, the person lightly kicks their legs in an alternating fashion.
From a standing position, the person rhythmically sways their upper body.
The person swings their arms in large back-and-forth motions while lightly lifting their left and right legs.
From a standing position, the person lightly swings their arms back and forth.
Starting from a standing position, the person bounces by bending their knees.
While bouncing their body to the rhythm, the person raises and lowers their left arm. Then, they bounce their torso in time with the rhythm.
random: The person turns around and slides back like a moonwalk.
A person pops their chest forward while pulling their bent arms up and inward. Simultaneously, they slide their right foot forward to meet the left, dropping into a low, crouched stance.
A person glides from side to side, smoothly shifting their weight from one leg to the other. Their arms remain extended, swinging to maintain balance as the body leans left and right.
The individual executes a sharp upward pop of the chest and shoulders while popping their arms, immediately followed by a quick slide of one foot and then the other.
The person drops their raised right leg to the ground while waving their arms in front of their body, then slowly leans forward into a right-leg lunge stance.
The person kneels on the ground, leaning forward with their arms extended straight ahead. They then push each knee forward while lifting their torso, eventually returning to a standing position.
The person turns their body, pivoting on the left leg while rotating the right.
The individual stands upright with their left arm raised and their right arm by their side. They then bring both arms forward to shoulder height and hold them there.

The person extends their right arm out and up while stepping back into a lunge with their left leg. They then quickly bring both arms down across their chest, simultaneously sliding their right foot back slightly.
The person executes a sharp, isolated twist of the upper body to the side. Simultaneously, the legs bend and swing while the person moves to the side.
The person hops with their legs wide apart and arms held in front of their chest. They then raise both arms upward with bent elbows while turning their torso to the left.

The person stands upright with arms at their sides and elbows bent, the left forearm pointing outward. They then punch their left arm to the side and rotate their body counterclockwise while sliding forward.
The performer sharply pops their chest and shoulders while extending their right arm, followed by a swift forward slide and tap of the right foot.

The person performs a sharp, sudden twist of the upper body, primarily involving the chest and shoulders, while their arms swing dynamically. This motion is executed from a wide stance with the left leg extended backward, keeping the lower body relatively stable.

A person stands in a wide stance with their knees bent and arms extended forward, elbows bent. They shake their left forearm and step their feet together, then point their forearms upward while keeping their arms extended in front.
Starting from a crouch, the individual abruptly pops their chest and flings their arms out to the sides while simultaneously sliding their left foot outward.
The person keeps their upper and lower body still while rotating their waist and pushing their arms forward.
The dancer starts bent over with their arms in front, bent at the elbows. They then straighten up and step their left foot back, pulling their arms to the sides, before bending over again.

The figure stands in a wide stance with arms extended to the sides and elbows bent, forearms pointing forward. They vibrate their body to the right while straightening their posture, bringing their hands together in front of their face with forearms pointing upward. Maintaining this arm position, they bend forward at the waist. While bent, they swing their arms out to the sides and then return to a standing position with their forearms pointing forward.

The person performs an isolated upper body wave, starting with the left arm moving inward toward the head as the chest contracts. The movement concludes as the person straightens up and extends both arms out to the sides.

The figure stands with their feet wide and arms pointed forward. They rotate their arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointed down, then bend their knees inward. Shifting their torso and turning to the left, they move their forward-pointing forearms up and down. They then shake their arms up and down and rotate 90 degrees clockwise to face forward, finishing with their arms at their sides, elbows bent, and forearms pointing forward.
The person starts with a small jump, landing with their right foot forward and their left foot back. The motion then features a slight lag, pausing briefly before continuing.
Starting from a wide stance with knees bent inward, the person jumps to bring both feet back to the center and then rhythmically shakes their body.

Starting from a crouched squat, the person jumps while maintaining their low posture and a bowed torso, swinging their arms backward for balance. They then stand up to turn and raise their left arm high.

The figure stands with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They step forward, dropping their arms to their sides before pushing them forward with bent elbows while running forward and to the left. After slowing down and lowering their arms, they bring their hands together with elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They then rotate counterclockwise and raise their arms to their sides with forearms pointing upward. Stepping to the left, they rotate clockwise 180 degrees and punch their left arm forward. They rotate back, keeping the right arm at their side with the elbow bent and forearm horizontal, then rotate clockwise again with the left elbow bent and forearm pointed up. They move the left forearm forward and back as they rotate, finally crossing both forearms horizontally in front of the torso.
The person swings their arms and legs independently.
The individual quickly drops and twists their upper body to the left while simultaneously sliding their right foot forward and out to the side.
The person quickly steps backward while swinging their arms forward, then raises their right leg and extends both arms to the sides to maintain balance.

The performer stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They thrust their hips forward and to the left while swinging their arms up and to the right, then bring them around in front as they spin counterclockwise. After completing the rotation, they step forward and to the left and spin counterclockwise again, raising their arms above their head and back down while shifting back and to the right. They continue to spin counterclockwise while moving their forearms up and down, then rotate 90 degrees clockwise and step forward with their arms at their sides.
A person stands with knees slightly bent, raising both arms with bent elbows. They then gesture by moving their arms upward and then down to the sides.

The individual performs an isolated upper-body movement, sharply extending their right arm straight up while crossing their left arm over their chest. Simultaneously, they execute a quick, unpredictable foot slide, extending the right leg forward to tap the ground.
The person jumps and twists their body to turn while maintaining a wide stance.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, held slightly in front of the body. They swing their arms out to the sides and back, then slowly step backward with their elbows bent and forearms pointing forward, shaking them slightly up and down. Continuing this motion while stepping back and to the side, they touch their hands together in front of their body and bend forward slightly, then separate them while keeping the forearms pointed forward, maintaining the orientation of the arms.
While maintaining a right-leg lunge, the person executes an isolated, robotic upward rotation of both forearms. The rest of the body remains largely still, emphasizing the mechanical arm movement.

The individual stands with their feet wide and knees turned inward, arms out to the sides and elbows bent so the forearms are positioned in front of the upper arms. They rotate their torso slightly clockwise and drop the left arm to the side, pointing the forearm outward, then step the right foot back and lean forward slightly, shifting the torso to the right and pointing both arms forward. After stepping forward and straightening slightly while maintaining their arm position, they swing their arms in circles and jump their feet wide again. Finally, they bend their elbows to point their forearms upward before jumping their feet together and raising their arms overhead, maintaining the same arm orientation.

From a crouched position, the person performs a sudden, isolated movement by kicking their left leg out to the side while extending their left arm upward. As the body straightens, the right arm pulls back for counterbalance.
The person sharply twists their torso to the left, pulling their arms toward their chest while taking a small step forward with their left foot.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides, the right foot forward and the left back. They turn slightly counterclockwise, extending the right arm while bending the left elbow to hold the forearm horizontally across the body. Rotating clockwise to face forward, they point both forearms ahead, then turn to the side and slide the left leg out. They continue rotating the body in a clockwise circle, keeping the arms in front with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
The person performs a sudden full-body drop, transitioning from a dynamic mid-motion pose into a low crouch as they quickly slide their feet into the final squatting position.
The performer sharply pops their left shoulder and chest toward the right while simultaneously sliding their right foot out and extending their right arm.

The person starts in a staggered stance with the right leg forward and the left leg back, arms at their sides. They step the left foot to meet the right while moving their forearms forward and back, then bring their hands together in front of their body and lean their torso to the side.
The person sharply pops their chest and shoulders forward and to the left while simultaneously sliding their left foot in the same direction.
Viewed from the side, a person walks from left to right, taking a step forward with their left leg while swinging their left arm forward and their right arm back.

Starting from an extremely wide stance with the torso leaned backward, the person jumps to a standing position and brings their feet together. They then perform a squat jump in place, landing on the left leg while keeping their torso bowed forward.

The person stands upright with their arms at their sides and elbows bent, forearms pointing toward their hips. They lower their arms and rotate their torso clockwise, swinging their right forearm up to point straight forward.

The person stands upright with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They step forward with their right foot and slide their body to join it, lowering their arms to their sides as they move.
The person first turns into a left-leg lunge with their right arm extended backward. Then, they rotate their body and jump. The movement is dynamic and rhythmic.

The individual stands in a bent-over posture with their left leg forward and right leg back, their arms out to the sides and elbows bent so the forearms point backward. They rapidly rotate their forearms in circles while turning their torso 90 degrees clockwise, ending with their arms pointing forward. Keeping their knees bent, they bring their forearms horizontal in front of their chest, then rotate counterclockwise and extend their arms straight out. Finally, they pull their arms back by bending their elbows while leaning slightly backward.

The person stands bent over in a staggered stance, with their right leg forward and their left leg back and arms hanging down. Keeping the rest of the body still, they swing their arms back and then up to shoulder height, bending them at the elbows.
Standing with arms extended to the sides, the person quickly brings them together in front of their chest while the lower body remains stationary.

A person stands with their left leg forward and right leg back, arms extended to the sides and forearms pointing down. They step their right leg forward and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise, swinging their arms back, then forward, and out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms positioned in front of their upper arms. They then drop their arms to their sides and lean back slightly.
The person sways their hips in a circular motion with a wide stance. They then rotate their hips while standing in place before bowing forward.

The person lunges forward onto a bent left leg while extending the right leg backward. Leaning their upper body forward with bent arms held out, they conclude the movement with a quick, unpredictable tap of the right foot.

The person performs a two-handed forward push from a raised position with a slight rightward torso twist while stepping their right foot toward the front-left. This is immediately followed by the left foot stepping forward as the entire body executes a sharp turn to the left.
Standing upright with arms at their sides, the person moves their right leg forward and then rotates counterclockwise to face the side.
The dancer moves their arms up and down while twisting their knees.
The person steps forward in a low stance while swinging their hands up and down in front of their body.
A person steps forward while shaking their arms from side to side.
Step back with the left leg, reaching far back, and then move around.
Starting from a T-pose, the person brings their arms toward their body and begins bouncing their torso up and down while swinging their arms, picking up the rhythm.
The person walks forward, swinging their legs from the side to the front. Then, they take a large step backward while popping their body.
The person steps forward while performing a body pop, then moves backward in a moonwalk-like fashion, hopping and mimicking a walking motion with their arms and legs.
Standing with arms at their sides, the individual turns slightly clockwise, then takes three steps backward while swinging their arms and moving their forearms in front of their body.

The person stands with their feet apart and arms at their sides. They bring their knees toward each other and slide to the right, extending their right arm out to the side at shoulder height. They then step forward and bend over slightly with their arms at their sides. Touching their hands together, they walk in a counterclockwise circle before straightening up and rotating further counterclockwise. Finally, they swing their left arm back to touch their hip while pointing their right arm forward with the elbow bent.
The person waves their shoulders and then turns to the left into a left lunge stance.
The figure walks backward in a slight crouch with arms by their sides, swaying from side to side.

Starting with arms at their sides, the person slides forward onto an extended left leg, swinging their left arm over it. They stretch their arms forward and then straighten up, moving their forearms up and down with elbows bent and pointing forward. Finally, they lean forward slightly with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
The person bounces to the rhythm while circling their left arm from back to front, kicking their right leg backward as the arm moves forward.
The individual sharply twitches their upper body and shoulders while simultaneously sliding their left foot outward, keeping the right foot anchored.
The dancer performs a rapid contraction, pulling their arms and legs inward while moving their right arm up and down, followed by a sharp, isolated chest pop and a sliding tap of the left foot.

A person steps their right leg behind their left and lowers into a squat. From the squat, they untwist their legs to turn to the right. They then perform small jumps in place while continuing to rotate, keeping their torso hunched forward and their arms held back for balance.
A person pops their chest while flinging their arms out to the sides, rapidly sliding their feet to shift their body to the right.

Standing with arms at their sides, the person pivots 180 degrees clockwise on their left foot while raising their left arm upward. They then rotate another 90 degrees clockwise, circling their arms in front of their body while leaning back with knees bent. Next, they step forward and swing their arms across to the right while performing a 180-degree clockwise rotation. Finally, they bend their elbows at their sides with forearms pointing forward before finishing with a slight counterclockwise rotation.
With the left leg extended straight forward, the person leans back and draws the leg in with an exaggerated movement. They then step back with the left leg while swinging the left arm forward.

Starting from a squatting position, the person stands up and steps to the right while extending both arms forward. They then bring their left leg to meet their right and perform a body pop while rotating their torso.

Starting from a standing position with the right arm raised, the person bounces their shoulders and arms. They then step backward into a squat while bouncing their body. The movement concludes in a squatting position with an abrupt motion.

The person begins with a sharp, isolated forward pop of the chest and shoulders. Simultaneously, the right leg slides back and to the right while the left knee bends, finishing in a dynamic, low stance.
The person steps their right leg sideways, crossing it behind their left while swinging their left arm out to the side, then untwists their legs to perform a turn.
A person pops their chest forward while raising their arms in a bent position, then quickly slides their left foot forward and to the side into a low, wide stance.
The person swings their arms and body to the rhythm while moving from side to side.
A person jumps, crosses their legs in mid-air, and then quickly unfolds them.
With small hops, the person spreads their legs apart and then brings them back together while twisting their body.
The person kicks backward while dragging back, then swings their leg back and forth in a wide motion.
The person sways their body sideways in a hip-hop style dance move, finishing with both forearms folded in front of the body.
The dancer quickly twists their upper body to the left, popping their chest and raising their arms while stepping forward with their right leg into a dynamic pose.

The person leans forward and twists their upper body to the left, raising their right arm to their head and extending their left arm outward. Simultaneously, they slide into a low, wide stance with the right foot forward and the left foot back.

The individual stands leaning slightly to the left with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms positioned in front. They lean further to the side while moving their forearms up and down, then lift their right leg and kick it slightly outward.
Step the left foot out and bring it back while extending the arms to the sides.
The performer executes a sharp, isolated pop of the chest and shoulders to the left, extending the arms outward. This movement is accompanied by a quick, subtle tap or slide of the left foot.
A person turns left with their hands at their sides.
The person steps around while popping their chest out, swinging their arms back and forth rhythmically.
The person takes large steps while walking forward.

The person stands upright with their arms at their sides, angled back. They step back into a wide stance, swinging their arms to the right and then to the left. They rotate their torso as they step back, extending their arms straight out to the sides. They shake their arms slightly and drop them to their sides, returning to a straight standing position. Finally, they swing their arms straight out at shoulder height.
Starting from a low stance, the person kicks their right leg out to the side and immediately follows with a quick, sharp sweep of the foot behind the body.
A person steps back with their left foot while simultaneously pulling their left elbow back, then repeats the same movement on the right side.

The person stands with their left leg extended forward and their right leg back, arms held straight out at shoulder height. They pivot their torso to face the opposite direction, keeping their upper arms at shoulder level while bending their elbows to point their forearms straight up.
The person steps back on their right leg while turning their body, then shakes rhythmically.

The performer stands bent forward with their arms pointing toward the ground. They pivot 180 degrees on their right foot, swinging their arms out to the sides as they turn, then take two steps to the side and point their arms upward.
Turn to the left while waving the arms.

The individual performs a sudden, isolated pop with their chest and shoulders, lunging their upper body forward and upward, followed immediately by a quick, unpredictable slide of the right foot forward.

A person stands with their arms at their sides and the left elbow bent, forearm pointing forward. They bend the right leg forward and the left leg back, leaning the torso slightly backward. They rotate 180 degrees clockwise and walk forward with their arms held in front, forearms pointing forward. They swing their arms out to the sides and then back to the front until they are pointing straight ahead. Starting to walk backward, they swing their arms out to the sides and back in, then point them straight up as they continue to move backward.
Standing with arms out at shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward, the person rotates counterclockwise on their right foot.
A person turns to the left and moves sideways using grapevine steps while waving their hands rhythmically.
