The person starts with their arms raised to the sides and quickly walks backward while swinging them forward, backward, and forward again. They then come to a stop and point their arms straight forward.
The individual raises both arms high and to the sides in an isolated upper body movement, followed by a quick, unpredictable slide or tap with one foot.
The performer stands with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of the torso. They rotate their forearms in small circles while pulling them toward the body and pushing them straight out.
Standing with legs apart, the person performs an isolated upper-body movement, slightly raising and bending their left arm while the right arm moves subtly.

The figure stands upright with their arms at their sides. They shake their left arm out to the side from the shoulder. They then vibrate their hips, shoulders, and knees while stepping back with the left foot, pointing their forearms forward with elbows bent. Finally, they throw their arms to the sides and step forward, swinging them back as they move.

A person stands bent over with their arms at their sides. They step forward with their right leg while alternately pushing each arm down, then clap their hands in front before straightening up with their arms at their sides and elbows bent.
The person waves their arms from right to left, then quickly kicks their right leg backward while swinging both arms back and forth.
The individual stands with their elbows bent and forearms in front of the body. They rotate clockwise and step back with the right foot while maintaining their arm position in front of them.
The person pushes their arms forward, up, and down while moving their feet around.
Standing upright, the person extends their right arm to the side and their left arm forward. They then bend their knees and bring their arms to their sides, pointing their forearms forward.
The individual twists their upper body while circling their arms downward and then back up. Simultaneously, they bend their knees and shift their weight, sliding their right foot slightly backward.
The person performs a sudden chest pop while flinging both arms out to the sides, then quickly retracts their arms and slides one foot back.
The person rotates to the left with their arms raised while extending their left foot.
The person raises both hands into the air and rotates to the right.

The individual stands on their left foot while leaning forward, with the right leg extended behind them and arms stretched straight out to the sides. They swing their arms down and rotate 90 degrees clockwise, keeping their elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They then rotate clockwise and back, lifting their arms so the forearms point forward with the elbows still bent. As they step back, they thrust the left arm forward and raise both forearms to point upward, then lower their arms to their sides while stepping forward.
The person rhythmically twists their shoulders from side to side while stepping forward with their right leg, extending the opposite arm out to the side with each twist.
Standing upright with arms at their sides, the person claps their hands. They then bend forward and step forward, returning their arms to their sides.
The person steps to the right while raising their right arm.
The person stands upright with both arms raised. They then lower their arms and move their left hand toward their right shoulder while shaking their right shoulder.

The individual stands with their left arm at their side and their right arm bent at the elbow, with the forearm held horizontally in front of the body. They rotate counterclockwise on their left foot and swing their arms forward, then step their right foot back while holding both arms bent at the elbows, forearms horizontal across the torso.
Standing in a wide stance and leaning forward slightly, the person points their arms to the right. They then take rapid steps forward, swinging their arms from side to side as if running.

Standing on the left foot in a bent-over position, the individual keeps the right knee bent and the right foot pointed backward. The left arm is extended to the side while the right arm is held down, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing left. They lean forward and then straighten, bringing both arms to the front with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They pivot counterclockwise on the right foot and point their arms forward upon facing the back. Next, they swing the right arm, step the left foot back, and rotate slightly clockwise while pointing both arms forward with a slight bend at the elbows. Finally, they step forward and stand upright, bringing their arms to their sides.

The performer stands on their left leg with their right leg lifted to the side and bent at the knee, arms at their sides. They rotate and bend their torso forward while maintaining the position of their arms and the lifted leg.

Standing in a wide stance, the person points their left arm upward while keeping their right arm bent at their side with the forearm held in front. They then bend forward and rotate their torso counterclockwise.
The person returns to a wide stance with both arms at their sides and their feet spread apart.
The person raises both arms to shoulder height.

The individual begins in a bent-over position with their arms at their sides and forearms pointing downward. They rise slightly, swinging their arms to the side while rotating 90 degrees clockwise and stepping forward. After stopping, they rotate 180 degrees clockwise and point their forearms forward with bent elbows, then drop their arms to their sides. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise and extend their arms out to the sides.

Starting with hands on their hips, the individual performs a sharp, isolated shift of the upper body and head to the left. As the torso moves, the right arm lifts and bends while the feet remain in place.
The person holds both arms out to the sides and steps onto their left foot while shifting their weight to the left.

The figure stands with the right arm at their side and the left arm bent at the elbow, forearm pointing forward. They shift their hips back and kick the right leg backward, then rotate 90 degrees clockwise while raising their arms in front of their head, with elbows pointed up and forearms bent. They extend the left arm to the side and rotate the shoulder in a circular motion before leaning slightly to the right while maintaining their arm positions.

A person stands slightly bent forward with their arms at their sides and forearms held in front of their body. They step back, straightening their posture and swinging their arms backward, before bending forward slightly again.
Starting in a squatting position, the person swings their arms from the front toward their back hips, then twists their right shoulder forward.

Starting from a squat, the person stands up and brings their legs together while swinging their arms in a hip-hop style. They then walk forward while continuing hip-hop arm movements, concluding the motion with their forearms in front of their body and elbows bent out to the sides.

Starting with elbows raised and forearms held in front of the chest, the person turns 90 degrees clockwise and steps forward on their left leg while swinging both arms straight forward. They then rotate their right arm sideways, followed by a torso rotation as they move their right leg backward to complete a full turn. After swaying their arms, they take a step backward.
A person steps backward while lightly bouncing their raised, bent arms up and down in front of their body.

The performer stands with their knees turned in and feet pointed out, holding their arms above their head with bent elbows. They jump off both feet to rotate 180 degrees clockwise, extending their arms upward and outward as they land.
The person steps forward onto their left foot while swinging both arms forward and across their body from left to right, mimicking a baseball bat swing.
The dancer turns left, raises both hands, and then lashes forward.

The person stands in a wide stance with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent. They rotate their torso 90 degrees to the right while swinging their arms up and down, then jump to switch their leg positions. Simultaneously, they thrust their left arm straight forward from the shoulder and pull their right arm back with the elbow bent and the forearm pointing forward.
A person performs a sudden chest pop while flinging their arms out and upward, simultaneously rising from a low, wide stance into an upright position by bringing their feet together.
The person steps back while moving both arms up and down, then rolls their forearms in front of their chest while lifting their right and left knees.
The person swings their right arm sharply upward across their chest while simultaneously sliding their right foot forward and across their body to the left.

The person sweeps their left leg out to the side while lowering into a squat and swinging their arms widely. They then rise, bringing the leg back to the starting position as their arms continue to swing in coordination with the leg movement.
The person starts a wave from their left hand that traverses through the upper body and down to the right leg, finishing with a high back kick.
The person leans forward, raising both arms toward their head with elbows bent. They extend their right leg straight back while keeping the left leg bent, preparing for a quick slide or foot tap.

Standing with elbows bent at their sides, the person rotates 90 degrees clockwise and thrusts their arms forward. They then step back, throwing their arms out to the sides. Turning another 90 degrees clockwise, they hold their arms out with elbows bent and forearms pointing up. Finally, they adjust their hips and vibrate their torso and arms while maintaining the position before hopping back.
The person nods and bounces downward.

Starting from a low, wide stance, a person performs a quick, isolated pop of the chest and shoulders. This is immediately followed by a subtle inward slide of the left foot while the arms and legs otherwise maintain their positions.
The person stands upright with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They jump, lifting the left leg and then the right, pushing each arm down as the leg on the same side rises.
The person performs a quick, isolated upper body pop to the left, lifting the left shoulder while the right foot swiftly slides or taps out to the side.
The person suddenly steps back.
A person drags their right foot forward from far behind to meet their left foot, then steps backward while swinging their arms up and down in a popping style.

The person stands in a slight crouch with their right arm extended to the side, elbow bent and forearm pointing down, while the left arm is held low with the forearm horizontal in front of the body. They kneel, lowering their torso to punch the floor twice, then lean their body back and forward. After rising to a standing position with arms extended to the sides, they step forward with the right foot and hold their arms in front, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
The person raises their hands into the air and pulls their elbows downward. They then slowly lean back by bending their knees forward while swinging their forearms back and forth.
With knees fully bent and the torso leaned back, the person slowly walks forward while swinging their forearms in the air.
The individual executes a sharp, isolated pop with the chest and shoulders, followed immediately by a swift, unpredictable slide or tap of one foot along the ground.

The person stands in a wide stance with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent with the forearm across the torso. They straighten the left arm to their side and rotate their torso to the opposite side while keeping their legs stationary.
Starting in a squatting position, the person hops sideways on both feet. They then swing their right leg backward while swinging both arms in the same direction, followed by a step back.

Starting in a squat, the person jumps in place while swinging their straight arms back and sideways. They then squat while swinging their straight arms in circular motions. Maintaining the squat and the arm swings, they jump forward and suddenly stand up with both arms extended straight forward.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They step forward and rotate 90 degrees clockwise, shifting their arms so the forearms point upward. They then lift their left leg with a bent knee and hop in place twice, lowering their arms to their sides before extending the left arm out to the side.
The individual performs a quick, isolated upper body pop, extending their left arm while walking forward.
The individual performs a sudden, isolated pop of the chest and shoulders to the right while quickly sliding their left foot out to the side and slightly back.

The person stands slightly bent over with their elbows bent at their sides. They straighten up and turn in a clockwise circle, then point their arms forward with bent elbows before pointing them to the right.

The individual stands leaning back with their right leg forward and left leg back, arms out to the sides and bent at the elbows with forearms pointing forward. They shake their arms from the elbows while turning 90 degrees clockwise, then hold their arms in front of their body with knees bent. Next, they step forward while continuing to shake their arms, turn right, and run in a circle while swinging their arms in front. After stopping, they hold their arms above their head with the elbows bent and forearms forward, then drop their arms to their sides. They thrust their left arm up and to the side, slide to the right, and finally thrust both arms upward with their legs together.
A person performs a quick, isolated pop with their chest and shoulders while keeping their arms bent and raised. They then cross their left foot over their right and step to the right.
Raise your arms in the air and bounce up and down.
The person slowly rotates their torso in a wide circular motion after turning their body to the left with their left arm.
A person alternately slams their arms down, then turns to face another direction and slams them down again.

After extending both arms and legs sideways, the person brings them together. They then swing their arms in front of their body and extend both legs by twisting their feet on the ground, ending in a squatting stance. From the squat, they step backward while continuing to swing their arms in front of their body.
In a squatting stance, the person walks forward step by step while swinging their arms in front of their chest.
Standing upright, the person swings their left arm across their body from the left side.
The individual sharply pops their left shoulder and chest forward as their left foot simultaneously slides forward in a swift, subtle motion.
A person stands on their left foot with their right foot in the air while punching forward with their left hand.
The figure suddenly drops into a low crouch, leaning the torso forward. Simultaneously, the arms swing down and forward in a quick, isolated motion while the feet perform a rapid tap.

Starting from a full squat with a bent torso, the person jumps lightly while maintaining a low, crouched posture with bent knees. They then slowly stand up and walk forward with their arms in the air, ending the movement with both hands raised above the head and elbows bent.
With both hands raised above the head and elbows bent, the person rotates their body while straightening their arms.

Starting from a standing position with arms raised above the head and elbows slightly bent, the person rotates their torso 180 degrees. They then move their forearms overhead before stepping forward with the left foot and turning 90 degrees clockwise.
The person repeatedly stomps their left foot. Synchronized with the rhythm of the stomping, their widely raised arms move slightly up and down.
From a standing position, a person quickly turns 90 degrees to the right while dropping into a deep squat and swinging both arms up and down. They then jump to the right.
The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms positioned in front of their body. They extend their arms slightly while vibrating their body.
A person steps backward while rolling their hands in front of their head.
The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms positioned in front of their head, then rotates 90 degrees clockwise while swiveling their knees from side to side.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their upper arms. They raise their arms overhead and then bring them down to point straight forward. Next, they rotate clockwise while keeping the left arm straight at their side and the right arm bent horizontally across the front of the body. They turn 90 degrees counterclockwise and swing their forearms downward, then rotate another 90 degrees counterclockwise while swinging the right arm overhead in a wide backward circle. Pivoting clockwise on the right foot, they swing their left arm and leg around to face backward, crouching with both knees bent and the left forearm pointing forward. Finally, they step back with the left leg and raise their forearms, holding their arms out to the sides with elbows bent.
The figure raises its right knee while extending its left arm to the side, then quickly drops into a low crouch with a sweeping arm motion before rising and extending both arms into a T-pose.
The person kicks their right leg across behind their left while extending both arms upward and outward. They then rhythmically swing their left leg and both arms.

The individual stands with a slight forward bend, arms out to the sides, elbows bent, and forearms pointing downward. They then straighten slightly and raise their forearms to a horizontal position while shifting slightly to the right.

The person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm raised vertically. They slowly lower their left arm until the forearm points forward, then lean forward slightly and rotate their forearms in circles around each other in front of their body.

Starting in a squat, the person swings their arms out to the sides. They then bring their left leg toward the right while swinging their arms in front of their body. After swaying their body from shoulders to feet, they raise their right hand with a bent elbow, extend their left arm straight forward, and rotate their torso several times.

With the torso fully bent forward and balancing on the left leg while the right leg is raised backward for balance, the person twists their body while keeping the right leg elevated. After turning, they land the right leg behind them and shift their weight to the right.
The figure sharply drops their torso down and to the left while bending the knees, simultaneously sliding the right foot outward and forward.
The person shifts their weight slightly to the left and raises their arms.

The individual begins with their arms bent and forearms pointing forward, standing with their left foot ahead of the right. They step the left foot forward and to the left, rotating their forearms in front before moving them out to the sides while still pointing forward. Next, they extend the right arm straight out to the side while rotating the torso slightly counterclockwise. They then bring the arm back to the side and move the right leg forward, drawing the knees together before stepping straight ahead with the arms held at their sides. They shake their arms at their sides with elbows bent, then step the right foot behind the left and the left foot out to the side, continuing to shake their forearms before finally dropping their arms.
The performer executes a sudden, isolated upper-body pop, causing the arms to flail outward, followed immediately by a quick, unpredictable foot slide.
The individual sharply flicks their left arm up and away from their head while tilting their upper body to the left, followed immediately by a quick backward slide of the right foot.
Starting with a fully bent torso and a slight squat, the person stands up and raises their left arm straight up.
The individual suddenly pops their torso to the right while sliding their left foot out and extending both arms wide.

The person hops to the right, leading with the right leg and following with the left. During the jump, their hands point to the left and wave rhythmically. They then perform a turn while swinging their right arm horizontally.
The person takes a large step back with their left leg while raising both hands with bent elbows.
The individual begins with a sharp, isolated chest pop, followed immediately by a swift sliding step to the left.
The dancer performs a quick chest pop forward and to the left, followed by a swift backward slide on the right foot as the left leg extends forward.

The figure performs a running-in-place motion, starting with a high forward kick of the right leg while the left arm swings up. The movement continues by alternating legs and arms in a dynamic, skipping-like sequence.
The person pulls both arms back.

The person sharply twists their upper body to the right, raising the left arm while extending the right arm out. Simultaneously, they lift their left leg with a bent knee in preparation for a quick tap.

The person executes a quick, sharp upper-body pop, jerking their chest and shoulders upward and to the right. Their legs remain bent and relatively stationary, subtly adjusting to support the sudden movement.

The performer stands with their arms at their sides. They turn 90 degrees clockwise, raising their arms with bent elbows and forearms pointing forward. Moving forward in quick hops, they keep their arms raised in this position. They then bend their knees and lean forward slightly, pumping their elbows up and down with their forearms pointed toward the floor.

The individual stands with their arms extended forward. They lean forward and bend their elbows, bringing their forearms in front of their body. They then push their left forearm forward before thrusting both arms forward, deepening their knee bend and slightly straightening their torso.

A person stands with their arms pointed forward. They lower their arms and raise them again, bending the right elbow to hold the forearm horizontally in front of the torso. They lower their arms slightly and kick the left leg up to touch the knee to the left hand, then lean back and hold their arms at their sides with elbows bent. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, bending their elbows to hold their forearms in front of their upper arms. Finally, they straighten and drop the left arm to their side while pointing the right arm out to the right, keeping both elbows bent.
The person initiates with a sharp, isolated pop in the chest and shoulders, followed immediately by a quick slide of the right foot to the side while the left foot remains stationary.
The person performs a quick, isolated pop of the right shoulder and chest to the right, while simultaneously sliding the left leg backward and stepping the right leg slightly forward.
The performer executes a quick, isolated backward pop of the chest and shoulders while raising both bent arms. The feet stay mostly in place, with a subtle shift or tap on the ground.
Starting from a slightly forward-leaning T-pose, the person suddenly steps forward and prepares to kick.

The dancer stands with their left leg in front of the right and their arms bent at their sides. They shake both forearms while stepping back with the right leg, then continue shaking only the left forearm after completing the step.
The person performs a sharp, isolated pop with the left shoulder and arm, extending them upward and outward, followed immediately by a quick shuffle as the right foot slides forward and back.

The individual steps forward quickly, moving their arms rapidly up, down, and to the sides in front of them. They then extend their arms at shoulder level with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward, standing with their feet wide apart. Finally, they slap their left hand against their left thigh, leaning forward and to the left while stretching their right arm across to the left.
The person bounces with their right arm extended to the right and their left arm bent, hopping between each bounce.
The person jerks their right arm up and to the side while simultaneously sliding their left foot backward and outward in a quick, unpredictable motion.
After stomping the right leg into a wide stance, the person drags both feet together. Simultaneously, they raise both arms with the elbows bent.
The person repeatedly thrusts both arms forward.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They rotate 180 degrees clockwise and thrust their arms out to the sides. Stepping back, they swing their arms across each other in front, then turn 90 degrees clockwise and hold their arms at their sides. They step to the left and swing their arms in that direction, then step forward and push their arms forward before rotating clockwise to face forward with their arms at their sides. Finally, they step back and point their arms to the left, rotate their shoulders slightly, and step forward with their arms at their sides.

Starting in a left-leg lunge, the person swings both arms forward in circular motions while leaning their torso forward. They then jump sideways, maintaining the lunge stance and touching their right hand to the ground. Finally, the person stands up straight while swinging both arms back and forth.

The figure stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent in front, forearm pointing forward. They rotate their torso clockwise and straighten slightly while maintaining their arm positions. They then bend forward and rotate 180 degrees counterclockwise, extending their arms straight forward as they step back. Rotating 90 degrees counterclockwise, they stretch their right leg and arm out to the side, keeping the left arm bent with the forearm horizontal in front of the torso. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise, thrusting their arms straight back while bending the right leg forward and the left leg back, leaning the torso slightly forward.
A person bends down, touching the ground with their right hand while extending their right foot backward.
The person steps forward with their left foot.
The person rolls their left hand with their right arm extended to the side.

The person swiftly contracts their upper body inward, pulling their arms in and down, then immediately releases explosively, throwing their arms up and wide while stepping forward with their right leg.
The person flings their arms out to the sides in a sudden, isolated upper-body movement, followed immediately by a quick, unpredictable slide or tap of one foot.
Starting in a wide stance with their torso bent downward, the person jabs toward the ground. They then jump, bringing their feet and hands together, before transitioning into a squat.

A person stands with their right leg forward and left leg back, both knees bent and arms at their sides with elbows flexed. They rotate counterclockwise, bringing their arms up to shoulder level with the forearms positioned in front of the upper arms. They then rotate their forearms downward, turn their torso 90 degrees clockwise, and point their forearms upward. Next, they rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and widen their stance, extending their arms straight up and out to the sides. Finally, they rotate their torso 90 degrees clockwise while maintaining the position of their arms.

The person stands with their right arm extended straight to the side and their left arm out, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise and punch their left arm straight forward from the shoulder while lowering the right arm to the side, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward. Next, they rotate another 90 degrees clockwise into a wide stance, sliding both arms to the right before bringing them down to their sides. Finally, they swing their forearms from side to side in front of their body and extend their arms forward, bending the elbows to hold the forearms horizontally.

The person stands with their arms pointed to the right. They rotate clockwise on their left foot while pointing their left arm up and to the side, then lean to the right onto their right foot, lifting their left leg and pointing both arms to the right.

The performer stands with elbows bent and forearms in front. They roll their forearms around each other while stepping forward with the right foot, then lift and slightly lower their arms—keeping the hands pointed upward—as they bend their torso.
The person performs an arm motion around their chest while in a bent posture.
The performer executes a quick chest and shoulder pop while lifting their left leg back with a bent knee. Keeping the arms close to the body emphasizes the sharp, isolated movement.
The person rises to a standing position.
The person bends down and lowers one arm at a time.

The person begins in a low stance, stepping their left leg forward and back while twisting their torso. They then rise to perform a sequence of isolated arm and shoulder movements, finishing with their arms extended to the sides.
The person steps forward in a zigzag pattern, lifting their knees high with each step. They then transition to walking backward while swinging both arms across the front of their body.
The person bounces up and down with both fists held near their chest.

The dancer stands with their arms in front, extends their left leg forward, and moves their arms over it. They then bring their knees together and apart, finishing with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent.
Standing on the left leg, the person swings their right leg in a circular motion, then squats and jumps while twisting their hips and knees, swinging their arms in the opposite direction.
The person moves their elbows up and down.
A person stands with their arms extended forward. They then turn counterclockwise, moving their forearms in front of them while shuffling slightly backward.
Holding both hands together in front of the chest, the person sways their arms and torso in an upward arc to the right, then back toward the center and upward.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms in front of their body. They swing their forearms in circular motions while keeping their upper arms at their sides, eventually stopping with their forearms pointing forward.
In a hunched and squatting stance, the person swings their left arm downward while twisting their torso. They then step forward, continuing the downward swing primarily with their forearm.
The person is moving their arms up and down.
The person thrusts both fists forward and pulls them back several times.
A person performs a sudden jerk, throwing their left arm up and back while their right arm moves forward, then quickly slides their right foot back and to the right.

The person stands slightly hunched with their arms at their sides. They walk forward a short distance while swinging their arms, then widen their stance and extend their arms to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. After returning to an upright position with a slight arm swing, they bend their knees with feet turned out and hold their arms out with elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They then extend each leg in turn, lowering the corresponding arm to point to the knee—first the left, then the right—before finally straightening up.

The dancer performs a quick upper-body pop, jerking the right arm upward so the hand is near the head. This is immediately followed by a fast, unpredictable slide to the side on the left foot while maintaining a crouched position.
Starting from a crouched position, the person sharply pops their upper body while pulling the right arm inward, then performs a quick, sliding tap forward with the left foot.
A person pushes their hips back while extending both arms out to the sides, then jumps forward and immediately performs another jump while turning to the left.
The person moves their elbows back and forth.
The person swings their left elbow backward in a circular motion, then extends their left arm sideways before pulling the left elbow back.
The performer executes a quick, isolated upward pop of the right shoulder and chest. Simultaneously, the right foot slides back and out into a wider stance as both arms extend to the sides.
A person takes light, rhythmic steps while swinging their straight arms from side to side across the body, gradually lowering into a squat while maintaining the movement.
The person sharply pops their left shoulder while lifting their bent left arm. Simultaneously, they tap their right foot behind them while keeping their left leg bent for support.
The person extends both arms forward.
The person repeatedly moves their forearms up and down.

The figure stands upright with their arms at their sides. They step slightly out with the right foot, bending the knee and rotating clockwise, before bringing their arms forward and extending them out to the sides.

The dancer stands on their left leg with the right knee lifted and bent, holding their arms in front with the elbows bent. They step the right leg to the side and shake their forearms in front of their body, keeping their elbows bent.

Starting in a right-leg lunge with a bent torso, the individual turns backward by shifting their weight from right to left. They then perform a downward jabbing motion while taking side steps to the right.
A person leans their upper body to the left while performing a quick, low kick or slide with their right leg, then immediately retracts it to the starting position.
Standing upright with arms at their sides, the person rotates their torso 90 degrees clockwise and then bends their elbows to point their forearms forward.
The dancer performs a quick, isolated chest pop while pulling their arms in, followed by a swift forward tap of the left foot.
The person slides around with complex upper body movements.

The person performs a quick, isolated pop, sharply jerking their chest and shoulders to the left as their arms swing down in the same direction. They maintain a low stance with the right leg bent forward and the left leg extended back.
A person stands with their arms bent at their sides. They push their arms forward until they are straight, drop them back to their sides, and walk forward.
Starting in a slight squat, the person lightly shakes their knees and right shoulder before bringing their right hand to meet their left.
The performer stands with their arms pointing forward. They lean further forward, pushing their arms ahead and lifting their right foot.
The dancer performs a sudden, isolated pop of the right shoulder and chest while the left foot slides back and to the right in a quick, shuffling motion.

Starting with the right leg extended to the side and the right arm pointing down and forward, the person swings their right arm and leg backward to land in a wide squat. Maintaining this low position, they take a step backward while swinging their arms alternately. Finally, they rise from the squat and begin dancing in a hip-hop style.
Maintaining a wide squat, the person steps backward while repeatedly swinging their left arm forward.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms straight, pointing downward and to the sides. They pivot counterclockwise on their left foot and step around with their right foot, pushing their arms forward and back in an alternating motion.

The individual stands with a slight forward lean, their left arm extended to the side and their right arm bent horizontally in front of the torso. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and push their bent arms forward, clapping their hands and then pointing their forearms outward. They slide backward while maintaining their leg positions, moving their forearms up and down, then step forward and bend over while pointing their forearms toward the ground. Finally, they push their forearms forward and step back, raising the left arm to the side with the elbow bent and forearm up, while extending the right arm forward.
With a wide stance, the person leans to the left while jabbing their left arm downward, then swings their left forearm rhythmically.
The dancer performs a quick, isolated upper-body movement, raising their bent arms up and outward, followed immediately by a fast, unpredictable foot slide while maintaining a low, crouched position.
The person performs a quick, sharp downward contraction of the upper body and arms before expanding back up. They then twist their torso while sliding their left foot back and to the side.
The person is performing a variation of a locking pose.
The performer begins with a sharp, isolated chest pop, followed immediately by a quick slide of the right foot out to the side and back to the center.

The individual stands with their right arm extended forward and their left arm at their side, elbow bent and forearm pointing up. They shake their arms while maintaining these positions, then step their right leg to the side, pulling their arms across their body.
In a wide squat, the person leans their torso forward toward the ground and swings from side to side, letting their arms hang and sway loosely with the momentum.

The person stands in a wide stance with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms pointing forward. They straighten up and swing their arms to their sides while keeping the elbows bent. They position their left arm at their side with the forearm in front, then jump back, rotating slightly clockwise as they drop their arms. Finally, they step back to face the front and cross their arms in front of their body.
The figure sharply pops the left shoulder forward as the torso twists, while the right foot slides back and to the side into a wider stance.
The dancer swiftly lifts their left knee toward their chest while pulling their arms in, followed by a rapid sequence of alternating forward and side kicks with opposing arm swings.
The person points their arm in different directions while performing a 180-degree rotation.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, with the left elbow bent and the forearm pointing forward. They bring their left hand and left knee toward each other, return them to their starting positions, and then rotate slightly counterclockwise while pushing both arms forward until they are straight.

The performer executes a sharp, isolated upper body pop, snapping their shoulders and arms to one side. This is followed immediately by a quick, unpredictable slide on one foot while the other leg remains bent to maintain balance.

The person performs an isolated upper-body pop, drawing their arms inward from an extended position. They then quickly slide their right foot forward to meet the left, transitioning from a lunge into an upright stance.
The person shakes their arms and legs.
The person rhythmically steps backward while swinging their opposite elbows back. They then rotate their body, swinging both arms side-to-side in sync with their torso.
The person’s arms move in a circle in front of their chest, parallel to the ground.
The dancer performs a sudden, isolated chest pop to the left while keeping the hips stable, simultaneously sliding the left foot back and across to the right.
The dancer performs a swift, isolated downward pop with the upper body while pulling their arms inward, then quickly taps their right foot to the side before returning to the starting position.

A person stands with their arms at their sides. They vibrate their body slightly from side to side and kick their right leg behind the left, then touch their left hand to their right foot twice by leaning back and to the left and extending their right arm out at shoulder height. They slide forward and bend over, pointing their right arm to the ground, then straighten and step back with their arms at their sides once more before rotating their body 90 degrees counterclockwise.
The person puts one hand up and the other hand down.
The dancer performs an isolated chest pop while holding their arms in a bent, dynamic pose, followed immediately by a quick, short slide of the left foot to the side.
The person takes a large step back with their left leg, then rhythmically swings both elbows back and forth.

From a wide stance, the person swings their left elbow out. Next, they quickly pull their right elbow back and cross both arms in front of their body. They then step forward while pulling both elbows back to open their chest.
The person performs a sudden, isolated upper body pop, primarily thrusting the chest forward, followed immediately by a quick, unpredictable slide of the left foot forward and across the body.
The person performs a sudden, isolated upper-body pop involving the shoulders and chest, followed immediately by a quick, sharp slide of the right foot backward and to the side.
A person performs a sudden, isolated pop of the right shoulder up and back while simultaneously sliding their right foot backward.
The person performs a sharp chest pop while leaning back with their arms bent and raised, then immediately slides to the right on their right foot, drawing the left foot in for a tap behind.
A person leaps forward and lands on their right leg, then immediately turns to step backward while swinging their right arm out to the side.
The dancer turns around and walks.
Standing with their arms at their sides, the person takes a few steps back while turning 90 degrees counterclockwise and raising their forearms to point forward.
Balancing on the left leg with the right leg extended to the side, the person twists their torso to bring the right leg back to the center while rhythmically swinging their arms up and down.
The person hops to the right and bounces up and down.
A person performs a sharp pop in the upper body and shoulders, pulling their arms toward the torso while executing a quick twist and a subtle shuffle of the feet.
The person steps back with their left leg, then taps the right foot and brings it back while rotating their elbows from back to front. They conclude the movement by tapping their left foot forward.
The person leans forward and steps back while their upper body bounces up and down.

Starting with arms outstretched to the sides, the person quickly bends their elbows to bring their hands up and then down to rest on their hips while simultaneously sliding their feet apart into a wider stance.
The performer drops their body while flinging their arms out to the sides, followed by quick footwork as they return to an upright position.
The person steps forward with both hands in front of their chest while bouncing. They then lower their body by bending their knees and twisting their torso.

The person steps forward on their left leg while swinging their arms out to the sides, then immediately pulls the left foot back to tap the toe behind them. They follow this with several rhythmic steps backward in a fluid, hip-hop style.
A person sharply extends their left arm and leg to the side while keeping the rest of the body still, emphasizing the suddenness of the movement.
Starting from a squatting position with hands held at chest level, the person dynamically alternates raising each knee while pulling both arms downward.

A person performs a quick, sharp drop by deeply bending their knees and hips in a sudden, full-body movement. Their arms and legs pull inward as they rapidly transition from a semi-crouched pose into a low squat.
The person steps forward while swinging their torso back and forth, using steps similar to a shuffle dance.

A person stands on their left foot with their right leg bent behind them, reaching their left arm back to touch their right foot. They kneel and sit down before standing back up and spinning clockwise in a circle with their arms at their sides.

The dancer performs a quick, isolated pop of the upper torso to the right while keeping the lower body relatively still. Both arms remain bent and held at chest and shoulder height throughout the movement.
A person sharply pops their chest and shoulders to the right while sliding their left foot back and to the side in a quick, unpredictable motion.
The person steps in place while twisting their torso to the side.

The person pops their chest while raising their arms into a sharp, bent position, holding the left hand near the head. They follow this with a quick tap of the left foot behind and to the side while bending the right knee.

The person performs a sharp, isolated pop of the left shoulder upward and backward. Simultaneously, they slide their left foot back and to the side while making sharp, angular adjustments with their arms.

The performer sharply pops their chest and shoulders, flinging both arms out and up. Simultaneously, they take a quick, asymmetrical step, moving one foot back while the other steps forward and to the side into a wider stance.
The dancer executes a sudden, isolated chest and shoulder pop while bringing the right arm down. Simultaneously, the right foot taps forward as the left foot slides to the side.
The person sharply twists their upper body, bringing their right hand toward their head while simultaneously performing a quick, subtle shift in footing from a bent-knee stance.
The person bounces up and down while walking backward.

With a bowed torso and the left arm hanging straight down, the person raises their left leg and left shoulder simultaneously. They then step forward with the left leg and perform bouncing steps backward.
The individual performs a quick, isolated chest and shoulder pop to the right, followed by a swift backward slide and a forward tap of the right foot.
The individual performs a rapid body drop by bending their knees and lowering their hips toward the ground while extending their arms, then quickly pops back up into an upright stance.

Starting with their arms at their sides, the individual steps their right foot back and to the right while pushing their arms out to the sides and then in front of their body, bending their elbows during the movement.
The person steps backward and then forward while bouncing their body.
A person stands upright with their right arm at their side and their left arm raised with the elbow bent. They lean slightly to the right at the hip and then return to a neutral standing position.

The person performs a sharp upward pop with their chest and right shoulder while keeping their arms bent, simultaneously tapping their right foot out to the side while maintaining a low, crouched stance.
The dancer executes a swift chest drop, sinking into a crouch. They immediately pop back up, extending both arms out to the sides in a T-pose.
The dancer performs a quick, isolated chest and shoulder pop while leaning slightly to the right, followed immediately by a short, swift slide of the left foot back and to the left.

Standing in a wide stance, the person extends their left arm straight out and points their right hand to the left. They then lower into a deep squat while keeping their upper body and arms completely still.
A person jumps high into the air while crossing their legs.
The dancer throws their left foot back and performs a 360-degree rotation while the foot is behind them.
The dancer steps back, turns to face a different direction, and waves their arms around.
The dancer raises both arms and pops their right shoulder and chest while simultaneously extending their bent left leg forward to tap the ground.

The performer initiates the movement with a sharp, isolated pop of the chest and right shoulder while holding a stylized arm position, followed immediately by a quick, sudden forward slide of the right foot.
A person performs a sharp, isolated pop of the chest and shoulders while twisting to the right, sliding their right foot as the left foot taps the ground behind them.
The performer executes a quick pop by extending their arms and chest outward, then immediately twists their torso to the right while sliding their right foot back into a lunge.
The person begins with a quick, isolated torso twist and a shoulder pop while simultaneously performing a rapid foot slide, switching from a left-foot-forward to a right-foot-forward stance.
The person steps forward and turns around, then steps forward and turns around again.
The performer begins with a sharp, isolated pop of the chest and shoulders to the right, followed immediately by a quick backward slide of the left foot that returns to its original position.
The person puts their arms forward and shifts their weight to the right while twisting their ankles, then hops forward and waves their arms around.
In a wide squat stance with the torso leaned to the left, the person rotates their torso to the center while maintaining the squat stance.

Starting bent over with arms extended to the sides and elbows slightly bent, the person steps forward with their left foot and shakes their forearms while straightening up, ending with their arms at their sides and elbows bent.
The person begins with a quick, isolated chest pop and a shoulder shrug. They then perform a swift slide to the right, tapping their right foot next to their left to complete the move.
The dancer steps back and swings their left arm.

The individual stands with their elbows bent and forearms held in front. They step forward while moving their forearms up and down, then rotate 90 degrees clockwise and jump while continuing the arm motion. They rotate back to the starting position to repeat the forward step, then turn 90 degrees clockwise again to perform another jump with the same arm movement before finally returning to face forward.
Balancing on the left leg, the person swings their right leg back and forth along the ground as their arms swing in rhythm with the movement.
A person stands in a staggered stance with their left leg forward and arms bent upward. They perform a quick, isolated jolt of the upper body, subtly twisting the torso and shifting their arms.
The performer executes a sharp, isolated chest and shoulder pop while shifting their upper body to the right, followed by a quick slide of the right foot forward and back.
The performer executes a quick, isolated upper-body pop with their arms and chest while their left foot slides slightly backward.
The dancer executes a swift twist, pulling their arms and legs inward before extending their arms out to the sides, finishing with a sharp, isolated wobble of the upper body.
A person performs a quick upper-body twist with sharp, isolated arm movements, followed by fast, unpredictable foot shuffling on the spot.
After stomping on the left leg, the person raises their hands with bent elbows. They then rotate their hands downward while keeping their elbows bent, resembling a scarecrow posture.
The person glides their feet across the ground in a circular motion while waving their arms from side to side.
The person moves rhythmically sideways, then raises one knee and both hands before moving in the opposite direction.
The person stands slightly bent over with their arms at their sides, turns 90 degrees counterclockwise, and swings their arms up before performing two forward hops and repeating the sequence.
A person turns counter-clockwise with a gentle waving motion through their torso and arms.
The person performs a rhythmic shake while lifting both elbows up.
With a hunched torso, the person twists their feet while moving sideways, then jumps into rhythmic steps with one leg forward and the other back, concluding in a T-pose.
Beginning in a left-leg lunge, the person swings their left arm from the outside toward their body. Subsequently, they rotate their torso 180 degrees to the right.
The individual performs a sudden downward chest pop while bending their knees and rocking their arms up and down.

The dancer stands with their left foot in front of the right and knees bent, arms held at their sides with elbows flexed. They slide slightly backward, swaying their torso from side to side while moving their forearms up and down.
Holding both hands in front of the pelvis, the person rhythmically swings their torso from side to side while stepping backward in a light squat stance.
The dancer lifts their left knee high and to the side while extending their arms outward, followed by a quick, sudden slide or tap of the foot.
Starting from a left leg lunge, the person turns their body to the right by untwisting into a squat stance. They then raise both hands with bent elbows, squat down, and stand up.
The individual suddenly twists their torso to the left while quickly sliding their right foot forward and across their body.
The figure performs a quick, isolated chest pop, thrusting the upper body forward and upward.
A person performs a sharp, isolated pop of the chest and shoulders to the right while simultaneously sliding their left leg back and to the side in a quick, unpredictable motion.

The person starts with their right leg back, their left arm at their side, and their right arm bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing up in front of their body. They rotate 270 degrees clockwise, raising the left arm to point upward by the end of the turn, then step back with both arms straight in front of them.
The person steps forward, then back, and bounces up and down.

A person steps forward while swinging their arms back and begins bouncing at the knees. They then alternate extending each arm forward from a high-elbow position, mimicking a slow-motion freestyle swimming stroke.
A person steps backward.
The person steps to the side while holding their hands together and waving them around.

The dancer performs a sharp, downward pop with their chest and shoulders while tucking their arms in. Then, their left foot quickly slides back and to the side as the torso returns to an upright position.
A person rotates their upper body in a large circular motion while stepping in place.

The performer executes a quick, isolated upper-body movement, pulling the left shoulder back and up while dropping the right arm to create a chest pop. The lower body remains in a stable, bent-knee stance throughout the motion.
The person sharply jerks their left shoulder up and back while twisting their torso, simultaneously sliding their right foot back and to the side to drop into a low crouch.
Start in a low, athletic stance and rotate the upper body.
The person pops their chest and shoulders while flinging their arms out to the sides, simultaneously taking a quick step to shift their weight from the right foot to the left.
The dancer sharply pops their right shoulder and arm upward and outward, then immediately slides their left foot back and to the left while leaning their upper body forward.
The performer stands upright with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They kneel and bend their torso forward before straightening back up.
The person walks around with a bouncy step.
A person drops down while swaying their arms forward and back.

The person executes a sharp, isolated upper-body pop, primarily using the chest and shoulders to flick the arms outward. This is followed by a quick backward slide of the right foot as the left leg straightens.
The person bounces forward and backward.
A person rhythmically swings their arms up and down while bouncing with alternating knee bends, keeping their right foot in front of the left throughout the movement.

The dancer begins bent over with their arms pointing downward and forward. While maintaining this posture, they rock their torso back and forth while swinging their arms backward. They then stand upright and point their left forearm upward before hopping forward and to the right. Finally, they walk in a clockwise circle, swinging their arms in circular motions with bent elbows and straightening them out to the sides as they move.
The person sharply jerks their upper body to the right with arms bent and raised, then quickly slides their right foot back as the left foot taps in place.
The person bounces their upper body while swinging their forearms toward their chest and extending them sideways.
A person rhythmically steps forward, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs while bouncing their torso back and forth.
The person steps lightly in rhythm while swinging their upper body side-to-side with raised, bent elbows.
The person spins around and then extends both arms.
The person stomps their right foot forward while swinging both arms across their body from a wide position.
The person raises their right knee and kicks forward. Simultaneously, they bring both hands to their chest and swing them forward and upward.
happy feet: With knees bent, the person shuffles their feet rapidly back and forth close to the ground. Their arms are held out to the sides for balance while the upper body remains relatively stable.

Maintaining a low, crouched stance, the person performs quick, shuffling footwork in place. They rapidly alternate between tapping their heels and toes while keeping their arms bent and active for balance.
The person jumps, repeatedly stepping their legs outward and crossing them in a rhythmic pattern.
The person spreads their feet and punches down.
The individual stands with their knees bent, performing a rapid in-place shuffle by quickly tapping their heels and toes. Their arms are bent and held up, moving in opposition to their legs.

The person stands with their arms in front and rotates 90 degrees counterclockwise, thrusting their arms out to the sides. They then bend forward slightly, pulling their arms in and pushing them out again. Next, they rotate slightly clockwise with their knees bent and feet turned out; they bring their arms forward with bent elbows, straighten them, and finally point their forearms down and forward.
The person performs a small jump, landing with their feet apart while swinging their arms outward. They then quickly bring their feet and arms back together, repeating the movement rhythmically.
The person performs shuffle steps, alternately jumping to cross one leg behind the other and stepping out, while naturally swinging their arms back and forth.
The person jumps and lands with their feet apart before quickly shuffling them back together. Simultaneously, the torso twists as the arms swing in opposition, one forward and the other back.

The person stands with their arms extended forward and elbows slightly bent. They jump, moving their knees out and back together while rotating their torso to the side and back. Simultaneously, they cross their arms in front of their body and return them to the starting position, then push them alternately forward and back.
The dancer performs a "happy feet" move by quickly shuffling and tapping their feet in place. They keep their arms bent and raised to the sides as one leg kicks up behind them.

A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms in front of their torso, hands touching. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, swinging the left arm back while the right arm remains in front with the elbow bent and the forearm held horizontally. They then rotate counterclockwise into a wide stance with their arms extended out to the sides and pointing downward. Next, they pivot 90 degrees clockwise, bring their arms in front of the torso, and swing them back to jump once, landing with the left arm extended to the side and the right arm at their side. Finally, they bend forward and swing their arms to the sides while rotating the torso clockwise, then swing back to the front while remaining bent over with their arms at their sides and elbows bent.

In a low stance, the person performs a rapid shuffle in place by quickly alternating heel and toe taps. Their arms are bent and raised to the sides, while the upper body sways slightly in rhythm with the fast footwork.
In a low, crouched stance, the person leans forward with their arms bent close to the body. They perform a rapid shuffle, quickly alternating their feet back and forth while staying in place.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm extended outward, elbow bent and forearm pointing up. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise while maintaining this posture. They then bend their knees and widen their stance, turning back to face the original direction, while bringing both arms out, bent at the elbows with the forearms in front of the upper body. They rotate clockwise and straighten their arms downward, then bend their elbows to pull them back, pointing the forearms forward with the arms held above the head. Finally, they thrust their arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height before bringing them back to their sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward as they bend their knees.

The person repeatedly performs a hopping motion, jumping their legs out to the sides and back together. During the hops, they move their arms either side-to-side or forward and backward while slowly advancing forward.

The performer shuffles in place, alternating their left and right feet in a quick, forward heel-toe pattern. Their bent arms swing in opposition to the leg movements, maintaining balance and enhancing the rhythmic motion.
crisscross: The person starts with their legs apart and arms bent, crosses their right leg in front of their left, and then immediately steps back out to return to the initial wide stance.
Starting from a wide, crouched stance, the person crosses their left leg in front of their right while bringing their arms down and forward in front of their torso.

A person stands with their feet wide and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They perform alternating crossover steps by crossing one leg in front of the other, stepping back out, and then repeating the motion on the opposite side. As the legs cross, the arm on the same side as the front leg moves slightly forward while the other arm moves slightly back.

Starting from a crouched position, the individual crosses one leg in front of the other while pulling their arms inward. They then uncross their legs by stepping one foot out to the side and extending their arms back out. This movement is repeated rhythmically, alternating legs with each step.

A person stands slightly bent forward with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They move their knees and arms inward and outward while keeping their elbows bent, then lean further forward and push their forearms downward.

The person rhythmically performs crisscross steps, jumping to spread their legs wide and then jumping again to cross one leg behind the other. In sync with the leg movements, the arms swing out to the sides as the legs separate and pull back in as they cross.

The person stands with their arms extended to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They jump and spin clockwise while pushing their forearms outward, then jump back to the starting position. This sequence is repeated twice.

In a crouched posture, the person performs repetitive crisscross steps, crossing and uncrossing their feet. During the first half of the movement, they hold their upper body steady with elbows raised and hands positioned in front of their head. For the remainder of the motion, they rhythmically swing both arms from side to side.
two step: The figure steps to the right with their right foot, shifting their weight before bringing the left foot in to join it. Their arms are held in a dynamic pose with both elbows bent, while the right arm swings with a larger range of motion than the left.

Starting with arms at the sides and the left forearm pointing forward, the individual rotates 90 degrees clockwise on the right foot while thrusting both arms forward. They then step the right leg forward and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise to face front, bending their elbows so the forearms are in front of the body. Next, they jump back and rotate 90 degrees clockwise with their arms bent at their sides, then step forward and rotate another 90 degrees clockwise to face backward with their arms pointing forward.
The person performs a two-step by stepping to the right and bringing their left foot to meet it, while rhythmically moving their bent arms in front of their chest.
The performer steps back with the right foot.
The person stands on their left leg with their right knee raised and arms at their sides. They then step to the right, raising and pointing their arms in that direction while rotating their forearms.

Starting from a crouched position, the person performs a two-step to the left by stepping out with the left foot and bringing the right foot in to meet it. Their arms are bent and held high, swaying in coordination with the body's side-to-side movement.
The person steps to the right and brings their left foot in to meet it, shifting their weight. Their arms are bent and held near the chest, moving rhythmically with the body's side-to-side motion.
The person steps to the right, shifting their weight onto the right leg. Simultaneously, they raise and extend their left arm outward while keeping their right arm bent.

Starting from a T-pose, the person steps forward with the right foot while rolling their forearms in front of their body, then steps forward with the left foot while extending both arms sideways. They then bring their left leg back while rolling their forearms, and finally step their right leg sideways while extending both arms sideways.
The person repeatedly raises and lowers their arms while stepping side-to-side with their right and left feet.

The individual stands with their left leg forward and right leg back, knees bent, and arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They rotate 180 degrees clockwise, swinging their arms in front, then step forward with the left leg. As they step, they point their right arm forward while the left forearm points up. Finally, they step the left foot back and lean forward slightly, with both elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
A person performs a side-to-side two-step, shifting their weight from one foot to the other. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, moving in sync with the steps.
The person stands with their arms bent in front of them. They turn 90 degrees clockwise, extending the left arm forward and the right arm to the side, before returning to their original position.

The figure performs a two-step by first stepping to the left and bringing the right foot in, then repeating the movement to the right. Their arms are bent and raised, swaying from side to side in coordination with the steps.

The person stands with their arms bent in front of them. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise and step their right foot to the side, bringing the left foot to meet it. While leaning right and bending the left elbow, they kick their left foot and arm out twice. They then step forward and to the side with their arms pointing back at their sides, before swinging the right arm up and straight out to the side at shoulder height.

Starting from a low crouch, the person performs a two-step to the right by stepping out with the right foot and rising. They then bring the left foot in to meet the right while swinging their arms up and extending them out to the sides.

Starting with knees bent and hands touching in front, the performer jumps their feet apart while pushing their arms forward and then out to the sides at shoulder height. They then kick their right leg back, bending the knee to touch the foot with their left hand.

The individual stands with their arms extended to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They move their arms slightly in and out while rotating counterclockwise. They then take a step back, maintaining their arm position. Finally, they rotate clockwise to face the opposite direction, bringing their arms to their sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.

The person steps side-to-side in a low squat, alternately extending each leg with a locking rhythm. Simultaneously, they hold their elbows up and out to the sides, moving them up and down in sync with their steps.
The person steps to the right with their right foot and brings their left foot in to meet it, keeping their arms raised and bent at the elbows throughout the movement.
rolling arms: A person stands with their arms straight at their sides and takes two steps forward, bending their knees. Simultaneously, they bring their hands together in front of their body, pull them back slightly, and then push them forward with their elbows slightly bent.

A person performs a rolling arm motion, lifting their left arm near their head while positioning their right arm across their chest. They maintain a wide, stable stance with a slight twist in the torso to support the upper body movement.
The person turns right and steps forward while slowly lowering into a squat, continuously rolling their arms in front of their body.
Standing with knees bent, a person rolls their arms forward in large, alternating circles, starting with the right arm followed by the left.

A person in a low, wide stance leans forward with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their torso. They roll their arms around each other while turning counterclockwise and walking sideways, then stop to kick their right leg forward before standing upright with their arms at their sides.
A person circles their left arm in front of their chest while turning their body to the right.
The individual stands with knees bent and initiates a large, circular rolling motion with both arms.

Standing in a wide stance with knees bent, the individual performs alternating forward arm rolls, starting with the left arm. The body sways from side to side, shifting weight in rhythm with each roll.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms out and elbows bent, holding their forearms in front of their torso. They rotate their forearms around each other in a circular motion and then turn slightly counterclockwise.

The person stands on their left foot with the right foot slightly lifted, pointing their right arm diagonally upward while keeping their left arm at their side. They kick the right foot forward and bring their arms together in front of the body, then rotate their forearms around each other and pull their arms back to their sides while returning the right foot next to the left.
The person stands straight and then suddenly rolls their arms in front of their body.
The person stands with their right foot forward and left foot back, extending their forearms and rolling them around each other.
The person steps back while lowering their right arm and raising their left arm.
A person stands in a wide stance, performing a hip-hop arm roll. The bent left arm is raised and moves in a distinct forward circle, while the right arm executes a similar, lower roll.
A person leans forward in a low stance while rolling their arms, with the right arm circling forward and down as the left arm moves backward and up.
Standing with knees slightly bent and arms bent at the elbows with forearms pointing upward, the person turns 90 degrees clockwise while throwing rapid punches before beginning to step forward.
A person walks rhythmically in place while rolling their forearms in front of their body.
A person performs arm rolls by rotating the left arm forward in a circular motion, followed immediately by the right. The upper body sways from side to side in rhythm with the arm movements.

In a crouched stance, a person performs a hip-hop arm roll with their arms bent in front of their chest. They initiate the circular motion by raising their right arm up and across their body as their left arm moves down.
A person stands with bent knees, rolling their bent left arm in a circular motion in front of their upper body.
A person stands and alternately rolls their arms in a circular motion in front of their upper body.
A person stands and rotates their right arm forward in a large circular motion, starting near the head, moving down, and then back up. The lower body remains stationary.
Angled slightly to the side, a person performs a hip-hop arm roll. The right arm circles forward and downward while the left arm simultaneously moves upward and back to begin the same rolling motion.
Stepping forward and turning with the left leg, the person rolls their left arm in a circular motion in front of their body while keeping the right arm raised with a bent elbow.
body roll up: The person extends their right leg wide to the side, then rotates their body to the right, shifting their weight to land in a lunge on the right leg.

A person initiates a body roll by pushing their hips forward, creating a fluid wave that ripples up through the torso and chest. The motion concludes as the wave reaches the shoulders and the head tilts backward.

With the left arm extended to the side and the right arm bent with the hand near the head, the person initiates a body roll from the hips, creating a fluid wave that travels up through the torso and chest to the head.
After stepping onto the right leg, the person executes a fluid upward body wave and then twists their body to the right and back by rotating their shoulders.
Twist the heels, then continue the twisting motion upward through the knees, hips, and shoulders, finishing at the head.

A person performs an upward body roll, beginning with their hips pushed back and their torso bent forward. The motion originates in the hips, creating an upward wave that travels through the torso and chest.
The person turns their knees to the left, followed by their body.
A person performs a body roll, starting from a crouch and pushing their hips forward and up. The motion flows sequentially up the torso and through the head as the body straightens.
The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms in front of their body. They move their arms to their sides and kick their right foot back and forth before vibrating their shoulders.
The person assumes a low squat with their right hand held in front of their chest, then performs a subtle, rhythmic full-body shake while maintaining the pose.

The performer executes an upward body roll, sending a fluid wave from the hips through the chest to the head. As the torso arches back, they balance on a straight left leg while lifting the right knee high toward the chest.
The person stands with their arms at their sides, bent at the elbows, and leans slightly to the side. They then straighten up and step forward, keeping their arms in the same position.
Standing with the right foot forward and the left knee bent, the person raises both arms to the left and performs a body wave that travels from the knees up through the torso.
Starting in a low crouch, the person initiates a body roll from the hips. This fluid, wave-like motion ripples upward through the torso and chest, culminating in a final movement of the head.

Starting with knees slightly bent, the person initiates a fluid body roll by pushing their hips forward. This wave-like motion travels up through the torso, chest, and shoulders, finishing as the head follows through.
slide: Starting from an upright position, the person slides their left foot backward and outward while keeping their weight on the right foot. As they slide, the torso leans forward, the right arm swings forward, and the left arm moves back for balance.
The individual slides to one side, extending one leg while bending the other into a low stance. Their arms are spread wide for balance, with one arm bent at the elbow and raised.
A person stands with their arms at their sides. They then bring their forearms forward and step to the side, swinging their arms out and straightening them at shoulder height.
The individual shifts their weight onto a bent left leg while sliding their right leg straight back. Their left arm is raised and bent, while the right arm extends downward and back.
The person stands with their arms at their sides, then slides to the side while pointing their arms back toward their starting position.
In a low, hunched stance, the person slides widely from side to side, mimicking a skating motion.
A person slides their left foot back, bending their right knee and lowering their body. They lean forward, extending their left arm forward and their right arm back.

The individual leans to the right, shifting their weight onto a bent right leg while extending the left. They then slide their left foot across the floor, moving sideways with their arms raised and bent for balance.

The person slides their right foot back and to the side while bending their left knee and leaning their torso forward. Simultaneously, the left arm reaches up and forward as the right arm moves back to maintain balance.
A person slides across the floor.

With arms extended to the sides, the person slides their right foot backward and inward while bending the supporting left leg. The torso twists slightly to the right, following the movement of the arms.
The person leans forward and to the right, balancing on their bent left leg. They then extend their right leg and slide it sideways across the floor while keeping their arms bent for balance.
The person slides their right foot diagonally backward, then initiates a turn by drawing the left leg in to meet the right.

Starting from a T-pose, the figure slides the right foot backward while keeping their weight on the left leg. Simultaneously, the torso leans forward and twists to the right as the arms swing in opposition for balance.
Starting in a wide stance with both arms raised straight out to the sides, the person drags their left leg toward their right. They then slide forward with their left leg while swinging their arms.
A person slides across the floor while raising and lowering their arms.

The individual begins standing with their arms at their sides. They move forward by repeatedly jumping from side to side while pointing their arms above their head. Next, they slide to the left, swinging their bent arms forward and to the left. Maintaining this arm movement, they step to the right and walk backward, finally pointing their arms upward and out to the sides.

The person slides their left foot out to the side while swinging their left arm in the same direction. The left leg then slides forward across the body, followed by the right foot sliding in to meet the left.

A person balances on a bent left leg and lowers their center of gravity while sliding their right foot backward and to the side. Simultaneously, they twist their torso to the right with their arms raised and spread apart.

A person stands upright with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height and elbows bent, positioning their forearms in front of their body. They shuffle from side to side while moving their forearms up and down, maintaining the height of their upper arms.
The person slides to the right, shifting weight from the left foot to the right. As they move, the arms swing in opposition: the right arm rises and moves back while the left leg trails behind.

A person slides their right foot forward along the floor while balancing on a bent left leg. Their arms are extended to the sides, and their upper body leans back slightly to counterbalance the forward motion.
