The person slides to the left while raising both arms wide overhead. They then roll their knees in a circular motion while keeping both arms raised.
A person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent, forearms pointing forward. They walk forward in a wide stance, clapping their hands together in front of their body.
The person slides their left foot out to the side while keeping their weight on a bent right leg, with arms extended horizontally at shoulder height.
bounce: A person stands facing forward, bouncing by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing dynamically in rhythm with the motion.
The person bounces rhythmically, alternating knee bends while holding both arms up with the elbows bent.
The person alternates between bouncing on both knees and kicking one leg back, while keeping their arms raised and elbows bent.
The person crouches and leans their torso forward, swiping the ground with their left hand while maintaining a bouncy motion.

The person rhythmically bounces by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees from a low, semi-squatting stance. With their torso leaning slightly forward, their arms are bent and raised, with the left arm held higher than the right.
The person steps backward while maintaining a low, hunched stance.

A person stands with their right arm extended to the side at shoulder height and their left arm at their side, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward. They rotate their body 45 degrees clockwise and raise their arms above their head, thrusting their forearms and hips forward twice. They then slide forward and repeat this motion, this time with the right arm lowered. After sliding to the right and repeating the movement with both arms overhead, they turn 90 degrees counterclockwise, step forward, and perform the motion again. Finally, they straighten up and repeat the sequence while kicking their left leg forward, then step forward while leaning back and repeat the motion once more.
A person performs a rhythmic bounce by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees, moving up and down while traveling sideways. Their arms swing rhythmically, mostly in front of the body.
While moving backward, the person performs a rhythmic bouncing motion by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. They then move to the right while continuing to bounce.
The person bounces rhythmically by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees while maintaining a low posture. Their arms are held out to the sides at shoulder height with the elbows bent.

A person performs a rhythmic bounce by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, moving slightly in sync with the body's up-and-down movement.
A person bounces rhythmically by bending and straightening their knees. They hold their arms up and bent, with the right arm positioned slightly higher and further forward than the left.
The person steps forward, popping their chest out and bouncing up and down through a squat.

A person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent. They rotate slightly clockwise and move their shoulders out and back, bringing their hands nearly together in front of their body. They then step forward with the right leg followed by the left. Finally, they move their arms out to the sides with bent elbows, rotating the left arm down and out while moving the right arm up and out.
A person bounces rhythmically by bending and straightening their knees, with their arms held out to the sides and elbows bent, moving in sync with the body.

A person performs a hip-hop bounce, rhythmically bending and straightening their knees in an up-and-down motion. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving in sync with the body’s bounce.
A person bounces rhythmically by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees, while keeping their arms extended to the sides and leaning their upper body slightly forward.

A person bounces rhythmically by bending and straightening their knees. Simultaneously, their bent arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving forward across the body while the left arm swings back.
A person rhythmically bounces up and down by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, as the upper body follows the bouncing motion.
A person bounces rhythmically by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. Their arms are raised and bent, with hands held near their head and shoulders.

A person bounces rhythmically in place by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. Their left arm is raised and bent with the hand near the head, while the right arm is extended out to the side.
A person rhythmically bends and straightens their knees, bouncing up and down in place. Their arms are bent at the elbows and held out to the sides, moving in sync with the body's motion.

A person rhythmically bounces by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees, causing their entire body to move up and down. Their torso is slightly leaned forward, and their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The person performs a rhythmic bounce by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees, causing their body to move up and down. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent, moving in sync with the body's motion.

The figure performs a rhythmic bounce by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees, causing the whole body to move up and down. Their arms are held high and bent, swaying slightly in sync with the body's motion.

Angled toward the right, a person bounces by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. The left arm is extended forward and to the side, while the right arm is bent with the hand held near the chest.
A person performs a rhythmic bounce by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, moving up and down in sync with the body's motion.

The person performs a rhythmic bounce by repeatedly bending and straightening their knees, moving their entire body up and down. Their bent arms swing back and forth in an alternating pattern, synchronized with the bouncing motion.
sneeze: Starting from a low crouch, the person performs a quick, sharp popping motion, jerking their upper body and head upward and slightly backward. The arms are thrown outward and upward as the torso moves, simulating the convulsive reaction of a sneeze.
body roll down: With both arms raised in front and elbows slightly bent, the person waves their arms and shoulders, then rotates their wrists.
The person circles their head and then pushes their chest and upper body forward and back.
The dancer initiates a wave from the raised left hand, traversing it through the chest, hips, and knees.
The person stomps their right foot, then bounces once and shifts forward.
The person waves their hands and body together. Then, they step with their left leg and swing their left arm sideways.

The individual stands with the left leg forward and the right leg back, both knees bent and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They step forward with the right leg while vibrating their torso, then step both legs out and stand with their arms extended and forearms pointing downward.

The person stands in a wide stance with their right arm extended to the side and their left arm bent at the elbow, forearm in front of the body. They move their left elbow and right knee toward the left; then, as the right knee returns to its original position, they lean forward and cross their left arm over their right leg.
A person starts a body wave at the shoulders that moves downward.
bart simpson: The performer performs a side-to-side two-step, shifting weight and leaning into the direction of each step. Their arms are held back with bent elbows as the trailing leg is brought toward the leading leg, creating a gliding motion.

A person rhythmically steps to the side and slides the other foot inward to meet it, creating a gliding two-step motion. Throughout the movement, their arms are held back and out to the sides, complementing the lateral weight shift.
The dancer glides side-to-side, extending one leg and dragging the other to meet it. Their arms are held bent behind their forward-leaning torso.

The person glides from side to side, stepping out with one leg while sliding the other foot across the floor. They maintain a low stance with their arms extended, one forward and one back, to stay balanced.

The person performs a side-to-side gliding motion, shifting weight onto one bent leg while the other remains extended. The trailing leg is then drawn inward to meet the leading foot, with the arms held out to the sides for balance.

A person performs a side-to-side two-step, stepping out with one foot and sliding the trailing foot inward to create a gliding motion. They maintain a crouched posture with the torso leaned slightly forward and arms held back with bent elbows.

The person turns 90 degrees to the left while stepping their right leg back, then returns to the center. This motion is then mirrored: they turn to the right and step their left leg back, followed by swinging the left leg out to the side.

A person starts in a hunched position with their arms in front of their torso, elbows bent, and forearms pointing upward. Their feet are set wide with the knees turned inward. They swing their arms to the left and straighten up, keeping their elbows bent at their sides and forearms in front. Next, they swing their arms in a circle to the right while stepping the left leg to the side, finishing with their arms pointing to the right. They then shuffle to the right, moving their arms in the same direction with the forearms pointing forward. Stepping left and leaning forward slightly, they extend their arms out to the sides at shoulder height and rotate their forearms. They then rotate their body counterclockwise and slide to the right while circling their forearms, finally turning 90 degrees counterclockwise and pointing their forearms forward with their elbows bent and arms held out to the sides.

A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They move their arms down and up while kicking their left leg out, then step to the left, swinging their arms for balance. Rotating counterclockwise, they move their forearms up and down, then step forward and continue the rotation while straightening up and pointing their forearms upward. They turn around as they step forward, keeping their arms in the same position. Next, they slide to the left, extending the right arm straight out and bending the left elbow so the forearm is held in front. They bend over, pointing their forearms down, then bring their arms down while lifting the right knee and returning it to the floor. They slide to the right, moving their forearms right and left while keeping the upper arms still after straightening. Finally, they stop, point their forearms out, and move their arms down and back up while lifting and lowering the left knee.
side rock: A person rocks from side to side, shifting their weight between their feet. As their hips swing with the motion, their arms are held up and bent at the elbows in a dynamic pose.
The body rocks from side to side, shifting weight between the feet and causing the hips and torso to sway. The arms start in a bent, raised position before extending out to the sides.
A person rocks from side to side, shifting their weight from one foot to the other. This movement causes the hips to sway while the upper body and arms remain relatively still.
The person rocks from side to side by shifting their weight from foot to foot. This movement creates a prominent hip swing, while the upper body and arms follow the rhythmic sway.

From a low squat, the person shifts their weight by bending both knees inward to the right, bringing their bent left arm toward the center of their body. Following this side rock, they take a step backward.

The figure starts slightly bent forward with their arms pointing down. They straighten and push their arms forward, then rotate 90 degrees clockwise and repeat the motion. Next, they push their arms out to the sides and rotate counterclockwise back to the starting position. They bend their elbows, crossing their arms in front of the body and rotating them around each other in circles while leaning forward slightly. Straightening up, they throw their arms straight out to the sides, then turn slightly clockwise and punch the left arm forward twice, bending at the elbow and pointing the arms back.
The individual rocks from side to side, shifting weight between their feet as their hips sway. Their arms move in opposition, with one arm rising as the body sways toward the opposite side.
A person stands in place, swaying their hips and upper body from side to side by shifting their weight between their feet. Their raised, bent arms move in sync with the swaying motion of their torso.
A person stands with their arms extended to the sides, bends forward, and returns to an upright position. They then place their right hand on their left shoulder and bend forward once more.
A person rocks from side to side, shifting weight between their feet while swinging their hips. Their arms are raised and bent, swaying in rhythm with the body's movement.
The person starts bent over with their arms bent at the elbows, then straightens up while leaning to the side. They then extend their left arm out to the side and stretch their left leg.

The individual rhythmically shifts their weight from foot to foot, creating a side-to-side rocking motion centered at the hips. Their hands remain fixed in front of the hips as the upper body swings in the direction of each weight shift.
A person stands and sways from side to side, shifting their weight between their feet. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, moving in sync with the swaying motion of the torso and hips.

A person shifts their weight from one foot to the other, creating a rhythmic side-to-side rocking motion led by the hips. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows, swaying in sync with the movement of the upper body.

Starting from a low squat, the person rotates and sways their hunched torso to the left, swinging their left arm across their chest. They then begin to bounce rhythmically on their knees while shifting their weight and torso to the right and then back to the left.
patty duke: The person kicks their right leg diagonally back behind the left while simultaneously raising both arms out to the sides for balance. They then pull the right leg back in by twisting the knee while swinging their arms.
The person rotates their left leg outward and stomps, then hops backward on the left leg. They land on their right foot, leaning their torso forward as the left leg extends back.
The person steps backward, shifting their weight between each foot while their head and bent arms swing.

A person stands with their right arm pointed out to the side and upward, with their left arm at their side. They rotate clockwise while swinging the left arm up, then move their hips forward and back as they return to the original orientation and step forward. They stretch their arms forward into a wide stance, then swing their arms up while stepping back. Finally, they point their arms forward again and swing them out to the sides as they take a small step to the right.

A person performs a hip-hop step, shifting their weight from the left foot to the right. The arms move in opposition to the legs, with the opposite arm swinging forward and upward in a synchronized motion.

Standing with the right foot forward and the left back, knees bent, and arms pointing upward, the person rotates their torso 180 degrees counterclockwise while pushing their arms up, then returns to the starting position.
The person steps back with their right leg and turns their body while extending both arms outward.

A figure stands slightly bent over and slides their feet sideways while raising their arms above their head. They rotate their arms in circles, pointing them to the sides and then back up. They then stand with their arms in front, elbows bent and forearms pointing up, and move them upward while shifting their right leg out to the side.

A person steps from side to side while keeping their feet close to the ground. With each step, they raise the opposite arm forward with a bent elbow and turn their head away from the direction of the step.
A person steps side to side, shifting their weight and leaning their upper body and head away from each stepping foot. Their arms swing up and down alternately in time with the steps.

A person performs the Patty Duke by stepping side to side, bringing their feet together between steps. As one foot steps out, the head and bent arms swing in the opposite direction, creating rhythmic opposition between the upper and lower body.

The individual performs a rhythmic stepping motion, alternating feet by stepping forward and across the body. With each step, the opposite arm swings forward and across the torso, creating a coordinated, oppositional movement.

From a low, hunched stance, the person steps their left foot out to the side while shifting their upper body weight to the right. As the right foot returns toward the center, the arms and head move in opposition. This sequence repeats while maintaining the hunched posture.

In a low, hunched stance, the person steps one foot out to the side while shifting their upper body, arms, and head in the opposite direction. They then return to the center and repeat the movement on the other side.
bounce step: The performer repeatedly crosses their right leg behind their left with a rhythmic bounce, extending both arms outward for balance.

The figure performs a rhythmic bounce, continuously stepping and shifting weight between the left and right feet. With each bounce, the knees bend and the arms, also bent, sway in time with the body's vertical motion.
The person turns 90 degrees and then steps forward with their right leg.

The individual bounces on their right leg, lowering their body while keeping the left knee raised. Their right arm is extended to the side and their left arm is bent in front of the chest, both moving in sync with the bounce.

A person performs a rhythmic bounce, continuously bending and straightening their knees while shifting their weight from side to side. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in coordination with the bouncing motion.

The person bounces rhythmically by bending their knees, maintaining a low center of gravity and a wide stance. They shift their weight from side to side while holding their arms loosely out to the sides.

A person performs a rhythmic bounce step, shifting their weight from side to side by bending one knee while extending the other leg to the side, creating a bouncy motion similar to Patty Duke leg movements. Both arms swing sideways in rhythm with the steps.

A person performs a rhythmic bouncing step, shifting their weight from side to side by alternately bending one knee and extending the other leg outward, similar to Patty Duke movements. Both arms swing sideways in coordination with the steps, moving toward the side of the extended leg.
A figure bounces rhythmically in place by repeatedly bending their knees. They then take a small step to the right while maintaining the bounce, keeping their arms bent and raised.
forward rock: The dancer leans forward, shifting their weight from the back leg to the front in a rocking motion. Their arms remain raised and bent, with the left arm forward and the right arm back.

The individual bends their knees and ankles, shifting their weight forward onto the balls of their feet. As the upper body leans forward, the arms are kept bent and slightly raised near the chest for balance.
Standing in a wide stance with knees bent, the individual holds their arms out at shoulder level with the elbows bent. They then straighten their arms and pull them inward, shaking them as they move.

The individual performs a forward rock by shifting their weight onto the balls of their feet, bending their knees, and leaning their torso forward. Their arms are bent, with the left arm held near the shoulder and the right arm positioned lower.
With forearms crossed in front of the chest, the person bows while lightly squatting to the rhythm. They then step backward while maintaining the bowing motion.
The person leans forward and rocks up and down.

Starting from a neutral stance, the person rocks forward by bending their knees and hips, shifting their weight onto the balls of their feet. Throughout the movement, they hold their arms out to the sides with bent elbows.
Standing upright with arms extended behind them, the individual leans forward and then back, vibrating their arms at their sides as they move backward.
The upper body leans forward and down, lowering the chest and head while pulling the arms in toward the torso. The knees bend to support the forward shift in weight.
Starting from a crouched position, the body rocks forward, shifting weight onto the balls of the feet. The left arm is raised and bent as the torso leans forward with the head tucked down.

A person stands with their left foot in front of their right and their arms held in front, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They bend forward and move up and down at the knees while bringing their arms out to the sides, then rotate their torso counterclockwise.

The performer leans their torso forward, shifting weight onto the ball of their bent left foot while extending the right leg backward. Simultaneously, both arms are raised and bent out to the sides for balance.

The person rocks forward, leaning at the hips and shifting their weight onto the balls of their feet. As they rock, their arms transition from a front-to-back position to being held out to the sides with bent elbows.
Starting in a crouched stance, the individual rocks forward onto the balls of their feet with their arms bent out to the sides for balance. As they rock, they take a step backward.

The person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They turn 90 degrees counterclockwise and repeatedly draw their shoulders and knees toward each other. They then extend their left arm upward and to the side while keeping the right arm in place, continuing to sway from side to side while moving their knees.

The figure stands with their elbows bent, the left forearm pointing up and the right pointing down. They gyrate their hips and swing their arms to switch positions while leaning forward slightly and taking a step forward.
A person rhythmically sways their torso back and forth while alternately tapping their feet.
The person rocks forward, bending their knees and leaning their upper body to lower their stance. Their arms remain bent at the elbows, moving in synchronization with the torso.
After clapping, the person rhythmically swings their torso back and forth while alternating leg taps.

The individual stands with their right arm extended upward and to the side, while their left arm is bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing up and to the left. They then rotate slightly clockwise and bend at the waist, bringing their arms to their sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing downward.

The individual leans forward, bending at the waist and knees to shift their weight onto the balls of their feet. As they rock forward, they keep their arms bent at the elbows, with the left arm positioned higher than the right.

The person rocks forward, leaning their torso over slightly bent knees while raising their bent arms. This movement shifts their weight onto the balls of their feet, lifting their heels off the floor.

The person rocks forward, bending their knees and leaning their upper body to shift weight onto the balls of their feet. Their arms are bent at the elbows and held up, moving slightly in sync with the body's rocking motion.
The individual leans their entire body forward, hinging at the ankles and hips to shift weight onto the balls of their feet. Their arms are held slightly out to the sides with elbows bent.
glide shift: The right foot glides back while the lower body turns left.

The figure stands with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent. They rotate clockwise and return to face forward while swinging their arms, then turn 90 degrees clockwise and raise their arms to point forward. Finally, they walk in place while lowering their arms back to their sides.
biz markie: The person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent. After turning 90 degrees to the side, they thrust one arm up and outward while simultaneously extending the leg on the same side, keeping the opposite arm at their side.
A person balances on their left leg while tracing a circle with their right foot near the ground. Simultaneously, they extend their arms outward from a bent position to shoulder height.

From a squatting stance, the person rhythmically sways their body while dynamically swinging their arms back and forth. Simultaneously, they shift their weight by alternately lifting each leg up and down.
The person repeatedly jumps sideways, swinging their arms in the opposite direction while lowering their torso.

A person stands slightly bent over with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They bend their torso to the left and push their arms forward while stepping back, then hop to the right and thrust their arms upward. Next, they lean to the right, thrusting the left arm forward and the right arm back, before straightening with their arms at their sides. They jump sideways to the right and push their arms up, then lean forward and push their arms out, before straightening and repeating the movement to the left. After jumping to the right and repeating the sequence again, they bend their elbows, shift their shoulders from side to side, and straighten. Finally, they jump to the center and thrust their arms out at shoulder height, then rotate clockwise and extend the left arm forward while keeping the right arm at their side.
Standing on the left leg, the person crouches to tap their right foot in front. They then circle the right foot to the side and back while rising and extending their arms to the sides.

The person kicks their right leg forward while swinging both arms straight forward. Then, they rhythmically sway their hips from side to side by bending and extending their knees while swinging their arms.

The person stands with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent. They walk backward while bending over, then rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and straighten up, swinging their right arm across to the left. Finally, they extend both arms out to the sides at shoulder height, with the elbows bent and forearms pointing downward.
running man challenge: The person performs a running man step, lifting their left knee high while the right foot is positioned back. They then quickly switch legs, pulling the right knee up and bringing the left foot down in a continuous, rhythmic running-in-place motion.
running man: The individual performs the Running Man by lifting their left knee high while the right leg slides back, then alternating to lift the right knee as the left leg slides back. Throughout the motion, the arms remain bent and held out to the sides, moving in opposition to the legs.

The figure stands with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. Rotating slightly clockwise, they step forward and back while swinging their arms by their sides. They then lean forward and step the right foot back, extending the left arm to the side while holding the right arm in front of the body, bent at the elbow with the forearm horizontal. Next, they rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and step back, reversing the arm positions. They then rotate 180 degrees clockwise, step back with their arms raised, and jump back, leaning slightly forward with the right arm out to the side and the left arm held horizontally in front. They rotate counterclockwise on the left foot and straighten, stretching their arms out to the sides at shoulder height. Finally, they step into a wide stance and lower their arms to their sides, held slightly away from the body.
A person performs the Running Man by alternately lifting each knee high while sliding the supporting foot backward. Their arms are held out to the sides for balance, moving in sync with their legs.

The person lifts their right knee toward their chest while sliding their left foot straight back along the ground. They lean forward over the raised knee, holding their arms out to the sides in a bent running posture.

The figure performs the running man, alternating high knee lifts as the opposite foot slides back along the ground. Their bent arms swing in opposition to the legs, creating a fluid running-in-place motion.

The figure performs the Running Man by lifting the left knee high while sliding the right foot back, then switching sides. The arms pump in opposition to the legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left knee rises.
The person performs a rhythmic hip-hop step, swinging their arms up and down in a continuous, alternating motion in time with their steps.

The figure stands with a wide stance and their arms out to the sides at shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They bring their legs together and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise while pushing their arms up and out to the sides, before walking backward with their arms at their sides.
A person runs in place, lifting each knee high while the opposite foot slides backward. Their arms are bent and swing in opposition to the legs, synchronized with the weight shift of each step.

The individual performs the Running Man by alternately lifting one knee high while the opposite foot slides backward. Their bent arms swing back and forth in coordination with the movement of the legs.

The figure performs a running man motion, lifting the right knee toward the chest while the left foot slides backward. The arms swing in opposition, with the left arm moving forward as the right knee rises to maintain balance and rhythm.

A person performs the running man by lifting their left knee high while the right foot is back. They then switch positions, sliding the left foot back as the right knee rises, with their arms swinging in opposition to their legs.

A person lifts their right knee high while sliding their left leg back, leaning their torso forward. They then smoothly alternate legs, raising the left knee and sliding the right leg back to simulate running in place.

The figure runs in place, lifting the left knee high while sliding the right foot backward along the floor. With the upper body leaning slightly forward, the arms swing in opposition to the legs to maintain balance.

The figure performs a running man step by lifting the right knee toward the chest while sliding the left foot backward. The arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving back and the left arm forward to maintain balance.

The person performs the running man by lifting their left knee high while sliding their right foot back along the ground. They maintain a forward lean with their torso as their arms swing in opposition to their legs.
The figure lifts the left knee high while sliding the right foot backward in a running-in-place motion. With the body leaning forward, the arms swing in opposition to the legs for balance.

A person performs the running man by lifting their left knee while sliding the right foot backward. They then hop to switch, raising the right knee as the left foot slides back, with their arms swinging in opposition to their legs.

The figure performs the running man by alternately lifting one knee toward the chest while the opposite foot slides backward. The arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in a reciprocal running motion.

A person performs the running man dance move by lifting their right knee while sliding the left leg back. They then switch legs, lifting the left knee as the right leg slides back, creating a fluid running-in-place motion.
A person performs the running man by lifting one knee high while sliding the opposite foot backward. They alternate this motion between sides, swinging their arms in opposition to their legs.

A person performs the running man by lifting their left knee toward their chest while sliding their right foot backward. Leaning slightly forward, they swing their arms in a rhythmic running motion to maintain balance.

A person performs the running man by lifting their right knee high while sliding their left leg back. Their torso leans forward, and their arms are extended to the sides to maintain balance throughout the movement.

A person performs the running man by alternating high knee lifts while sliding the opposite foot back along the ground. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in opposition to their legs.

The figure performs the running man by raising the right knee with the left leg extended back. It then hops to switch positions, lifting the left knee while the right leg slides back, as the arms swing in opposition.

A person performs the Running Man by lifting their left knee while sliding their right foot back along the ground. With their upper body leaning forward, they swing their bent arms in opposition to their legs to mimic a running motion.

The figure performs the running man by lifting the left knee high while sliding the right foot back, causing the torso to lean forward. The arms swing in opposition—with the right arm bent forward and the left arm back—as the figure prepares to switch legs.

The person performs the running man, lifting the right knee high while sliding the left foot backward. The movement then alternates, with the left knee lifting as the right foot slides back, while the arms swing in opposition throughout.
toprock: The person steps forward on the right leg and squats, opening their chest and extending their arms to the sides. They then pull the right leg back, swing the right arm forward, and hop on the left leg to begin a series of rhythmic steps and hops.
whoop rico: A person takes a wide step to the right, sweeping their left arm high and outward while leaning into the movement and shifting their weight onto the right foot.
kick and drop: The person kicks their right foot forward and back.
The person kicks their left leg forward and up while dropping into a squat, simultaneously raising their right arm up and back for balance and style.
dougie: Starting from a squat, the person rhythmically and dynamically swings their body.

After taking a step back with their left leg, the person performs a rhythmic and dynamic body-swinging dance motion. They then raise their right hand in front of their face and sway their body while keeping the hand in place. Finally, they bow with both hands pointing straight down and stand back up after swinging their arms from side to side.

The person sways their upper body from side to side, leading with the shoulders. Their arms, bent at the elbows, swing back and forth in a rhythmic, opposing motion while the legs remain relatively stationary.
A person sways their upper body from right to left while maintaining a wide, bent-knee stance. Their arms swing across the body, moving in sync with the torso.
The performer sways their torso from side to side, shifting weight between slightly bent legs. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in rhythm with the motion of the upper body.

The individual performs the Dougie, swaying their torso from side to side while keeping the lower body relatively still. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in coordination with the upper body’s motion.

A person sways their upper body from side to side, shifting their weight between slightly bent legs. Their arms swing rhythmically in opposition to the torso, with one arm crossing the body as the other swings back.

A person sways their torso from side to side while maintaining a wide, bent-legged stance. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing rhythmically in the opposite direction of the upper body’s movement.

The performer sways their upper body from side to side, shifting weight between their feet. Coordinated with the sway, their arms swing rhythmically in an alternating pattern across the body and out to the sides.
A person rhythmically sways their torso from side to side while maintaining a crouched position. Their bent arms swing across the body in opposition to the torso's movement.
up bounce: The person lowers their upper body and bounces up and down.
ground switch: Maintaining a low, forward-leaning crouch with bent arms, the person executes a quick ground switch by rapidly shuffling their feet back and forth along the floor.
Starting from a low crouch, the person rapidly switches their feet. The left leg, initially in front, swaps places with the right while the arms are held out to the sides for balance.
harlem shake: The person performs a rapid full-body shake, with their shoulders and upper torso moving energetically and erratically side-to-side and diagonally. Simultaneously, they slowly descend into a squat and rise back up to a standing position.

The individual performs a rapid, full-body shake, moving their shoulders and upper torso energetically and erratically side-to-side and diagonally. Simultaneously, they slowly squat down and then return to a standing position.

A person stands bent forward with their elbows tucked at their sides and forearms held horizontally in front of their torso. They bend further down, extending the right arm upward while touching the left hand to the ground. Straightening up, they rotate clockwise to a standing position with their arms returned to their sides and elbows bent. They bend over again, lifting the left knee while rotating 90 degrees counterclockwise. Continuing the rotation, they step forward while swinging their arms back and forth. They straighten up, maintaining the arm motion, then bend over once more, pointing the right arm toward the ground and the left arm upward. After returning to a standing position with elbows bent and forearms forward, they twist their torso clockwise to face the opposite direction, then bend over and swing the left forearm out to the side and back.

A person performs the Harlem Shake, rapidly and rhythmically shaking their upper body from side to side. Their arms are bent at the elbows and held out, moving in sync with the vigorous torso and shoulder movements.

Standing with bent knees, the performer initiates a rapid full-body shake that originates in the shoulders. Their raised and bent arms move erratically and loosely in sync with the upper body's rhythmic motion.
cabbage patch: With arms bent in front of the chest and the body leaning slightly forward, the individual steps out with the left foot, turns 90 degrees clockwise, and walks forward, lowering their arms to their sides with the forearms pointing forward.
The upper body twists from side to side while the arms, bent at the elbows, circle in front of the chest. Simultaneously, the legs remain bent as the feet perform a shuffling, stepping motion.

The upper body twists from side to side as the arms, bent at the elbows, perform circular scooping motions in front of the torso. The legs remain bent at the knees, shuffling in place to support the twisting movement.
Starting with their arms at their sides, a person turns 90 degrees clockwise and brings their arms in front of them. They then walk forward while rotating their torso in a circular motion.
The upper body twists from left to right as the bent arms sweep across the chest in a circular motion, while the feet shuffle and shift weight in coordination with the torso's rotation.
hip thrust: The person rhythmically thrusts their hips forward and back while leaning their upper body in the opposite direction. Their arms are held up with bent elbows, and their legs maintain a stable, bent-knee stance.
jerk: A person stands with their arms at their sides. They cross their right leg in front of their left, straighten their legs, and extend their arms outward with the forearms pointing down. After bringing their arms back to their sides and walking forward, they rotate slightly clockwise, bend their elbows, and step back. They raise their left arm above their head with the elbow bent and the forearm pointing right, then lower it straight to their side while stepping back and to the left; meanwhile, the right arm remains at their side with the elbow bent. Finally, they step forward slightly and extend both arms straight out at shoulder height before stepping back, lowering their arms to their sides, and extending them back out to the sides at shoulder height with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
pony: The figure performs a pony step, hopping on the left leg while tapping the right foot out to the side. With arms bent at the elbows, they pump back and forth in rhythm with the hopping motion.
march step: A person marches in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating fashion. Their arms are bent at chest level and move in sync with their legs.

A person marches in place, rhythmically lifting each knee high toward the chest. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left knee rises, and vice versa.
The person marches in place, lifting their knees high toward the chest in an alternating rhythm. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in opposition to the leg movements.

The person marches in place, lifting their knees high in a rhythmic, alternating motion. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the right arm moving forward as the left knee lifts, and vice versa.
A person marches in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating motion while swinging their bent arms in opposition to their legs.
A person performs a high-knee march in place, lifting the right knee toward the chest while swinging the left arm forward, then alternating sides in a rhythmic and energetic motion.
A person marches in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating pattern. Their arms are bent and swing in opposition to their legs, creating a dynamic and rhythmic motion.
The individual marches in place, lifting their knees high toward their chest. Their arms swing in opposition, with the right arm moving forward as the left knee rises and vice versa.

A person marches in place, alternating high knee lifts toward their chest. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs—moving the right arm forward as the left knee rises and vice versa—while the body sways slightly.

The person marches in place, rhythmically lifting each knee high toward the chest. The arms are bent and swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward as the right knee rises, and vice versa.
A person marches in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating pattern. Their arms swing back and forth in opposition to their leg movements.
A person marches in place, alternately lifting each knee high toward the chest. The arms swing in opposition to the legs, with the left arm moving forward as the right knee lifts, and vice versa.

The person marches in place, lifting each knee high in an alternating rhythm. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, with the left arm moving forward as the right knee lifts, and vice versa.
A person stands with their arms at their sides. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and step back while pushing both arms forward, then return their arms to their sides.
The individual marches in place, rhythmically lifting each knee high toward their chest. Their arms swing back and forth in opposition to their legs with elbows bent.
Viewed from a three-quarter angle, a person marches in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating rhythm while swinging their bent arms in opposition to their legs.
A person marches in place, lifting their knees high and alternating between their left and right legs. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to their legs.
A person marches in place, lifting their knees high in an alternating fashion. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, with elbows bent as each arm moves forward.
A person marches in place, rhythmically lifting their knees high toward the chest. Their arms are bent and swing in opposition to their legs, creating a bouncing motion.
wu tang: Standing in a wide squat, the person rhythmically swings their torso up and down while alternating their arm movements, pulling one hand to the chest and extending the other straight back.
punch out: The person stands upright with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They turn slightly clockwise and walk backward, pushing each arm forward along with the opposite bent leg. Finally, they stand straight with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms positioned in front of the torso.
Starting in a low stance with bent knees, the person thrusts both arms forward in a simultaneous punch, moving from a tucked position to full extension at shoulder height.
side scoop: A person performs a series of steps, raising one knee toward their chest while leaning their torso and arms forward. They then extend that leg and lower into a squat, repeating the sequence while moving in a circle.

A person performs a series of steps by raising one knee to their chest while leaning their torso and arms forward. They then extend that leg and lower into a squat, repeating these movements while moving in a circle.

The person stands with their arms extended forward. They clap their hands and lean forward slightly, holding their arms out to the sides at shoulder height with elbows bent and forearms pointing toward their knees. As they slide backward, they move from side to side, rhythmically pulsing their knees and elbows in and out while swaying their torso.
gucci: A person hops from side to side on both feet while swinging their elbows forward in rhythm with the jumps. In the middle of the movement, the person performs a squat.
chug: A person stands with their arms extended sideways at shoulder height and forearms pointing down. They shake their forearms from the elbows while moving slowly forward.
From a crouched stance with both arms extended, the person executes a sharp forward thrust of the hips and chest, propelling the entire upper body forward.
The person stands with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent so that their forearms point down. They shuffle forward in small steps, rotating their torso as they move.
point: A person stands with their arms extended forward and angled downward. They swing their arms to the left while rotating their torso 90 degrees counterclockwise, ending with their arms pointing straight ahead once more.
The person points their left arm straight out to the side while simultaneously extending their left leg straight back to touch the ground, maintaining a low, crouched position.
The dancer kicks their left leg out to the side with a pointed foot while simultaneously extending their right arm in the same direction, creating a dynamic pointing gesture with both limbs.

stomp: The person lifts their right knee and forcefully stomps their foot onto the ground. The upper body leans forward and to the left during the stomp, while the arms swing in opposition to add momentum to the movement.
A person lifts their left knee high while keeping the right leg straight, then stomps their left foot down forcefully as their arms rise in a dynamic, bent gesture.
With arms bent and raised, a person lifts their right knee high and then stomps their foot down forcefully while keeping the left foot planted.
Balancing on their left leg, the person raises their right knee high and then forcefully brings their right foot down to the ground while keeping their arms extended to the sides.
The person taps their left foot forward and then moves it back.
The individual lifts their right leg with a bent knee and forcefully stomps their foot onto the ground, leaning their upper body forward and to the right.

The person lifts their left leg, bending at the knee while keeping their right foot planted, then forcefully stomps it down. They maintain bent arms raised near their upper body throughout the movement.
The person raises their left knee high while spreading their arms out for balance, then forcefully stomps the foot onto the ground.
The person taps their right foot out and then moves their left knee toward it while bending down.
The person keeps stepping one foot down heavily to the side and then repeats the movement with the other foot.
The person lifts their left leg high with a bent knee and forcefully stomps it down. Their arms, initially spread wide, swing down and back up in coordination with the stomp.
The person takes a small hop and forcefully stomps one foot onto the ground. Their arms swing energetically, with one arm extending forward and the other swinging back.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They extend the right leg forward and the left arm up, while pushing the right arm back and bending the left knee. They spin 90 degrees clockwise and thrust their bent arms upward, straightening their legs as they jump back. They then lower their arms to their sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.

Balancing on their left leg, the person lifts their right knee high and then forcefully stomps their foot down. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides as the upper body twists and leans into the movement.
The person steps down heavily with their right leg from a position behind their left leg.
The person lifts their right knee high toward their chest while leaning their upper body back, then forcefully stomps their right foot down while flinging their arms out to the sides.

The person raises their left knee high while holding their arms in a tense, powerful posture. They then drive their foot forcefully into the ground, lowering their arms and crouching slightly to add weight and impact to the stomp.
A person raises their right knee high before forcefully driving their foot down into a stomp. Their bent arms swing in coordination with the body to add power to the movement.
The person raises their left leg high with a bent knee and forcefully stomps their foot down. Their torso leans forward into the movement, and their bent arms move in opposition for balance.
The person stomps their left foot on the ground twice while rotating to the right, with their left arm raised and their right arm lowered.

Standing on their left foot, the person kicks with their right foot while swinging their arms back at their sides. They then step back onto the right foot, turning slightly while swinging their arms forward, then back and up.
A person lifts their left knee high while leaning forward with arms raised, then forcefully drives their foot down into a powerful stomp.
Starting in a low crouch, the person raises their left leg high with a sharply bent knee before forcefully stomping it down, keeping their arms bent and tensed in front of their body.
The person lifts their left leg with a bent knee and powerfully stomps their foot onto the ground. The upper body leans forward into the stomp as the bent arms move in opposition.
A person balances on their right leg while raising their left, then forcefully stomps the left foot down, shifting their weight forward.
Standing with knees bent and arms held in front with slightly flexed elbows, the person lifts and lowers their left knee twice while simultaneously bringing their hands together twice.
With arms bent and raised, a person lifts their left leg high and then forcefully stomps it down while leaning forward.
A person lifts their left knee high before powerfully driving it down to stomp the ground. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides, moving in sync with the forceful downward motion of the body.
Crouching low, the person raises their right knee high with arms bent in a tense, expressive pose. They then forcefully drive their right foot into the ground to complete the stomp.
A person stomps their left foot, driven by a rotation in the left hip and knee. Their arms swing energetically in rhythm with the lower body, creating a dynamic motion.

A person raises their right knee toward their chest and forcefully stomps their foot onto the ground. Simultaneously, the upper body leans forward and twists to the left, while the arms remain bent to maintain balance.
The person stomps heavily on their left foot.
The person moves to the side and lands heavily on their left foot.
Balancing on the left leg, a person lifts their right knee high in preparation for a forceful stomp. The torso leans forward while the arms extend to the sides for balance and power.

The performer lifts their right knee high, then aggressively drives the foot straight down into a powerful stomp. The torso leans forward and twists while the arms remain bent and engaged, adding force to the movement.
The person stomps heavily on their right foot.

Standing bent over with their arms pointed down, the individual claps their hands and steps forward with the right leg. They then turn to the side and pull their arms and shoulders apart while keeping their elbows bent.

Starting from a low stance with the upper body leaning forward, the individual lifts their left leg and stomps it down powerfully. The arms remain bent and held out to the sides throughout the movement.

A person stands with their arms extended forward, their left leg forward and their right leg back. They swing their arms down and then up while lifting their right knee, then rotate 90 degrees clockwise. They step forward with their right leg, leaning forward slightly and bringing their right arm in front with the elbow bent and the forearm horizontal.
Crouching low, the person balances on their left leg while lifting their right knee high. They then stomp their right foot down forcefully, keeping their arms bent and held out to the sides.

The person raises their right knee toward their chest before forcefully stomping their foot onto the ground. As they stomp, their arms swing downward and their torso leans forward, adding power to the movement.
Balancing on the right leg, a person lifts their left knee high. They hold their arms out to the sides with bent elbows, preparing to stomp the raised foot down forcefully.

Raising their right leg with arms bent and held high, the person forcefully stomps their right foot onto the ground in front of them. The upper body leans forward and down, following the momentum of the powerful stomp.

The person balances on their right leg and raises their left knee high with their arms bent and lifted. They then stomp their left foot down forcefully, bending both knees and lowering their body with the impact.

The person lifts their left leg, bending it at the knee, and forcefully drives it down into a powerful stomp. Simultaneously, the torso leans slightly forward and to the right while both arms are bent and held out to the sides.

The person lifts their right knee high before forcefully stomping their foot straight down. As they stomp, their upper body leans back and twists to the left, with arms bent and held out to the sides for balance and emphasis.
Standing on their left leg, the person lifts their right knee high toward their chest while leaning their torso forward, arms bent in a dynamic, aggressive pose.
A person lifts their left knee high while keeping their right leg straight. Leaning forward with their arms bent near their head, they prepare to stomp their left foot down forcefully.

Crouching low, the person raises their left knee high before aggressively stomping their foot onto the ground. Their arms are held in a raised, bent, and tense posture that emphasizes the power of the movement.
A person stands on their right leg with their left leg raised and right arm extended forward. They then powerfully stomp their left foot down while simultaneously lifting their right leg.
Starting in a low, wide stance with arms bent and raised, the person lifts their right knee high and forcefully stomps their foot down and forward, leaning their upper body into the movement.
The individual raises their right leg and stomps it forcefully onto the ground. Simultaneously, they lean their torso forward and extend their arms to the sides for balance and emphasis.
A person walks forward in an exaggerated manner, planting each foot heavily with every step.
The person raises their right knee toward their chest while extending their arms to the sides, then forcefully stomps their right foot down, landing in a low crouch with the torso leaning forward.

Starting from a wide stance, the person lifts their left leg and forcefully stomps it onto the ground. The upper body leans to the right while the arms swing backward for counterbalance during the stomp.

The person raises their right knee high, leaning their upper body back slightly. They then forcefully drive their right foot into the ground, crouching low and swinging both arms forward and down to add power to the stomp.

The individual starts in a wide stance with knees bent and arms held slightly in front. They straighten while stepping forward with the right foot, then thrust their torso forward twice, swinging their arms straight back behind them.

The individual stands in a wide stance with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent across their body. They bring the left forearm down while lifting the right knee up and back three times.
A person forcefully stomps their right foot onto the ground, crosses their right leg behind their left, and stomps again.
The person takes a large step forward and stomps forcefully with a straight left leg, swinging their left arm back for counterbalance, then returns the leg to its starting position.
leg swing slow: Balancing on their left leg, a person slowly sweeps their right leg in a wide, circular arc from back to front. The torso twists and leans throughout the movement, with the arms extended to the sides.
The figure steps forward lightly, finishing a small left-leg kick with a brief pause before moving backward. This rhythmic sequence combines a delicate forward advance with a retreat.
A person balances on their right leg while slowly swinging their left leg forward and outward in a large circular motion, keeping their arms extended to the sides for balance.
A person in a crouched position slowly swings their left leg outward in a circular motion, leaning their torso forward and holding their arms out to the sides for balance.

A person balances on their right leg while slowly swinging their left leg in a large circular motion. They lift the left leg forward, sweep it out to the side, and lower it back down, keeping their arms extended for balance.
Standing on their right leg, a person lifts their left leg and slowly swings it forward and outward in a deliberate circular motion.

A person balances on their left leg, slowly sweeping the right leg in a wide circular arc from the front to the side. Their arms are extended to the sides for stability throughout the controlled movement.

A person performs a slow, circular leg swing, extending their left leg forward and to the side. Simultaneously, they lean their upper body back, keeping both arms raised and bent in a dynamic posture.

The individual slowly swings their right leg in a wide circular arc from front to side while leaning their torso to the left. Both arms are extended outward and slightly bent at the elbows to maintain balance.

A person balances on a slightly bent left leg while slowly sweeping their right leg forward and then out to the side in a wide circular motion. Their arms are extended to the sides for balance throughout the slow, controlled movement.
Balancing on the right leg, the person slowly swings their bent left leg in a forward circular motion. The upper body leans back and to the right, with arms extended to the sides for balance.

A person balances on their left leg while slowly swinging the right leg up and across the body in a circular motion. With arms extended to the sides, the torso leans to the left to counterbalance the leg swing.
The dancer kicks their right leg forward and then brings it back into a backward step. Their arms swing in opposition—left arm forward and right arm back—to maintain balance during the kick and step.

Balancing on the left leg, the person slowly swings their right leg in a high arc from back to front, crossing it over the body. The arms extend to the sides while the torso leans back slightly for balance.
The person slowly swings their left leg outward in a semi-circle, followed by a high back kick with the right leg as the torso leans forward and the arms adjust for balance.
While balancing on the left leg, the individual slowly swings their right leg forward and out to the side in a wide circular motion, extending their arms for stability.

A person slowly swings their right leg in a wide circular arc, moving it from a raised position in front out to the side. Their torso leans to the left to counterbalance the movement, while their arms are held in a raised, bent position.
With arms extended to the sides, the person slowly swings their left leg in a wide, circular motion in front of their body. The right supporting leg remains stationary and slightly bent for balance.

Balancing on their left leg, a person slowly swings their right leg forward and out to the side in a circular motion. The right arm is extended straight out to the side, while the left arm is bent with the hand held near the head.

The person quickly swings their right leg in a wide arc from front to back. As the leg swings, the torso leans back and twists while the arms are bent and held high for balance.

They hop backward, swinging the left leg and right arm back while the left arm swings forward. The person thrusts both arms to the upper right and pulls them back. Then, they draw a circle with both arms in front of their chest.
The person raises their right knee forward and holds it in the air while rolling their hands with their elbows bent and raised.
A person powerfully swings their left leg forward, leaning their torso into the movement as their bent arms swing in opposition.
