They lean forward and extend their left leg backward, then return to an upright position. Placing the left foot down, they jump to the left, stretching the left arm out to the side and bending the right elbow to hold the forearm horizontally in front of the body.
Balancing on the left foot, the person performs a swift, high swing of the right leg in a large circular arc. The torso leans back and twists with arms raised to counterbalance the leg's momentum.

A person stands on their left leg with their arms at their sides and their right leg extended straight. They move their right leg slightly to the side while shaking their shoulders. Then, they lift and bend the right knee, rotating it clockwise while pulling their arms back to the starting position.

A person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They thrust their arms to the right and step to the right, shaking their right arm and left leg out to the side. They then rotate the left leg to the left and step onto it, pushing both arms back and to the sides while kicking the right leg out behind them. Finally, they pivot 90 degrees clockwise on the left foot, swinging the right arm back and holding the left arm in front with a bent elbow and forward-pointing forearm, while keeping the right leg raised and bent at the knee.

The dancer performs a swift, powerful swing with their right leg, lifting it high in a forward arc. Their torso leans back to counterbalance the movement, while their arms extend to the sides for stability and expression.

A person swiftly kicks their right leg high and out to the side in an arcing motion. To counterbalance the powerful swing, they lean their body to the left and move their arms in opposition for stability.

Starting in a wide stance with arms at their sides, the person kicks their left foot forward while pulling their arms in. They then swing their arms out to the sides and kick with the left foot again, before retracting the leg and bending at the torso with their arms extended, elbows bent, and forearms pointing downward.
The person performs a rapid sway and forward kick with the left foot, then repeats the motion with the right foot.

Crouching low on their right leg, a person executes a swift, high circular swing with their left leg. Their torso leans forward with arms bent and held up dynamically, creating a powerful and expressive movement.
The individual performs a swift, circular swing of their right leg while leaning their torso back and to the side. Their arms are raised and bent, adding power to the dynamic movement.
Standing on their left leg, a person performs a rapid circular swing with their right leg, moving it forward and up, then sweeping it out to the right and back down in one continuous motion.

The person performs a high backward jump, launching off the right foot while kicking the left leg back and up. They swing their arms out to the sides for balance and land on the right foot to complete the movement.
A person performs a fast swing with their left leg, moving it up and down. To maintain balance, the upper body leans to the right, and the arms are held up and out to the sides.

The person stands bent over with their forearms crossed in front of their torso. They rise and bring their arms to their sides with bent elbows, then extend their left leg and move it in a circle from left to right while rotating their shoulders and swinging their arms in the same direction. Finally, they rotate their torso 90 degrees counterclockwise and stretch their arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
Swing the right leg forward and back.
low kick: The person performs a low kick, extending their left leg forward while keeping it close to the ground. They hold their arms out to the sides for balance, maintaining a rhythmic bounce in their stance throughout the movement.

Leaning forward, the individual performs a low kick with their left leg extended straight and close to the ground. Simultaneously, their arms fling outward and move erratically in a powerful, rhythmic bounce that engages the entire body.

The person performs a low kick, swinging their left leg forward and outward while keeping it close to the ground. Their entire body follows the movement with a rhythmic bounce as their arms remain extended to the sides.
A person bounces rhythmically on a bent right leg while performing a low forward kick with the left leg. Their arms are held up with bent elbows, moving in sync with the body's energetic motion.
A person performs a low kick forward and to the side with their left leg as their upper body and arms drop in a sharp, rhythmic bounce.
The person kicks one leg outward to the side a few times and then kicks the other leg. Both arms are extended outward, coordinating with the leg movements.
A person performs a low forward kick with their right leg while bouncing on their bent left leg. The upper body leans back and to the left, with both arms held in a dynamic, flexed position.

A person performs a low, sweeping kick forward and to the side with their right leg while bouncing on their left leg. The torso twists and leans forward as the arms swing in opposition to maintain balance and rhythm.
From a low crouch, a person rhythmically bounces on their bent left leg while kicking their right leg straight out near the ground, keeping their arms raised and bent near their upper body.

A person in a slight crouch rhythmically bounces on their right leg while kicking their left leg forward and low to the ground. Their arms move in coordination, with the right arm bent and the left arm extending forward with each kick.

Starting with hands on their knees, the performer kicks their left leg out, then turns and kicks their right leg three times while pushing both arms up. They then turn to the opposite side and repeat the motion with their left leg.

Starting from a crouched stance, the person executes a low forward kick with their right leg. Simultaneously, the left arm swings up and out to the side while the right arm moves across the body, all accompanied by a rhythmic bounce.
A person bounces on their bent left leg while kicking their right leg low and forward, leaning their torso back slightly with their bent arms held up and out to the sides.
Starting from a low crouch, the person kicks their right leg forward and low to the ground with a rhythmic bounce. The upper body leans forward, and the arms are held up and bent in a dynamic pose.

Standing with arms extended to the sides, the person performs a quick, low kick forward with their right leg. The supporting left leg bends slightly as the upper body maintains a subtle rhythmic bounce.

The person performs a low kick with their right leg, swinging it forward while leaning their upper body back. With arms extended to the sides for balance, the motion features a quick, rhythmic bounce as the leg kicks and retracts.

In a low stance, a person performs a low kick with their left leg, keeping it close to the ground, while hopping rhythmically on their bent right leg. The right arm is raised and the left arm is lowered, both moving in sync with the body's bouncing motion.

A person performs a low kick, extending their left leg forward close to the ground while deeply bending their right leg in a crouched stance. Their torso leans forward, and both arms are raised and bent in a dynamic, expressive gesture.

The person stands with their left leg forward and their right leg back, with the left arm raised and the right arm bent at their side. They then switch their leg positions while moving the right hand toward the left knee, swinging the left arm down and bringing the right arm across the front of the body.
The person kicks their right leg out.

A person performs a low kick with their right leg while maintaining a wide stance and leaning forward. They bounce rhythmically on their left leg with arms extended, the right arm forward and the left arm back.

The performer executes a low kick, extending their right leg forward near the ground while balancing on a bent left leg. Their upper body is slightly crouched, with arms bent and held in a dynamic posture.

A person performs a low forward kick with their right leg while bouncing rhythmically on their left. Their arms are held in a raised, bent position, with the right arm extended higher and further out than the left.

The performer leans forward and executes a low kick, extending their right leg straight out near the ground. They maintain a rhythmic bounce on their standing leg while holding their arms in a raised, bent, and dynamic posture.

The person performs a low kick by extending their right leg forward near the ground while balancing on a bent left leg. They maintain a hunched posture with raised, bent arms, executing the movement with a rhythmic bounce.

Standing slightly bent over with arms at their sides, the person rotates 90 degrees clockwise and thrusts both arms upward. They then pull their arms back in, continue turning clockwise, and step back while keeping their arms straight.
A person crouches on one leg while sweeping the other forward in a low kick close to the ground, accompanied by a rhythmic bounce on the supporting leg and coordinated arm movements.
A person performs a low kick, extending their left leg forward parallel to the ground. They maintain a low stance, bouncing on a bent right leg with their arms bent and held near the chest.

Starting from a low crouch, the individual kicks their right leg forward while keeping it close to the ground. They maintain a rhythmic bounce on the bent left leg as the upper body leans forward and twists.
The person waves both arms up and down and then kicks their right foot.

The performer kicks their right leg forward and low to the ground while leaning their upper body back. Simultaneously, they bounce rhythmically on their bent left leg, creating a powerful stomp as the right foot lands.

The person powerfully kicks their right leg forward from behind, swinging both arms forward simultaneously. They then switch legs mid-air into a low side kick with the left leg, leaning their torso to the right for balance.

A person performs a low kick by extending their right leg straight forward, close to the ground. They support their weight on a deeply bent left knee, leaning their torso forward while keeping their arms bent and raised.
From a crouched stance with the right leg lifted, the person takes a large stomp with the right leg while leaning their torso backward and raising both arms with bent elbows.
The person stands on their right leg with their arms and left leg extended behind them. They swing their arms and left leg forward and shake them twice.
Crouching low on a bent left leg, the individual extends their right leg straight back into a low kick near the ground while keeping their arms bent and raised.
Maintaining a crouched, squatting stance, the person hops on their right leg while kicking their left leg forward.
The person kicks their left foot forward and puts both fists forward.

A person performs a low kick by extending their right leg forward and low while keeping the supporting left leg bent. The upper body leans forward with arms tucked close to the torso, creating a dynamic, coiled posture that suggests a bounce.

From a low crouch, the person executes a powerful forward kick with their left leg, keeping it close to the ground. They bounce rhythmically on the bent right leg while holding their arms up in an expressive pose.
A person steps forward while swinging their arms, executing a right turning back kick. They seamlessly spin from the kick into a high left knee strike, performing a martial arts combination.
The person bounces on their supporting left leg while thrusting their right leg forward in a low kick close to the ground, maintaining a crouched position with their arms bent in a dynamic pose.

Starting from a low crouch, the individual performs a sweeping kick forward and to the right with their right leg, keeping it close to the ground. Their arms are bent and held high, moving energetically in sync with the body's rhythmic motion.

The person stands with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They step their right foot forward next to the left, bringing their arms forward with the elbows bent, and take a slight jump to the side.
Standing on their left leg, the person kicks their right leg forward while shaking their torso and head.
A person kicks their right leg low and forward while rhythmically bouncing on their supporting left leg. The upper body follows the motion with the arms extended to the sides.

Crouching low, the performer executes a swift forward kick with the right leg while maintaining a rhythmic bounce on the supporting left leg. The arms are bent and held wide to the sides, contributing to the energetic and expressive motion.

Starting from a crouched position, the person performs a swift, low forward kick with their right leg while maintaining a rhythmic full-body bounce. As the kick finishes, they throw their arms out wide to the sides for balance and emphasis.

The individual leans back and to the left while performing a low forward kick with their right leg, maintaining a rhythmic bounce on the supporting left leg with arms extended to the sides for balance.

Starting from a low crouch, the performer executes a low kick with the left leg, keeping it close to the ground. This is followed by a quick, rhythmic full-body bounce as the arms move and the kicking leg retracts.

Starting with their arms at their sides, a person kicks their left foot out while swinging their arms. They then turn slightly counterclockwise and repeatedly kick their left foot out while swinging their right arm.
Standing on the left leg, the performer executes a low forward kick with the right leg. The upper body remains upright with a slight bounce, while the arms are bent and held out to the sides.

The individual crouches slightly and performs a low forward kick with their right leg, keeping it close to the ground. Their upper body and arms bounce rhythmically with the motion while the left leg remains bent for support.

A person kicks their right leg forward, keeping it low to the ground, while their left leg remains bent. They bounce rhythmically on the supporting leg with their arms bent and raised, moving in sync with the body.
While balancing on the right leg, the person performs two low, rhythmic kicks with their left leg.

A person performs a low kick, extending their right leg straight and low to the ground while balancing on a bent left leg. The upper body leans back with arms bent and raised, and the movement is punctuated by a rhythmic bounce on the supporting leg.
high kick: A person performs a powerful high kick, forcefully extending their right leg high in front of them. To maintain balance, their torso leans back while their arms move in opposition to their legs.
A person performs a high kick, forcefully lifting their right leg straight up and forward while leaning their torso back and extending their arms to the sides for balance.

The person performs a forceful high kick with their left leg, raising it nearly parallel to the ground. To maintain balance, the torso leans forward as the arms swing back and forth to stabilize their center of gravity.
A person performs a forceful high kick with the left leg, lifting it straight up while leaning the torso back. The arms are bent and raised in a dynamic pose for balance and expression.
The person performs a high kick with their right foot, swinging it forward from far behind while their arms move up and then down.

A person stands with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height, leaning slightly to the right. They slide forward and kick their right foot forward while clapping their hands in front of their body. Then, they step back with their right foot and bend their elbows, bringing their forearms horizontally in front of the body, and lean back while holding their arms in this position above their head.
The person throws their left arm and legs out. The left leg kicks high and then rotates clockwise.

The person performs a forceful high kick, swinging their right leg straight up in front of their body. As the kick reaches its peak, the torso leans back slightly and the arms are thrown wide for balance and dramatic effect.

A person executes a powerful high kick with their left leg, driving the knee upward and pointing the toes. As they kick, they lean their torso back and to the right, keeping both arms raised and bent for balance and flair.

A person performs a powerful high kick with their right leg, extending it straight up. The torso leans significantly backward to counterbalance the movement, while the arms are bent and held out to the sides for stability.
An individual twists their upper body to the left while delivering a powerful high kick with their right leg, leaning their torso back and to the side to counterbalance the motion.
The person starts with shoulder movements and then suddenly kicks their right foot high.
A person performs a powerful high kick, driving their right knee toward their chest with arms bent, then extends both arms into a T-pose as they lower the leg.
The person performs a high, forward kick with a bent right knee while leaning their torso back for balance. Simultaneously, they raise and spread their arms to emphasize the forceful motion.
A person performs a forceful high kick, swinging their right leg straight up. Their torso leans back to counterbalance the motion while their arms are held in a bent, raised position.

The person aggressively kicks their left leg high into the air in a forceful, explosive motion. Their torso leans back to maintain balance as their arms fling wide, emphasizing the power of the movement.
A person leans back while forcefully kicking their right leg high into the air in front of them. Their arms swing back and then forward to drive the powerful motion and maintain balance.
After twisting their body, the person performs a series of powerful high kicks with their right leg. As the leg rises, the left arm swings down to meet either the foot or the knee.
A person performs a forceful high kick, extending their left leg straight forward. Their right leg remains planted for support as their arms are raised and bent in a dynamic pose.
The person performs a forceful high kick with their right leg, leaning their torso backward for balance while keeping their arms raised and spread wide throughout the movement.

The person performs a powerful high kick with their right leg, extending it straight up. Their arms swing in dynamic opposition for balance, with the right arm moving forward and the left arm moving back.

The person stands with their arms in front, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They push their arms up and kick their left leg forward, swinging the left arm back while keeping the right arm bent with the forearm in front of the torso.
The person simultaneously kicks and jabs both hands forward, then squats while swinging both arms sideways, repeating the motion to the rhythm.

Starting from a crouched position, the person explosively kicks their right leg straight up and forward. As the kick reaches its peak, the torso leans back and the arms are thrown out to the sides for balance and emphasis.

While balancing on the right leg, a person forcefully kicks their left leg forward several times, simultaneously rotating both hands inward and outward to the rhythm before finishing with a forward bow.

A person performs a powerful high kick, forcefully extending their right leg straight up in front of them. As they kick, their torso leans back and their arms swing dynamically to maintain balance and add emphasis to the movement.

A person performs a powerful high kick, extending their left leg straight up into the air. As the kick reaches its peak, they lean their torso back to maintain balance, keeping their arms bent and raised.
The performer delivers a forceful high kick with their left leg to the side. As the leg extends, the torso leans back and to the right in a counter-motion, while the arms swing outward for balance.

A person performs an energetic high kick, swinging their right leg high into the air while leaning their torso back. Their arms are thrown out to the sides for balance during this forceful, krump-style movement.

The individual executes a powerful high kick, thrusting their left leg straight up and forward. The torso leans significantly backward to counterbalance the motion while the arms swing outward for stability.
arm swings slow: A person stands with knees bent, performing slow and powerful arm swings in a loop. They alternate arms while swaying their hips from side to side and shuffling backward.
A figure performs a slow krump arm swing, moving the right arm down and back while the left arm swings down and forward. The torso twists slightly with the arm movements while the knees remain bent.
The person stands in a wide stance and rotates 180 degrees, moving their left foot first while their arms swing very slowly.
Standing still, the person quickly brings their left hand across their chest to their right shoulder and immediately returns it to their side.
The performer slowly sweeps their left arm downward across their body in a wide, deliberate arc.
The person rotates and sways their forearms in various directions.

The person starts in a low, wide squat with arms extended to the sides, then twists their torso to the right. As they twist, they take a large step backward with their right leg while keeping their body low to the ground, swinging their arms powerfully as they move.
Standing with knees bent, a person swings their arms straight up, then twists to the side while pulling their elbows back.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms straight at their sides. They lean their torso back and to the side, shaking their arms from the shoulders, before returning to the starting position and straightening their legs.
The person starts in a low, athletic stance with bent knees. They perform a slow, powerful arm swing, bringing the right arm forward and down across the body while the left arm swings up and back.

A person stands with their right knee bent forward and the left leg back, extending their left arm forward at shoulder height while keeping the right arm at their side. They twist their torso counterclockwise and swing the left arm to their side, bringing the right arm out to shoulder height with the elbow bent and forearm pointing forward as they bring their knees together in a bent position. They then adjust the left arm to extend out to the side with the elbow bent and forearm forward, kicking their right foot up to touch their left hand. After placing the foot back down, they bend forward slightly with their arms straight, then straighten up and reach their arms upward with the elbows bent while keeping their knees flexed.
With straight arms extended outward, the person slowly shifts their weight to the right leg by bending the knee. They then suddenly pull both arms inward and swing them back out again.

A person stands with their feet wide and arms extended straight out to the sides. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise and bend their elbows to point their forearms forward. They then punch forward and rotate 90 degrees clockwise to face the front again, bringing their arms to their sides.

A person stands on their left leg with their arms raised and right leg extended. They punch their arms upward one after the other and kick their right foot out before stepping it behind the left leg to shift their weight.

Starting from a low, athletic stance with bent knees, the individual performs slow, powerful krump arm swings. They move their arms in large, alternating up-and-down motions, engaging their torso and leaning into the movement.
The person brings both arms back and then points downward.
A person performs a strong throwing motion with their left arm while executing a large side jump. During the movement, the left leg swings forcefully backward as the torso bends fully forward.
Starting from a right lunge, the person turns left while sweeping their arms wide and high in a slow arc, finishing by lifting the right knee to the chest while balancing on the left leg.

A person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They slide to the left, raising their bent left arm above their head as they lean their torso to the right and kick their right leg across to the left with a bent knee. They then lean forward and to the right, kicking their right leg out and pointing their left arm in that direction. Finally, they straighten up, extending their arms to the sides with the elbows bent and the forearms pointing forward.

Starting with knees bent and arms extended to the sides, the person rotates their torso to the side. They bend their right elbow to point upward while straightening their left arm down along their side.
A person in a wide, low stance slowly swings their arms toward the center of their body, then sweeps them outward and upward in a broad, circular motion.

The person starts in a squatting stance with their right arm raised and left arm extended forward. They then swing their arms in an alternating motion, lowering the right arm as the left arm moves upward.

From a low, wide stance, a person performs a slow, powerful arm swing, raising their right arm straight overhead. Simultaneously, the left arm is lifted with a bent elbow as the body leans back slightly during the upward motion.

Standing in a wide stance, the person performs slow, powerful arm swings, extending one arm to the side while pulling the other back. The torso follows the movement, swaying from side to side with each swing.
In a wide, deep squat with their torso bent forward and both hands touching the ground, the person swings their arms outward while the rest of the body remains in a low, static pose.
Viewed from the side, a person in a low crouch with their torso bent forward performs a slow arm swing, extending one arm forward and the other back in an opposing motion.

A person stands with knees bent and arms held out to the sides with elbows bent. They vibrate their arms while turning their torso to one side, then clap their hands in front of their body and lean forward slightly.
The person slowly drags their left leg toward their body.
The person swings their arms wide while rotating their body and moving backward.
The person spins while pivoting on their left leg, swinging their right arm first and then their left arm sideways.

The performer executes a slow krump arm swing, sweeping the left arm down and across the body while the right arm moves up and back. The torso sways left with the movement, and the legs remain bent in a grounded stance.

The individual begins in a low, crouched stance with bent knees, shifting their weight onto the left leg positioned behind the right. They then turn their body by pivoting on the left leg and rotating the right, while swinging both arms around their head.
The person stands upright with their arms at their sides. They slowly raise their straight arms to shoulder height, extending them out to the sides.

A person starts with their left arm extended to the side and their right arm bent upward. They then slowly swing their arms, bringing the left arm down across the body while the right arm moves up and back, creating a slight twist in the torso.

A person stands with their arms at their sides and raises them straight to shoulder height. They then drop and shake their arms, pushing the left arm forward and pointing the forearm toward the left hip while raising the right arm straight up and out to the side.

A person performs a slow krump arm swing, bringing their right arm down and across the body while the left arm moves up and back. The torso twists significantly to the right as the right leg steps forward to support the movement.

A person stands in a low stance with bent knees, performing slow, deliberate arm swings. They swing their left arm upward, alternate with their right arm, and finish by raising both arms high above their head.

The person maintains a low, stable stance with bent knees while performing slow, deliberate arm swings. The arms move in opposition—one swinging forward and up while the other swings back—before both extend horizontally to the sides.

A person begins with their arms raised above their head and forearms pointing forward. They swing their arms to the right and rotate their left knee to bring the left leg behind the right. They then move their arms down in front of their body and hold them straight at their sides, standing in a wide stance with bent knees. Finally, they bend their torso forward and stretch their arms straight out to the sides.

The person stands upright with their elbows bent at their sides. They slowly swing both arms forward, then lean slightly forward while swinging the right arm up to the right and the left arm down to the left, keeping their elbows bent and shifting their lean slightly to the right.
A person stands and slowly swings their left arm up and out to the side. Simultaneously, they raise their right arm and swing it across their body as their torso leans slightly to the right.
A person in a low crouch performs a slow krump-style arm swing. The left arm swings forward and upward, while the right arm is held bent near the torso.
Starting from a low, athletic stance with knees bent, the person slowly swings their right arm upward and across their chest, twisting their torso slightly to the left to follow the movement.

The person stands with elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They step back with the right leg and bend the left knee, rotating 90 degrees counterclockwise while extending their arms out to the sides at shoulder height. They then jump their feet together and move to the right, leaning forward and bringing their hands together in front of the body with bent elbows. Finally, balancing on the right leg, they kick the left leg to the right and then stretch it out, straightening the knee while swinging their arms above their head.
A person stands with their arms at their sides and knees bent. They rotate their torso and clap their hands together in front of their body, then point them upward with their elbows bent.

A person stands with their arms bent at the elbows and raised to shoulder height, forearms pointing outward. They then move their arms above their head and swivel their torso from side to side with their knees bent.

A person stands slightly bent over with their arms held forward and elbows bent. They step their left leg to the side and their right leg back while pushing their right arm down and their left arm to the side. Finally, they pull their right leg forward, returning both arms to the front with bent elbows.
Standing with their arms bent at their sides, the person rotates counterclockwise while swinging their right arm, then pushes both arms straight up once they are facing the opposite direction.

A person performs slow, deliberate arm swings in opposite directions across their body. The right arm swings down and to the left from a raised position while the left arm swings up and to the right, accompanied by a slight twist in the upper torso.

A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held in front. They vibrate their forearms while rotating clockwise, tilting the left forearm slightly downward and the right forearm slightly upward.

The person starts with a slow arm swing, bringing the right arm across the body to the left and twisting the upper torso. The left arm follows in a smaller, complementary motion as the body leans forward slightly.
With the right elbow raised sideways, the person brings their right leg to the left while swinging their left elbow to the left side.
Standing with bent knees, the person performs slow, alternating arm swings in large, circular motions. The left arm swings downward and then high.
Starting with the right arm extended forward and the right leg raised in a forward-leaning stance, the person brings the right leg down to the side while swinging their arms.

The person steps back with their right leg and turns to the right, bringing their left leg around. Simultaneously, they lift their left knee high and raise both arms. As the left leg lands back to the side, their arms swing.

The individual performs a slow arm swing, bringing their left arm across the chest. The torso follows the movement with a slight twist, while the lower body remains relatively stationary with knees bent.
Starting from a low, forward-leaning stance with knees bent, the person performs a slow arm swing, sweeping the left arm down in front of the body while drawing the bent right arm backward.
Standing with knees bent and arms extended forward, the individual moves their arms in wide circles and takes a large step back with their right leg, rotating counterclockwise.
The person assumes a squatting stance with their knees turned inward, swinging their arms up and down in front of their chest.
The person turns, steps forward, and swings their arms, ending in a wide squat stance with both arms extended sideways.
From a squat stance, the person swings their left arm diagonally downward while turning their body to the right. Then, they move their right arm forward and raise both arms up.
A person performs slow, deliberate arm swings in a krumping style, swinging their left arm high across the body while the right arm moves down and back, then reversing the motion.
A person rolls their crossed arms in front of their chest while leaning back and twisting their upper body to the side.
The person moves in a circle, moving one arm at a time in one direction and then pointing the other arm in a different direction.

Starting from a low, wide stance with knees bent, the individual slowly swings their bent left arm downward and away from the chest. The right arm remains extended to the side, adjusting slightly during the movement.

The person performs a slow, dynamic movement, crouching while twisting their torso to the right as their right arm swings forward and up and their left arm swings back. They then rise and reverse the motion, twisting to the left as the left arm swings forward and the right arm swings back.

The person extends their arms from a raised, bent position into outward semi-circular arcs while rotating their torso to the left. Simultaneously, the left foot steps left and the right foot steps toward the front-right before retracting to align horizontally with the left foot, resulting in a lateral shift.

Starting from a low, staggered stance, the individual swings their left arm slowly and powerfully across the body to the right. The right arm remains bent and held high as the torso twists to follow the motion.

A person performs a slow, expressive arm swing characteristic of Krump, raising their arms to the sides with bent elbows. They maintain a low, dynamic stance with one knee lifted high and the other leg extended backward for balance.
A person turns slowly to the left with a large, counter-clockwise arm swing, their torso naturally twisting to follow the momentum.

A person stands with knees bent and arms extended to the left at shoulder height. They rotate 180 degrees counterclockwise, then step back while swinging their right arm forward and pulling their left arm back, keeping both elbows bent.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, then rotates 180 degrees on their right foot and steps back with their left. As they turn and step back, they hold their arms out to the sides and spin them. They then take another step back, pushing their arms straight forward before extending them out to the sides.
Standing in a low, wide stance with knees bent, the person performs a slow, powerful swing, raising their right arm back and up while moving their left arm forward.
In a low athletic stance, a person slowly sweeps their left arm in a wide arc from across their chest up and out to the side, while keeping their right arm bent and still near their torso.
Swing the arms back, then move backward while swinging the arms up and down.

Starting in a squat with arms crossed at the chest, the individual performs a slow, deliberate arm swing, rotating the left arm outward in an arc while turning the body to the left. They then step forward and swing the left arm up and down, finishing with the arm extended straight at shoulder level.
The person swings their left arm outward from their body, then turns their body to the left.
Standing in a wide, low stance with knees bent, a person slowly swings their arms inward from their sides, crossing them in front of their chest.
A person stands in a wide stance and swings their left arm down from a high position. Then, they turn to the left and wave both arms.
In a left-leg lunge stance, the person swings their left arm forward while rhythmically swaying their torso.
Very slowly rotate both arms from left to right over the top of the head.
Starting from a squat, the person slowly rotates their torso to the right, swinging their right arm in a deliberate motion while keeping it straight in front of the body.

The performer stands with their elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They step forward while swinging their arms in wide circles at their sides, then come to a stop, bending their knees and stretching their arms out to the sides. Next, they lean forward and push their arms forward with elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. Finally, they swing their arms down and rotate their torso 180 degrees counterclockwise, extending their arms out to the sides and then upward, finishing with the left knee bent forward and the right knee bent back.
The person takes a large step to the left and slowly drags their right leg toward their left foot. They then perform a slow spin while clapping their hands in front of and behind their body.

The person performs a slow krump arm swing, bringing their left arm across their body while simultaneously swinging their right arm up and out. With knees bent, their torso twists slightly to follow the momentum of the arms.
The person performs a slow overhead throw with their left arm while holding their right arm out to the side, followed by a large, slow, circular forward swing of the right arm.
The person steps forward and waves their arms randomly.
Starting in a low athletic stance, a person slowly swings their arms upward. Their left arm reaches high above the head while the right arm lifts, and the torso leans back slightly.

A person performs a slow arm swing, raising their right arm up and to the side while moving their left arm down and behind their body. The torso leans to the left as the right leg kicks slightly forward.
With both knees bent in an inward stance, the person slowly swings downward toward the center in a circular motion. The movement is smooth yet robotic due to its constant speed.

Standing in place, the individual performs slow, deliberate arm swings characteristic of krumping. The arms move in opposition: the left arm swings up and across the body while the right arm moves down and back, then the sides alternate.

A person performs slow, krump-style arm swings, alternating arms in powerful, wide arcs that sweep upward and across the body. They take a step forward, shifting their weight in coordination with the deliberate arm movements.

A person performs a slow krump arm swing, starting with the left arm extended to the side. They bend the left elbow, bringing the hand toward the shoulder, and then lower the elbow while making a circular motion with the hand.

The person performs a slow, powerful krump arm swing, pulling one arm high across the chest while the other swings down and back. They maintain a low, grounded stance with a bent torso and flexed knees throughout the movement.
The person swings their left arm to the right while rotating to the right.

The person performs a slow arm swing, lifting their right arm high with a bent elbow while extending their left arm down and back. They lean their torso forward and bend their left knee, shifting their weight.
The person turns their body by stepping with the right leg, swinging their arms into a pose with the left elbow raised forward and the right elbow pulled back.

The person swings their arms in a circle to point to the right, with their left arm extended straight and their right arm bent at the elbow above the head. They then turn their torso and swing their arms outward from the center while bending their knees.
With knees bent and arms extended sideways, the person pulls their arms in toward their body while widening their stance into a squat.

Starting in a squat, the person pivots to the left into a lunge on the left leg while raising both arms forward. They then drag their right leg toward the left and swing it backward with a bent knee while raising their right arm. Finally, they turn into a lunge on the right leg while slowly swinging their left arm forward.

Starting in a right-leg lunge with the left arm extended forward, the person lowers both arms while looking down. They then slowly pull both elbows back while shifting their weight backward. Suddenly, they step their left leg forward while lowering both arms. From a standing position, they slowly swing their arms forward and then downward.
The person lowers their left arm and swings it in a slow, wide sideways arc, leading the body into a turn to the left.
The person stands with knees bent and arms extended forward, hands joined. They swing their arms up and to the side while keeping their hands together and maintaining their leg and torso posture.
A person dances in place, turning their body and stepping to the rhythm. They perform a sharp jab with their right elbow, kick their right leg back, and lift their left knee.

A person performs a slow, powerful arm swing characteristic of krump. The left arm sweeps down across the body while the right arm simultaneously swings upward and outward, with the torso twisting slightly to follow the movement.

The person stands upright with their arms raised, then pivots 180 degrees on their left foot, punching their arms forward while sweeping their right foot in a wide semicircle to land next to the left foot.
The person steps forward while slowly waving their arms.
In a squatting stance, the person slowly swings their arms in the same direction in a semi-circular motion.
The individual stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm raised and bent at the elbow. They then rotate clockwise on their left foot while shaking their left forearm up and down.
A person performs a slow krump arm swing, extending both arms to the sides. They swing one arm upward and outward while lowering the other, accompanied by a slight twist of the torso.
A person takes a slow step forward with their left leg while pulling their left elbow back and swinging their left arm down. Afterward, they stand still and swing their arms in rhythm.

With the right arm bent and the hand pointing up, and the left arm extended straight forward, the individual rotates their body to the right. This rotation swings both arms from the front to the side while they maintain their relative positions.
The individual performs a slow krump arm swing, lifting their left arm high with a bent elbow while keeping the right arm down. The movement is supported by a grounded stance with bent knees.

Balancing on the left leg, the person swings their right leg far back while leaning their torso forward. They then quickly swing the right leg up and forward, clapping their hands in front of their chest as they straighten their body.
The person lowers their arms forward while rotating 180 degrees. After completing the turn, they raise both arms into the air.

The person stands with their right arm extended high and their left arm across their chest, with both legs bent. They then slowly swing their right arm down and forward while twisting their torso slightly to the left.
A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent at the elbow, with the forearm held in front of them. They bend forward and rotate their torso counterclockwise.
The person turns to the left while swinging both arms downward. Then, they kick their left leg sideways while swinging their left hand up by bending the bicep.

A person stands with their arms bent at the elbows by their sides. They step to the side and extend their arms straight out at shoulder height, then walk backward with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their body.
A person stands with a stable lower body, swinging their bent arms from the sides to the front of the chest while keeping their forearms parallel.
Both arms move slowly from the upper right to the lower left.
The person swings both arms from the inside outward and then raises them sideways while turning their body to the left.
The person moves sideways while swinging their arms.

A person slowly swings their left arm down and across their body as their right arm swings up and back. Their torso twists and leans to the left in coordination with the arm movements, maintaining a stable, low stance.

A person stands with their arms in front, elbows slightly bent. They turn slightly clockwise while extending their arms out to the sides, then step back, returning to their original orientation and bringing their hands together.
The person is swinging their left arm and left foot.

Starting bent over with the right leg forward, the left hand touching the ground, and the right arm pointing up, the person rises and performs a 360-degree counterclockwise turn on the right leg, keeping their arms bent and forearms positioned in front of their torso.
The person moves forward while swinging their arms.
The person steps back and rotates while swinging both arms independently at a moderate pace.

The person stands with their feet wide apart and arms at their sides. They then slide to the side while shaking their shoulders, bending their elbows and bringing their forearms in front of their body.
Starting from a low, wide stance with bent knees, the person performs slow, alternating arm swings. One arm moves forward and up while the other swings back and down in a reciprocal motion.
Starting in a squat, the person swings their right arm out to the side by extending the forearm, then quickly jabs their left arm across their body to the right.
A person performs a slow arm swing, bringing their left arm down across the body while raising their right arm up and out. The upper body leans to the right, following the momentum of the swing.

A person performs a slow clockwise spin, starting with a backward semi-circular turn. They complete the rotation by stepping back with their right leg while simultaneously sweeping their right arm in a wide horizontal arc.

Starting from a right-leg lunge, the person sweeps their right leg back and across the left while overlapping their hands in front of their hips.

Starting in a wide stance, the person sharply twists their torso 90 degrees to the right, letting their left arm swing forward with the momentum. They then lean their upper body back and take a step backward with their right leg.

The figure performs a slow, powerful Krump arm swing, sweeping the right arm down and across the body while the left arm swings up and back. The torso twists and leans forward to follow the momentum, supported by a wide, grounded stance with bent legs.
The person stands in a wide, low stance with their arms bent and raised. They then perform a slow arm swing, bringing the left arm down and out to the side while swinging the right arm upward.
The person assumes a low, wide stance, leaning forward at the waist. They slowly and deliberately swing their left arm down across the front of their body while their right arm swings back.

The person stands with their arms in front of their body, the left arm pointing slightly up and the right arm slightly down. They rotate counterclockwise, swinging the left arm above their head with the forearm pointing outward while keeping the right arm at their side. Rotating clockwise, they straighten the left arm and swing it down, simultaneously bending the right elbow to hold the arm at shoulder height with the forearm pointed forward. They lean forward and bend the right knee, kicking the foot up to touch the left hand. Finally, they step back and stretch the left leg behind them, standing straight with both arms extended forward from the shoulders.
The person swings their shoulders, letting their arms sway back and forth in a relaxed manner while taking small, alternating steps.
Standing in a wide stance, the person performs a slow arm swing. Starting with arms outstretched, the right arm swings down and across the chest while the left arm simultaneously swings downward.

Starting from a squat stance, the person takes a large step with the right leg. They then bring the left leg to cross the right while swinging the right arm outward by extending the forearm. Throughout the movement, a hunched upper body and squatting posture are maintained.

Starting with the left arm lowered and bent, the person slowly swings it downward and across the body to the right. Simultaneously, the torso twists to the left as the right arm moves slightly backward.
The person slowly swings both of their arms back.
A person extends their arms to the sides while walking sideways.

The person turns their body by stepping to the side and lowering into a hunched, squatting stance. They then perform slow, powerful arm swings, initiated by twisting their shoulders so that the forearms follow the momentum. Starting with the right arm bent upward at the elbow and the left arm at their side, they step back and drop the left arm. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, bringing both arms in front of the body with elbows bent, then swing them downward while turning slightly counterclockwise. Leaning back, they swing the right arm back in a circle, then bend forward slightly and swing both arms to their sides as they walk backward and straighten up. Shifting forward, they swing their arms at their sides, then hold the left arm horizontally in front of the body with the elbow bent. After rotating counterclockwise, they drop the arm and swing the right arm back in a circle while rotating clockwise. They step back and straighten, raising their arms to point upward. Next, they bend over and extend their arms from the shoulders, punching forward several times as they continue to lean down. They swing the right leg forward, raise the torso slightly, and then hold their arms out to the sides while rotating counterclockwise before dropping them. Finally, they shuffle backward while rotating from side to side, eventually standing up straight and swinging their arms slightly at their sides.

A person pulls back their left elbow, then their right, before stepping back with their right leg and swinging both arms upward. They then step forward with their right leg while swinging their arms sideways and moving their torso up and down.

The person stands bent forward with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their torso. They rotate their torso to the side and shake their left arm from the shoulder, extending it outward until straight before lowering it.

Finally, they shift their weight to the right leg into a lunge and slowly jab their left arm straight forward.

A person stands with their arms at their sides. They step forward, swinging their arms to the front and then pushing them out to the sides at shoulder level while walking. Finally, they bend over and push their arms downward, then straighten up and repeat the motion while rotating slightly clockwise and counterclockwise.

The person swings their left arm horizontally from right to left, finishing with a bent elbow, and then repeats the motion with their right arm. Simultaneously, they take slow, wobbly steps forward in a drunken manner.

The person stands with their arms in front, elbows bent and forearms held forward. They swing their elbows to the sides, extend their arms out, and then reach forward. They step back while pushing their arms straight behind them, then turn slightly and push their arms straight forward.

Starting from a low, wide stance, the individual performs slow and deliberate arm swings. The left arm sweeps across the chest and extends upward and outward, while the right arm swings across the body in a bent position.

A person stands in a squatting position with their left arm extended to the side. Their right arm slowly moves upward and to the side.

Slowly lowering the arms.

arm swings fast: A person rapidly swings their arms, flinging their left arm high overhead while the right arm is flung back, then reversing the motion. Throughout, they lean forward slightly with their knees bent.
Starting from a low squat, the person performs large, powerful arm swings up and down, moving rapidly and forcefully as if striking something in front of their chest.

A person stands bent over with their knees and elbows bent and their arms at their sides. They bend their knees and swing their arms up to their sides with their forearms pointing forward. Maintaining a low, crouched stance, the person executes powerful and rapid arm swings in opposing directions. The right arm swings down and across the body while the left swings up and back, causing a significant torso twist.

A person performs a series of fast, powerful arm swings, whipping them from side to side in wide arcs across the body. The torso twists and leans aggressively with the momentum of the swings, while the legs remain bent and grounded to maintain balance.

The person stands with a slight bend, keeping their arms at their sides with the elbows bent. They rock their torso back and forth while shaking their arms in position, then move their arms out to the sides.

Standing in a slight crouch, a person performs fast, aggressive arm swings. They raise their right arm high and swing it forcefully down across their body, while the left arm moves in an opposing, complementary motion.

A person stands with bent knees and their torso leaning slightly back, arms at their sides. The person leans forward into a lunge, kicking their right leg back while performing a powerful, rapid swing with their right arm. They then turn and repeat the throwing motion with the same arm.
Starting from a low, athletic stance with bent knees, the person swings both arms downward with power and speed. The arms cross in front of the body as the torso leans forward.

The person performs a rapid arm swing, whipping their right arm upward and across the body while the left arm moves down and back. The torso twists with the momentum of the arms as the lower body remains grounded in a stable, bent-knee stance.
The individual performs fast, powerful arm swings, moving them energetically up and down. They maintain a low, grounded stance with bent knees to support the explosive upper-body motion.
A person in a low, wide squat rapidly swings their arms across their body. The right arm swings down and to the left, while the left arm simultaneously moves up and out to the side.

A person performs an energetic krump dance, swinging their arms in powerful, rapid arcs both vertically and horizontally. Their torso twists and sways, following the momentum of the aggressive arm movements.
The person slides forward while swinging their arms up and down.
The person rapidly swings their left arm from left to right, followed by their right arm from left to right.
The person moves their right arm from pointing down to pointing up at a quick speed.

The person powerfully swings their arms from a wide position to cross at the chest while pivoting on a bent left leg and extending the right. They then swing their arms down as they jump, landing in a low, crouched stance.

Starting in a low, twisted squat with arms outstretched, the person performs a powerful, rapid arm swing, bringing both arms down and inward toward the body. They finish in a hunched pose with the torso bent forward and arms hanging straight down, resembling a gorilla.
A person stands with knees bent and arms extended straight in front of them. They then shuffle slightly to the side, swinging their left arm up in a wide arc while keeping it straight.

A person executes a krump arm swing, rapidly driving both arms upward and outward with bent elbows. They maintain a low, athletic stance, engaging the entire body in the powerful and energetic movement.

A person in a low, athletic stance performs a fast Krump arm swing. With their elbows bent, they move their arms rapidly and energetically in opposing vertical directions, swinging the left arm down while the right arm swings up.
The person swings their arms forward and to the side.
Starting from a low squat, the individual swings both arms down and forward in a sharp, parallel motion, then immediately raises them back up to repeat the percussive gesture.

The person starts in a low, wide stance with their torso twisted to the right and their left arm raised high with a bent elbow. They then execute a sharp, forceful downward jab, driving the left arm across the body toward the right knee.
From a low squat with a hunched torso, the person kicks their right leg while remaining in the squat. They then swing their left arm up and down several times with a bent elbow.
The person performs a rapid arm swing, driving the right arm high and forward while the left arm swings back. The torso bends forward and twists powerfully in sync with the movement of the arms.

The person steps to the left with their left leg while extending their right arm straight to the side and lifting their left elbow. They then pivot on their left leg, raising their right knee and swinging their left arm forward.

Maintaining a low, bent-knee stance, the person performs fast, wide arm swings in opposing vertical directions. The torso twists as the right arm swings high above the head while the left arm swings down.
From a low, athletic stance, a person performs powerful krump arm swings. The left arm swings down and back while the right arm simultaneously swings up and forward in a rapid, alternating motion.
A person aggressively swings their right arm in large, fast, alternating arcs in front of their body.

Starting from a low, grounded stance, the person performs a series of rapid, powerful arm swings. Their arms move in wide, alternating arcs, sweeping up, down, and across the body with high energy.
The person steps backward with their left leg while swinging it.

The person assumes a low, athletic stance with bent knees, aggressively swinging their arms in opposing vertical directions. The left arm sweeps down and across the body while the right arm swings upward.

The person performs an energetic Krump move, swinging their arms rapidly and widely in opposing vertical directions. They maintain a low stance with bent knees and a slight upper-body twist to facilitate the powerful arm motions.

Starting in a squat with elbows raised high, the person powerfully and quickly swings both arms down and forward while bending their torso.

Starting from a low, athletic stance, the performer executes a series of rapid and aggressive arm swings. The right arm is thrown forcefully overhead while the left arm swings down and back, creating a powerful alternating motion.
The person is swinging both arms very rapidly around their upper body.
A person in a low, wide stance executes a fast and powerful arm swing, slamming both fists down from above.
Starting from a low crouch, the person performs a rapid and powerful arm swing. The right arm sweeps down across the body as the left arm simultaneously swings up and back, twisting the torso.

The person performs fast, powerful arm swings from side to side with their elbows bent. The lower body remains in a stable, grounded stance with bent knees, while the torso twists to follow the arm movements.
With the right leg bent and the torso crouched, the person brings both hands toward the chest while leaning their body weight to the left.
The person slams both arms down and forward while stepping with their right foot.

A person stands with their arms extended to the sides, then rapidly swings both arms down and forward while bending at the elbows. They immediately swing their arms back up and outward in a fluid, powerful motion.
