They then step the right foot down and swing the left leg in a circular motion from the knee to step behind the right foot, swinging their arms back to point toward it while arching their torso slightly backward.

Starting in a squat, the person rapidly rolls their arms in front of their chest while lifting their left leg, culminating in a forward throw of the right arm. They then step backward while raising their right arm straight up. Finally, they powerfully swing their left arm forward in a throwing motion while lifting their right knee.

A person performs rapid, alternating arm swings characteristic of krumping. The right arm swings forward and down while the left arm simultaneously swings back and up, creating a twisting motion in the torso.
A person stands with their knees turned out and arms extended to their sides. They swing both arms forward and then step their right foot back, bringing their arms to their sides with bent elbows.

The dancer starts in a bent-over position with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent. They then straighten and rotate their torso to the right, swinging their arms upward and backward before bringing them forward and swinging the left arm horizontally at the elbow.

A person stands with their arms bent at their sides. They step forward with their right foot while straightening and pushing their arms forward, then jump back and turn to the side. As they jump, they move their arms out to the sides at shoulder height with the elbows bent and forearms pointing straight up.

A person stands and performs rapid, alternating arm swings in an up-and-down motion. The right arm swings down and back while the left arm moves up and forward, with both arms quickly switching positions.

Standing in a wide stance, a person performs rapid, forceful arm swings in opposite directions. The left arm swings down and across the body while the right arm swings up and out to the side, causing the torso to twist slightly.

A person executes a powerful, rapid arm swing, thrusting their right arm high and forward while the left swings low and back. The torso twists in coordination with the movement as the legs remain bent in a stable, low stance.

A person performs a powerful, rapid arm swing, thrusting their right arm up and forward while swinging their left arm down and back. Maintaining a low, dynamic stance, they twist slightly and shift their weight forward onto the right leg.
A person executes a powerful, rapid arm swing, throwing both arms high and out to the sides. The upper body twists to drive the movement while the legs maintain a wide, stable stance.

The person stands with their arms in front and elbows bent, forearms pointing down and to the sides. They rapidly rotate their forearms and lean back, shifting their arms upward so the elbows are bent with the forearms pointing forward. Turning 90 degrees clockwise, they point their arms forward while stepping back. They then rapidly move their arms up and down while kicking their left leg up and down.
From a low crouch, the person rapidly swings their arms, sweeping the left arm down and across the body as the right arm simultaneously swings upward.

From a high position, the person performs a fast and powerful downward swing with their right arm. The torso twists sharply to the right in sync with the movement, while the body remains in a low, dynamic stance.
The person leans deeply to the left, bending their left leg and lifting the right leg off the ground. Simultaneously, both arms perform a dynamic and continuous circular swing in front of the body.
A person stands with their knees wide and arms at their sides. They spin clockwise while bending their arms, bringing their forearms in front of their body.
A person performs a quick and powerful arm swing, moving their raised right arm up and down in an arc along the side of their body.

The dancer executes a powerful Krump arm swing, sweeping the right arm down and across the torso while the left arm simultaneously swings up and back. This rapid, opposing motion creates a dynamic twist through the upper body while the lower body remains relatively stable.

Starting from a low, athletic stance, the individual performs a rapid and powerful arm swing across the body. The arms move in opposition, with one swinging down toward the waist while the other swings up toward the head.
The person stands upright with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They then rotate their arms while turning to the side and walking slightly backward.

Standing on their left leg with the right arm extended to the side, the individual executes a sharp turn to the right. They lead the motion by twisting their torso and bringing the bent right knee across their body while raising both arms high.

A person stands in a low, wide stance with knees bent and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They then turn clockwise, stepping back with their right leg and swinging their arms in the direction of the movement.

A person maintains a low, wide-legged stance while performing large, sweeping arm swings. Their body twists, steps, or jumps to accommodate the powerful movements, which swing in sync with the musical rhythm.

Maintaining a wide, low stance, the person extends both arms from a raised, bent position. With their feet planted, they rotate their torso to the left as both hands sweep outward in semi-circular arcs.
A person drops into a deep, one-legged squat on their left leg while keeping the right leg extended. As they lower themselves, their arms swing for balance, and their left hand touches the ground.

A person stands slightly bent over with their arms extended to their sides. They lift their right leg up and back down, then turn their body counterclockwise, swinging the left arm down while raising the right arm and shaking the forearm.
The person stands on their left leg with the right knee raised, then rotates their elbows above their head with their hands pointing forward.

A person performs a dynamic Krump dance move, swinging their arms forcefully and rapidly up, down, and side-to-side. They maintain a low, wide stance with bent knees and a slight forward lean to add power to the energetic arm movements.

A person stands with their arms out to their sides and elbows bent. They lean forward and push their arms straight ahead, then rotate slightly counterclockwise and push their arms out before bending their elbows to point their forearms toward their hips. They then extend their arms out and pull them back in by bending the elbows while moving their body backward. Next, they lean back and to the left, stretching the left arm down and the right arm up, then switch to leaning back and to the right, stretching the right arm down and the left arm up. Finally, they return to an upright position with their arms extended straight in front and pointing downward.

A person is bent over with their right hand touching the ground and their left arm held at shoulder height with a bent elbow. They straighten their torso and swing their arms up and down from the shoulders before finally straightening their legs.

A person performs a Krump move, swinging their arms rapidly and energetically. The left arm swings high across the body while the right arm drives downward and behind the back in a powerful, coordinated motion.
A person stands with their arms at their sides, raises them to punch forward, and then lowers them back down.
The figure lifts the right knee and kicks the leg forward to take a step, shifting weight onto it. The arms are bent and held out to the sides for balance throughout the movement.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms extended to the sides. They draw their arms and right knee inward, then step the right leg out and swing their arms in circles from left to right while stepping the left leg out. Leaning forward slightly, they move their forearms in quick motions, reaching their arms down before bending their elbows and pulling them back toward the body, maintaining the slight forward bend.
Step back and lift the right foot behind you, followed by a small hop and circular arm waves.
With both arms forming a rectangular shape, the person swings them right and left while twisting their knees.

A person performs a rapid, forceful arm swing characteristic of krump, driving their right arm down and across the body. The upper body twists and leans forward to power the energetic swing, while the left arm moves in opposition.

The performer starts with their arms at their sides. They rotate 270 degrees clockwise while throwing their arms above their head with bent elbows and forearms pointing forward, vibrating them from left to right. Finally, they slide to the side and lean slightly left, holding their arms in front of their body.

A person performs a rapid and forceful arm swing, with the left arm swinging down and across the body while the right arm swings down and to the side. This movement is driven by a powerful torso twist to the right, with the lower body remaining relatively stable.
A person stands with knees bent, left leg forward and right leg back, and rotates 180 degrees with their arms raised and bent at the elbows while shaking their forearms.

A person performs a powerful, wide arm swing, extending their left arm up and back while the right arm moves down. They maintain a low, wide lunge with the torso leaned forward, adding force to the motion.
With knees bent, the person rapidly swings their arms up and wide before crossing them forcefully in front of their chest. The torso leans and twists in sync with the energetic arm movements.
From a standing position, the person jabs their left arm diagonally.
A person stands with their knees bent inward, performing fast and powerful arm swings that trace large circles in front of their body.

Maintaining a low, athletic stance with bent knees, the person executes fast and powerful arm swings. With elbows bent, the arms swing forcefully in an alternating pattern, moving up, down, and across the body.

The person performs a rapid, forceful arm swing characteristic of krump. The left arm is raised high and out to the side before being swung down and across the body, as the torso twists and the legs bend to absorb the momentum.

Starting from a T-pose, the person jumps in place and turns their body. They then open their chest by swinging their right arm and left elbow sideways. Finally, the person spins while swinging their right arm and then their left in a large circle.
A person stands upright with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms pointing up. They move their hands together and then apart.
A person performs a krump dance, swinging their arms rapidly and forcefully up, down, and from side to side. They maintain a low stance, their torso twisting in sync with the energetic arm movements.

The person turns and then swings their hands side-to-side in front of their body while leaning their torso slightly forward and standing on their left leg. As they swing, their elbows are slightly bent and flared outward.

Starting from a low, athletic stance, the individual executes a powerful arm swing. The left arm sweeps up and forward across the body while the right arm swings down and back, twisting the torso to the right.
The person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They step back while swinging their right arm up and to the side, straightening it as they rotate their torso 90 degrees clockwise.

The person stands on their left foot with their right leg bent behind them and their arms extended to the right. They shake both arms and their right leg before leaning forward and pulling their bent arms back to their sides.

The individual stands in a low, wide-legged stance, leaning forward with a bent torso. They perform a rapid, powerful arm swing, raising the left arm high and bent near the head while the right arm is positioned lower across the body.

The person performs a powerful, fast Krump arm swing, aggressively whipping their right arm down and across their body. Simultaneously, the left arm swings up and out while the torso twists and leans to support the dynamic motion.
The person turns right while waving their hands up and down.
The person bends their knees and rolls both forearms in a circle, then walks to the right.
The person swings their arms in and out and then forward and backward, while stepping backward and rotating their body.

A person performs a fast and powerful Krump arm swing, driving the left arm from a high position down and across the body. The torso follows the swing with a twist and a slight crouch, adding to the movement's intensity.
A person turns to the right by swinging their right leg while simultaneously swinging their left arm with a bent elbow. They then sweep both arms outward in a large, circular motion.
From a low, wide stance, a person executes a fast, powerful arm swing, moving the left arm down and across the body while the right arm simultaneously swings up and back.
A person rapidly swings their right arm down and across their body while their left arm swings upward and their torso twists slightly to the left.
Starting from a squat, the person rotates their left shoulder inward toward the center. They then perform a fast, percussive arm swing, moving both hands up and down together in front of their body.

The person performs powerful alternating arm swings, throwing the right arm forward and up while the left arm swings back. The body remains in a dynamic, forward-leaning lunge throughout the movement.

A person performs a krump dance move, swinging their right arm high and back while their left arm moves low in opposition. Their body is crouched with bent knees to power the rapid and forceful arm swings.

A person stands bent over with the left leg forward and the right leg back, arms at their sides. They swing the right leg forward and their arms to the right, bending at the elbows, then bring the left leg straight back while pointing their arms to the side. They rotate 180 degrees counterclockwise, swinging the left arm back while pointing the right forward, before finally standing with their arms at their sides.
A person swings both hands down to the left while kicking their right leg back, then jumps and extends their arms and legs into a star position.

Starting in a crouched stance, a person performs a quick, forceful swing, driving their right arm down and whipping it back up. The torso twists and leans to accommodate the powerful movement, while the left arm provides a counterbalance.

The figure performs a powerful arm swing, sweeping the left arm down and across the torso as the right arm moves upward and back. The upper body twists slightly to the right, following the momentum of the arms.

The person stands with their elbows bent at their sides, the left forearm pointing up and the right pointing down. They shake their forearms and swivel their knees together before clapping their hands and sliding slightly to the side.

The person stands leaning slightly forward, with their right arm at their side and their left arm extended to the side, elbow bent and forearm pointing downward. They step back, stretching the left arm forward while pulling the right arm up, bending the elbow so the forearm points forward. They lean back, swinging their arms to the front, bending and then straightening them. Finally, they step forward with their arms held straight, then lift their right knee while pointing their right arm upward and their left arm out to the side, elbow bent and forearm pointing forward.

Starting from a low, bent-over stance with arms extended forward, the person explosively swings both arms down and back while raising their torso. The right arm follows a wider arc, finishing higher behind the back than the left.
A person performs rapid, forceful arm and hand swings in a continuous rolling motion with elbows bent and raised wide, while simultaneously squatting and turning their body.
The person dynamically and rhythmically swings their arms after kicking their left leg forward in a hip-hop dance motion.
Swing arms around while walking to the right with cross-steps.

The figure performs a powerful and rapid right-arm swing, sweeping the arm high across the chest in a large arc. The torso twists aggressively with the movement as the left arm provides a smaller, counterbalancing swing.
The person performs fast, short swings with their left arm by flexing the bicep while the elbow is raised outward, mimicking a punch to the head from the left side.
After stepping back with the left leg, the person quickly swings their hands up and down while keeping both elbows raised wide.

The person stands with their arms at shoulder height and elbows slightly bent. They walk forward while turning slightly clockwise, holding their forearms in front and shaking their left arm. They then stop, push both arms forward, and straighten them at their sides.
The person bows forward while rolling their right hand in front of their body.

The dancer performs a powerful krump movement, rapidly swinging their arms vertically and side-to-side. Their entire body is engaged, with bent knees and a twisting torso that adds force to the energetic arm motions.

A person rapidly swings both arms in large, asynchronous circular motions in front of their body. While swinging, the person moves sideways and continues to roll their arms even after they have stopped moving.
The person swings their forearms in a circular motion while stepping in place by dragging their feet backward, then turns their body.
The person moves from side to side while swinging their forearms in a circular motion.
The person lashes both arms up and down while leaning forward and lifting their left leg.
The person swings their arms in backward circles and then steps forward.

A person stands on their left foot with their arms at their sides and their right knee bent, foot pointed back. They pivot 180 degrees on their left foot while swinging their right arm in a circle, finishing by stepping down onto their right foot.

A person stands bent over with their elbows bent and arms pointing downward. They shake their arms out and back in, crossing their forearms in front of their torso while kicking their left leg behind them. After putting the leg down, they extend their arms to the sides and rotate counterclockwise while hopping backward, pulsing their forearms in and out. They then begin to straighten up and clap their hands in front of their body. Continuing the counterclockwise rotation while moving backward, they shake their forearms up and down as they fully straighten, eventually bringing their arms in and lowering the left arm to their side while pointing the right arm upward and then forward.
The person extends their arms out to the sides and swings them in a circle.
The person drags their right leg to the left, then performs rapid arm swings, moving both hands back and forth above their head.
A person performs rapid, aggressive arm swings, moving them up, down, and across the chest. Staying low to the ground with bent knees, their torso twists and leans with the momentum of the arms.
While stepping onto the right leg, the person forcefully pumps both arms up and down by bending and extending their forearms.
The individual takes a low, wide stance with their torso leaning forward. They execute a fast, powerful arm swing, raising one arm high while swinging the other down and back in opposition.
The person swings their left arm around and then swings their right arm.

A person stands in a low athletic stance, powerfully swinging their arms in large, opposing motions. One arm sweeps down across the body as the other swings up and back, with the torso twisting to follow the movement.
In a squatting stance, the person rapidly swings their hands and elbows up and down while keeping their elbows raised and extended to the sides.
The person quickly moves both fists down and back up.

A person performs a rapid krump arm swing, raising their left arm high before swinging it down forcefully across their body. Their torso twists sharply with the movement as the right arm swings back for balance and the legs remain bent in a wide stance.
The person is shaking their left forearm very quickly.
From a low stance, a person performs a rapid, forceful arm swing, whipping their left arm down and across their body while their right arm swings up and out to the side as their torso twists sharply.
A person runs in a circle, shaking their forearms at their sides with their elbows bent and arms pointed outward.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and lower their arms to their sides, keeping their elbows bent and forearms pointing toward their hips. Then, they rotate 270 degrees counterclockwise on their right foot and point their arms upward with the elbows bent.
From a low, wide stance with bent knees, the person executes powerful, rapid arm swings. Their arms move in large, alternating arcs, swinging forcefully up and down.
The person takes a low, wide lunge with their torso leaning forward, then rapidly and forcefully swings both straight arms out to the sides.

While in a low squat, the person extends their left arm forward and holds it at the elbow with their right hand. Keeping this arm position locked, they rapidly swing their upper body from side to side and then slightly up and down.
The person steps forward with their right leg while rotating their forearms in a circular motion in front of their body, concluding with both hands pointing to the right.
A person swings their arms around.
The person steps sideways to the left and then swings their right arm out to the side, initiating a krump arm swing movement.
The person swings their right leg forward past their left, then spins clockwise while sweeping their left arm in a large arc.
While in a left-leg lunge with elbows raised high, the person rapidly rotates their forearms in a circular motion. The torso remains stable, emphasizing the quick and isolated arm movement.

A person rapidly and powerfully swings their arms in an alternating up-and-down motion in front of their body. Their upper body twists and bounces in sync with the arm movements, while their legs remain in a wide, bent stance.
In a wide squat with their torso leaning toward the ground, the person rhythmically jabs their left arm by extending the forearm. Maintaining this stance, they swing their left arm in a large arc.

The person performs rapid, powerful arm swings, with each arm moving independently up, down, and across the body. The legs mirror this motion, swinging widely and stepping in coordination with the dynamic twisting of the upper body.
Starting from a low, compact crouch, the individual explosively flings both arms out to the sides. The body expands with the powerful movement, transitioning from a coiled position to an open stance.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms held in front of their body. They lift and shake their forearms, then perform a 180-degree clockwise pivot on their right foot, returning their arms to the starting position during the turn.

The individual assumes a crouched, forward-leaning stance, executing powerful and rapid arm swings. The arms move in opposition, with the left arm swinging down and forward while the right arm swings up and back, causing the torso to twist.

A person stands on their left foot with the right knee bent and the foot pointed back. Their arms are positioned in front of the body with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They lower the right foot and move their arms out to the sides at shoulder level, maintaining the bend in their elbows. Stepping forward, they swing their arms straight out to the sides and pull them back so the forearms point forward again. They then rotate 90 degrees clockwise, shake their left forearm, and finish by bending forward and straightening up with their arms held straight at their sides.

The person stands with their arms at shoulder height and elbows bent, positioning their forearms in front of their body. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise while pushing their arms out, then turn another 90 degrees, swinging the left arm out to the side while bringing the right forearm across their chest with the elbow bent.
A person steps forward and backward while quickly swinging their left arm back and forth over their head.
The person swings their arms back and forth while stepping at the same time.

A person performs an energetic Krump arm swing while keeping their legs relatively stationary. Their arms move in opposition, swinging vigorously up, down, and across the body as the torso twists with the momentum.

A person begins with their arms raised and bent, then aggressively swings them down and inward toward their body. Simultaneously, they rhythmically raise and tap one leg to the side before switching legs to repeat the step.
The person steps back and moves forward with their arms at their sides, swinging forward and backward.

The person takes a step forward with their left leg while quickly jabbing upward with their left hand. They then rhythmically step backward, swinging their right shoulder while keeping their left hand raised.
Balancing on the left leg, the person swings both arms in a wide semi-circle from above their head down to their sides, then abruptly raises their right elbow toward the center of their chest.
The performer stands with knees bent, executing rapid horizontal arm swings that extend to the sides and cross in front of the chest.
From a squatting position, the person dynamically swings their elbows up and down in a wavy motion.
Starting from a squat, the person swings their elbows up and down in a dynamic, wavy motion.

The individual stands with slightly bent knees, keeping their torso stable while executing rapid, wide arm swings. They quickly swing their left arm forward in a throwing motion while simultaneously raising their right knee.
Starting in a low, athletic stance, the dancer executes a fast and powerful arm swing across the body, with the right arm swinging up and back while the left simultaneously swings down and forward.

Starting from a low lunge with arms extended sideways, the person rapidly swings both arms toward their chest and then forcefully back out to the sides. Simultaneously, they step forward while maintaining a low stance with bent knees.

The performer stands with their arms in front. They step back, swinging their arms into a T-pose, and move their forearms up and down before straightening their arms out to the sides at shoulder height.
A person stands in a wide stance with bent knees, performing a rapid and powerful arm swing that thrusts to the side.
The person steps back and forth, rhythmically and forcefully swinging their arms from side to side.
The person moves to the left and swings their left arm to the left.

Starting from a low, athletic stance, the individual executes a rapid and forceful upper-body twist to the left. This movement is driven by a powerful arm swing, with the right arm arcing high across the body and the left arm sweeping low and back.

A person stands with their left arm at their side and their right arm bent at the elbow, forearm positioned in front of the torso. They then step forward with their left leg and rotate 90 degrees clockwise, extending their arms straight out to the sides while leaning back slightly.
Leaning forward in a low, wide stance, the person performs a powerful arm swing. Their right arm flings down and back while their left arm is raised high and bent near their head.

Starting from a left-leg lunge, the person rapidly swings their left forearm in a circle while keeping the elbow raised and bent. Simultaneously, they pivot on their feet, transitioning from the left lunge through a wide squat and into a right-leg lunge without taking any steps.

The person stands with their arms extended sideways at shoulder height. They push their arms forward while extending the left leg to the side, then rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise while swinging the left arm up and out. Finally, they step forward and bend slightly, bringing their arms in front of their torso with bent elbows and forearms held forward.
From a low, wide crouch leaning forward with bent knees, the individual executes a rapid and powerful arm swing, pulling the left arm down and back while driving the right arm forward and up.
The person stands in a slight squat with a hunched torso, alternating between rolling their forearms in front of their body and swinging them sideways.

A person stands with their arms extended at shoulder height, elbows bent so that their forearms point downward. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, pointing their arms to the right, before shifting back 45 degrees and pointing their forearms upward with their elbows still bent.

Standing in a low, wide stance with bent knees, the person performs a quick arm movement. With their elbows bent, the arms move in opposition: the right arm swings up and across the body while the left arm swings down.

The performer executes a powerful, rapid arm swing, raising their bent right arm high while positioning the left arm forward and low. They maintain a wide, low stance with a twisted torso, providing a strong base for the energetic movement.
From a squat stance, the person turns their body to the left. Keeping the right foot planted, they step forward with the left foot while quickly swinging both arms.
The dancer moves their left arm around rapidly.
Starting with the torso slightly bent forward and hands overlapped at the hips, the person quickly swings their arms backward and brings their left leg to meet their right.
The dancer moves around quickly with both arms raised toward the sky.

A person stands with their feet wide apart and their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms held in front of their torso. They step their legs together while extending their arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height, then lean forward slightly and point their arms toward the floor.
intro windup: From a standing position, the person turns their body to the right.
The person is slowly walking in a clockwise circle.
The person starts in a full squat with both hands on the ground. They slowly rise while maintaining a crouched position and a bent torso.
Starting in a squat with a bent torso, the person slowly extends their right leg forward and sideways. After planting the foot, they suddenly shake their body back and forth.

A figure plants their left foot forward to anchor themselves, tensing their core in a low, wide stance. They then perform a slow, controlled roll of their right arm across their chest from right to left.
The figure starts in a T-pose, then slowly lowers their arms to their sides. They then gently perform light popping movements with their left arm while squatting, preparing to catch the rhythm.
The figure stands upright with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent, swaying gently and occasionally bringing their hands together.
The movement begins with the person looking around.
Starting from a T-pose, the figure drops into a grounded stance with the left foot forward and knees bent, then performs a slow, controlled rolling motion with both arms across the chest.
The person remains in a T-pose.
Starting from a T-pose, the person lowers their arms and then leans slightly to the right with bent knees.
jab out: A person stands upright with their arms extended upward and to the sides, stepping backward while circling them. They then step back and to the side, lifting their left leg up and down while swinging their right arm, before stepping back again and raising both arms.
A person forcefully jabs both arms straight forward twice while lifting their right leg.
Starting from a low, wide squat, the person executes a powerful right jab toward the ground while keeping their left arm down. After the jab, they bring their right hand near their left elbow.
The person punches both arms out.
The person punches with their left arm twice while shaking their legs twice.
The person starts with both arms extended forward, parallel to the ground, and then punches toward the ground.
A person stands in a wide stance with their knees bent and their arms at their sides, bent at the elbows. They lean forward slightly and throw alternating forward punches.
The person punches forward with both arms.
A person starts in a bent-over position with their arms pointing down. They then stand up and wave their arms, first the right and then the left.
The person jabs their left arm out.

A person stands upright with their arms above their head, elbows bent so that their forearms point downward. They bend their knees to lower their torso while pushing their arms down and slightly across each other. Then, they thrust their right arm straight out to the side at shoulder height and drop their left arm to the side with a bent elbow, while simultaneously bending their right knee and pointing the lower leg backward.

A person stands with knees bent and arms at their sides, bent at the elbows. They bring their knees together while swinging their arms inward, then punch both arms forward and pull them back, keeping their elbows bent.

A person in a low crouch looks down, holding their right arm bent in front of their chest while forcefully jabbing their left arm straight down. Simultaneously, they perform alternating backward kicks with their left and right legs.
The person points both arms down.
In a wide squat with both arms raised and elbows bent, the person powerfully jabs downward while maintaining a crouched torso.
Starting in a squat, the person throws a rapid succession of alternating jabs to the front and sides.
The person stands with knees bent and arms extended to the sides. They move each arm back, to the side, and forward from the elbow before straightening both arms in front of their body.
From a low, athletic stance, the person explosively thrusts their arms forward and then pulls both elbows back.
A person quickly jabs their right arm forward and to the side, then holds it rigidly in the extended position.
The person jabs both arms out and then lowers them, finishing with both arms extended to the sides.

A person performs a krump jab, forcefully extending their right arm to the side and holding it in a straight, rigid position. The torso leans slightly to the left, and the left arm is bent with the hand held near the chest.
The person jabs their left arm and leg out.

With the right elbow raised high and the left arm extended forward, the person sways their upper body from side to side. The arms remain in a fixed position, moving only as the torso and shoulders shift.

A person stands slightly bent over with their arms at their sides, their knees and elbows slightly bent. They rotate their torso toward the left leg and straighten their arms, pulling the left arm upward. They then rotate their torso to the side and hold their arms forward with the elbows bent, before straightening them to point upward. Next, they return their torso to its original position and extend the left arm straight out to the side while keeping the right arm in place. Finally, they rotate their torso 180 degrees clockwise, extending both arms out to the sides.
The person randomly throws out a fist and pulls it back, starting with the right fist followed by a left jab.
The person jabs their left fist out before rotating their shoulder.
The person thrusts both arms up and back.

The person performs powerful, rapid alternating jabs, extending one arm forward while pulling the other back. The torso crunches forward and down in sync with each punch, adding intensity to the movement.
The person performs a downward jab while bowing their torso forward.
The person rapidly jabs outward with their hands and elbows, kicking their right foot backward in between.
The person stomps their right leg from a wide stance while swinging their arms in a circular motion.
From a squatting position, the person performs quick, synchronized jabs with both hands, punching diagonally upward and outward.

A person stands with their right arm bent at shoulder height and their left arm pointing upward. While sliding to the side, they punch once with the right hand, once with the left, and three more times with the right, before finally extending the right arm straight out to the side.
From a low, wide stance, the person leans forward and aggressively jabs and swings their arms from the outside in. Because of the forward lean, the arms swing toward the ground.
The individual adopts a low, wide stance and powerfully extends their left arm straight out to the side in a rigid jab, keeping their right arm bent with the hand held near the face.
Starting with knees bent and the torso leaning back, the individual holds their arms up with elbows bent near the head and performs quick, alternating upward jabs.

A person stands upright with their arms at shoulder height, bent at the elbows with their forearms in front. They raise their hands together without moving their elbows, then swing their arms down in wide loops, straightening them to clap in front of their body.

A person begins in a low crouch and explosively jabs their left arm out to the side while their right arm is raised and bent over their head, with the hand pointing left. Immediately after, they mirror this upper body posture on the opposite side.
A person extends both arms, pointing upward and then pulling them back. They then point to the middle and finally downward.
Point the arms forward and up, then forward again multiple times.
The person takes a side step with their right leg. While landing on their left leg, they jab both arms out in front of their body.
The person jabs both arms forward, then swings them in a circular motion to their left.
The person swings both arms upward and forward while stepping back with their left leg.
The person thrusts both arms forward multiple times.
A person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They raise and lower their forearms, then move their legs outward.
The person jabs forward with their left and right arms while stepping back and turning.
The person thrusts their arms forward, one at a time.
The person sharply jabs their right arm forward, holding it rigidly straight out from the body. Their torso is bent forward, while the left arm remains bent and tucked in close to the side.

Starting from a wide squat, the person leaps to the right while throwing a forceful cross-body jab with their left arm. They then hop on their right leg and repeat the jab, this time with a more hunched torso.
The person jabs their left fist out and then pulls it back.
The person punches out with their left hand.

Standing on the left leg, the person swings their right leg backward while turning to the right and delivering several forward jabs with their left arm. They then perform a right leg kick and a left knee strike in a martial arts style.

The performer executes a powerful jab, thrusting the left arm forward and out while keeping it rigid. They maintain a low, athletic stance, leaning forward with the left knee raised and the right leg extended back for balance.
The dancer bounces up and down while slamming their arms down one by one.

The person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They step back with their left foot while extending their arms out to the sides at shoulder height, then step back to center and lower their arms with bent elbows. Next, they step forward with the right leg and lean slightly to the right, extending their arms to the sides, pulling them back, and pushing them out again. They straighten up and step forward with their elbows bent, then lean to the right and thrust the left arm up and to the left. They repeat this motion on the left side with the right arm, then thrust with the left arm once more. Finally, they straighten, thrust both arms out to the sides at shoulder height, and drop them with bent elbows slightly in front of the body.
A person stands in a wide stance with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They hop slightly to the side while alternating forward punches with both arms.

From a low, wide stance, the person jabs their left arm straight forward, holding it in a rigid, extended position. Their right arm remains bent and tucked against the chest as the body maintains its low posture.
The person steps back with their right leg and turns their body by untwisting their legs while extending both arms straight out to the sides. They then repeat the turn once more.
The dancer thrusts their right arm out.
buck: The figure’s upper body performs a forceful bucking motion, thrusting the chest forward and then pulling it back. The arms are held bent at the sides, while the legs maintain a stable, bent-knee stance.

The person stands with knees bent, the right arm at their side and the left arm bent at the elbow, forearm held horizontally in front of the body. They straighten slightly and swing the left arm straight up, then drop it to the side and swing the right arm out, punching it up and down, before extending both arms to the sides at shoulder height with the elbows bent. They straighten their arms and move them up and down, then kick the right foot out and step down. Straightening up, they point the right arm upward while keeping the left arm pointed to the side, elbow bent and forearm pointing forward.
A person circles both forearms while holding their left foot in the air.

Starting from a low crouch, the person forcefully thrusts their hips and chest forward in a bucking motion. Their arms are held with bent elbows, pulling back slightly to emphasize the forward push of the torso.
From a squatting position, the person forcefully thrusts their chest forward and then bends their torso downward while moving both arms straight up and down.
The person extends both arms and leans back.
From a squatting position, the person kicks their left leg backward while pushing their chest out, maintaining both arms extended straight to the sides throughout the movement.

A person performs a Krump buck, forcefully thrusting their chest and hips forward from a coiled stance with hands near their head. As the torso drives forward, the right arm extends while the left pulls back, and the legs transition into a lunge with the right leg forward.
The person pushes their body forward.

Starting from a low, crouched stance, the individual aggressively thrusts their chest and upper body forward before sharply pulling back. Their bent arms are held out to the sides, moving in sync with this powerful, rhythmic bucking motion.

The performer aggressively thrusts their chest forward and downward, bending deeply at the waist while maintaining a low, bent-knee stance. Their arms are held close to the upper body in a tense, powerful, and bent posture.

A person stands with bent knees and arms extended forward, then pulls their elbows back while keeping their forearms in front of their body. They lean further forward and extend their arms out to the sides, pointing downward, before kicking their right leg back at the knee and touching their left hand to their right foot.
Starting from a crouch, the upper body thrusts forward and upward in a forceful bucking motion, as the arms simultaneously extend outward and upward from the chest to accentuate the movement.
In a squatting stance, the person rotates their upper body in a large circle.

Starting from a low, athletic stance with knees bent, the person forcefully thrusts their chest and upper body forward and then pulls back. Their bent arms move in sync with the sharp, powerful motions of the torso.

The individual stands with their upper body hunched forward, forcefully thrusting their chest forward and back in a powerful, repetitive motion. Their arms are raised and bent, moving in sync with the aggressive torso movements.
The person stomps their right foot while punching both hands downward and leaning their torso forward.
The person stands upright with their arms at their sides, then swings their arms upward while bending their elbows to bring their forearms inward.

Standing on their left leg, the individual leans forward with their right leg raised and knee bent, while holding their arms at their sides with bent elbows. They then rotate their hips to bring their torso into an upright position without changing the configuration of their limbs.

The person explosively thrusts their chest forward and back in a powerful, repetitive motion. Their knees are bent to absorb the impact, while their arms swing back and forth in sync with the aggressive torso movement.

The person stands with a slight forward lean and their arms pointing backward. They swing their arms forward and up, then push them out to the sides at shoulder height while leaning back and bending their knees.

A person stands with their arms at shoulder height and elbows bent, forearms pointing downward. They rotate their torso forward and back while taking a large step forward and out with their left leg, bending the knee.

A person steps to the left, extending their right arm to the side and bending their left arm upward. They then lift their right leg forward while raising both arms with elbows up and hands forward, simultaneously shaking their hands and head back and forth.

Starting in an upright position with their arms bent at their sides, the individual swings their arms from the elbows. Simultaneously, they kick their left leg behind their right, bending it at the knee.

Starting from a low, wide squat, the person performs a krumping motion by forcefully pumping their arms up and down. Their upper body remains low and stable, emphasizing the powerful, rhythmic arm swings.

The person steps forward with their arms bent at their sides. They push their arms forward, then out to the sides, and forward again, swinging them from the elbows throughout the movement.
The person stands with their arms raised straight overhead, swings them down to shoulder level, and then throws their right arm back up.
Starting in a right lunge with arms extended to the sides, the person quickly jabs their left arm diagonally downward across the body.
Jab one arm out, followed by a cross punch with the other arm, while keeping both feet planted on the ground.
The person crosses both arms.
The person crosses both arms, moves to the side, and then jabs before crossing their arms again.

A person stands upright with their knees bent and arms at their sides. They cross their arms in front of their body, then swing them up to shoulder height at their sides, keeping the elbows bent and forearms pointing outward.

Starting from a wide squat, the person kicks their left foot back and stomps it back into the starting position. They then raise their elbows to head height and begin rapidly swaying their hips in a circular motion.
The person throws both arms forward and crosses them in front of the chest.

The individual stands upright with their right arm extended outward and upward and their left arm bent in front of their body. They then circle their forearms around each other in front of their torso.

wrist rotation: A person stands in a low, wide stance with bent knees and arms raised to chest level. They rotate their wrists in a fluid, continuous circular motion while keeping their upper arms relatively still.

The person assumes a low, wide stance with bent knees and arms extended to the sides, then fluidly rotates both wrists simultaneously, tracing small circles with their hands.

A person stands upright with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent. They raise their arms to shoulder height at their sides, making rapid circles, then bring them back in front and move their forearms in and out.

The person stands upright with their arms at their sides, except for the left arm, which is bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward. They drop the forearm and begin to shuffle forward, leaning slightly to the left and back, then rocking slightly forward and back. They lift their arms and push them forward, bending over slightly as they continue the forward shuffle. Next, they lean to the left and back while still moving forward. They then lean slightly right and back, raising their arms up and forward to move their forearms in front of their body. Finally, they drop their arms to their sides, bending forward and back, before stretching their arms out to the sides and back while shuffling forward, pushing them slightly forward as they move.

A person stands with their arms extended to their sides. They bring their arms in front of their body, bending the elbows to point the forearms upward. They then lower their elbows and bend their knees inward with their feet turned out, lifting the left arm up and to the side—leading with the elbow—while leaning the torso to the right. Rotating the torso clockwise, they bring their arms in front with the elbows bent and forearms held horizontally. They straighten up and step back, pointing the right forearm up while pushing the left forearm from left to right. After swinging the left arm back in a circle followed by the right, they stop and align the right arm in front of the body, bent at the elbow with the forearm horizontal. Finally, they swing the left arm up and down with the elbow bent and the forearm pointing forward.

The person stands with both arms bent and raised, holding their left hand near their head and their right hand extended forward. The primary motion consists of fluidly rotating both wrists and forearms back and forth.

Maintaining this position, they bend forward and shake their forearms slightly. Finally, they stand up, continue vibrating their now upward-pointing forearms, and step forward with the right leg.
In a slight squat, the person rhythmically rotates their right wrist while bouncing on their legs.

The person circles their left wrist at the side of their body while the rest of the body remains stationary.

Standing in a low, dynamic stance with arms bent in front of the chest, the person performs fluid, circular wrist rotations while keeping their forearms relatively stable.

Standing with the left leg forward and the right back, the individual extends their left arm out to the side at shoulder height and bends their right arm at the elbow so the forearm points left. A person rotates their left arm up and down.
Viewed from a front-right perspective, the person holds their arms up with elbows bent. The primary action is a continuous, simultaneous circular rotation of both wrists.
Standing with the left arm extended forward, the person rotates their left forearm in a circular motion.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent at the elbow, forearm pointing up. After stepping down, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise with elbows bent and the right forearm pointing up. They shake the left arm and bring both forearms in front of the torso, then step to the right while circling the forearms around each other. Finally, they move their arms in front of the body, pull them apart, and cross them again in front of the torso.
The person rotates their wrists around each other while holding both arms in front of their chest.
A person stands with their arms extended to the sides and elbows slightly bent. They then rotate both wrists simultaneously in a fluid, continuous, circular motion.

The performer stands slightly bent forward with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They then rotate their forearms and step their left foot forward and out before leaning slightly to the opposite side.
The person steps back with their left foot.

The person stands upright with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They vibrate their left foot while lowering their bent left arm toward the left knee, moving it up and down again. Finally, they shift their weight onto the left foot and lift the right foot slightly.

The person steps backward while swinging their right arm vertically, then stops to swing their right and left arms in sequence. To finish, they pivot on their right leg and rotate their left to execute a turn.

Starting in a wide, crouched stance, the person performs a sudden, powerful forward chest thrust. This bucking motion is accompanied by a sharp, aggressive swing of both arms forward and slightly to the sides.
The person takes a step to the right with their right leg and then shakes their entire body, from the hips through to the arms.
The person stands upright with arms at their sides, steps their right leg to the side, and bends over while extending their arms straight out to the sides. They then rotate their torso to the left.
After waving their shoulders, the person turns by dragging their left leg in a circular motion.
From a squatting stance, the person swings their arms and rotates both shoulders with elbows raised. They then kick their left leg backward while twisting their torso to the left.
A person performs a popping move, initiating a wave that travels from the right arm, across the chest, and through the left arm. The motion then flows down the torso, culminating in a sharp pop.

The figure stands with the right arm extended and the left arm bent at the elbow, holding the left forearm horizontally in front of the torso near the head. They rotate the body clockwise back to the original orientation, step the left foot in a circle to spin 360 degrees clockwise, and shift their knees from side to side while sliding to the left. Finally, they turn the torso 90 degrees counterclockwise and move forward while keeping the arms in the same position.

With hands held together in front of the chest and the torso bent forward, the person jumps sideways while kicking their right leg back. They then stand up straight while swinging both arms out to the sides.
