The figure stands with their arms extended sideways at shoulder height. They swing their forearms, bringing the left forearm across the body while pushing the right arm upward and to the right. Finally, they bring both arms in front with bent elbows while balancing on the left leg.
The person leans back while shaking both forearms.
The person stands bent forward with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent. They vibrate their shoulders before turning to the side and taking two large steps.
The person steps forward with their right leg while swinging their torso back and forth, ending in a right-leg lunge stance.
The person slides their left foot while raising both arms.
The person steps back while extending their arms.
The person stands up from a bent position.
A person raises both elbows.
The person keeps stabbing their right elbow upward.
The person performs a sudden, powerful chest drop, leaning their torso forward and down, followed immediately by a sharp, aggressive swing of the right arm across the body.
Starting from a crouched squat, the person slowly rotates to the left by swinging their left elbow backward before returning to the initial position. The entire movement is performed in slow motion.
A person walks forward in a drunken manner, starting with the right foot.
The person is drumming with both hands.
The person switches their stance from right foot forward and left foot back to left foot forward and right foot back.

Starting with the right knee bent and the left leg extended, the person twists their body from right to left, tapping both hands on the ground to the left. After standing up, they settle into a right lunge and lean their torso to the right.
jab in: Standing upright with their elbows bent in front of them, a person quickly circles their arms around each other.
A person performs a sharp krump jab, thrusting their arms forward and pulling them back.

A person stands in a wide, low stance and performs a forceful jab by thrusting both arms straight forward. They then immediately and sharply retract their arms toward their chest, engaging their entire upper body in the motion.
The person stands in a slight crouch with their elbows bent at their sides. They lean to the side, punching both arms downward in rapid succession.
Starting from a right lunge with the torso leaning back, the person raises both arms with bent elbows. They then lift their right leg while maintaining the backward lean for balance.

A person stands with knees bent and arms held in front with the elbows bent. They shift to the side while pulsing their knees and elbows in and out, then repeat the inward and outward movement before pointing their arms upward with the elbows still bent.
Starting in a low squat, the person forcefully jabs both hands down from overhead to chest level, then sharply pulls them toward the chest with their elbows flared out to the sides.
The person throws their left arm forward.
The person moves backward while pulling back both elbows.
The person jabs their right elbow backward while moving their left hand downward, then swings their elbows.
Starting with their arms at their sides, a person walks sideways while rotating, bringing their hands together in front of their body and then returning them to their original position.
The person steps back, waves their left arm, and then brings it back.

A performer stands with their arms pointing straight down in front of their body. They raise their arms up, move them out, and then bring them down and back in to cross in front of each other while simultaneously bending their knees and shifting slightly to the right.

A person stands with their elbows bent at their sides and forearms pointing forward. They step back, leaning their body backward and rapidly shaking their shoulders while straightening their arms to point downward and outward. They then rotate 90 degrees clockwise and step back, throwing their arms out to the sides. Turning 90 degrees counterclockwise, they step back again with their arms bent in front of them, finally bringing their hands together in front of their torso.
While in a wide squat, the person sharply swings their arms up and down in an alternating fashion in front of their body. This dynamic and rhythmic motion resembles the act of playing drums.
Thrust the left arm inward.
From a standing position, the person rotates their arms forward and raises them upward with bent elbows.
A person performs a Krump jab by thrusting both arms forward and downward. Simultaneously, they bring their left leg in from a wide stance toward their right leg and then step back with the left.

Beginning with a slight shoulder shake and hands near the pelvis, the person swings both arms forward and then jabs them back toward the chest. They conclude the movement by jabbing their left arm straight forward.

A person stands in a wide stance with their right arm bent at the elbow and the forearm held across the torso, while the left arm is extended upward and forward. They punch their left arm out twice, then bring it to their side and step their left foot in.
A person rhythmically sways their torso back and forth while swinging their arms out to the sides, stepping and leaping forward as they move.
chest hit: Starting in a wide squat, the person strikes their chest with both hands. They then twist their torso to the right, jabbing their left arm across the body while pulling the right arm back.
In a low, wide stance with bent knees, a person strikes their chest with one arm while extending the other straight out to the side for balance.

The individual stands in a wide, crouched stance with their left arm extended for balance. They then forcefully strike their chest with their right hand, causing the upper body to recoil sharply from the impact.
The person beats their chest with both fists and then throws their arms up and down.

A person stands upright with their arms at their sides and elbows slightly bent. They lean to the right while bending their left elbow, moving their forearm across the front of their body and then back out and in as they lean.

Starting in a low, wide stance, the person raises their left arm and swings it down forcefully to strike their chest. Upon impact, they lunge forward and to the right, extending their right arm for balance.
The person slams both fists down three times.
The person stands upright with their arms extended in front, then swings them back and forth at their sides. They then turn slightly to the side and push their arms forward.
Starting from a slight right lunge, the performer brings their arms inward from a wide, raised position to strike their chest, then lowers them while stepping back with their right leg.
The person slams down both fists.
With the right arm extended straight forward and the left elbow raised high, the person swings their left arm in a circular motion before waving their upper body.
A person stands in a wide, crouched stance, pulls back their right arm, and then thrusts their fist forward in a sharp, powerful motion to strike their chest.

A person stands in a low, wide stance with knees bent, leaning forward from the hips. They raise their right arm, bending it at the elbow to strike their chest, while extending their left arm out to the side.
The person stands in a wide stance, dragging their left leg slightly inward while repeatedly hitting their chest with both hands using upward swinging motions.
The person kicks their right leg back and forth, then jumps forward. Throughout the kicks, the arms move dynamically in a raised stance.
The person raises their bent left arm and forcefully strikes their chest while simultaneously leaning forward into a slight crouch and extending their right arm to the side.

The performer adopts a wide stance with their arms held forward, elbows bent, and forearms angled downward. They lean back and swing their forearms back and forth, then straighten to push both arms and the right leg forward. Stepping back with the right leg, they bend over and punch both arms forward, then swing both arms back while turning slightly clockwise as they continue to step back.
A person stands in a wide stance, striking their chest with a bent right arm while holding their bent left arm out to the side in a dynamic and powerful posture.
chest pop: The person pops their chest forward.
The person pops their chest out.
The person is shaking their torso from side to side in a popping style.
head roll: The performer performs a head roll while maintaining a low, wide krumping stance. Their arms are bent and held out to the sides for balance, with the right arm positioned slightly higher than the left.
The person stands upright with their arms held in front of them, vibrating their shoulders and arms.
The person performs a popping motion with their left arm while rolling their head in a circular motion.
In a right lunge stance with both arms extended straight sideways, the person swings their torso backward and then brings it forward again.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides. They shake their arms, torso, and head while kicking their right leg in and stepping it back out. They then point their forearms toward their hips and bend forward slightly before straightening back up.
A person stands with their arms bent in a tense posture and begins to roll their head in a circular motion, tilting it toward their right shoulder.
In a wide squat with bent knees, the person rolls their head.

With both arms bent and raised high, the individual performs a full circular head roll, tilting the head left, back, right, and forward. The torso and hips sway slightly left to counterbalance the motion.
A person stands in a crouched position with their arms bent and raised high, then rolls their head in a circular motion while the rest of their body remains relatively still.

From a low, wide stance with bent arms raised, the person performs a head roll by tilting their head back and rotating it in a circular motion. The torso and arms remain relatively stable, emphasizing the movement of the head and neck.

A person assumes a low, dynamic krump stance, leaning forward at the waist with their arms raised and elbows bent. They then execute a quick head roll, tilting to the right to initiate the circular motion.
The person performs a head roll, circling their head to the right and back. The torso is leaned slightly forward with arms raised and bent, while the lower body remains relatively stationary.
The person stands in a low, wide stance with their arms bent and extended outward. They then perform a circular head roll, tilting their head to the left, back, right, and forward.
From a standing position, the person rolls their head while lowering their right elbow. They then turn their body after swinging the right elbow forward to the chest.
The individual leans forward with the left arm raised and bent in a tense posture. They then execute a quick head roll, dropping the head to the right and circling it down and around to the left.
A person stands in a wide, bent-knee stance with their arms spread wide. They perform a circular head roll while leaning their torso forward.
A person walks in a clockwise circle while performing a head roll.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides and slightly vibrates their shoulders.
Rolling the head while leaning forward with both arms extended outward.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides, rotating their neck while swaying their upper torso slightly forward and back.

The individual performs a head roll, tilting their head to the left, then backward, and around to the right in a circular motion. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, while the rest of the body remains stationary.
A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They slightly sway their head, torso, and shoulders from side to side while keeping their legs stationary.

A person stands slightly bent over with their right foot in front of the left and arms extended to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They rotate their head and vibrate their arms as they bend further forward. Finally, they cross the right leg behind the left and step back while remaining bent over.
The dancer rolls their head in a circle with the right arm up and the left arm down.
elbow: Dropping into a lower stance, the person sharply thrusts both elbows outward as their chest pops forward, emphasizing the power and aggression of the movement.

The person steps to the right while placing their right hand on their left shoulder and swinging their right elbow back and forth. They then place their left hand on their right shoulder as well, swinging both elbows up and down.
The figure sharply thrusts the left elbow out to the side in a quick, popping motion and then moves it downward.

The individual stands upright with their arms at their sides and the left arm slightly bent at the elbow. They raise the left arm and bend it further at the elbow, vibrate it in place, and return it to the starting position before slightly lowering their head.

Starting from a crouch, the individual aggressively thrusts their elbows outward and upward in a sharp, quick motion. The torso twists as the arms extend forcefully, bringing the hands up toward the head.

The individual stands with their arms in front and elbows bent, the right forearm pointing upward and the left forearm pointing to the right beneath the right elbow. Maintaining this arm position, they move their arms up and down while leaning back, extending their right leg forward and sinking their hips.

A person stands upright with their arms at their sides and the left forearm pointing forward. They reach the left hand across the body toward the right knee while leaning forward, then swing the left arm outward and lift the right knee. While balancing on the left leg, they rotate slightly clockwise, keeping the right arm extended to the side with the forearm pointing forward.
A person steps forward with their left leg in front and their right leg back, then rotates their elbows.
The dancer moves their elbows to the right.
isolations: In a squatting stance, the person moves one body part independently while keeping the rest of the body stationary.

Starting in a low, grounded stance with bent knees and arms raised, the individual performs an arm isolation by sharply extending the left arm straight out to the side while keeping the torso and lower body stationary.
The person is performing arm isolations.

The person performs an upper-body isolation by twisting and shifting their chest and shoulders from side to side. Their hips and legs remain mostly stationary, anchoring the movement to the upper torso.
The person performs an isolation movement, starting with their left arm and then moving both arms up and down.

Standing in a wide, low stance with the lower body held still, the person performs sharp chest and arm isolations. The left arm remains mostly extended, while the bent right arm moves up and down in coordination with the torso’s movement.
A person moves their shoulders up and down and then shakes their legs.
The person performs an isolation movement by moving body parts separately.

Starting in a wide stance with arms outstretched, a person quickly drops into a low crouch. While maintaining the crouch, they perform a torso isolation by swaying their upper body sharply from side to side.
The person first lifts their left hand and then their right hand.
The person moves one body part independently in a popping motion while the rest of the body remains stationary.
A person twists and bends their arms one at a time in sync with the rhythm.
The person stands with their arms extended straight in front and shakes them. They then bend their right arm at the elbow to point the forearm forward and clap their hands together.
The person performs an upper body isolation, shifting their chest and shoulders from side to side while maintaining a stationary, wide, bent-knee stance.
The person isolates their right arm, and a wave propagates through the rest of the body.

The person extends both legs into a wide stance by twisting their feet without lifting them off the floor. In rhythm with the twisting, they hold their bent arms up and wave their hands in a circular motion in front of their head.
Starting with their arms bent at different heights, a person extends both arms straight out to the sides at shoulder level while keeping their torso and legs stationary.

A person stands slightly bent over with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They pull their arms in slightly and move their knees out into a wide stance, maintaining the slight forward bend. They then bring their hands together in front of their body with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally, before pointing their arms back out to the sides.
A person stands in a low, bent-knee stance and performs an upper body isolation. Their lower body and torso remain relatively still while their bent arms wave around.

With arms raised and bent, the individual performs a sharp chest isolation, sliding their upper torso from right to left. The lower body remains firmly planted, highlighting the independent movement of the chest.
The individual performs an upper-body isolation, shifting their chest and head to the left. The lower body remains stationary, anchoring the movement while the arms adjust slightly.
The dancer performs a chest isolation, quickly popping their upper torso to the right. The lower body and arms remain stationary to emphasize the isolated movement.

The dancer leans forward, performing an upper body isolation by twisting their torso and raising their arms. Their lower body remains grounded with legs apart, emphasizing the independent movement of the chest.
A person stands and performs an upper body isolation with their bent arms raised near their head. Their chest and shoulders move independently while the lower body remains relatively stationary.
With arms crossed in front of their hips, the person turns their body to the right.
Starting from a slight crouch, the performer thrusts their chest sharply forward while simultaneously moving their right hand toward the front-left and their left hand toward the back-right.
From a wide stance with knees turned inward, the person widens their leg to twist their body to the left.
A person isolates their upper body by sharply twisting and tilting their torso and head to the right while keeping their lower body completely stationary from the hips down.
The figure performs arm isolations, first extending the right arm up and to the side while keeping the torso still, followed by the left arm extending upward and across the body.
Standing with feet apart, a person performs a chest isolation by shifting their upper torso from side to side. The arms and lower body remain still, emphasizing the independent movement of the chest.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise and point their forearms upward while keeping their elbows bent. They then cross their forearms horizontally and rotate counterclockwise back to the original orientation. Extending their arms to the sides, they lower the right arm and raise the left, then lower the left arm to match the height of the right. Finally, they bend the right leg forward and the left back, rotating their torso clockwise as they kneel.
Standing in place, a person performs sharp, isolated movements with their upper body. The torso twists, bends, and snaps side to side and forward, while the lower body remains relatively stable.
The individual executes a sharp upper-body isolation, pushing their chest and head to the right while their lower body remains relatively stationary and their arms extend to the right.

The person stands with elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They punch their left arm forward and clap their hands while stepping slightly back. As they continue stepping back, they swing the right arm under and then over the left, maintaining the position of the arms. They then step forward and rotate slightly clockwise while swinging the left arm under and then over the right. Finally, they move the right arm under the left as they rotate counterclockwise and step forward, dragging the right foot out in a circular motion.
The person isolates their left arm by thrusting the forearm forward and then pulling it back, while the rest of the body remains mostly stationary.
The figure performs chest isolations, smoothly shifting the torso side to side with arms extended and legs held stationary in a wide stance.

The dancer executes a sharp upper-body isolation, quickly shifting their torso and head to the right. Their lower body remains still, anchoring the movement and emphasizing the separation between the upper and lower body.

Starting from a crouched stance with knees turned inward, the person raises their right hand to their left shoulder, followed immediately by their left. Both hands then sweep together diagonally down from the left shoulder toward the right hip.

Starting from a low, wide squat with the left hand on the ground, the performer raises their torso and brings the left hand up in front of their head. The body then rotates to the right, shifting into a right-leg lunge.
The person tilts their head upward with both hands raised and elbows bent forward. They then turn their body to the left with stiff, robotic movements.
The dancer performs an isolation movement with the left arm, waving it while the rest of the body remains stationary.
A person stands upright with their arms extended forward. They then pull their arms in by bending their elbows and shake their forearms in and out in front of their torso.
The person isolates their upper body, holding their right arm up with the elbow at shoulder height, then turns their knees from the front toward the left.

Starting from a T-pose, the individual isolates their upper body, bending the right arm and wrist to bring the hand toward the head. The left arm also bends as the torso shifts slightly, while the legs remain stationary.

The individual stands with their arms raised and elbows bent, keeping their lower body relatively still. They then perform an upper body isolation, leaning their torso forward and to the right while moving their arms independently.

With elbows bent and raised sideways and forearms held in front of the body, the person twists their feet outward. They then suddenly move their arms up and down. Next, they sway their lower body while isolating the upper body, turning to the right into a right-leg lunge. Finally, they swing their arms and rotate their torso to the left, ending the motion in a squatting stance with their knees turned inward.

The individual stands with their arms raised and elbows bent. They bend forward at the waist, hop 90 degrees counterclockwise, and straighten back up while maintaining their arm position. They then stretch their arms up and back and lean to the right, kicking their left leg out to the right, before finally straightening and reaching their arms upward.

A person stands upright with elbows bent at their sides and swings their right arm forward in a wide loop. They lean back slightly and repeat the motion with the left arm, then perform a slight kick with both legs and shake their left forearm while stepping forward with the left leg.

Starting from a standing position, a person performs a controlled deep squat. As they descend, they hold their arms out to the sides and move them independently, isolating the motion of the upper body from the lower body.
The person performs isolated movements with both arms while the rest of the body remains stationary.

The performer stands in a wide stance with knees turned inward and arms out to the sides, elbows bent, while leaning slightly to the left. They move their forearms up and down. Stepping the left leg to the side, they rotate their torso counterclockwise, extending their arms with elbows bent and forearms pointing down before thrusting them out to the sides at shoulder height. They move their body up and down by bending the knees, then lean the torso forward over the left knee. Finally, they step the right leg to the left and straighten up, swinging their arms across the front and then out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.

The person stands with their feet wide and arms extended straight in front, pulled slightly back. They bend their elbows and straighten their back, then return to the initial position while swinging their arms out to the sides and back. Simultaneously, they move their knees in and out. Next, they swing their left arm upward and their right arm forward, elbow bent with the forearm held horizontally across the body. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise, drop the right arm to their side, and begin to step forward.

A person stands with their arms at their sides, pointing down. They swing their left forearm in a circle in front of their torso and rotate clockwise, ending with the forearm pointing up in front of their chest. They then rotate counterclockwise to face the original direction, extending their left arm forward while bending their right elbow so the forearm points up. Next, they rotate their torso 90 degrees counterclockwise and switch arm positions, pointing the right arm forward and the left forearm up. Finally, they straighten their arms forward and then bend at the elbows to hold their forearms horizontally in front of their body, stepping back while shaking their left forearm in and out.

The person stands in a wide stance with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They drop their arms and rotate their torso clockwise, then counterclockwise, shaking their shoulders and throwing their arms out. They then bend their elbows and bring their arms in front of their body, forearms pointing up and hands touching in front of their face. Maintaining this position, they move their arms to the left and push forward twice, then lean to the right and repeat the movement.

Standing in a wide stance, the person shifts their weight to the left while sweeping their left hand up the right arm toward the shoulder, creating a dragging effect in the upper body. They then rhythmically touch their right shoulder and elbow with their left hand.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides, shuffling sideways by moving their knees in and out. They then vibrate their right arm while holding their left arm up and to the side.
Standing in a wide stance, a person isolates their upper body. The right arm traces a large circle from front to back over the head, while the chest and left arm perform small, sharp movements.
Standing in a low, wide stance, the person performs a sharp upper-body isolation, snapping their chest and head from side to side while the lower body remains stationary.

The person stands bent over with their knees bent and arms pointed downward. They widen their stance, pushing their legs out while slightly raising their torso, then pull their arms up and push them back down. Next, they bend over their left knee before swinging their torso to the right, positioning their arms over the right knee. They bring their left leg toward the right, step it back out, and swing their torso back over it. Finally, they rise and straighten their torso, extending the right arm forward while bending the left elbow to point the left forearm across the body and upward.
Both arms and the right leg move independently of each other.
The individual performs an upper-body isolation, twisting their torso and shoulders sharply from side to side. The left arm moves independently of the body.

A person stands with their elbows bent at their sides and their right forearm held in front of their torso. They straighten their left arm forward while stepping forward and to the right, then crouch and lean over with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally, rotating the right forearm. They then straighten up and turn counterclockwise to face left, walking forward with their right arm extended behind them and their left arm bent in front of their body.
Standing with arms at their sides and the left forearm pointing upward, the individual rotates clockwise while waving their left forearm from side to side.

The performer performs an upper body isolation, leaning back and to the right while maintaining a wide, grounded stance. The arms are held in a raised, bent position to accentuate the independent movement of the chest and shoulders.
A person performs isolated shoulder movements while bending down.

A person performs a series of sharp torso isolations, moving their upper body forward and from side to side. The lower body remains mostly stationary, anchoring the dynamic movements of the upper body.

Standing in a low, wide stance, the person performs chest isolations by shifting their upper torso from side to side. Their arms are held up with elbows bent, while the head and lower body remain relatively still.
The person walks forward while performing a right-arm isolation, repeatedly bringing their right arm toward their body and pulling the elbow back.

Starting from a low stance, the individual rises while twisting their torso to the left. They perform an upper body isolation, bringing their left arm across their chest while pulling their right arm back with a bent elbow.

A person isolates their head and arms, keeping their legs and torso still. The arms move in a controlled, flowing motion, with the left arm crossing the chest and the right arm rising toward the head.
The person performs arm isolations while continuously crossing their arms.

The person stands with their right arm bent and held horizontally across the chest, while their left arm is bent and raised, forming a crossed-arm pose. They move the left arm up and down around the right arm. While maintaining this crossed position, they slowly rotate their arms.

A person stands in a squat with their knees turned inward and arms crossed in front of the body. They raise their bent right elbow while lowering the left, rolling the right hand around the left to swap their positions. The movement finishes with the left hand in front of the head and the right hand held at shoulder height in front of the torso.

The person stands with their left arm bent horizontally in front of their chest and their right hand raised to shoulder level. They roll their head and then bring both hands in front of their chest while leaning slightly to the left.

The person stands with their hands in front of their chest and elbows bent at shoulder level. They move one arm at a time downward to the left and back up. The movement is isolated, with only one body part moving at a time.
Standing in a wide stance, a person isolates their upper body and head, smoothly shifting their chest and head from right to left while keeping their lower body stationary.
The person moves their arms independently, first moving their bent left arm, then bending their right arm, and finally moving only their left arm.
A person moves both arms independently while the rest of the body remains still.

The performer isolates their upper body by sharply dropping the chest and head down to the right. With elbows bent, the arms follow the torso's movement while the lower body remains stationary in a wide stance.

The individual performs a krump isolation, sharply twisting their upper body and head from side to side. Their legs remain bent in a stable, wide stance, anchoring the powerful, independent movement of the torso.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms at their sides and their left elbow bent, forearm pointing up. They lean their torso to the right while shaking their forearms and lower left leg. They then vibrate their knees toward each other and return the left arm to their side to match the right.

A person stands upright with their arms at their sides. They bend their left elbow, bringing the forearm in front of the torso while leaning forward slightly. They then slide the right foot to the side and extend the right arm outward with a bent elbow, before rotating slightly counterclockwise with both arms held in front and elbows bent. The right forearm rotates up and then back to point forward, followed by pushing the left arm out to the side and back. Finally, they straighten the right arm to the side at shoulder height while rotating clockwise.

The person stands with their hands in front of their body, the right elbow bent and raised while the left is bent and lowered. They turn their body to the left. Keeping the rest of the body still, they fold only the left forearm. After a brief pause, they turn their body to the right. They then move their arms one at a time while isolating the rest of the body. Finally, they raise their left knee while lifting both arms sideways with bent elbows at shoulder level.
With the left knee raised and both arms held sideways at shoulder level with elbows bent, the person alternately swings their left hand and left foot.

With the left knee raised and both arms held out to the sides with elbows bent at shoulder level, the person alternates between moving their arms up and down and shifting their left foot from side to side.

Starting in a squat with the left arm extended overhead to the right, the person performs an upper body wave by swaying their left arm and right shoulder. While continuing the waving motion, they slowly lower the left arm to their side.
A person performs an upper-body isolation, sharply dropping their head, shoulders, and arms forward and down while keeping their lower body stationary to emphasize the isolated movement of the torso.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides and the left forearm positioned across the torso. They extend their arms slightly forward while raising the left arm, then move their body sideways to the right with their arms held in front. They pull their arms back and bend the elbows, pointing the right forearm forward and the left upward. After turning 90 degrees clockwise, they drop the right arm to their side while keeping the left arm raised. They shake their forearms and bend over with their arms bent at the elbows by their sides. They then rotate their torso 180 degrees and bring their hands together in front of the body. Stepping forward, they roll their forearms around each other and straighten their posture. Finally, they point their arms straight ahead and move them up and down as they walk forward.

The individual stands with their arms held forward, elbows bent and forearms positioned horizontally. They move their arms to the left while maintaining this posture, then adjust the left arm so the forearm points upward. They rotate the left arm and straighten it forward, then bend it at the elbow again and raise both arms above their head. Rotating their forearms in circular motions, they kick their right leg forward, then bring their arms down and pull the leg back while bending forward at the waist. Finally, they step forward into a wide stance, straightening up and extending their arms at shoulder height with elbows bent and forearms pointing upward.

The person stands with their arms out to the sides at shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They move their arms slightly up and down while bending their knees, then step forward with the right leg into a lunge.

Starting in a right-leg lunge with arms at shoulder height, elbows bent, and forearms pointing upward, the person pulls their arms back and then leans their torso backward. These movements are performed sequentially. Finally, they step the right leg behind the left and rotate the lower body clockwise to the side.

Standing on the left foot with the right leg pointed out and arms at the sides, the person lifts the right leg up and then out. They bend their elbows to bring their forearms in front of the torso, then raise their right arm while lowering the left.

A person stands with their right arm down and their left arm raised. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise, bringing their forearms horizontally in front of their torso, then extend their arms to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.
A person performs a Krump isolation, sharply shifting their upper torso and head from side to side. Their arms are held in a raised, bent position while the lower body remains relatively stationary.

intro windup: The person transitions from a T-pose into a backward movement while lowering their arms. As they begin to dance, their arms swing rhythmically in time with their steps.

The figure initiates a fluid body roll, twisting the torso to the left while lowering the arms from a T-pose and bringing the left hand toward the chest. Simultaneously, they take a large, stylized step forward with the right leg, bending deeply to shift their weight forward.

Starting with arms outstretched, the figure fluidly lowers them while twisting the torso and crouching slightly. This movement synchronizes with a stylized weight shift, stepping forward onto a bent right leg, followed by the left.
Starting from a T-pose, the figure lowers its arms and sways slightly while stepping its feet outward. It then extends its right arm to the side and raises its left arm, bending it at the elbow.

A person stands straight in a T-pose, lowers their arms, and steps back. They then kick their right leg back and step forward, swinging their right arm back and left arm forward with both elbows bent.
Starting from a T-pose, the person steps backward and forward while lightly bouncing their body.
Starting from a standing position, the person steps backward.
Starting from a T-pose, the figure lowers its arms toward its hips while initiating a body roll. Simultaneously, it takes a stylized step forward and to the right, shifting its weight.
The person steps in a very relaxed manner.
The person starts in a T-pose with their arms extended to the sides, then lowers their arms and slowly walks backward.
Starting with arms extended to the sides, the figure draws them inward toward the chest. Simultaneously, the upper body rolls forward, contracting the core and hunching the shoulders.
Starting from a T-pose, the figure lowers their arms to their sides. They begin stepping backward, swaying slightly and stepping their feet slightly outward while gently swinging their arms.
While bouncing lightly, the person steps forward and then turns their body twice.

A person performs a tap step, rapidly striking the ground with their right heel, the ball of the foot, and then the heel again. The left leg remains planted for support, while the arms are held slightly out to the sides for balance.
With arms extended to the sides, a person strikes their right heel on the floor and then taps the ball of the same foot.
toe heel toe: A person stands and repeatedly taps their toes and heels while swinging their arms back and forth.
From a standing position, the person repeatedly taps their toes and heels while swinging their arms back and forth and bending their torso.

The person stands and performs a tap dance move, striking their right foot against the ground in a toe-heel-toe sequence. Their left leg remains bent for support, and their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows.

The individual performs a toe-heel-toe tap sequence with the left foot, bending the knee with each strike while maintaining their weight on the right leg. The arms remain extended to the sides for balance throughout the movement.

A person performs a tap sequence with their right foot, starting with a toe touch behind the body, followed by a forward heel strike, and finishing with another toe tap behind. The left leg provides support while the arms are held out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
A person performs a tap sequence with their left foot, starting with a toe touch next to the body, followed by heel touch on the ground.
A person performs a toe-heel-toe tap sequence with their right foot while extending their right arm straight out to the side.

Viewed from the front-right, a figure performs a tap step with the right foot, striking the floor in a toe-heel-toe sequence. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows while the left leg remains stationary to provide support.
The person steps forward with one leg and then to the side.

A person stands upright with arms extended in a T-pose for balance. They position their right foot slightly forward and perform a quick tap dance move, striking the floor with the toe, the heel, and the toe again in rapid succession.

A person balances on their left leg while performing a rapid toe-heel-toe tapping sequence with their right foot. Their arms are raised and bent at the elbows to stabilize the upper body during the intricate footwork.
The person jumps in place, then kicks their left leg and then their right leg sideways in alternation.

Standing on the right leg with arms extended to the sides for balance, the performer executes a quick tap sequence with the left foot, striking the floor with the toe, heel, and toe again in rapid succession.

The person hops and kicks one leg forward, then repeats the movement with the other leg.
A person extends their right leg forward, tapping the ground in a toe-heel-toe sequence while holding their left arm out to the side and their right arm in a raised, bent position.

With arms extended to the sides for balance, the person leans forward slightly at the waist. They perform a quick three-part tap with the left foot, extending the leg backward and striking the ground in a toe-heel-toe sequence.
The person performs a tap dance step, bringing their right foot forward from a raised position to strike the ground in a rapid toe-heel-toe sequence.

A person performs a "toe-heel-toe" tap with their left foot, striking the ground with the toe, heel, and toe in succession. The right leg remains bent for support while the upper body stays relatively still.

A person performs a "toe-heel-toe" tap with their right foot, striking sequentially with the ball, the heel, and the ball of the foot again. They lower into a slight crouch during the movement, swinging their arms in opposition for balance.
brush: The individual stands on their left leg with their arms bent at their sides. They kick their right leg in a circular motion and step it forward in front of the left leg, performing a slight hop on the left foot while lifting their torso and arms. The movement is then repeated, alternating legs.

Standing on their left leg, the performer brushes their right foot backward along the floor in a smooth, sweeping arc. The upper body remains stable, with arms bent and held out to the sides for balance.

Viewed from an angle, a person balances on a slightly bent left leg. They extend their right leg forward, brushing the foot along the floor, while holding their arms out to the sides in a bent position for stability.
Standing on their left leg, a person brushes their right foot forward along the floor in a swinging arc, holding their arms out to the sides for balance.
Standing on their left leg, the person brushes their right foot forward and backward in a continuous motion. Their arms are raised with bent elbows as the upper body sways slightly to the rhythm.

Standing on their left foot, a person brushes their right foot forward across the floor while maintaining balance with their arms bent and held out to the sides.

heel: A person jumps in place with their arms raised. Upon landing, they perform side kicks or steps, first with the right leg and then the left, while gently swinging their arms in rhythm with their steps.

toe: Starting from a standing position with knees slightly bent and arms held out, the individual extends their right leg forward. The right toe quickly strikes the ground in front of the body before retracting.

A person glides around while rotating.
Pushing off the left foot, the person glides smoothly to the right and balances on the right foot. Throughout the movement, the arms are held up with the elbows bent.

Viewed from a three-quarter perspective, the person glides to the left by smoothly sliding their right foot from behind to in front of their left. Their arms are held bent in front, shifting slightly in coordination with the leg movement.

A person stands with their right leg in front of their left, arms bent at the elbows and forearms pointing forward. They step back with their right leg, moving slightly backward while maintaining this posture.
A person smoothly slides their left foot backward and to the right while moving their right foot forward. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows to maintain balance.
The person performs a smooth glide by sliding their left foot backward and away from the body while leaning forward with bent arms held up and out to the sides.
A person glides smoothly to the left by sliding their feet, keeping their arms bent and raised in front of their chest and head throughout the motion.
The person glides to the right by smoothly sliding their right foot followed by their left, while maintaining a stable T-pose with their arms extended.
With arms raised to shoulder height and elbows bent, the person moonwalks backward. Maintaining this posture, they turn while sliding to the side.
A person slides to the left with their right foot, moving without lifting their feet off the ground.

A person performs a backward glide by smoothly sliding their feet in opposite directions. The right foot slides backward while the left foot moves forward, with the upper body and arms remaining relatively stationary.

A person performs a smooth glide, sliding their feet sideways across the floor. They hold their right arm straight out in front while their left arm is bent with the hand near the chest, moving the left arm up and down as they advance forward.
With both arms raised and elbows bent, the person glides sideways and backward by sliding their feet on the floor. The arms swing in time with the rhythm.
arm pop: A person stands in a slight crouch with their left arm extended to the side and their right arm bent in front of their body. They pivot slightly on their feet while keeping the rest of their body still, then rotate their torso to one side while maintaining their arm positions.
The person steps forward with their right foot and pops their left forearm.
The person steps back while popping their arms.

A person stands with their arms in front and elbows slightly bent. They shake their left arm and right foot, then turn 90 degrees clockwise while bending their elbows to point their forearms forward. They stretch their forearms forward and bend both knees, leaning back slightly, then straighten and step forward while pushing both forearms forward. Finally, they lower their left arm to their side while keeping the right arm bent at the elbow, with the forearm pointed forward and upward.
Maintaining a low, hunched-over stance with the torso turned to the right, the individual executes a popping motion by sharply and abruptly swinging the left arm forward and upward.
The person pops their arms outward, extending the elbows out and then bringing them back in.
Starting with the right leg forward, the person pops their left shoulder back and forth. They then step forward with their left foot while popping both arms in front of their chest.

A person stands with both arms bent and raised, with the right arm positioned higher than the left. They execute a sharp, simultaneous pop in both arms, causing them to jerk quickly outward and back in.
The dancer stands in a bent-forward position while popping their arms.
A dancer pops their arms out while rotating in different directions.

A person stands facing forward with their arms at their sides. They bend their elbows to bring their forearms into a horizontal position in front of their torso, then return their arms to the starting position.

A person stands with their arms at their sides. They swing their left arm across the body while rotating clockwise, then step to the side and pull the left elbow back, lowering the forearm in front of the torso. They swing the arm out and back, maintaining the position of the arms while rotating the torso counterclockwise. Stepping the right foot out, they extend the left arm upward, then lower both arms to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They lean back, swinging their forearms up and down, then bend slightly forward and move their knees from side to side while pointing their arms down. They step the left foot in front of the right to move sideways while pointing their arms up, then step the right foot past it. They point the right arm down and to the side before reaching it upward, while bending the left elbow to point the left forearm up, facing the side. Finally, they straighten the left arm upward and bend the right elbow while maintaining the same direction.
A person stands with their arms at their sides and their left elbow bent, forearm pointing forward. They vibrate slightly, then move both arms and their left leg slightly outward.
Push the arms forward, pause briefly, and then resume the movement.
A person stands with their left arm bent and raised to the side, performing a quick, sharp pop through a subtle but distinct muscle contraction.
A person raises both arms and pops them.

Standing in a T-pose, the person performs a quick, sharp pop with both arms simultaneously. This motion is characterized by a rapid contraction and release of the arm muscles, resulting in a visible twitch.
A person stands in a grounded stance with knees bent and arms raised. They execute a sharp pop by quickly contracting the muscles in both arms.

A person stands with their right forearm raised vertically and their left forearm positioned horizontally across their torso. They then rotate their entire body 90 degrees counterclockwise while maintaining this posture.
The dancer moves their arms to a position, pauses, and pops their muscles, then moves to a different location and repeats the movement.
The individual sharply extends both arms to the sides from a bent position and then quickly snaps them back.

With elbows bent and raised at shoulder height, the individual rotates their forearms in opposite directions while kicking their right leg. They then bring both forearms to their chest and swing them to form a cross.
A person stands with their arms raised to the sides and performs a sharp, quick contraction in both arms. This popping motion is achieved by rapidly snapping the elbows and wrists.
A person extends their right arm forward and their left arm down, then pops both arms.
A person steps with their right foot, reaches out with their left hand, and then pops it.

The individual stands with their arms raised, elbows bent, and forearms pointing forward. They lower their arms to their sides and slide from side to side. As they slide, they vibrate the knee of the outer leg and extend the outer arm while vibrating it, keeping the other arm at their side.

The figure executes a sharp pop with the right arm, rapidly extending it from a bent position in front of the torso out to the side and down, while the left arm performs a smaller, mirrored contraction.
A person moves around, popping their arms and shoulders throughout the motion.

Starting in a low crouch with both arms pointing down, the person rises slightly and rotates clockwise while extending their left arm. They then straighten and reach both arms upward before moving them to the sides, bending their elbows to point the forearms down as they step forward and lean over. Finally, as they step forward with the left leg, they straighten up and rotate their arms until the forearms point upward.
The person leans back, pops their shoulders, and shifts their weight to the right before popping again. They continue to pop their shoulders while repeatedly shifting their weight from side to side.
A person forms an angular shape with their arms, bringing their left hand near their chest, followed by swift arm pops.
Standing with both arms bent and the right arm held higher than the left, the person executes a sharp, simultaneous pop.

The individual extends their right arm forward with a sharp pop while simultaneously shifting their weight to the right. They then retract the arm and continue with a series of rhythmic, alternating arm pops.
A person performs a series of arm pops by sharply extending their left arm followed by their right. The sequence repeats, concluding with both arms extending outward simultaneously.

The person performs a series of sharp arm pops, primarily with the right arm. The right arm snaps straight out to the side and retracts, while the left arm executes a smaller, corresponding pop upward and forward.
The dancer changes direction to the right while popping their arms.
A person stands with their hands on their hips and elbows pointing outward. They then execute a quick, sharp pop with their right arm by contracting and releasing the bicep and tricep muscles.
A person in a low stance extends their left arm to the side and performs a sharp pop by quickly contracting and relaxing the elbow. Their right arm remains bent near the chest.
A person stands with their arms raised to the sides and elbows bent in a flexed position. They perform an arm pop by sharply contracting and releasing their biceps and triceps.

A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms pointing up. They straighten their arms out to the sides, lower them to their sides, and slide from side to side. They then point their left arm forward and swing their arms back and forth while standing in a wide stance.

The individual performs a sharp, quick pop with their left arm by contracting the bicep, followed by a similar motion with the right arm. The body rocks slightly back and forth in sync with these sharp, isolated movements.
Wave the arms around and finish with a pop upon stopping.
The person stands with arms extended to the sides and performs a sharp popping motion by quickly bending both elbows inward, then immediately extending them back out to the starting position.
boogaloo: A person performs a boogaloo-style wave, initiating the motion at the feet and flowing through the lower body to the chest and arms. The movement continues as a smooth, rolling wave down through the torso and hips.

The dancer executes a fluid boogaloo roll, leaning forward and twisting their torso to the right. They lift their left knee high and extend the right leg back while holding their arms out to the sides.
The upper body rolls fluidly to the right, twisting the torso and raising the left shoulder as the right leg steps forward into a dynamic pose.

Starting in a wide stance, the person performs a fluid upper body roll while leaning to the left. They then take a side step to the left, raising their bent arms in front of their chest while keeping the torso still, before initiating another body roll.

The individual stands with their arms bent in front of them. They rotate their torso and step to the side while pushing their arms straight in the opposite direction. After repeating this sequence twice, they step forward with their arms at their sides.

A person performs a boogaloo roll, creating a fluid, wave-like motion that travels from the left leg through the hips to the right leg. Their arms are held forward with elbows raised to the sides, maintaining this posture throughout the movement.

A person performs a fluid, rolling motion that travels up through the twisting torso and shoulders. The movement flows continuously out through the arms in a wave-like fashion, while the legs remain bent to support the upper body's motion.

Standing upright with arms at their sides, the individual slightly vibrates their shoulders while rotating 90 degrees clockwise, extending their forearms forward and kicking their right lower leg back. They then shake their shoulders and bend their knees.
A person performs a body wave that ripples from their upper body down to their knees, then takes two wide steps forward, crossing one foot over the other with each stride.
The person performs a fluid body roll, initiating a wave that travels from the chest down through the torso, hips, and legs, then reverses direction to flow back up to the shoulders.
In a squatting stance, the person waves the left side of their body to the right while turning, then waves again.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides. They shimmy their shoulders and then move their knees to the right and back twice while staying in place.

Fluid, rolling motions propagate from the legs through the chest to the arms. The person then spins while swaying their legs and holding their upper body rigid. They squat to swing their right leg back and side-to-side.

Starting from a right-leg lunge, the person turns their torso to the left while raising the left elbow. They then move laterally by stepping the left leg across the right. The person rotates by uncrossing their legs while swinging the left arm forward. After swaying their body, they lift the left knee and sway the lower body, finishing in a squat with both knees turned inward.
From a standing position, the person rolls their arms in front of their chest.

Standing with feet apart and arms extended, the person performs a smooth, fluid roll through their chest and shoulders, creating a wave-like effect. The movement is continuous and focused on the upper body, with the arms moving in sync with the torso.
The person performs a fluid body roll, contracting their torso and bending their knees inward. This creates a smooth, continuous wave that flows through the limbs as they bend and extend.

The dancer rotates to the left with their left hand positioned in front of the chest, then turns clockwise by stepping the right leg back. Following the turn, a fluid sway initiates in the legs and rolls upward through the upper body.

A person performs a fluid, wave-like upper body roll, shifting their torso and shoulders from side to side. Their arms, bent at the elbows, follow the movement while their head moves in sync with the torso's continuous roll.

The dancer assumes a low, crouched stance, lunging forward on the right leg while extending the left leg back. With arms bent and spread to the sides, the upper body leans forward, initiating a fluid, rolling motion.

Starting with their arms at their sides, the individual performs 90-degree clockwise rotations until they face forward again. With each turn, they bring their knees toward each other while swinging their forearms outward at their sides.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They lean to the side and then back, gyrating their hips before bending over and pointing their arms to the right, with the left elbow bent and the forearm held in front of the body. They straighten up and step forward with the left foot, bringing their arms back to their sides. They then step the left leg to the left, straighten their arms at their sides, and shift their knees from side to side to slide their body to the left. Finally, they stop and step the right foot forward.

The person performs a fluid arm wave, starting from the right hand and flowing across the chest to the left. Simultaneously, the torso and hips sway side-to-side in a smooth, rolling motion that complements the arm movement.
Standing with knees bent, the individual shifts their weight while smoothly rolling their chest and shoulders. Their arms move in sync with the torso’s fluid, wave-like motion.

The person performs a fluid, rolling motion, swaying their upper body from side to side. The movement originates in the torso and ripples upward through the shoulders and head, while the legs remain mostly stationary.

The individual stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They shift their knees inward and their feet outward. While shaking their arms up and down, they rotate their torso in a counterclockwise circle, keeping their knees bent and feet turned out.
Starting from a crouched, twisted pose, the figure unfurls their torso in a fluid, rolling motion. Their arms and legs simultaneously extend into a wide, open stance as they execute a partial turn.

Starting from a squat, the person waves to the right while leaning their torso to the right and straightening their left leg sideways. They then turn their torso to the right while bending the right knee and leaning slightly, before stepping backward.
The performer sways from side to side, initiating a fluid, rolling motion through the chest and shoulders. They then move their left foot to the right and turn their body to the right.

The performer stands bent over with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointed downward. They slide the left leg forward with the knee bent while twisting the torso back and vibrating their arms. They then turn clockwise and thrust both arms straight forward, bending over once again.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They rotate slightly counterclockwise, shifting one forearm down and the other up while taking a small step to the left. Rotating clockwise, they push their hips back and move their arms in front of their body, then drop the right arm to the side while pointing the left arm and right leg forward. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise, ending with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward.

The person executes a fluid body roll by contracting their chest and shoulders, moving them forward and down to the left in a smooth, circular motion. Their arms follow this wave-like movement, with the right arm crossing the body as the torso twists.

A person stands with their arms at their sides. They move their knees in and out while turning slightly to the side, swinging their arms from side to side while keeping their forearms pointed forward. They then push their knees outward and slide sideways in the opposite direction, shifting their arms toward that side and lowering their forearms.

The performer initiates a smooth, rolling twist to the right, involving the entire torso and hips. Simultaneously, they lower their stance by bending their knees as their arms fluidly contract inward.

Starting from a standing position with the torso leaning to the right, the person performs a body wave by shifting their torso to the left. They then step forward while executing a 360-degree turn and swaying their legs. Next, they pause in a right-leg lunge with the torso leaning to the right and the right arm raised with a bent elbow. Finally, the person steps sideways back toward the starting position, turns, and moves backward, concluding the sequence by swaying their entire body in place.
The person performs a fluid body roll, initiating a wave from the chest that ripples down through the hips. Their arms follow in relaxed, circular paths, enhancing the continuous flow.

The dancer executes a fluid body roll, initiating the movement at the hips and sequentially twisting the torso and shoulders to the left. This wave-like motion travels up the body, causing the arms to move in smooth, oppositional arcs.

The person stands with their arms bent at the elbows and pointing upward. They move their hands to their hips and rotate their torso in a counterclockwise circle, then slide to the right and bend forward, keeping their hands on their hips while swaying their torso from side to side.

The dancer performs a fluid body roll, contracting the torso and rotating the shoulders from right to left. The hips move in a smooth, continuous wave, creating a flowing motion throughout the entire body.
The dancer kicks their left leg and then shifts their body weight to the left.

Starting from a squat with the left arm raised, the person rolls their hips in a circular motion while stepping sideways, finishing in a wide stance with elbows bent at shoulder height and hands pointed downward.
With forearms crossed in front of the body, the person rolls their hips in a circular motion while stepping sideways.

A person performs a fluid, wave-like roll with their upper body, shifting their chest and shoulders from side to side. They maintain a stable stance with their right hand held near their head and their left arm bent across their chest.
glide shift: The person glides smoothly from side to side, shifting their weight while keeping their feet close together. Their upper body remains relatively still, with their arms bent and held in front of the chest.
A person stands with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent. They step back with their right foot and kick their left foot forward while swinging their arms back and forth.
The person glides smoothly from side to side, creating the illusion of floating across the floor. They hold their arms out with elbows bent while keeping the upper body relatively still.
After a small step on the left leg, the person takes a large step to the side with the right leg.

Standing upright with their arms bent in front, the person pushes their arms forward and swings their left leg around. They then drop their left arm to their side while swinging their right arm forward and around.
The person steps back and then turns their body to the left with their arm held up at chest level.

Standing upright with feet together, the person smoothly glides their body horizontally to the left. This seamless shift is driven by the twisting and pushing of the legs, while the upper body remains relatively stable.
The figure performs a smooth lateral glide, shifting weight from the left leg to the right. Simultaneously, the arms rise and move in a fluid, wave-like motion in coordination with the shift.
The person glides forward on their left leg with their right hand on their chest, twisting their feet as they move.

The performer glides smoothly to the right, shifting weight onto the right leg as the left leg straightens and extends to the side. The arms are held up with bent elbows, remaining stationary while the lower body moves.
full body pop: A figure stands with arms extended to the sides at shoulder height and elbows bent. They then step back with their left leg and lean their torso backward, subtly vibrating their body.
The person bends forward at the hips, bowing their upper body toward the ground with their arms hanging limply. Their legs remain relatively straight with a slight bend in the knees.

A figure stands upright with their arms at their sides. They shift the right leg forward while extending their arms out to the sides at shoulder height. They then cross their arms in front of their body and pull the right leg back, bending it at the knee to stand on the left leg.

Standing with arms out to the sides and elbows bent, the individual leans forward slightly and kicks their right leg back, bending it at the knee. They then step forward while pushing their arms ahead, keeping the elbows bent.
Starting from a squat with the left arm extended straight to the side, the person begins popping their entire body.
A person performs a full-body pop, contracting all their muscles simultaneously to land in a crouched stance with bent knees and arms raised and flexed near the shoulders.

Standing with arms bent and legs apart, the person performs a sudden full-body contraction. This quick, sharp movement involves a simultaneous jerk of the arms, legs, and torso, creating a popping effect.

The person starts in a slight crouch with their left arm bent toward their head and their right arm across their chest. They then perform a quick, sharp full-body muscle contraction, sending a subtle but distinct twitch through their entire frame.
With the right leg forward and the left elbow bent at shoulder level, the person pops their body.
A person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They then straighten their arms at their sides while rotating their body slightly counterclockwise.

The individual kneels with their left leg forward and right leg back, crouching low to the floor. Their arms are held out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They slowly rise and straighten, extending their arms out to the sides.

With their forearms crossed in front of their chest, the person performs a body pop, then swings their arms out to the sides until they are fully extended. They step backward. The movement is characterized by sharp, popping motions and brief pauses.
Standing with their right leg bent and left leg extended back, arms raised and bent, the person executes a sharp, full-body contraction, jerking their entire body into a new pose.
The person slides sideways while maintaining a wide stance, keeping both feet on the floor. Simultaneously, their raised arms with bent elbows move upward in a quick, sharp, jerking bounce.

The person begins in a low, contracted stance with their limbs pulled in toward the body. They then execute a sharp, full-body pop, extending their arms upward and outward while straightening their legs and torso into a static pose.
body direction shift: The individual stands with their left leg forward and right leg back. Their left arm is extended to the side at shoulder height, and their right arm is bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing to the left. They lift the right forearm while rotating it slightly clockwise, then move the left forearm up and down twice. This sequence is then repeated two more times.
With knees bent inward and arms extended forward, the person sways their torso from side to side in a spring-like motion.
A person spins counter-clockwise with their arms raised and elbows bent.

Starting with their upper body and head turned to the left, the person has their left arm bent upward and their right arm downward. They then perform a quick, sharp twist of the torso to the right, simultaneously raising the right arm and lowering the left in a popping motion.
Standing upright with arms at their sides, the person turns to the left and then shifts slightly forward.
In a right-leg lunge stance with their left hand in front of their chest, the person rotates their torso to the left.
A person performs a quick, sharp twist of the upper body to the right, leading with the shoulders. The right arm moves down and across the chest while the left arm swings up and back.
The person shifts direction to the right.
A person shifts their direction to the right.
Turn to the right with both arms in front of the chest.
random: A person circles both arms while moving forward.

After swinging their arms in a squatting stance, the person slowly performs a large forward swing of their right leg with exaggerated arm motions, then jumps forward. They land in a squat with an open chest.

The person starts with their arms extended to the sides, then leads the left arm to the right while stepping back with the right foot to rotate 180 degrees. The movement ends with both arms held parallel to each other in front of the chest.
The dancer raises their arms and rotates their hips in a circle.
The person extends their arms into a T-pose, followed immediately by a sharp full-body contraction that pulls the arms back in to an abrupt stop.

A person stands with their left elbow raised to shoulder level and their hand pointing up. Then, in slow motion, they raise their straight left leg while swinging their left arm down and their right arm up.
A person tilts to the left and then rotates to the left while keeping the rest of their body still.

The individual starts with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. They turn 90 degrees clockwise and swing their left arm in a circle to point forward, matching the right arm's forward position. They then rotate another 90 degrees clockwise and swing their right arm in a circle to point out to the side, so that both arms are once again extended at shoulder height. Finally, they vibrate their torso slightly.

The person stands with their right foot forward and left foot back, holding both hands in front of their pelvis with elbows bent and flared to the sides. They swing their arms sideways while slightly rotating their body to the right. Finally, they lean their torso back, looking upward while raising their arms to chest height.
The person leans back slightly while looking upward and raising their arms to chest level, then slowly moves their hands in front of their chest.
The person steps back with their left leg while swinging their arms down and forward.

The individual stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms in front of the torso. They move their arms forward and out to the sides while stepping the left foot forward to meet the right.
In a slight squat, the person takes side steps to the left, then performs a popping motion by raising both elbows and extending their forearms in sequence.
The person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent, slightly vibrating their entire body.

The person slowly raises their right knee while swinging their left arm forward and their right arm backward. As they lower their right knee, they continue the backward swing of their right arm before bringing it back to its original position.

A person stands with their arms extended to the sides and forearms pointing down. They step back and to the side, bringing their arms in front while keeping their elbows bent. They then rotate their body 90 degrees counterclockwise and lean forward slightly.

A person stands slightly bent forward with their left arm extended to the side and their right arm bent at the elbow, forearm held across the torso. They then walk forward and slide their right leg behind their left, maintaining their arm positions throughout the movement.
The person rotates their body to the left while gently moving their hands back and forth in front of them.
Starting from a standing position with elbows raised to the sides and hands held forward, the person executes a quick popping motion that ripples down from the upper body through the legs.
A person rotates slowly, occasionally popping their left arm during the rotation.

Beginning in a low squat, the person sways their hips and knees from side to side. They then sweep their left leg forward and outward, landing in a lunge with the right leg forward and bent. As they lunge, they raise both arms straight out in front. To finish, the front right leg straightens, pushing the hips backward.

A person raises their right arm into a rigid, angular shape across the chest while extending the left arm. This isolation is immediately followed by a quick full-body contraction that stops abruptly in the final pose.
The person slowly raises their bent elbows up and out to the sides, then quickly drops them in a popping motion while swaying their legs and hips to the side.

The person squats with their knees turned inward while swinging their left hand downward. They then step sideways, bringing their left hand from the hip toward their body, and sway their hips once before stepping backward.
A dancer shakes their left arm while moving forward in slow motion.
A person slowly lifts their left arm.

A person stands with their arms at their sides, then bends their left elbow so the forearm points forward. They slide to the side, maintaining this position while extending their left leg. Then, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise and step their right foot back to meet the left, lowering their arms back to their sides.

The person performs a tutting motion, creating sharp, angular shapes with their arms held out and up. This is followed by a sudden full-body pop, causing them to freeze abruptly in a pose with their knees slightly bent.
The dancer steps to the left while twisting their ankles and raising their arms toward the sky.
Returning to a standing position from an open stance with the arms and legs spread wide.
Starting with both knees bent inward, the person twists their knees outward while raising their bent right elbow.
Raise the left arm while bent forward.
The individual stands upright with their right arm at their side and their left arm extended at shoulder height. They swing their left arm upward while stepping their right foot to the right.
Shift weight forward and then pop the shoulders and arms.

The person stands with their arms out to the sides and shifts their knees slightly back and forth. They then push their right arm out and shift their hips to the opposite side while extending their left elbow. They pull their left arm to the side and kick their right leg out slightly before moving their left knee inward and then back. While moving their forearms forward and back, they slide to the left with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward.

A person performs a tutting sequence, creating sharp, angular shapes with their raised arms. This is followed by a sudden full-body pop, where the knees bend and the body jolts before coming to an abrupt stop.

The individual holds their arms in sharp, angular bends before swiftly sweeping the left arm down across the chest. This motion is accompanied by a quick full-body pop that shifts the hips and torso, followed by an immediate, abrupt freeze in the final pose.
The individual stands with their right arm at their side and their left elbow bent with the forearm pointing upward, rotating their torso clockwise.
The person stands upright with their arms bent at the elbows and held away from the body, hands positioned near the waist.

Keeping the upper body stable, the person slowly moves their left leg sideways and then forward, sliding the foot along the floor. They then rotate by sweeping the left leg in a clockwise circle while pivoting on the right foot.
The person squats down and then quickly swings their hands in front of their body.
The performer creates rigid, angular shapes in a tutting motion, followed immediately by a quick full-body contraction and an abrupt stop.
The performer stands with their arms extended forward, turns 90 degrees clockwise, and runs in place, swinging their arms in sync with their leg movements.

A figure stands with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms held in front of the torso. They turn slightly while slowly extending their arms out to the sides until they are straight at shoulder height.
A person waves their left forearm, steps to the side, and then moves back.

The person stands with their arms held forward and bent at the elbows. They pivot counterclockwise on their right foot to face the opposite direction, then pivot clockwise on the same foot to face the original direction, ending up four steps away from their starting position.
A person stands bent over with their elbows bent and forearms crossed in front of their torso. They then straighten up, slightly lifting their forearms as they rise.

With the right leg extended forward and the left knee bent, the upper body rotates to the right and back to the front. Simultaneously, the arms are held out to the sides with the forearms swinging up and down.
Starting with arms raised and bent in a rigid, angular pose, the performer executes a sharp, full-body pop, contracting the chest forward and bending the knees.
