Starting in a crouched stance, the performer creates rigid, angular shapes with their arms in a tutting style, followed immediately by a quick, full-body pop that ends in an abrupt stop.
The dancer moves their hips backward and then forward.
The person leans back and then lifts their right arm.
Starting from a standing position, the person executes a large circular torso rotation, beginning with a lean to the left and then circling backward, to the right, and forward.
A person rolls their left hand.

Starting in a right-leg lunge, the person twists their upper body to the left while raising their left arm and pulling the elbow back. They then untwist, returning to a forward-facing stance with both hands in front of the body.

The person performs a rigid, angular movement by extending the right arm forward and the left arm back, followed immediately by a full-body pop as they contract downward, bending their knees and lowering their torso.

The individual stands with their arms extended to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They step back with their left foot and clap their hands in front of their body, then step forward with their right foot while extending their arms forward and upward.

Starting with arms extended to the sides, the person sharply pulls them inward and down in a rigid, angular motion. This is synchronized with a quick full-body pop before they abruptly return to the starting pose.

The person starts bent over with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They jump up and swing their arms to the left while rotating their body slightly counterclockwise. Continuing the rotation, they point their forearms outward with their elbows still bent, then step back with their left foot and bend forward at the torso.

The person starts with arms extended straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground. They then perform a sudden full-body pop by sharply dropping their arms and bending their knees to an abrupt stop.

A person taps their right foot to the side while moving both arms out, in, up, and down. They then step forward onto their right foot, reaching their left arm straight up and bending their torso to the right.
The performer snaps their arms into a rigid, angular pose to the sides, followed by an immediate and abrupt full-body stop.
Starting from a standing position with both hands in front of their hips, the person leans back slightly while raising their shoulders.

A person performs a cross-stepping motion, stepping one leg across the other and then uncrossing it by stepping back and to the side while swinging their arms in opposition to their legs. They then step the same leg forward and outward, keeping their elbows bent at shoulder height while swinging their hands up and down.

Starting with legs apart and the torso leaning to the right, the person brings their right leg to meet the left while straightening up and raising both arms. They then lean their upper body to the left, initiating a circular rotation of the torso.

Starting from a position with arms raised and bent, the person steps back with their left leg while pulling their hands toward their body. They then bring their feet together, raise their arms again, and bow their torso forward.
The performer stands with their arms at their sides and forearms pointing down. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, bend over, and kneel, touching their right hand to the ground.
A person stands up from a crouched position.
Starting with their arms raised in a rigid, angular pose, the person executes a quick full-body pop, abruptly extending their arms outward and slightly upward.
With a hunched torso and elbows bent at shoulder level, the individual steps forward onto their right leg while sharply popping their upper body and arms downward and upward.
The person steps forward with their left foot and raises their arms toward the sky.

Starting bent over with their arms at their sides, the person runs in a half-circle. They then lean back with their elbows tucked at their sides and forearms pointing up. Finally, they step back and bring their bent arms in front of their body, positioning their forearms across their torso.
Starting from a kneeling position on the left knee, the person swings their bent left elbow up and down while slowly standing up.
With both hands raised and elbows bent at shoulder height, the person bends their torso and raises their right knee.
A person alternately raises each hand with a bent elbow in a popping motion that engages the arms and body, then crosses both forearms in front of their chest with another pop.
Step forward with the right foot from a crouched position.

The person draws their left arm in from the side, then pushes their right arm outward and brings it back. They swing their left arm up and down while dragging their right foot toward the left. Finally, both arms swing from the sides toward the center as the legs move with a popping-style motion.

The person starts slightly bent over with their arms at their sides. They straighten up, extend their arms out to the sides, and then lower them. Leaning slightly to the left, they move their right leg out, rotate 90 degrees clockwise, and extend their left arm straight out at shoulder height. They lower the left arm and extend the right at shoulder height before turning counterclockwise with both arms out to the sides. They then lift and lower the right knee while bending and straightening, pointing both arms straight forward and crossing them in front of each other. Finally, they swing their arms down and back while lifting and lowering the left knee.
body wave: A person initiates a fluid, wave-like motion from the head that travels down through the torso and hips. The undulation continues through the legs as the knees bend, while the arms, held out to the sides, move in sync with the body.
intro windup: Standing upright with arms at their sides, the person slightly vibrates their body in place.
The person stands upright with their arms at their sides and slightly vibrates their body in place.
A person stands with their arms extended at shoulder height, then shakes their knees as they lower their arms to their sides.

A person stands with feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended to the sides in a preparatory pose. They then transition into an introductory windup, performing subtle arm waves while bringing their feet together.
The dance sequence begins with a person walking in circles in a relaxed manner.
Standing upright with arms at their sides, the figure bends their elbows, bringing their forearms up in front of their torso.
From a squatting stance with both hands raised in front of the chest, the person subtly vibrates their forearms and knees.
A person slowly squats while uncrossing their forearms from their chest and sweeping them down and out.
Starting from a squatting position, the person slowly rises to a standing position.
Starting with arms extended to the sides, the person slowly brings them down and forward in a fluid, wave-like motion while sliding the left foot inward to close the stance.
Starting from a T-pose, the figure drops their arms to their sides, steps back, and then walks forward.
The motion sequence starts with the person in a T-pose.
This is an introduction to a dance sequence starting from a T-pose. The person lowers their arms and turns left while sliding their feet.
From a standing position, the person lightly taps their left foot.
From a standing position, the person performs a light popping dance.
With bent elbows raised sideways, the person steps their left foot back and then forward. As they step their right leg forward, they lower their arms.
Standing upright, the person performs light popping motions with their arms.
From a standing position, the person lightly swings their left leg.
From a standing position, the person rhythmically swings their torso back and forth, then taps their right foot.

The person starts with arms extended to the sides and slowly draws them toward the chest in a controlled, wave-like motion. Simultaneously, the right leg slides inward to bring the feet together on the ground.
Starting from a standing position, the person lightly swings their left leg up. They then begin lightly popping their upper body while stepping.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides, swaying slightly.
The performer stands upright, initiating a subtle, slow ticking motion with their arms while their feet, starting slightly apart, slowly drag together.
strutting: The figure performs a stylized, rhythmic walk forward with stiff-legged steps. The arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in coordination with the leg movements, waving as they move.

From a standing position with forearms crossed in front of the chest, the person raises their right knee while rotating the left forearm to lift the hand upward. They then lift the right knee again while swinging their arms in front of the body. After turning to the left, they raise their left hand with a bent elbow while leaning to the left. Finally, the person transitions into a right-leg lunge.
Starting from a right-leg lunge with hands folded in front of the body, the person takes two steps forward while swinging their arms in a popping style.

A person stands with their right arm extended straight to the side at shoulder height and their left arm at their side. They vibrate their knees toward each other while bending their elbows to bring their forearms in front of their body. They then extend their arms forward, pull them back to the previous position, and rotate their body 90 degrees clockwise.

The person turns their body to the right while swinging both arms straight forward, then steps forward with the left foot, swinging their arms out to the sides. They then step to the right with the right foot, pulling their arms toward the chest with bent elbows and raising the right hand higher. Finally, they turn to the left, opening their chest by swinging their elbows out to the sides.

Starting with both hands on the waist, the person swings their hands up and down, then twists their hips from side to side. After tapping the right foot forward and bringing it back, they sway their body side to side. They then bend their knees inward while swinging their arms inward from the sides.

The person starts in a bent-over position with their arms pointed outward. They vibrate their body while rotating 90 degrees clockwise, shifting their arms to point forward. Stepping forward, they extend their arms to the sides at shoulder height with the elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They then step forward again and kick their left leg out, lift it, and lower both the leg and their arms to their sides. Finally, they lift both arms and the left leg once more.

The individual struts forward with a rhythmic, stiff-legged gait and deliberate, alternating steps. Their arms are held bent and swing in opposition to their legs, while the torso twists with each step, creating a stylized walking motion.
A person stands with their arms at their sides. They lift their right knee and step forward while swinging and straightening their left arm forward at shoulder height.

Starting from a right-leg lunge with forearms crossed in front of the chest, the person steps forward onto their left foot, turning their body to the left while uncrossing their arms and swinging them forward.
A person performs a rhythmic strut, walking forward with stiff, exaggerated leg movements. Their bent arms swing in sync with their steps as their upper body bounces along with the motion.

A person stands with their arms at their sides, moves them out slightly, steps forward, and sways their knees from side to side. They step their right foot forward and back while bending their elbows to hold their forearms horizontally in front of their torso, lifting them as they rotate 90 degrees clockwise. They then point their arms upward and move them forward while bending over. As they straighten their torso, they bring the right arm up and the left arm down, straightening both. Widening their stance, they turn counterclockwise while pointing their forearms forward, then bring the right forearm down and the left up. They step the left leg forward and lean their torso forward while extending the right arm back and to the right in a circular motion. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise, extending the left arm straight forward and the right arm back.

A person stands in a staggered stance with their right leg forward and knees bent, holding their arms at their sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They lean back, straightening their arms and right leg, then step forward while lowering their arms to their sides and rotating 45 degrees counterclockwise.
A person performs a strutting walk by taking stiff, deliberate steps forward in a rhythmic manner. They then suddenly bend their right arm and both legs at the knees.

A person stands with their left arm pointed forward and their right arm at their side. They step back into a wide stance and rotate 90 degrees clockwise, bending their elbows to bring their forearms horizontally in front of their torso. Leaning forward, they rotate another 90 degrees clockwise on their right foot while lifting their left knee and pointing their left forearm upward. They then rotate a further 90 degrees clockwise and step forward with their left foot while punching both arms forward. Finally, they turn 90 degrees into a wide stance, extending their arms out to the sides.

The person starts with their arms at their sides and forearms pointing forward. They lean forward while rotating their torso slightly clockwise. They step forward with the left leg, then raise both arms to shoulder height with the forearms pointing forward, specifically pointing the right arm upward. Bending the torso forward, they point their arms to the left, with the right elbow bent so the forearm is positioned in front of the body. They then bring the right leg forward and straighten their body, dropping their arms to their sides. Finally, they step back while pointing their arms forward, then turn 90 degrees counterclockwise and stretch their arms out to the sides.

From a standing position, the person twists their torso downward to the right while raising their right arm. They then step sideways and turn their body to the right, bringing their left leg toward the right while raising both arms forward.

Starting from a standing position with both arms raised, the person bows forward, squats, and stands back up while keeping their arms raised. They then turn into a left-leg lunge with both arms still held upward and finish by kicking their right leg forward.

Starting from a left-leg lunge with both arms raised, the person turns to the right and kicks their right leg forward while extending their left arm forward. As they land their right leg back, they turn to face backward while raising their right arm. After swinging their left arm upward and their right arm downward, they turn their body.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm raised, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing upward. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise, return to the starting position, and step back with their right foot. Then, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise and take two steps backward.
The person steps forward on their left leg with a rhythmic knee pop, holding their arms close to the body in sharp, angular positions.

A person struts forward with a stiff-legged, rhythmic gait, deliberately placing one foot in front of the other. Their bent arms swing back and forth in sharp, robotic motions synchronized with each step.

The individual stands with their arms bent at the elbows and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, then punch their left arm forward while stepping back with their right leg.
The person performs a strutting walk, taking stiff, rhythmic steps forward and to the right. Their bent arms move in a stylized, coordinated motion in sync with their legs.
A person takes stiff, rhythmic steps forward, alternating legs with a slight bounce. Their arms are bent and move in short, sharp motions in opposition to their legs.

The person begins with their forearms crossed in front of the chest. They step to the left, raising the right hand while keeping the right arm across the chest. They then swing the right leg forward and tap the right foot back. Finally, the person transitions into a hunched stance with both forearms held in front of the chest.

The person swings their left leg forward, taps it back and to the side, and then transitions into a left-leg lunge. They bring their right leg toward the left while raising bent elbows out to the sides. Finally, they step backward while performing a body pop.
Starting with the right hand raised and the left hand held in front of the body with both elbows bent, the person switches their hand positions.
A person struts forward with a stiff-legged, rhythmic gait. With each step, their arms swing in opposition and their upper body twists from side to side.

The person raises their left arm forward while stepping their right leg forward and back. As they bring their left leg toward the right, they raise their right arm forward. They then widen their stance and extend their arms out to the sides. After swinging both arms up and down, they drop into a squat with both forearms held in front of their chest. Finally, while bringing the left leg toward the right, they bend forward with their arms still positioned in front of their chest.

With a bent torso and both arms held in front of the chest, the person rotates clockwise by twisting their torso in a large circular motion, ending in the same bent posture. They then stand up and swing their right leg and both arms. Finally, they bring their left leg to the right and move sideways after bowing.
Step forward with the right foot and lower the left arm.
A person struts forward with two rhythmic, stiff-legged steps. Their arms swing in opposition to their legs, and their upper body sways from side to side with each step.
The performer executes a strut walk, moving forward with stiff, rhythmic steps. Their bent arms swing in sync with the opposite leg, while the upper body subtly pops with each step.

Starting in a squat with both arms extended outward, the person brings their left leg to meet their right while pulling their elbows into their body with hands held up. They then lift their right knee while positioning their left hand in front of their chest with a bent elbow.

A person stands upright with their arms held in front and elbows bent. They step their feet out and rotate their torso 90 degrees counterclockwise while moving their arms and forearms up and down in a robotic manner. They then step forward with their arms extended out to the sides and rotate 90 degrees clockwise, bringing their arms to their sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing down. Finally, they move their arms further in front of their body while maintaining their posture.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They clap their hands while bringing their knees together, then slide their feet to the left. Next, they lift their left knee and kick the leg out slightly while pushing their right arm toward it and extending their left arm outward.

A person stands in a staggered stance with their right leg forward and left leg back, both knees bent. Their right arm rests at their side while their left arm is bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing upward. They lean back and to the side, lowering their arms in front of their body with the elbows bent. They pull their arms toward their torso and then extend them out to the sides, straightening their posture and leaning back as they do so. They then lean to the opposite side, straighten up, and hold their arms out with the forearms pointing downward while maintaining a wide stance.
A person struts forward with a stiff-legged gait, taking deliberate, rhythmic steps. Their arms swing in an exaggerated, stylized motion opposite the legs, while the torso twists with each step.
With both arms raised sideways, the person brings their arms and right leg to the center, then steps the right leg forward and back. Finally, they stomp their right foot out to the side.

The performer executes a rhythmic, stiff-legged strut, stepping from side to side. Their arms move robotically, contracting inward and extending into sharp, angular poses synchronized with the footwork.

The person raises their straight right leg while bending their torso forward with both hands in front of the body. As they stomp their right leg down, they squat with both forearms crossed in front of the chest. They then stand up and step sideways while jabbing their right arm straight out. After bringing both forearms back to the chest, the person steps forward.

The person swings their arms from the sides toward the center and turns their body to tap their right foot. They then turn to the left and step sideways. They raise and lower their left knee while swinging their arms, then place their left foot out to the side. Finally, they sway their body.

A person stands with a wide stance, arms at their sides, elbows bent, and forearms pointing forward. They vibrate their arms rapidly while turning 90 degrees counterclockwise and kicking their right leg forward with a bent knee.
With the right arm raised straight forward, the person performs a series of popping dance moves while moving around.

After popping their body, the person transitions into a right-leg lunge. They lean their torso fully backward while raising their left arm, then twist their torso into a left-leg lunge to complete the turn.

The figure performs a strutting walk with several stiff-legged steps forward. The arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to the legs, while the upper body and head make small, sharp, rhythmic movements.

The figure struts forward with stiff, rhythmic steps, adding a slight bounce to each stride. While the upper body remains upright, the arms perform sharp, angular movements in sync with the steps, with the left arm bent and pumping.

The individual stands with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height. They then bring their arms to their sides, bending the left elbow so the forearm points upward. From this position, they swing their arms in alternating vertical directions while repeatedly bending and straightening their torso.

The figure performs a strutting walk, taking a large, deliberate step forward with the right leg while swinging the right arm up and the left arm back. As the right foot plants, the torso twists to the right and the left leg lifts, completing a single, stylized, stiff-legged step.

Starting in a squat stance, the person swings their bent left elbow outward. They then bring their left leg toward the right while raising their right hand upward with a bent elbow. After bowing, they kick their right leg forward and land back in a squat stance.

The person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They pivot their forearms forward and step their right foot forward, then rotate their torso to the right and halfway back. Finally, they bend over their right leg, returning their arms to their sides with the forearms pointing down.
The figure performs a rhythmic strut with stiff, deliberate steps forward. Their arms are held bent and swing in opposition to the legs, contributing to the stylized and rigid motion.
The person mimics a walking motion in a popping style, swinging their right leg back and forth and moving their arms without stepping forward.

The figure stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise, pointing the left arm forward and dropping the right arm to the side. Rotating another 90 degrees clockwise to face the opposite direction, they let both arms hang at their sides. They then rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise back to the previous orientation and step forward, before rotating back and bringing their hands together in front of their body. Finally, they step to the side and point their forearms upward, keeping their elbows bent in front of the body.

A figure struts forward with a rhythmic, stiff-legged gait, swinging their arms in opposition. Each step is accompanied by a slight, sharp bounce in the upper body, giving the walk a stylized, robotic quality.

The individual performs a rhythmic, stiff-legged walk, stepping forward with the left leg followed by the right. The arms are held in a bent position, with the left arm tucked near the chest and the right arm extended to the side, accentuating the popping style of the movement.
The person struts forward with stiff, rhythmic steps. Their bent arms swing in opposition to their legs, while the torso twists and pops with each step.

After twisting their torso, the person takes a side step by bringing the left leg to the right. They then bow with their hands overlapped at the chest. While maintaining the bent torso, the person pulls both elbows back and squats with their knees turned inward.

A person performs a rhythmic strut in place, taking stiff steps forward and backward. They swing their arms in wide, opposing motions, bringing the right arm up and across the chest as the left arm swings back.
A person struts while raising their right arm with a bent elbow and twisting their legs outward to widen their stance. They then plant their right foot forward, settling into a fixed right-leg lunge.
A person performs a stylized, rhythmic walk with stiff legs. With each step, the upper body sways from side to side, and the bent arms swing in opposition to the legs.
Facing forward, the person takes stylized, stiff-legged steps. Their arms swing widely in opposition to the legs as the torso twists with each movement.

After a popping motion, the individual steps forward on their right leg while raising their left hand to chest level with a bent elbow. They then lift their right knee, bringing their right hand up to meet the left in front of the chest.

The figure stands with their arms in front and elbows bent. They circle their right leg out to the side while rolling their forearms around each other, then extend their arms to the sides with elbows bent—the right forearm pointing up and the left pointing down. They shake their arms at their sides and rotate 90 degrees clockwise before stepping forward and lowering their arms. They then pull their arms back and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise to face forward again. Finally, they step forward and twist their torso from side to side while keeping their arms in position, before swinging their arms back and stepping forward with the left leg.

A person performs a strut, a popping-style walk, by moving forward with stiff, deliberate steps. Their arms are held out and move in opposition, with the left arm extending forward as the right arm moves backward.
The figure struts forward with stiff, rhythmic steps and exaggerated knee bends. Their arms are held out to the sides, executing sharp, isolated popping movements in sync with the leg motions.
A person performs a strutting walk, moving forward with rhythmic, alternating steps. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing in opposition to their legs to complete the stylized motion.

Standing upright, the individual holds their arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They lean forward slightly and point their forearms down, then move their arms up to the right and then the left while keeping the torso still. After bringing their forearms in front of their upper arms and leaning back slightly, they repeat this motion at a faster pace. Next, they step forward and to the side, kicking the right leg forward and dropping their bent arms to their sides. They stretch the right leg back and lean over with their elbows bent at their sides. Rotating 180 degrees to face the opposite direction, they stand in a wide stance with their arms pointed forward. They then lean forward and extend their arms to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing up and forward. They kick the left leg forward, step down, and rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise, leaning forward slightly with their arms at their sides. Finally, they point their forearms up and then back down as they stand straight with their arms out to the sides.

Starting in a wide stance, the person rhythmically shifts their body from side to side, raising the corresponding arm forward with each movement. They then lift their left knee high while swinging their straight left arm backward before returning the leg to its original position.

Starting in a right-leg lunge, the person leans forward and crosses their arms in front of them, then straightens up while opening their arms to the sides. They step forward and repeat this upper-body sequence. Finally, they sway from side to side and bring their right knee up and across toward the left.

The person stands with their arms extended to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They stretch their arms out and pull them back in while lifting their right knee and turning their body to the right. Maintaining their arm position, they then raise them to shoulder height with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They lift and lower their left knee, then bend over it with their forearms held horizontally in front of the torso. Straightening up, they extend their arms to the sides with elbows bent and rotate 90 degrees clockwise before leaning forward slightly, keeping their arms to the sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward.

The figure stands on the right foot with the left leg bent at the knee and the foot pointed back. Their arms are extended to the sides, bent at the elbows with the forearms pointing forward. They bend forward and swing the left knee up, then place the left leg down and return to an upright position while keeping the arms in place. Next, they rotate their forearms upward and step back, maintaining an upright posture. They bend forward again, pointing the forearms down, before rotating 90 degrees clockwise. As they rotate another 90 degrees clockwise, they straighten their arms to their sides and then extend them outward with the elbows bent and forearms pointing up. They step the left foot forward and bend over, arms pointing down. They then step the right foot back and straighten up with arms pointing upward, before bending over again with arms pointing down and elbows bent. Finally, they rotate the torso 90 degrees clockwise and sway from side to side with elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body, ending by straightening up and extending the arms to the sides at shoulder height with the elbows bent and forearms pointing up.

The person stands on their right leg with the left leg extended behind them and arms pointing forward at shoulder height. They then bring the left knee forward and step back while keeping their arms bent at their sides.
The person performs a stylized walk, stepping forward with the right leg while leaning the torso forward. Their arms swing in a coordinated, rhythmic motion opposite the legs to complete the strut.

A person steps forward with their left leg, crossing it over the right while twisting their torso to the left. Simultaneously, they raise their arms with bent elbows and hands pointing upward. Then, they bring the left leg back while rotating their hands downward, keeping their elbows bent at shoulder height.
The person struts forward with a stiff-legged, rhythmic gait, their arms bent at the elbows and moving in sync with each deliberate step.
The dancer turns to the right and performs a strutting walk with stiff, high-knee steps, rhythmically swinging their bent arms.

A person stands hunched over with their elbows bent and forearms held in front of their torso. They lift their left knee and walk forward, turning to face the opposite direction while raising their arms. They step back and to the left with their arms at their sides, then step to the right and raise their arms while swaying their torso from side to side. Finally, they bring their left leg forward and point their forearms down, before leaning forward and pointing their arms to the left.

A person performs a stylized strutting walk, taking a long step forward with their left leg while extending the right leg back. They hold a dynamic pose, with the right arm bent near the head and the left arm extended to the side.

The person performs a strutting walk, taking stiff-legged steps forward starting with the left leg followed by the right. The arms move in opposition with a rhythmic, popping motion, swinging forward as the opposite leg steps.

Starting from a right-leg lunge with the torso bent downward, the person raises their left knee and returns it to its original position while swinging their arms in opposition. They then raise both hands with elbows bent at shoulder height and bow their upper body.

The person starts hunched over with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of them. They straighten and step forward with the left leg, then bend forward again, extending their arms to the sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They straighten, step back with the left leg, and bend over again in the same pose. Finally, they step forward with the left leg while repositioning their arms: the right arm is bent with the forearm horizontal across the torso, and the left arm is bent with the forearm pointing upward.
A person struts forward with a stiff-legged, rhythmic gait, swaying from side to side. Their arms are held out and swing in sharp, stylized opposition to their legs.

The person stands with their left foot forward and right foot back, arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They rotate 90 degrees clockwise and lean forward, pointing their forearms straight ahead. Leaning to the right, they lift their left knee and straighten their torso while keeping their arms positioned in front. They then step back with the left foot and rotate counterclockwise, maintaining the same arm position. Next, they pull their arms back to their sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. Stepping forward, they point their forearms down and then forward. Finally, they lower their arms, lean forward slightly, and rotate their torso to the left and then to the right.
The figure performs a rhythmic strutting walk with stiff-legged steps forward. Their bent arms swing in opposition to the legs, creating a stylized bouncing motion.

A person steps forward with a straight left leg, bending their right knee and swinging their right arm forward. They then transition into a squat, leaning the torso forward and pulling the left leg back. To finish, they rise and lift their left knee high while raising both arms upward, then lower the leg while bringing their forearms down in front of their chest.

Beginning with the left knee bent and the right leg extended forward, the person brings the left leg back while swinging both arms. They then straighten their torso while raising both hands with bent elbows.

A person performs a strutting walk, lifting their right knee high before stepping forward. As the right foot plants, the arms execute a sharp, coordinated popping motion, swinging the right arm down and across the chest while the left arm moves back.
The person struts forward, taking a stiff step with the right leg while leaning their torso to the right. Their bent arms move in opposition to the legs, with the left arm swinging up and forward.
A person leans forward while lowering both arms.
With the right foot planted, the person repeatedly raises and lowers their left knee while swinging their arms in opposition.

The person leans their torso forward, raising their elbows to shoulder height. They lift their left knee high and take a large step forward. As they step, they swing their arms up and down by rotating them, keeping their elbows elevated throughout the movement.

The person stands with their arms extended in front of their body. They bend their elbows and slide to the left, holding their forearms horizontally in front of them. Bending at the waist, they reach their arms to the right and then return their torso to the center while remaining bent over. As they straighten up, they shift their torso slightly to the left and then to the right, maintaining the position of their arms.

Starting in a squat with the right arm extended sideways and the left arm bent in front of the body, the person leans to the right while sweeping their left arm across the torso. They then swing from side to side before lifting their right knee and raising both arms upward.
A person twists their torso from left to right while in a squatting position, keeping their arms crossed over their chest throughout the movement.
A person sways from side to side, shifting their weight and lifting the corresponding arm with a bent elbow. Each sway is punctuated by a brief, sharp pause before the motion reverses.

The person performs a series of sharp, robotic popping motions, extending and retracting their arms in a staccato rhythm. They then twist their torso to the left and right while continuing the synchronized, sharp arm movements.
ticking: The person performs a series of rapid, isolated, and jerky movements with their head, shoulders, and arms. These staccato motions, where the arms flex and extend in quick bursts, create the effect of a ticking clock.
A person moves forward with stiff, rusty movements, pausing between each motion.

A person stands with their right leg forward and both hands in front of their chest, with elbows slightly raised from their body. They perform a series of quick, isolated, side-to-side jerking movements with their knees and torso, mimicking a ticking motion.

The individual stands with their right arm pointed up and to the side and their left arm at their side, both with elbows bent. They rotate their forearms in a robotic motion while shifting from side to side and sliding their left foot out and back.

The person stands with their arms extended to the sides, rotating their elbows to point their forearms forward. They move their forearms out to the sides and back, then step backward while pointing them upward. After pointing their forearms forward again and bending their knees to lower their body, they rotate their torso from side to side. Finally, they shift their torso back slightly and bring their arms up, pointing them to the right.

A person stands with their arms at their sides. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and slide sideways by pulsing their knees in and out. Simultaneously, they extend their arms to the sides and vibrate them, keeping their elbows bent and forearms pointed downward. They then rotate clockwise back to the original orientation, turn 90 degrees counterclockwise again, and finally return to the starting position.

A person performs a series of quick, isolated, and rhythmic movements with their upper body and arms, keeping their elbows bent. These small, jerky motions shift from side to side while the lower body remains relatively stationary.
Starting from a standing position, the person moves their arms up and down in a series of quick, small, clock-like ticking motions.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent at the elbow, with the forearm pointing up. They rotate their forearm in a circle from the elbow while keeping the rest of their body still.
From a T-pose, the person lightly moves both arms and knees.
Standing with arms extended to the sides and forearms pointing down, the person shakes their left forearm and left leg out to the side, then rotates slightly counterclockwise on the right foot.

The performer executes a ticking motion, snapping their head and torso from side to side in quick, isolated bursts. While the lower body remains still, the bent arms move in a staccato, clock-like rhythm.

The figure moves their arms and upper body in a series of sharp, isolated jerks, creating a robotic effect. Each position is hit distinctly and held briefly, breaking the motion into small, staccato intervals like the ticking of a clock.

Starting in an upright position with arms at their sides, the figure lifts the left arm slightly outward while shifting the hips and knees sideways. They drop the arm back to the side and kick the right foot forward, then slide side-to-side in a wide stance, moving the arms up and back at the shoulders. After rotating 90 degrees clockwise, they vibrate in place while swinging their arms back and forth, then turn to face forward while continuing to vibrate their shoulders. They rotate clockwise again and bend over with elbows bent and arms in front of the torso, pointing the forearms downward. Finally, they bend their knees and straighten their torso, pulling the arms up and bending the left elbow to bring the forearm horizontally across the body, with the right arm at the side and the feet spread wide.
The individual stands with their arms at their sides, then steps back while bending the right elbow to position the right forearm horizontally in front of the torso, which vibrates from side to side.

The person stands with their arms forward and elbows bent, holding their forearms in front of their torso. They bend their right knee and lean forward, shaking their torso from side to side as they bend over, then drop their arms to their sides as they begin to rise.

Standing in place, a person performs a series of sharp, isolated ticking motions with their arms, first alternating and then moving them together. Simultaneously, they lift and lower their left knee twice.
reverb: The dancer stands with their right leg forward and left leg back. Their right arm is at their side, and their left arm is extended to the side at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the forearm pointing down. They lean forward slowly while rotating their right arm in a slow circle.

The dancer stands with their right leg forward and left leg back. Their right arm is at their side, and their left arm is extended sideways at shoulder height with the elbow bent and the forearm pointing down. Maintaining this posture, they vibrate their body forward and back.
robot: Starting from a standing position with arms extended to the sides, the person executes a robotic motion by folding their left forearm forward, followed by the right. The sequence concludes with a sharp, mechanical bow of the upper body.
A person holds their left arm in front of their chest, maintains the angle of the arm, and then bends down.
Starting in a slight squat with both arms extended forward, the person lowers their body with robotic movements.

A person steps forward with their left leg while swinging their left arm forward, followed by a step back with the right leg that initiates a sharp, mechanical turn to the right. The entire motion is performed in a jerky, robotic style with isolated body movements.

The person performs a robotic dance characterized by sharp, isolated movements of the limbs and torso. They transition rapidly between crouched and upright stances, with their arms jerking into various bent, angular positions.

A person stands upright with their elbows bent and forearms in front of their torso. They extend their arms forward and lean over their bent right knee, shaking their forearms slightly while leaned over. They then straighten their torso back to a standing position with their knees bent, maintaining the same arm position while vibrating their forearms.
Walks forward with robotic movements, pausing all body parts between each step.
The individual stands in a wide stance, bent forward with their arms hanging down. They lift their head and push their arms downward, then slightly raise their torso.
Starting from a standing position with the right arm extended forward and the left hand in front of the chest, the person performs robotic movements by swaying their head and torso.
With feet apart and hands held out to the sides and forward, the person bows forward with robotic movements.

The individual stands with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the body. They bend forward at the waist and rotate their torso 90 degrees clockwise while maintaining their arm position. They then straighten up and return to their original orientation, stepping back while pushing their arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. Finally, they step back again and bring their arms forward, elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the torso.
The person begins with jerky, isolated arm movements, bending at the elbows. They then cross their arms over their chest before transitioning into a pose with one leg forward and the other back.

Starting from a standing position with both forearms held in front of the chest, the person opens their chest by pulling their bent elbows back. They then bow forward while maintaining a scarecrow-like upper-body posture. After swinging their arms side to side and back and forth, they lower themselves into a squat. Finally, they swing their arms forward with their knees turned inward and bring the right leg toward the left.

Starting from a bent position, the person stands up, raising their left knee while extending their right arm to the side. As the left foot lands, they swing their arms forward. After bringing the left leg back toward the right, they perform a sequence of arm swings while moving side to side.

Starting from a squat with a fully bowed torso, the person steps backward while maintaining the bowed position and rotating their hands up and down. They then stand up and raise their left knee while swinging both arms up and down. The entire sequence is performed with abrupt stops, creating a robotic effect.
A person performs a robotic motion, placing their left hand on their hip followed by a series of sharp, isolated side-to-side jerks of the head and torso.

Standing in a wide stance, the person performs a robotic pop primarily with the upper body. They raise their right arm with a bent elbow, followed by the left, moving each arm around. The person then turns and steps forward with robotic movements.

The person performs an upper body isolation, shifting their chest and shoulders side-to-side in a wave-like motion. As the torso moves, their arms gradually extend outward from the body, starting with bent elbows and finishing fully extended to the sides.
While holding the right arm raised sideways and the left hand in front of the chest with both elbows bent, the person bows with extended knees and then lunges on the right leg by bending both knees.

A person stands with their left arm bent at the elbow and forearm pointing forward, and their right arm at their side with the elbow bent. They lean their torso to the left and push their hips in that direction with a slight vibration, then return to their original position while keeping their arms still.
The individual performs a robotic dance move, stepping forward with the right leg while keeping their arms bent in a stiff, mechanical posture to isolate upper body movement.

The dancer performs a robotic movement, sharply bringing their right arm up and across the chest while the upper body twists to the left. The motion concludes with a quick, mechanical bend in the knees, maintaining an isolated and jerky style.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm extended to the side, elbow bent and forearm pointing forward. They swing their forearms upward and then hold both arms out to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing toward their hips.

The performer executes a robotic sequence, beginning with their left arm bent upward and their right arm across their chest. They perform jerky, isolated movements, raising their right arm and lowering their left as they transition into a lower crouch.
A dancer moves robotically, lowering their left arm with pauses between each motion.

A figure performs a robotic popping dance through a series of sharp, isolated movements. The arms bend at the elbows and wrists in a jerky, staccato fashion while the torso twists and contracts to the side.

Standing with forearms crossed above their head, the person performs a robotic bow. While maintaining this upper-body posture, they bend and straighten their knees. Finally, they return to an upright position with their hands held in front of their body. Short pauses between each movement create a mechanical, robot-like quality.

With both forearms crossed in front of the chest, the person extends their left arm sideways while keeping the elbow bent. They then swing their left elbow up and down while moving their left leg sideways.
Facing left, the person swings their left arm up and down, then lowers their body into a light squat. Short pauses between these movements create a robotic feel.

The performer stands with their arms at their sides and rotates 90 degrees clockwise. They then bend forward, swing their arms at their sides while straightening back up, and finish by punching their right arm forward.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms pointing up in front of their torso. They extend their arms out to the sides with their forearms still pointing upward, then bend forward while directing their forearms toward the ground. Finally, they shift their torso to the left while maintaining this posture.
A person performs a robotic dance characterized by jerky, isolated movements of the upper body. They extend one arm to the side followed by the other, then execute a sharp chest pop.

Starting with arms outstretched, the person quickly pulls them in, performing sharp, isolated movements with their arms and torso. They continue with jerky, robotic motions, alternating between extending and contracting their limbs while turning and crouching.

The individual stands with their arms extended at shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They bend forward at the waist, rotating their forearms toward the floor, then turn their body 90 degrees counterclockwise before returning to an upright position.
A person walks forward in a robotic manner, pausing between each step.
A person moves to the left in a robotic manner, raising their arms in the air with distinct pauses.

The individual stands with legs apart and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height, elbows bent with forearms pointing downward. They vibrate their arms slightly and move their knees in and out while bending forward, then straighten back up.
A person performs robotic, jerky movements with bent elbows while bending and turning their body.

The individual leans their torso forward, raising their arms with bent elbows and palms facing downward. They then sway their upper body from side to side while keeping their legs and hips stationary.
direction shift: The person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm raised above their head with a bent elbow. They wave their left forearm up and down while slightly rotating their torso from left to right.
A person holds their arms up in front of their chest, rotates to the left, and bends down.

The person performs a sharp shift to the right by twisting their torso and hips and planting their left foot forward. They hold both arms bent and raised, with the right arm higher than the left, to accentuate the sudden movement.
After lifting their right knee high with the left elbow raised and hand pointing upward, the person twists their body to the right while maintaining the pose.
Standing in a slight crouch with arms extended to the sides, a person sharply twists their upper body and head to the right while keeping their legs relatively still.
A person rotates 90 degrees to the left while shaking their shoulders.
In a squatting stance with elbows bent and raised sideways, the person rotates their body to the left with a robotic movement.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They then rotate 90 degrees clockwise.
A person quickly pivots to change direction, twisting their upper body while keeping their arms raised and bent.
A person performs a quick popping motion by sharply twisting their upper body and hips to the right. As the torso shifts, the left leg extends slightly forward while the right leg bends.
The individual performs a rapid upper-body shift, quickly pivoting their torso to the right.

A person stands with their arms extended to the sides and elbows bent, with the left forearm pointing down and the right pointing up. They cross their right leg behind their left and spin 180 degrees clockwise, shifting their arms so that both forearms point upward with the elbows still bent as they complete the rotation.
The person pivots sharply to the left, executing a quick change in direction. During the turn, the left arm is raised and bent high, while the right arm is held lower in front of the torso.
From a standing position, the person turns their body to the left and right alternately, with short pauses in between.

The performer stands upright in a wide stance with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent at the elbow, forearm pointing outward. Maintaining this posture, they rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise.
The individual performs a quick, sharp shift of the upper body to the left, moving the torso and head while the lower body remains stationary as an anchor.
The performer executes a quick, sharp twist of the upper body and head to the left as the arms snap into a bent, angular pose. This isolated torso shift then rapidly returns to face forward.
The figure stands with their arms at their sides and their left elbow bent, with the forearm pointing forward, as they slowly rotate clockwise.

Starting from a T-pose, the person sharply shifts direction, snapping their arms into a new configuration. The left arm bends with the elbow pointing upward, while the right arm pulls down and back, causing the upper torso to tilt.
A person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward, then spins 180 degrees clockwise.
The person holds the pose and then rotates their hips to the left.
Standing with the left leg forward, the person turns their body to the right and back.
The person holds the current pose and then rotates to the right.
The upper body and head twist quickly from side to side in an alternating motion. The arms follow this sharp movement, jerking and repositioning with each turn.
A person starts in a pose with the left arm raised and the right arm lowered, then rotates to the right while keeping the lower body stationary.
The dancer stands with their right leg forward and left back, knees bent and arms at their sides with elbows bent. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise twice.
A person stands with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height and rotates clockwise on their left foot.
The person twists their feet and crosses their legs with both hands raised, then spins while uncrossing their legs.
The person suddenly changes direction.
The person sharply shifts direction, twisting their torso and head quickly to the right while simultaneously raising their left arm toward their head.

A person stands upright with their right arm extended to the side and their left arm bent at the elbow, holding the forearm horizontally in front of the body. Maintaining this posture, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise while wiggling their arms.

A person snaps their upper body from left to right in a quick, sharp twist. This popping motion is accentuated by the arms, which swing in opposite directions to follow the abrupt change in direction.
The lower body turns to the left first, followed by the upper body.
scarecrow: A person balances on their left leg in a stiff, scarecrow-like pose with the right knee raised and arms bent outward. While maintaining this rigid posture, they execute a series of small, sharp, popping twitches with their head, arms, and raised leg.

The dancer performs a scarecrow move, holding their bent arms raised while jerking them up and down. Simultaneously, the left leg kicks forward and back in a stiff, disjointed manner while the right leg remains mostly stationary.

Starting from a standing position, the person lifts their bent arms to shoulder height and widens their stance into a squat, forming a scarecrow pose. While maintaining this rigid posture, they begin to sway slowly from side to side.

Starting from a scarecrow pose in a squat stance, the person slowly sways their upper body from side to side. They then turn their body while bending the left knee. Finally, the lowered and leaning upper body rises upward while performing a torso roll.
A person performs a scarecrow move by stiffly extending their left arm and leg outward, then repeats the same jerky, rigid motion with their right arm and leg.

A person performs a scarecrow move by stiffly extending their left arm and leg outward, then repeating the action with their right arm and leg. The movements are sharp, isolated, and jerky, with the limbs moving independently in a disjointed fashion.
Balancing on the right leg, the person kicks their left leg back while holding their arms in stiff, opposing, and angular positions to create a scarecrow-like silhouette.

A person leans their upper body forward and to the right, drawing their arms inward with a stiff, angular motion. The left elbow bends sharply, bringing the hand up toward the shoulder in a scarecrow-like pose.

The person starts in a wide squat with their arms raised and elbows bent at shoulder height. They rotate their arms in a circular motion, finishing with their arms still at shoulder level but with their hands pointing downward. Finally, they bow their upper body while squatting deeper and turning their knees inward.
The person stands with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing up. They then bend their knees and lean forward slightly, pointing their forearms downward.
A person stands with a wide stance, arms raised to shoulder height with elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They lean forward while repeatedly moving their knees inward and outward.
leg wave: A person performs a leg wave with their right leg, sending a fluid motion down from the hip. The wave travels sequentially through the knee and ankle while the upper body and arms remain relatively still.

A person performs a leg wave with their right leg, sending a fluid, undulating motion from the hip down through the knee to the ankle. The left leg remains straight for support, while the upper body stays relatively still with arms extended to the sides.
A person swings both legs to the side.
Starting from a slight squat, the person waves their left arm and leg, then the motion travels to their right leg.

A person performs a leg wave with their right leg, initiating a fluid, undulating motion at the hip that travels smoothly through the knee to the ankle. The left leg and upper body remain relatively stationary.
With elbows bent and hands held in front of the chest, the person moves their left leg toward their right and then sideways while swaying both legs.
With both hands held in front of the chest and elbows bent, the person waves their legs.
A person twists their legs to the side.

Standing on the left leg, the person initiates a fluid wave with their right leg, starting from the hip. This ripple-like motion travels down through the bent knee and concludes as the foot lifts off the ground.

A person performs a leg wave with their left leg, creating a fluid ripple effect. The motion originates at the hip and flows sequentially through the knee and ankle, while the right leg and upper body remain mostly still.

Standing on the left leg, the person initiates a fluid, wave-like motion in the right leg, starting from the hip. This ripple travels sequentially through the thigh, knee, and ankle, finishing as the foot extends to tap the ground.

Viewed from the front, a figure executes a leg wave with the right leg, starting from the hip and flowing down through the knee and ankle. The left leg remains planted for support, while the arms are held bent and out to the sides for balance throughout the movement.

While keeping their upper body and left leg mostly stationary, the individual performs a wave-like motion with their right leg. The movement originates at the hip, flows through the bending knee, and finishes at the ankle, creating a fluid ripple effect.

A person stands and performs a leg wave with their right leg, initiating the movement at the hip. The wave travels downward as the knee bends and the foot lifts off the ground, creating a fluid ripple through the limb while the left leg provides support.
A person performs a leg wave with their right leg while keeping the left leg stationary for support. The fluid motion originates at the hip and travels smoothly down through the knee to the ankle.

Standing on the left leg, the person initiates a fluid wave from the right hip. The right knee bends and moves forward as the foot lifts and extends to complete the wave, while the arms are held out to the sides for balance.

The performer sends a wave down their raised left leg, starting at the hip and flowing through the knee to the foot as the leg straightens. The right leg remains planted for support, and the arms are extended to the sides for balance.
A person performs a leg wave with their left leg, initiating the motion at the hip. The wave ripples down through the knee to the foot while the arms are held out to the sides for balance.

A person stands with bent arms and initiates a wave through their right leg, starting from a bent knee. As the wave travels down and straightens the right leg, the left leg bends to support the shift in weight.
Swings the left leg to the right.

A person performs a right-leg wave, creating a fluid, undulating motion that begins at the hip and flows down through the knee to the foot. The left leg remains straight for support, while the upper body and arms are held relatively still.

With arms extended to the sides, the performer sends a fluid wave down their right leg. The movement originates at the hip, ripples through the bending knee, and finishes at the foot, while the left leg remains straight.

Standing with arms extended to the sides, the individual sends a fluid, wave-like motion down their right leg, originating at the hip and flowing through the knee to the foot, while keeping the left leg straight for support.
chest pop: Forcefully pop the chest forward and backward.

The performer starts with their arms at their sides, then brings them forward as the chest contracts. This is followed immediately by a sharp, outward chest pop as the arms are thrown back and the head tilts upward.
leg pop: After performing a body wave from the upper body down to the legs, the person steps backward while popping their left leg.

A person stands on their right leg with their arms at their sides and their left leg pointed back. Leaning slightly forward, they kick their left lower leg back and to the right before straightening the leg and extending it to the side, maintaining their arm position throughout the movement.

Balancing on their left leg, a person performs a leg pop by sharply extending the right leg forward and to the side. This quick, percussive motion originates at the knee before the leg relaxes back to a neutral position.

A person stands on their left leg and performs a sharp muscle contraction in their bent right leg, which is held forward, creating a popping motion. Their arms are raised and bent as they slightly twist their upper body to the left.
glitch: A person stands straight with both arms raised sideways and remains motionless.
strobing: Starting with the left arm extended to the side, the person sharply snaps the left forearm inward while simultaneously making an abrupt turn to the left. This initiates a clockwise rotation and a shift to the left, performed in a popping style.
The person moves forward with quick, choppy steps in a strobing motion. Their arms and legs transition sharply between distinct poses, creating a jerky, stop-motion effect.
In a squat stance, the person waves their arms from right to left and then back to the right while turning their body to the right.

The person begins in a left-leg lunge with elbows bent and arms raised to shoulder height, palms facing down. They step forward with their right leg, swinging their arms in toward the body and then back out with palms turned upward. Finally, they take another step forward with their left leg while swinging their arms at shoulder height.

The person lifts their left knee while swinging both hands beneath it, then swings their left arm to the right and back. They begin popping their arms straight up and down in an alternating fashion. The left knee is raised again with both hands held in front of the chest. After lowering the leg, they raise both arms with elbows bent sideways in a scarecrow-like posture. Finally, they bow with both hands in front of their body. The movement is segmented into distinct, isolated poses, creating a strobing effect as the body transitions between crouched and extended positions.

Starting from a standing position with feet apart, the person lowers into a squat while raising their bent elbows to the sides. From this wide, crouched squat, they swing their left arm to the right and snap it back with a popping-style movement. The motion is segmented into distinct, isolated poses, creating a strobing effect as the body transitions between crouched and extended positions.
After turning around, the person steps forward while popping their arms and swinging them from side to side.
The performer slowly steps forward with their left leg while moving their right arm forward and their left arm back. They then lean forward slightly and step back with the left leg.

The individual executes a rapid, jerky switch between two distinct poses, creating a strobing effect. They step backward with their left leg and twist their torso to the left while extending their left arm out to the side.
tutting: The person stands with their arms extended to the sides at shoulder height, forearms bent upward. They lean forward slightly and rotate their forearms to point downward, then step back and return to an upright position, rotating their forearms back up. Finally, they step to the side and point their forearms down once more.

A person moves their arms through a series of angular poses, beginning with their arms extended to the sides. They raise their arms into a goalpost position with bent elbows before lowering and extending them outward again.
double strut: The person struts forward, stepping and then quickly tapping the same foot before repeating the sequence with the other leg. Their arms are bent at the elbows and swing back and forth in rhythm with each step.

The person starts bent over with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front. They rise and step their left leg to the side, extending their arms outward with the elbows bent and forearms pointing down. Then, they kick their left leg back and lean forward, pushing the left arm forward while keeping the right arm straight at their side.

A person struts forward, stepping with one foot while the other performs a quick double tap on the ground before advancing. Their arms are bent at the elbows and held out to the sides, with the upper body twisting slightly with each step.

The person starts slightly hunched with their arms extended to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They straighten up while stepping forward with the left leg, then transition into a stylized strut, stepping first with the right foot and then the left. Throughout the walk, their arms remain raised and bent at the elbows, moving in opposition to their legs.
Standing slightly bent over with their arms at their sides, a person wiggles their hips, rotates 90 degrees clockwise, and wiggles their hips again.

The person stands slightly bent over with their arms out and elbows bent so the forearms point downward. They lean to the right, pointing both arms in that direction, then shift their torso to the left, pointing the right arm across while bending the left elbow so the forearm points upward.

locking wrist rolling: Starting from a crouched position, the individual rotates their forearms and wrists in circular motions with their elbows held close to the body. They then sharply extend both arms out to the sides, freezing in a locked pose.
The person holds a locking pose while rolling their waist.

The person stands with their arms forward and elbows bent. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and return to the starting position, thrusting their left arm straight forward. They rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise again while circling their left forearm above their head, then kick their left leg out and straighten their arms to clap in front of their body. After lowering the leg, they thrust their left arm forward, then twist 90 degrees clockwise and thrust their right arm forward, bringing the left arm to their side.

The person stands with their arms extended to the sides and their feet wide apart. They bring their feet together and straighten their torso while raising the left elbow to shoulder height, crossing their arms in front of the body. They then quickly swing both arms forward, finishing with the left elbow bent at shoulder height and the hand pointing upward, while the right arm remains extended to the side.

With the left elbow bent at shoulder height and the hand pointing upward, and the right arm extended straight to the side, the person steps forward while slowly swinging the left arm forward and the right arm backward. As the left leg is brought toward the right, both arms swing in a wide circular motion. The person then alternates extending each leg to the side.

The person performs a wrist-rolling locking dance while swaying their upper body from side to side. While maintaining the wrist-rolling motion, they stomp their right foot backward and clap behind their back.
The individual stands with arms bent and raised, performing circular rolling motions with both wrists. They incorporate a variation where they touch their feet one at a time.

The person steps backward while raising their right arm high and swinging their left arm out and around, all while continuously rolling both wrists. They then bring both hands forward before swinging them to the side, maintaining the wrist rolls throughout the motion.
A person moves around and points in different directions.
The person is in a near-locking pose while rolling their left wrist.

A person performs a locking dance move, rolling their wrists in a circular motion before abruptly freezing. They hold the final pose with arms raised to shoulder height, elbows bent at ninety-degree angles and forearms pointing forward.
The person rolls their wrists around their head while standing.
With arms raised and elbows bent, the person performs a wrist roll by rotating their forearms in opposite directions, finishing as their arms and wrists lock into a new pose.

The person stands in a wide stance with their right arm at their side and their left arm bent at the elbow, forearm positioned in front of the torso. They raise their left arm up and to the side, then shake the forearm while rotating their body from side to side.
A person crouches with their right arm raised and their left arm extended to the side, rolling both wrists in a circular motion before locking them into place.

Starting from a squat, the person rolls their left wrist while swinging their arm across the body toward the right knee. Continuing to roll both wrists, they bring their hands together in front of the body and lift both elbows to shoulder height.

A person stands with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the torso. They move their arms out to the sides and back to the front twice, shaking them up and down throughout the motion.
Rolling the left wrist while moving the arm up and down.
Extend both arms while rolling the wrists, then close both arms. Repeat.
The person walks around, rolling their left wrist and swinging both arms side-to-side.
In a low, athletic stance with one leg forward, a person rolls their wrists in a circular motion near their shoulders, then extends their arms to the sides and snaps them into a locked position.

Starting from a left-leg lunge with the torso leaned forward, the person swings their right leg forward while dragging their left foot backward and swinging both bent elbows upward. After landing in a right-leg lunge, they turn their body to the left.

The person steps backward with their right leg and turns their body to the right. They then raise their right knee and swing it side-to-side. Throughout these leg movements, they roll their left wrist while swinging their left arm side-to-side and upward.
The person slowly raises their left arm to a middle position. Suddenly, they begin rolling their left forearm while continuing to lift it.

The person stands with their elbows bent at their sides and forearms pointing forward. They lift their left knee to the side and swing their left arm down to touch it, then repeat the movement on the right side. Finally, they step back while raising their arms with elbows bent and forearms pointing forward and down.

The person steps their right foot behind their left while lowering and rolling their left wrist. They then step forward with the right foot, raising and rolling their right wrist. Next, they lift their left knee and raise both elbows in a bent position. After stomping the left foot, they turn to the right and rhythmically swing their left knee inward and outward while shifting their weight to the left. Finally, they step forward on the right foot, raise their left elbow, and hop backward with both elbows raised and bent.

Starting in a squat with knees turned inward and elbows raised, the person rolls their forearms. They then slightly straighten their legs, leaning their torso to the right and twisting to the left while continuing the rolling motion.
intro windup: Starting in a T-pose with arms extended to the sides, the figure quickly snaps both arms up, bending at the elbows so the forearms are angled upward and inward.

The figure performs a quick wrist roll with the right arm while simultaneously executing a small step-tap with the right foot. This wind-up motion concludes with a sharp snap of the head and upper body into a fixed pose.

A person starts with their arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height, then lowers them to their sides. They then bend their elbows to point their forearms forward and rotate their torso slightly while performing alternating small side kicks, pulling each foot back after every kick.
Starting in a T-pose, the figure lowers both arms and immediately strikes a pose with the left arm extended sideways and the right hand positioned in front of the chest.

With the left arm raised straight sideways and the right hand held in front of the chest, the person lowers their arms. They then rhythmically swing their knees back and forth by bending them. From a standing stance, the person brings the left leg toward the right.
The person repeatedly taps their left foot on the ground while swinging both arms from right to left, then bounce rhythmically by bending their knees and swinging their arms from side to side.
The figure stands with feet apart and both arms bent and raised, performing a subtle right wrist roll while snapping its head to the right.
watergate: The figure starts with their elbows bent at their sides and forearms pointing forward. They slide forward and to the left, extending their arms straight ahead and downward. Next, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise and step out with the right leg, raising their arms to the sides with the elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They then rotate back to face forward and move their knees in and out while maintaining their arm position. Swinging their arms inward, they rotate slightly clockwise, pointing the right arm upward and the left downward in front of the body. They rotate back to face forward and thrust their arms out to the sides, with the left arm angled slightly up and the right down. Finally, they turn 90 degrees clockwise and lean forward slightly while swinging their arms at their sides, then rotate back to the front and step in place with their elbows bent and arms at their sides.

The dancer steps from side to side with bent knees, leading the movement with a circular hip roll. Their arms are held at different heights, moving in coordination with the rotation of the upper body.
The figure performs a circular hip rotation from right to left while stepping side to side. Throughout the movement, the arms remain bent, with the hands held near the chest and head.
lock up: The person takes a step back with their left leg while performing locking movements with both arms, featuring quick rolls and pops in both forearms.

The person stands in a staggered stance with their left leg forward and right leg back, both knees bent and arms at their sides with elbows bent. They rise and step backward while rotating 90 degrees clockwise. They extend their arms out to the sides at shoulder height with elbows bent and forearms pointing up, moving their forearms in an up-and-down motion.

Starting from a low reverse lunge with the left knee bent and elbows raised, the person shifts their hips to the right while rising quickly to a standing position. They then lock into an upright posture, holding both arms at shoulder level with the elbows bent.

Starting in a low right-leg lunge, the person quickly pops upward by extending their knees and raising both elbows high. Simultaneously, they roll their forearms and lock them into a rigid pose in front of the chest with bent elbows while dropping back into a squatting lunge stance.
Starting in a low stance with knees bent and arms out to the sides, the person performs a quick pop, extending their body upward while straightening their right leg.
random: Stepping forward with the right foot, the person brings their right arm up and across the body in a smooth motion. They then execute a sharp "lock" by extending the left arm to the side while holding the right arm bent across the chest in a brief, rigid pose.
The person settles into a relaxed position.

A person stands slightly bent forward with their elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They kick their left leg out and back three times while moving their arms in and out and rotating slightly counterclockwise.

A person steps to the left while extending both arms outward, then quickly locks them into bent positions at different heights. They briefly hold this rigid pose, with the right arm raised above the shoulder and the left arm held lower.

A person steps forward onto their left leg with fluidly swinging arms, then abruptly stops, locking into a rigid pose with the right knee lifted high, the right arm bent, and the left arm extended to the side.
