The person starts in a low, dynamic stance with extended limbs, their left arm raised and right leg back. They then quickly transition into a locked pose, pulling their arms together in front of their chest and bringing their feet closer together.
A person steps forward into a wide stance while raising both arms. They then sharply bend their elbows, locking their arms into a rigid pose with their hands near their shoulders.
A person moves their hips to the left.

After quickly swinging the left arm to the side and bringing it back to the body from a wide stance, the person quickly swings both forearms up and down in front of the body. Then, the person extends their right forearm to the right side while shifting their body weight to the left.
The person jumps upward with both arms held straight.
In a squat stance, the person swings both forearms up and down in front of their body while rhythmically swaying their torso from side to side.

The person pivots on their left leg, lifting their right knee high across their body while simultaneously raising their bent right arm and crossing their left arm over their chest, freezing in a compact, locked pose.
Starting from a crouched squat with the right hand raised and elbow bent, the figure swirls their hips while gently bouncing on their knees. They then step forward while continuing to swirl.
The person steps back with their left leg while throwing alternating forward punches.
A person stands with their arms at their sides, bends their elbows, and then pushes their arms forward while bending their knees.
A person executes a locking move, freezing in a dynamic pose with their left leg lifted back and up. The right arm is raised high with a bent elbow, while the left arm is tucked close to the body.

The person maintains a low, dynamic pose with their left leg bent forward and their right leg extended back. They perform a smooth, rhythmic body movement while keeping their arms locked in sharp, angular positions, with the left arm raised and the right arm held back.
The performer executes a locking move, sharply twisting their upper body to the left and freezing in a rigid pose with the left arm extended forward and the right arm pulled back.

Starting in a wide squat with arms extended to the sides and the torso leaned forward, a person sways their hips from side to side while raising their right arm straight up, maintaining the low stance throughout.

The person holds a rigid, locked pose with their left arm extended straight out to the side and their right arm bent. They maintain this static, slightly twisted stance with their legs apart, capturing the essence of a "lock."

The person stands with their arms raised diagonally upward and outward. They then bend their knees and lean forward, placing their hands on their knees, before rotating their torso 90 degrees counterclockwise and clapping in front of their body.
Standing with legs apart and arms bent in front of the chest, the person performs a smooth, rhythmic motion by lifting their upper body and head slightly upward and to the left.

A person smoothly steps to the right while raising their arms, then abruptly locks into a static, angular pose. This rigid position is held for a moment, with the right arm extended and the left arm bent across the chest.
A person raises their left knee and right arm while twisting their torso to the right, locking into a rigid pose with their left arm bent across their chest and a slight lean.
The person shakes their body with both elbows raised sideways, followed by a sudden step backward.

The performer steps forward onto the left leg while extending the right leg back. Simultaneously, they raise and bend both arms into a locked pose, with the left elbow pointing upward and the right arm held across the upper body.

The person rotates their raised right knee clockwise while swinging their right arm in a large, quick arc. They land with their left elbow raised and their right arm extended straight out to the side.
The person extends their arms forward, swings their shoulders, and reaches their right arm to the back.

Balancing on the left leg with both arms raised upward and forward, the person pushes their chest forward by pulling their arms down. After a brief body shake, they push their hips back by bending the left knee. They then turn their body by swinging the left leg sideways and moving the right leg backward.
Starting from a squatting position, the person raises their right hand forward and turns their body to the left while lightly bouncing their hips.
In a slight squat, the person moves forward by stepping only with their left leg while holding both hands in front of their body. Simultaneously, they clap and sway their shoulders.

Starting from a squatting position, the person moves backward by stepping back and rotating their torso. Simultaneously, they swing their right arm inward and outward while keeping their left arm bent with the elbow raised at shoulder height. They then lower their body, step forward, and clap.

The person steps forward with their left leg while simultaneously raising a bent right arm high and extending their left arm out to the side. The movement concludes by freezing in this pose, holding the arms rigidly in their bent, locked positions.
The dancer hops to the right and locks down while spreading their arms to the sides.
The person makes a knocking motion with their left hand.

Starting from a squat, the person jumps forward on both legs with their left arm extended straight ahead. They then perform a slow punch with their right arm while jumping forward in a squatting stance.

The person steps forward and across with their left leg while twisting their upper body to the right. Their right arm sweeps down and across the torso as the left arm extends backward, holding the final pose in a lock.
A person steps forward onto their right leg while lifting their bent left leg. They then freeze in a locked pose, raising their bent left arm and extending their right arm down and back.
A person stands slightly bent over with their arms at their sides and walks sideways, moving their left forearm and left knee out and back while vibrating their torso.
A person steps forward on their right leg and lifts their left knee high. Simultaneously, they raise their bent arms and freeze in an asymmetrical pose, with the right arm held higher than the left.

The person stands with their right leg forward and left leg back, arms hanging straight down. They then quickly bow while bending both their knees and elbows. Maintaining a hunched posture, they rotate their torso to the left while flexing and extending their arms.
scoobot: The individual twists their torso to the right while maintaining a low stance with the right leg forward and the left leg back. They perform a circular arm motion, raising their bent right arm forward and extending their left arm back.

The dancer performs large, scooping circles with bent arms in front of the chest, moving them upward and outward. Simultaneously, the torso twists from side to side, following the direction of the arms.
Maintaining a low stance with bent knees, the person twists their upper body from side to side while sweeping their arms in large circles in front of their torso.

A person performs a scoobot, circling their arms in opposite directions while twisting their torso. The right arm moves up and forward as the left arm moves down and back, with the legs remaining bent and grounded.

A person twists their torso from side to side while scooping their arms in large, circular motions in front of their body. The arms move alternately, with one rising as the other falls, in sync with the torso's rotation.

The person rolls their left forearm with the elbow raised and bent while extending their right arm straight sideways, simultaneously twisting and extending both legs. They then bring their arms together in front of their body. Next, the person performs a "scoobot" upper body movement while extending their right leg sideways and bending their left knee, before immediately switching the leg positions.

The person starts with their right leg extended sideways and their left knee bent. They roll their left forearm with the elbow raised while holding their right arm straight out. Next, they bring their legs together and clap their hands in front of their body. Finally, they swing their right leg backward and their right arm forward.

The person assumes a 'scoobot' stance with the left knee bent and the right leg extended sideways, while holding the right arm straight up and the left elbow at shoulder level. They then rotate into a right-leg lunge, crossing their arms in front of their chest. Finally, the person steps forward and squats while clapping behind their back. Throughout the entire sequence, they continuously roll their forearms in a circular motion.

The person sharply twists their torso to the left, raising their bent right arm near their head while extending their left arm. They maintain a low stance with bent knees as they step forward with their left foot.
Starting from a low, bent-knee stance, the person twists their torso from side to side as their arms follow the movement in large, circular scooping patterns in front of the body.

The person stretches their right leg straight backward while extending both arms sideways. Then, they bring their left leg to the right after punching forward. Immediately, the right leg extends to the left, crossing behind the left leg, with both elbows pulled back. The person then repeats these steps with the opposite leg while swinging their upper body.

The dancer stands bent over with their left foot back and arms in front, elbows bent with forearms held horizontally. They push their arms forward and lean back, kicking the right leg out while standing on the left foot, extending the left arm forward and keeping the right arm to the side with the elbow bent. Rapidly rotating their forearms, they step forward and throw their arms behind them. Stepping forward again, they swing their arms to the front to clap their hands.

The person extends their right leg and right arm straight to the side while keeping their left arm bent with the elbow at shoulder height. They then hop, pulling the right leg in and extending the left leg to the side while crossing their arms in front of their chest. Next, they kick their left leg forward and jump onto their right foot, lifting the right knee high while swinging both arms up and down. Finally, they land on their left foot and clap their hands in front of their chest.
uncle sams points: The person stands with their arms extended to their sides. They shake their left forearm to the left and their right forearm to the right, repeat the motion, and then stand with their arms at shoulder height and elbows bent so that their forearms point directly forward.

The person reaches their left arm straight up toward the sky while keeping their right arm bent. They then lower their left arm to point toward the ground while simultaneously dropping into a slight crouch.

The person stands with their upper arms extended to the sides and elbows bent, positioning their forearms in front of their torso. They straighten their arms out to the sides, then bend their elbows to bring the forearms back toward the torso. Next, they lower and straighten the right arm while raising the bent left arm. They then push both arms forward until fully extended and finally move them straight out to the sides at shoulder height, all while keeping their legs still.
A person stands in a wide, crouched stance, extending their left arm straight up toward the sky while simultaneously pointing their right arm straight down at the ground.

Starting with their hands in front of their body, the person extends their arms to the sides. They then move their right arm forward and their left arm down before quickly swapping their arm positions.
Starting from a low stance, a person points their right arm straight up toward the sky, then swings it downward in an arc to point at the ground in front of them.

Starting in a wide right-leg lunge, the person swings their left arm in a broad horizontal arc backward while turning their body. After rolling the left forearm, they extend both arms and legs wide to the sides. They then swing both arms straight forward before kneeling down with their forearms crossed in front of their body.

A person stands in a wide stance with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They straighten the left arm to the left, then punch it across their body to the right. They then straighten the arm, return it to its original position, and lower it. Finally, they slightly straighten the arm and hold the bent forearm in front of their torso. Throughout the sequence, the right arm and legs remain stationary.

The person quickly extends their left arm straight sideways, followed by the right. With both arms extended, the left forearm is quickly folded before rolling the left wrist. Then, the person rolls both wrists with their elbows raised at shoulder level. Throughout these dynamic upper body movements, the person twists their knees and feet.
A person stands with their arms pointing to the left. They rapidly push their arms downward and forward several times before pushing them back and straightening them.
In a wide stance with bent knees, the figure raises their right arm toward the sky and then swiftly lowers it to point at the ground, while keeping the left arm extended to the side.
The person points their arms to the sides, kicks their right leg forward, and points.
Both arms point in different directions.
A person stands and points both arms forward, then to the sides, and forward again.

The figure stands with their hands together in front of their torso and elbows bent. They swing their arms out to the sides at shoulder height, then bring the left arm across their body and back out to the side.

Starting from a standing position, the person extends their left arm straight forward while holding their right arm downward. They then extend both arms straight out to the sides and bring them back toward the body. Finally, the person repeatedly folds and extends their left arm side-to-side while holding their right arm straight out to the side.

The person rhythmically taps their left foot while pointing outward with one hand. First, they extend their right arm sideways and bring their left hand to their left shoulder, then extend their left arm forward. Finally, the right hand moves to the left hip, and the left arm extends sideways followed by the right arm to end in a T-pose. The left foot continues to tap rhythmically throughout the entire arm sequence.

The person stands with their elbows bent and forearms in front of their torso. They rotate their torso to the side and back while shaking their right forearm at their side. As they rotate, they punch their left forearm forward, then down and forward once turned, keeping it pointed forward as they rotate back.
The person quickly swings both arms down in front of their hips, out to the sides, and then back to their body while bringing their legs together.
From a crouched position, the person raises their right arm to point straight up, then quickly lowers it to point directly at the ground.

The person quickly transitions through several poses: first, a wide-legged stance with the right arm pointed straight down and the left arm forward; second, a squat with the right arm extended to the side and the left arm resting on the right knee; and third, a star pose. Finally, the person ends in a standing position.
A person stands in a low, wide-legged stance and sharply raises their right arm to point toward the sky. They then swiftly swing the arm down, bending at the torso to point toward the ground.
While stomping the right foot, the person extends their left arm straight out to the side and then brings it down. Then, they swing their right arm straight forward.
Starting from a wide, low stance, the person raises their right arm diagonally upward and then brings it down to point toward the ground while maintaining their low posture.
The person points their left hand in various directions.

The individual stands with their arms at shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing toward the hips. They shake their arms in various directions in front of their body, then thrust them straight back while sliding their feet from side to side.

A person performs the "Uncle Sam's point" locking move, first extending their right arm to point toward the sky. They then lower the same arm to point toward the ground while keeping their left arm extended out to the side.
A person holds a pose, twisted to the left with their right arm extended to the side, then suddenly breaks into an aggressive motion, swinging both arms up and down.
A person steps forward and brings the other foot to meet it in a pacing motion. Throughout the movement, the arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows, characteristic of a locking style.
toe tap: The figure stands with its left arm lowered and right arm raised, both bent at the elbow. It lifts its right knee, then extends the leg forward to tap its toe on the ground.

A person performs a toe tap with their left foot, bending the left knee while keeping the right leg straight for support. Their arms are held out to the sides with bent elbows in a characteristic locking stance, with the right arm raised higher than the left.
backward clap: The person rolls their forearms with elbows raised at shoulder level while simultaneously kicking their left leg backward, then claps behind their back and stomps their right foot forward.
Starting in a T-pose with legs slightly bent, the individual swings both arms backward to clap behind their back before returning to the initial position.
With knees bent, a person rhythmically swings their arms from front to back, clapping behind their torso. They maintain a continuous bounce, leaning forward slightly with each clap.
strong man: A person performs a "strong man" pose, holding both arms up and bent as if flexing. They briefly extend their arms outward before retracting them into a new flexed position.
A person raises their arms into a "strongman" flexing pose, then pulls their elbows slightly up and back to lock the position.
lock down: The individual maintains a rigid, crouched pose with their torso bent forward and both arms flexed near their head, rapidly dropping down and rising back up several times.

The individual stands with their arms raised to shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They step to the side while shaking their arms, bending their elbows and lowering them slightly.

A person stands with their arms at their sides, the left arm bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing toward the torso. They thrust their arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height, then lean forward slightly, bending their elbows so that their forearms point downward.

The person maintains a slightly hunched upper body posture with both elbows raised and pointed downward. They perform a light, quick hop on the right leg, then step backward while lightly swinging their elbows up and down, maintaining the same posture throughout.

The figure stands slightly hunched with their arms extended at shoulder height, elbows bent and forearms pointing downward. They vibrate in place while maintaining this posture, shaking their right arm more intensely than their left.
The individual strikes a rigid, slightly crouched pose with both arms raised and bent at different heights, then quickly drops straight down from the locked position.

The individual starts in a wide stance with arms extended to the sides, elbows bent, and forearms pointing downward. They step their feet together while rapidly moving their forearms, then pull their arms to their sides and extend them backward while leaning forward slightly. Finally, they return their arms to the starting position.

A person locks their body in a rigid pose with arms extended to the sides, then quickly drops into a low crouch by bending at the hips and knees. The upper body and arms remain still and locked in place throughout the rapid downward motion.
The person starts with their arms locked out to the sides. They then quickly drop their body by bending at the knees and hips while pulling their arms inward.
The person drops into a low crouch, bending at the knees and hips while leaning their torso forward. They maintain a rigid pose with their arms bent and locked to the sides and front.
The dancer locks their arms in a rigid pose and quickly drops into a low squat, landing with their body bent forward while maintaining the locked arm position near the ground.

The person stands in a wide stance with their arms out to the sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing upward. They lean slightly forward and to the right, lifting the left leg and bending it at the knee so it points backward while maintaining their arm position.
A person performs a locking motion while crossing their left foot over their right, followed by their right foot over their left.

A person stands with their arms out to the sides and elbows bent so that their forearms are in front of their upper arms. They lean forward and shake their arms slightly. While remaining bent over, they rotate their torso 90 degrees clockwise and repeat the shaking motion, then return to the original forward-facing position and repeat the movement once more.
A person rolls their hands up and down while stepping around.
funky broadway: The individual bounces in place with bent knees, holding their arms out to the sides with elbows bent at 90-degree angles while performing an alternating shoulder shimmy.
The dancer walks in place with a funky bounce, rhythmically lifting their knees high. They hold their arms out with bent elbows, accentuating the movement with continuous shoulder shimmies.
A person walks in place with a funky bounce, rhythmically bending their knees while shimmying their shoulders and pumping their bent arms up and down in front of their chest.
pose: The person suddenly kicks their right leg forward and raises both hands straight up while rolling their forearms. They then hold that pose for a moment without moving.
A person twists their torso to the right and extends their left forearm sideways, then holds a rigid pose with bent knees for a moment.

The individual strikes a dynamic pose with legs slightly apart and bent, holding the position rigidly. The left arm is raised high and bent, while the right arm is bent and held lower across the chest.

The person quickly transitions from a standing position into a locked pose, leaning to the right. They hold this rigid stance with the left knee raised, the left arm bent high toward the shoulder, and the right arm bent at a lower angle.
twirl: A person stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They jump backward while turning 90 degrees clockwise, moving their forearms out to the sides.
A person stands upright with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing forward. They slightly raise their forearms and shake them.
A person performs a locking dance move featuring rapid wrist twirls. The forearms roll in mirrored circular motions while the torso rotates as the arms extend forward and backward.
With elbows raised to shoulder height, the person quickly rolls both forearms in a circular motion.
Starting in a crouched, locked pose with bent and raised arms, the person performs a full 360-degree spin in place while maintaining this fixed posture.

The individual stands with their arms extended to the sides and steps back while bending their elbows to point their forearms upward. They then turn 90 degrees clockwise and point their arms straight back.

A person begins in a locked pose with both arms bent and raised, holding the right arm higher than the left. They then perform a clockwise twirl in place, crouching slightly while maintaining their arm position.

A person begins in a slight forward bend with their arms at their sides. They stand up, kicking their left leg forward while thrusting their arms upward. They then step their left foot to the side and rotate 45 degrees counterclockwise, extending their arms to the sides with elbows bent and forearms pointing downward.
The person rolls their wrists while swinging their bent elbows from head height straight down. Simultaneously, they move to the right by pushing off with their left leg and twisting their right foot.
funky chicken: The person alternates kicking each foot backward in an arc from the side to behind the hip while maintaining a hunched torso with their hands and elbows held in front of the body. They then extend both arms straight out to the sides while continuing the same kicking motion.
A person performs the funky chicken by bending their elbows, keeping their hands near their shoulders, and flapping their arms up and down while simultaneously kicking back one foot at a time.
The performer bends their elbows and flaps their arms like chicken wings while rhythmically swaying their hips from side to side.

The dancer stands with their arms at their sides and elbows bent. They bend slightly and lower their forearms, then rise and lean to the right, lifting the left foot and setting it back down. They then lift the right foot and set it back down, leaning to the side and lifting the foot between their hands in front of the body. They rotate their torso counterclockwise and lift the left knee, slapping the thigh against the left forearm. Next, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise and step back, lifting the right knee while circling their forearms over it. Finally, they rotate counterclockwise to face forward again, stepping the right leg forward and dropping their arms to their sides to point slightly backward.
forward clap: A person stands in a wide stance with their arms at their sides, pointing forward. They lean to the side and swing their arms up and down in front of their body.
pimp walk: The figure walks forward with a distinct swagger, swaying their hips and upper body from side to side with each step. Their arms swing widely in opposition to their legs, accentuating the stylized and rhythmic gait.
The person takes a step and then sways their knees.
pace: The performer stands slightly hunched over with their elbows bent and forearms held horizontally in front of the torso. Leaning to the left, they thrust their right leg and left arm straight out while balancing on the left foot. They then straighten and step the right foot forward, leaning slightly to the right while extending their arms straight back. Finally, they rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and raise their arms above their head with the elbows bent and forearms pointing upward.

A person stands in a wide stance with their knees turned inward, leaning forward with their arms extended to the sides and elbows bent so the forearms point downward. They slide to the right and kick their right leg out, then straighten up and point their arms directly in front of their body. They jump and land back in a wide stance, bending over with the right arm extended to the side and the left hand touching the ground. Finally, while remaining bent over, they thrust their left arm out to the side at shoulder height.

The person stands with their arms extended forward and shakes their forearms. They then turn slightly counterclockwise, extending their right arm straight out at shoulder height while holding their left arm out to the side, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing upward.

The performer stands in a wide stance with their arms at their sides and forearms pointing forward. They kick the right leg out, then bend the knee to bring it back in while simultaneously straightening their arms in front of the body. Next, they step the right leg in front of the left and thrust their arms out to the sides at shoulder height before dropping them back down.

A person stands with their right arm at their side and their left arm extended to the side, bent at the elbow with the forearm pointing forward. They rapidly shake their left forearm several times before bringing the arm across their torso and stepping to the right with their right leg.

Starting in a wide squat with elbows raised to shoulder height, the person quickly extends both forearms. They then swing their right leg straight out to the side while bending and straightening their left elbow. Finally, they tap their right foot on the ground in front of their left foot.

The person kicks their right leg straight out to the side while quickly swinging their left arm to the left, then immediately brings their right knee and left hand together in front of their body. As they stomp their right foot down, they raise their right arm straight back and their left arm forward.

The person stands with their arms at their sides, the left elbow bent and the forearm pointing forward. They rotate their torso 90 degrees clockwise and punch the left arm forward, then kneel while rotating back to the original position, extending the left arm straight out to the side. They then straighten and jump, lifting their arms with bent elbows and pointing their forearms forward. Upon landing, they rotate 90 degrees clockwise, drop their arms to their sides, and lean slightly forward.
funky guitar: The person takes a low, wide stance with bent knees, leaning slightly forward and to the right. They mimic playing a guitar, fretting with their left hand near the chest and strumming downward with their right arm.
leo walk: A person performs a stylized walk forward, kicking their left leg out while twisting their hips. Their arms are bent and raised, moving in coordination with each step.
The individual performs a stylized forward walk, twisting their hips with each step. As one leg kicks slightly forward, their bent arms are held up and move in opposition.
locking hop: The person hops on their right foot with a high left knee and a forward-leaning torso, holding a characteristic locking pose with their arms bent and frozen at different heights near the head and shoulders.
A person hops forward on their right foot with their left knee raised high, maintaining a locked pose with both arms extended to the sides.
The dancer hops on their right foot in a bouncing motion. Throughout the movement, the left leg is held up with a bent knee, and both arms are locked in a fixed position with bent elbows.
A person hops on their right foot while maintaining a locked pose. The left leg is raised and bent with the knee turned out, while both arms are held up and bent near the shoulders.
The person performs a small hop on their left foot while maintaining a locked pose. Their right leg is raised and bent, and their arms are held up with the elbows bent in a stylized manner.
The performer hops on their left foot while lifting their right knee high in front. Their arms are held out to the sides, bent at the elbows in a locked, static pose.
gimme 5: Starting from a crouched stance with both arms bent and raised, the person swiftly extends their left arm straight up as if giving a high five. Simultaneously, they lower their right arm toward their chest while rising into a more upright position.

bruh what: A person stands with their arms at their sides, elbows bent and forearms pointing down. They shake their arms up and down while walking backward.
